publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: The task of infrared and visible image fusion aims to generate a single image that integrates salient features and rich texture details from multi-source inputs. Ideally, the fused image should preserve the thermal radiation information from the infrared image and the texture details from the visible image. However, the fusion process often inevitably introduces certain noise from the infrared image, leading to the loss of local texture information. To address these issues, we propose an adaptive feature fusion-based network. Specifically, the network emphasizes meaningful infrared brightness information while suppressing high-frequency noise, thereby preserving local texture details. We conducted the evaluation using subjective assessment and objective indicators on the benchmark dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance on public datasets.

title: Backtracking-based Error Correction for Ultra-short-term Power Forecasting of Wind Farm Cluster
authors: Jingwen Liu, Qingyi Liang, Yanxu Chen, Yangfan Xia, Yongning Zhao
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Existing methods for correcting wind power forecasting errors ignore the underlying mechanism that generate these errors, which is not conducive to achieving precise correction and model optimization. Especially in wind farm cluster forecasting, the difficulty of error correction is further exacerbated. Therefore, this paper uses the regression tree algorithm to trace the generation mechanism of the errors. By extracting the maximum error path through backtracking analysis, this path is introduced as a new structural feature into the prediction model for multi-round iterative optimization. Experiments show that this method improves the accuracy of power prediction in cluster fields by backtracking errors and enhancing the model’s ability to perceive areas with high errors.

title: Adaptive Dual Fusion Network for All-in-One Image Restoration
authors: Tao Gao, Yuhan Cao, Yunqiu Lyu, Yuanbo Wen, Ting Chen
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Addressing degradations in a unified network is the goal of all-in-one image restoration. Due to the diversity of corruptions such as rain, haze, and noise, this is a major challenge. Although existing methods have made significant progress, they primarily operate in spatial domain, ignoring the unique characteristics that different degradations exhibit in frequency domain. To this end, we propose an adaptive dual fusion network (ADFNet), which aims to synergistically utilize features from both spatial and frequency domains. Adaptive dual fusion module (ADFM) is the central component of ADFNet. ADFM leverages spatial and frequency features through spatial branch and frequency branch, dynamically fusing them through adaptive gating. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ADFNet achieves state-of-the-art performance in image restoration.

title: EBIPFL: An Efficient Cloud-Edge Collaborative Federated Learning Approach that Supports Bidirectional Integrity Protection
authors: Cheng Qian, Li Lin, Kang Yang
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: Cloud-edge collaborative federated learning allows user terminals, edge servers and the cloud to jointly train a model without exchanging raw data, thus preserving privacy. However, traditional schemes weight updates only by dataset size, ignoring edge-training accuracy and inference-cost sensitivity, which leads to high classification expense and low edge accuracy. Meanwhile, tampered updates and highly skewed sample sizes further degrade performance. To address these issues, this paper proposes an Efficient cloud-edge federated-learning framework with bidirectional integrity protection called EBIPFL, where Merkle trees are employed to verify the integrity of encrypted model updates in both uplink and downlink directions. A lightweight small-sample partitioning strategy is presented to reduce on-device workload, while the aggregation rule fuses cost-sensitive error, precision, recall and trainer count to produce a more informed global model. Finally, theoretical analysis and experimental evaluations are conducted. The results demonstrate that EBIPFL not only improves model accuracy while maintaining acceptable storage and time costs, but also effectively defends against tampered update attacks.

title: Living Off the Land Attacks: Detection and Mitigation Strategies
authors: Onkar Mane, Yogita Hande, Anusha Pai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Living Off the Land (LOTL) attacks utilize legitimate system tools, including PowerShell, Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI), and scheduled tasks, to create malicious activity without being caught via traditional detection means. As attacks that operate with stealth and integrate themselves within systematic operations, LOTL is an extreme threat to cybersecurity and is particularly problematic in critical infrastructure contexts. The paper reviews LOTL attacks, including definitions, classifications, detection strategies, challenges, tools and frameworks, incidents, and prevention. Using academic sources, industry reports, and standards and guidance, we reveal the current state of LOTL detection and response by cybersecurity professionals and industries. We show that LOTL attacks are detected and classified best with behavioral analysis, machine learning, and logging, while recommending the use of resilience through multi-layer security. We also highlight considerable gaps in research and future directions.

title: An Interpretable Machine Learning Approach For Heart Disease Detection Using Explainable Ai Techniques
authors: Babby E, K. Rajakumari, Karthik V
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: Given the inadequacies in progressing. patient monitoring and diagnostic accuracy in. traditional healthcare services, early diagnosis and reduction of mortality rate is largely dependent on precise heart. disease prediction. To find out cardiac disease. this research examines the use of machine at an early age. XGBoost and Naive are the most common learning methods. Bayes. To identify the most significant features, it was necessary to define which features were most important. relevant to prediction, there were three subsets of features. The selection of (SF-1, SF-2, and SF-3) was through chi-square. ANOVA, and mutual information techniques. Synthetic It was Minority Oversampling Technique or SMOTE. in preprocessing in order to solve the imbalance of data. In addition to a number of cross-validation methods, all of which are public. The algorithms were evaluated using and private datasets. performance. According to experimental data, the SF-2 feature subset in conjunction with the XGBoost algorithm produced the best results, with 99.57% accuracy, 96.61% sensitivity, 90.48% specificity, 95.00% precision, a 92.68% F1-score, and a 98% AUC. XGBoost turned out to serve as the ideal predictors of heart disease, and Naive Bayes was fairly mediocre. We interpreted explain the behavior and importance of features in a SHAP to. offer openness in model selection.

title: Enterprise Carbon Emission Monitoring Method Based on Non-intrusive Perception of Industrial Load Operation Status
authors: Weitao Zheng, Xiang Yu, Qian Wu, Dong Wang, Jiahao Liu, Taojing Zhang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Real-time and accurate enterprise carbon emission monitoring is a key tool for shifting the “dual carbon” plan at the macro level to its implementation at the micro level. Traditional carbon emission monitoring methods ignore the production processes within enterprises, resulting in problems of poor explainability and high uncertainty. This paper considers the volatility of the total load power and proposes an enterprise carbon emission monitoring method based on non-invasive perception of the operating status of industrial loads. Firstly, the total load power data is extracted through the fast Fourier transform to extract the volatility characteristics of the aggregated power. Secondly, a deep learning model integrating CNN and Transformer was proposed, achieving the extraction and fusion of local and global features of the aggregated power of the total load. Again, integrate the time-frequency characteristics of the aggregated power data of the total load, and input the aggregated power and frequency curves of the total load into the CNN-Transformer model respectively to achieve the identification of the load operation status. Finally, the obtained load operation status is combined with the load carbon emission factors under different operation statuses to achieve real-time monitoring of the enterprise’s carbon emissions. The algorithm was verified using public datasets, and the results demonstrated the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Image Tampering Detection Using Error Level Analysis with SVM and CNN Models
authors: G Vimal Subramanian, D V Rahul Yadav, B Neeraj Kumar, S Sumanth Kumar Reddy, I Venkata Nithin
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The swift expansion of the online world in the form of social media, news, medicine, and forensic evidence has intensified scrutiny of image authenticity. Today, digital image editing software is highly sophisticated and the abundance of editing software has made even subtle image manipulation very difficult to detect as it is almost undetectable by the human eye. Therefore, dependably detecting manipulated imagery is critical. This study presents a hybrid machine learning framework for image tampering detection from images based on both classical and deep learning models. Error Level Analysis (ELA) is used to extract compression artefacts that are differentiating features that permit us to identify tampering in images. Two models were developed. One model is a Support Vector Machine (SVM) designed to accept ELA histograms as input to be recognized as a lightweight and interpretable classification scheme. The second model was a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) that accepts the tampered iteration and authentic/class as input. For the CNN we relied on ELA to supply the complex spatial features of the image that the researchers extracted for identification recognition purposes from tampered/content-class images. The system was evaluated for its robustness and accuracy distinguishing authentic from manipulated images using a benchmarked dataset - CASIA v2. As an additional demonstration of a reliable, user-friendly approach to EV detection, the selected/evaluated models were encapsulated into a website in which ELA would be applied to images accessed via a public-facing website. As a complete solution, the current framework offers a reliable and scalable implementation contributing to the literature and to the field of digital image forensics addressing an important aspect of the digital ecosystem.

title: ICAFT 2025 Opinion
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)

title: Comparative Analysis of Reinforcement Learning, LQR, and MLP Control Methods for Pupil Tracking in Teleophthalmology
authors: Ali Sultan Mayya, Zein Mohammad Moussa
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: lens motion control. Previous work modeled a direct-drive lens positioning system and evaluated Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR) and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) controllers for automated pupil tracking under fixed simulation conditions. This study extends the existing framework by introducing a reinforcement learning (RL)–based control strategy and performing a direct comparison with the previously reported methods. The RL controller is trained entirely in simulation using the same state-space model, reference trajectories, and evaluation metrics to ensure methodological consistency. Comparative results show that while LQR and MLP controllers provide stable and reliable tracking under nominal dynamics, the RL controller demonstrates competitive performance and reveals both strengths and limitations related to training convergence and reward design. These findings highlight the potential of reinforcement learning as a flexible and extensible control approach for future teleophthalmology systems, motivating further investigation into improved training strategies and hybrid control formulations.

title: A Novel Deep Q-network-based Energy Optimization and Management of Micro-energy Network
authors: Yubo Song, Chen Yang, Leiming Suo, Yachao Yang, Shun Zhang, Jinrui Tang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: As the energy structure transforms towards decarbonization and intelligence, its high uncertainty and complexity lead to unreasonable dispatch strategies for traditional energy optimization and management methods in the micro-energy network. In this paper, a deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based method is proposed for dispatch the various energy generation devices of micro-energy network. The multi-energy cooperative scheduling problem among these various energy generation devices is derived as a Markov decision process (MDP) with a seven-dimensional continuous state space and a three-dimensional discrete action space. The economic cost, security penalty, and heuristic guidance terms are innovatively integrated into a composite reward function to drive the intelligent body to learn a strategy that considers both economy and security. The deep Q-networks (DQN) algorithm is adopted to overcome the dimensional catastrophe problem in a high-dimensional state space. This is achieved through a dual-network architecture, an experience playback mechanism, and a parameter freezing mechanism. These components enable adaptive decision-making in dynamic environments. Furthermore, performance comparisons between DQN (Deep Q-networks) and Q-learning algorithms in micro-energy network management reveal that deep reinforcement learning is more feasible and superior to standard reinforcement learning for such applications.

title: Logistic Regression Optimization for Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: Anant Sahoo, Deep Patel, Sulaiman Mohyuddin, Sruthi Balammagary, Abubakar Mohammed, Kavitha Janamolla
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Credit card fraud remains a major concern in today’s digital financial systems. This study presents a Logistic Regression (LR) model for detecting fraudulent credit card transactions using a publicly available dataset from Kaggle. The dataset, containing over 200,000 records, was preprocessed by handling missing values, encoding categorical features, and scaling numerical variables to ensure consistency. The baseline model, trained with ten-fold cross-validation, achieved an accuracy of 92.4% and a false positive rate near 1%. To further improve performance, enhancements such as feature scaling, Principal Component Analysis (PCA), dataset stratification, and Lasso (L1) regularization were implemented. The refined model achieved an accuracy of 94.7%, precision of 91.4%, recall of 64.1%, and an F1-score of 75.4%. These results demonstrate that an optimized Logistic Regression model can effectively balance accuracy, interpretability, and computational efficiency, offering a reliable foundation for large-scale financial fraud detection and future work involving hybrid or ensemble methods.

title: Federated Learning–Driven Medical Chatbot for Privacy-Preserving Symptom Assessment
authors: U. Naresh Kumar, A. Ramesh Babu, Yerva Yasmithareddy, Kuluru Reddi Lakshmi, Mosa Venkata Siva, Kurapati Sai Harshith
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: To meet these challenges, this research introduces an artificial intelligence (AI)-based medical chatbot model which may predict infectious diseases and provide interactive medical aid. The chatbot is able to provide correct, context-based answers. because the system has an LSTM algorithm that was trained with a chosen set of medical question-answers. With the Google Translation API integrated, the chatbot will be able to respond in English as well as Telugu, and it can be accessed through voice or text interfaces. The 99% training accuracy of the model indicates that it can easily handle a vast array of user queries. This chatbot is an excellent device for making easy access to medical assistance available because it possesses modules to register, login, fetch conversation history, and converse in real-time.Smart, global health chatbot that enhance the provision of healthcare, especially in the environment with restricted resources, can be developed by combining deep learning with natural language processing, as exemplified in this work.

title: Evaluating a Word-Level LSTM Encoder–Decoder Model for English–Telugu Translation
authors: Panja Naga Laxmi, Ramesh Dadi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: English-Telugu neural machine translation is a hard task, mostly because of the lack of parallel corpora and morphological drastically different languages. Although modern models are rapidly starting to make use of large transformer-based models, the applicability and sturdiness of antique recurrent models in low-resource conditions are yet to be thoroughly comprehended. The proposed research is a systematic analysis of a word-level LSTM decoder-encoder model to translate English to Telugu, as opposed to making an advanced system suggestion. The methodology uses the unidirectional LSTM architecture combined with greedy decoding and planned minimum architectural adjustment. The choice of the design also allows a detailed analysis of learning processes, but it removes confounding factors including attention processes or extensive pre-training. The training is performed on the selective curated parallel corpus and the performance is measured through the universally adopted automatic tools, such as BLEU and ROUGE, to measure the consistency of lexical fidelity and n-gram overlap. The experimental findings prove the model to be capable of producing stable and competitive results in a low-resource environment, both in terms of learning lexical alignment and acceptable fluency. The importance of these results to practitioners is that recurrent neural models remain practical in translation activities that deem computational efficiency, interpretability of their models, and controlled experimentation as the most important. The work provides a stable basis of further improvements to English-Telugu neural machine translation and gives informed architectural choices in low-resource NMT studies by clearly defining both the strengths and weaknesses of this strategy.

title: XAI-Enhanced Regression Framework: Dietary and Lifestyle Determinants of Weight Prediction
authors: S. Vairachilai, Addagudi Shruthi, Nimmagadda Shree Deepthi
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The combination of various interactions between diet, sleep, stress, metabolism and behavior makes it hard to accurately predict the human body weight using the food intake and the lifestyle patterns. As digital health tracking emerges, machine learning (ML) provides a data-driven alternative to traditional models of calorie-balance, but most ML models cannot be exactly interpreted to be used in clinics. An explainable ML regression pipeline with the publicly available dataset of Comprehensive Weight Change Prediction (including demographic, dietary, lifestyle, physical activity, and baseline weight data) was used in this work. Ten regression models were compared based on MAE, MSE, RMSE, R 2, residual analysis, and learning curve with preprocessing (imputation, encoding, normalization, feature screening, and 80/20 split). Prediction interpretation Explainable AI techniques (SHAP, LIME, PDP and ICE) were applied to identify predictive variables, with the significant contribution of baseline weight, caloric intake, macro-nutrients, sleep quality, and stress. Lasso Regression performed the best (RMSE 0.0213, R2) which demonstrates that there is a sufficient linear relation within the data. Findings indicate that lightweight regularized models with XAI methods have the potential to provide effective, transparent, and clinically valuable weight-prediction systems to personalized nutrition and digital healthcare systems.

title: Enhancing Bone Fracture Classification in X-Ray Using Deep Learning Model
authors: A M Balamurugan, R Muniyappan, G D Vignesh, N Mohammed Sulaiman
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Failure to accurately and timely detect bone fractures leads to inefficient patient treatments; nevertheless, the manual interpretation of X-ray images is often a very long process and is also susceptible to human errors. Therefore, this research work has come up with a deep learning-based solution that can automatically locate bone fractures. The goal of this technology is to provide additional support to radiologists and orthopedic doctors through increased accuracy of identification and shortened interpretation time. The system is equipped with a new proprietary convolutional neural network (CNN) design with residual blocks that has been proposed and implemented. Simply put, the new addition of residual learning to the CNN, as opposed to the usage of traditional pre-trained networks like VGG16 and MobileNet, allows for better handling of the complex patterns of the X-ray images. In total, 8,923 X-ray images that show either a fracture or no fracture were used for the training and validation stages. The experimental results indicate that the suggested model achieves an accuracy of 84% which is 10% higher than that of VGG16 (74%) and 12% higher than that of MobileNet (72%). Also, it demonstrates better precision, recall, and F1 score, in particular, for fracture identification.

title: The Prospects of the Modal Control Method in Special Technological Equipment Automation
authors: Anastasiya V. Los, Alexey A. Gelashvili
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: The article considers the problem of modal control. The theoretical survey of the modal control method in the technological processes automaton tasks is carried out. The perspectives of applying the given method in the tasks of special technological equipment automaton are studied and the type of engineering tasks for which the modal control method is more effective is defined. The mathematical model of modal regulator is developed and the modelling of modal control by means of climate camera is presented. The efficiency and expediency of the method under study for the given tasks in terns of ensuring the quality indicators is shown by practical examples. The problems of synthesis and realization procedures are investigated and the ways of solving the problems are proposed.

title: Optimized ResNet50-Based Framework for Diagnostic Accuracy in Thoracic Radiograph Interpretation
authors: Shaffu, Ruchi, Gaurav Sharma, Jhulan Kumar, Ramy Riad Al-Fatlawy, Anurag Shrivastava, Deepak Banerjee
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Diseases of the lung such as pneumonia and opacities are among the major causes of morbidity and mortality globally. The diagnosis of these conditions is commonly performed using chest X-ray imaging, but since manual analysis may be time-consuming and prone to errors, automated methods are increasingly desired. To classify lung diseases into three categories, namely, Normal, Lung Opacity and Viral Pneumonia, this paper will suggest a refined ResNet50 model. The data, which is downloaded on the open-source will be presented on Kaggle, are 3,475 chest X-ray images spread among the three classes. Preprocessing (image resizing, image normalization, image augmentation) was used to enhance the model robustness. Instead, the ResNet50 backbone served as a feature extractor, and further fully connected, batch normalization, dropout, and softmax layers were added to improve classification. The model had a total accuracy of 96% and equal precision, recall and F1-scores across all categories. Analysis of the confusion matrix showed a high recognition rate, with few misclassifications between Lung Opacity and Viral Pneumonia. Effective convergence with no overfitting was verified with training and validation curves. These results indicate that the fine-tuned ResNet50 is a strong and effective framework to perform automated lung disease detection, and it has a great potential as a computer-aided diagnostic system to assist radiologists, as well as to improve clinical decision-making.

title: Early Detection of Oral Cancer Using Deep Learning
authors: Shikhar Singh, Ajay Kumar, Kakoli Banerjee, Harsh Pandey, Ayush Kumar Singh, Akash Yadav
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The article is focused on applying data-driven methods for the pre diagnosis of oral cancer based on image data. Oral cancer is the fourth most frequently identified cancer in the world, and early and accurate diagnosis is essential for improving patient survival. The research strives to develop an approach of efficient automated high-throughput model using algorithms such as: Convolutional Neural Network, K-Nearest Neighbours, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, and Gradient Boosting, to categorize oral images as malignant or benign. The research includes experimentation of these models on commonly used performance metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score. The research found the CNN deep learning model had the highest accuracy of 93.4%, because of superior feature extraction capabilities, compared to the other algorithms. This work provides a framework to assist pathologists and healthcare providers with expedited and accurate diagnosis of oral cancer using AI-based image analysis.

title: Posit Arithmetic Multiplier for High-Speed and Low-Energy Applications
authors: Ramanamma Parepalli, Sudhakiran Ponnuru, Rahul, Yashas L, Shashank M Gundad, Rohit Varale
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This work presents a high-performance, low-energy posit arithmetic multiplier tailored for visual contrast enhancement and image processing applications, effectively balancing accuracy and energy efficiency. Conventional IEEE 754 floating-point multipliers are often inefficient for such workloads, as their fixed precision results in higher power consumption and limited adaptability to the irregular data patterns commonly encountered in vision applications. The posit number system offers a compelling alternative due to its flexible regime–exponent–mantissa representation, which adjusts precision based on input data and delivers greater accuracy with a wider dynamic range at the same bit width. To further improve energy efficiency, particularly when the mantissa width is small, we propose a modular multiplier architecture. In this design, mantissa multiplication is divided into smaller units that are selectively activated according to the regime width, ensuring that only the necessary hardware is engaged at any moment. Key optimizations include an adder-based leading-one detector to reduce delay and area, modified Booth encoding for efficient partial product generation, and refined control logic to lower dynamic power consumption. Experimental synthesis results indicate energy savings of 28–30% and delay reductions of up to 18% for Posit (8,0), Posit (16,1), and Posit (32,2). These gains highlight the suitability of the proposed multiplier for real-time embedded image processing in wearable and edge AI systems, where energy efficiency and computational precision must be carefully balanced.

title: A Chunk-Based Cryptographic Framework for Detecting File Content Discrepancies in Cloud Storage Systems
authors: Lokesh Khedekar, Samyak Dawale, Tejas Joshi, Anish Kadu, Prathmesh Kalaskar, Yash Kathoke
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: This paper reports a large scale and secure cloud file integrity which the implementation put forward to protect out hosted data against illegal changes, attacks, or system failures. The proposed system addresses the issue of present cloud storage which does not have real time verification. System use cryptographic hashing (SHA-256/SHA-512) with a chunk-based approach to manage large files which also sees to that do not have delays. At the time of upload, the system has reference hash and metadata in a secure way. The proposed system always does a periodic or on demand check by which it generates a new hash and compares it with the stored reference. The proposed system has real time alert features, secure audit trails, versioning, and support for many clouds (AWS S3, Azure Blob, Google Cloud Storage). The system leverages Python for hashing, Node.js for interfaces, and MongoDB for managing secure metadata. Experimental evaluation demonstrates <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 \%}$</tex> detection accuracy without false positives. It managed to identify all the corrupted files and, as a result, it can send the notifications or the alerts immediately. The use of chunk-level hashing made the process of verification more efficient and it helped in exactly pointing out the regions which were tampered with.

title: Unified Price limits or Provincial-Specific Price limits: A Study on the Optimal Price Limit Mechanism for Chinese Inter-Provincial Medium and Long Term Centralized Electricity Trading
authors: Ouzhe Luo, Shanshan Huang, Yiding Jin, Tong Xing, Xuesong Li, Yu Ding
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: China has recently emphasized the construction of a unified national electricity market, where the price limit mechanism in electricity trading has a direct impact on market efficiency and regional equity. In the development of the cross-regional and inter-provincial long-term centralized electricity market (RPLEM), coordinating the benchmark electricity prices of different provinces to formulate reasonable price limits has become a critical issue. The current unified price limit mechanism faces challenges such as unstable benchmark prices and significant substitution effects of high-priced units. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Provincial-Specific Price Limits mechanism. Additionally, by constructing a dual-variable analysis framework involving the “price limit mechanism - floating ratio,” and developing a bidding and market clearing model considering these factors, MATLAB simulations are used to compare the results. The research shows that Provincial-Specific Price Limits have advantages in terms of social welfare and price levels. However, in the early stages of market development, using Unified Price Limits can make the impact of the reforms more controllable.

title: Compact and Flexible Antenna Design for Discrete Body-Worn Communication Devices
authors: M Ramesh, Ch V S Pavan Kumar, Suresh Chinnathampy Manickam, Jeyalakshmi Vijayarajan, T Srikanth, P V Ranthi Dev Varma
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: This paper presents designing and simulation of a wearable antenna that operates in the 5.8 GHz ISM band for healthcare communication devices. Despite the challenges in designing the wearable antenna and approaching miniaturization, mechanical flexibility, and low SAR while maintaining near to the human body. The design makes use of its low profile and simplicity of integration. our findings indicate the resonant frequency matched to 5.8GHz with a return loss of −16.85dB in flat configuration, 34MHz of an approximate bandwidth With a VSWR of 1.3 below the limit of 2.0, it shows the peak directivity of antenna at 7.07dBi providing directivity radiation away from the body and analysis of SAR shows only 0.50W/kg well below the 2.0W/kg ICNIRP Safety Guidelines. In addition to comprehensive bending analysis demonstrates mechanical robustness with antenna maintaining stable performance under deformation achieving return loss at −14.45dB and analysis of SAR shows 0.50W/kg even at 90mm bend radius. These results are shows that successfully combines compactness, flexibility and safety for practical wearable for health care monitoring applications. This work bridge gap between simulation based antenna designing and requirements for body worn system.

title: IoT-Based Wearable for Real-Time Epileptic Seizure Detection and Alerting
authors: P. Tamil Selvi, Priyanka V, R. Ramani, N. Gnanavel, Monisha R, S. Egasrimathi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Seizures are a hallmark of epilepsy, a neurological illness that, if left untreated, may have devastating consequences. Ensuring patient safety and reducing emergency risks require continuous monitoring and early response. This research introduces a wearable device that uses the internet of things (IoT) to continuously monitor physiological and mobility data to identify and warn users to epileptic seizures in real-time. Along with a microprocessor and a wireless connection module, the proposed wearable incorporates sensors including accelerometers, electromyography (EMG), and heart rate monitors. To detect irregular patterns linked to seizures, the sensor data is evaluated in real-time using built-in algorithms. Carers or healthcare practitioners get immediate warnings via a mobile or cloud-based platform upon discovery, guaranteeing prompt action. Data logging to the cloud is another feature of the system that allows for future study and medical reference. This IoT wearable provides a trustworthy, inexpensive, and non-invasive way to track seizures in real time, which improves healthcare responsiveness, patient safety, and quality of life generally.

title: Performance Evaluation of Hybrid Machine Learning Models for Heart Disease Prediction in U.S. Clinical Decision Support Systems
authors: Ekramul Hasan, Mustafizur Rahaman, Dipta Paul, SK Rakib Ul Islam Rahat, Nurtaz Begum Asha, Md Al Amin
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Heart-disease continues to be a major contributor to illness and death across the United States. This is highlighting the importance of reliable early-prediction tools. That can aid clinicians in making informed decisions. In this work, researchers analyze the effectiveness of multiple hybrid machine learning approaches for predicting heart disease using a set of clinically meaningful attributes. The evaluated models include Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), Gradient Boosting Machine (GBM), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and k-Nearest Neighbor (KNN). To enhance model relevance, univariate feature selection was used to determine the most influential predictors. Model performance was measured using accuracy (ACC), precision (PRE), and F1-score. This experimental analysis indicates that the Random Forest algorithm consistently delivered the strongest performance. This is reaching an accuracy of 98.31% on the test set. In addition, limiting the model to the eight highest-ranking features increased interpretability while maintaining high predictive accuracy. Overall, the results suggest that Random Forest both alone and in combination with feature selection provides a robust and dependable framework for heart disease prediction in U.S. healthcare environments. And provide also supporting improvement in clinical decision-making and patient care.

title: Progressive Optimization and Integration Methods for Semantic Segmentation of Small Targets in Urban Scenes
authors: Zichun Wei
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Urban scene semantic segmentation is a core technology in fields such as autonomous driving and intelligent monitoring. However, small targets (such as traffic lights, cyclists, motorcycles, etc.) have become a core bottleneck for improving segmentation performance due to issues such as low sample proportion, ambiguous features, and susceptibility to background interference. This paper uses the Cityscapes dataset as the experimental platform and proposes a small target segmentation improvement scheme that integrates improved Copy-Paste data augmentation and complementary model integration, which effectively breaks through the above bottlenecks. The core innovation points and the scheme of this paper are as follows: First, propose two levels of Copy-Paste data augmentation strategies, the enhanced version adds semantic restrictions (limiting reasonable pasting areas such as roads and sidewalks), category weighted sampling (higher sampling weights for scarce category samples), and multi-target batch pasting mechanism, precisely expanding the diversity of small target samples; Second, construct a model integration framework based on category complementarity, first establishing UNet++ with ResNet34 as the main model through progressive optimization, then selecting two complementary models that can cover the shortcomings of the main model in small target categories, and designing a voting method to integrate the advantages of multiple model predictions. Experimental results show that the improved Copy-Paste data augmentation increases the average IoU of small targets by 57.13% compared to the initial baseline. The final integrated model's average IoU for small targets reaches 0.4019, maintaining excellent performance in core categories such as roads and buildings. The research proves that the improved Copy-Paste data augmentation is the core means to break through the bottleneck of scarce small target samples, and complementary model integration can further enhance performance, providing an efficient and feasible technical path for urban scene small target semantic segmentation.

title: Methods of Spectrograms' Processing for Deep Neural Networks for the Real-Time Recognition of Radio Signal Modulation
authors: Maksim O. Zhegulin, Sergey V. Karamov
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Automatic recognition of radio signal modulation is a key factor in processing radio signals in various technical applications, such as radio communications, radar, direction finding, and others. In this study, methods for automatic modulation recognition applicable to embedded and edge hardware platforms with limited performance and power constraints are considered. The foundation of the proposed approach is the application of a conventional short-time Fourier transform to obtain a two-dimensional time-frequency spectrogram, subsequently, various combinations of spectrogram output parameters and their transformation functions are studied to generate the neural network's input features. Our initial assumption has been that traditional modulation recognition, which relies solely on the magnitude of the spectrogram, results in an inherent and undefined reduction of the essential information present in the original complex samples. The initial output complex parameters are introduced into the processing pipeline; these include the real and imaginary parts, as well as derived parameters such as magnitude and phase. An optimised CNN class neural network, tailored as an optimal architecture for processing two-dimensional spectrogram inputs, is constructed. By adjusting the neural network's parameters, the dataset is processed to obtain output quality metrics. A comparison is presented, detailing the accuracy and efficiency of radio signal modulation processing across all proposed spectrogram output parameter representation methods. Our research shows that introducing a combination of the real, imaginary, amplitude, and phase spectrogram parameters significantly improves neural network recognition, achieving 96 % accuracy, which is 25 % higher than conventional magnitude-only input. The observed result is invariant to the type of neural network utilised, introducing only a minor increase in network complexity and computational burden.

title: Attention-Enhanced YOLO Framework for Real-Time Human Detection and Counting
authors: Sudha Rani V, Katkuri Thrisha, Meghana Reddy Pingili, Pravalika Reddy Veligeti, Rajesh Bandari
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This paper presents Real-Time Human Detection and Counting, an innovative system developed using machine learning and deep learning techniques, with a focus on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). Beyond the core functionality of detecting and counting humans in real-time from video streams, the system integrates advanced features such as facial detection and dynamic adaptability to varying environments. These features enhance its accuracy and scalability for applications such as crowd management, security surveillance, and resource planning. The facial detection module extends functionality by enabling identity-based tracking or enhanced analytical insights. Leveraging frameworks like OpenCV and TensorFlow, the system ensures efficient processing, while optimized algorithms enable robust performance in diverse settings. By addressing challenges of real-time processing and scalability, this system offers a reliable, efficient, and accessible solution for human detection and counting in dynamic scenarios.

title: ANN-MPC Based Algorithm for Obstacle Aware Trajectory Tracking on Wheeled Mobile Robot
authors: Youcef Benslimane, Vladimir Y. Frolov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: Mobile robots are certainly becoming an important part of automation and logistics. The main challenge of their using is the trajectory tracking, where with Differential Drive Mobile Robots (DDMRs) there are nonholonomic constraints, and thus necessitate advanced control algorithms to give consideration to both accuracy and stability. The present paper proposes an improved hierarchical control structure for the DDMRs that uses Model Predictive Control (MPC) combined with Artificial Neural Network (ANN). The proposed ANN-MPC is intended specifically for eliminating steady-state errors, improving position control, and enhancing trajectory tracking accuracy with emphasis on guaranteeing robust obstacle-avoidance trajectory tracking performance. The simulation study was carried out in order to validate the control structure against the conventional PI-PI scheme and a hybrid ANN-SMC (Sliding Mode Control) method. The output results showed a considerably higher performance for ANN-MPC compared to the other two schemes in steady-state error minimization with low settling time and high accuracy.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)

title: Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The 4th International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT 2025), held successfully in Nanjing, China, from December 28 to 30, 2025. The conference served as an important interdisciplinary forum for the exchange of recent advancements in cognitive systems and intelligent technology, integrating perspectives from computer science, neuroscience, and psychology.

title: Decision Making System for Landslide Monitoring
authors: K. Vanitha, Deepak S, Mathan J
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Landslides represent a critical natural hazard in mountainous and hilly terrains, frequently resulting in loss of life, damage to infrastructure, and disruption of essential services. Timely identification of unstable ground conditions is therefore essential for effective disaster mitigation. This work presents a low-cost and scalable IoT-enabled landslide monitoring and early warning framework designed for continuous real-time observation of environmental factors influencing slope stability. The proposed system integrates an Arduino-based sensing unit with soil moisture, vibration, rainfall, and gyroscopic sensors to capture dynamic variations in terrain conditions across landslide-prone zones. Sensor data are transmitted to a cloud platform, where machine learning models analyze temporal patterns and detect abnormal behaviors indicative of potential slope failure. Upon identifying risk conditions, the system immediately triggers local alerts through an on-site buzzer mechanism, enabling rapid awareness among nearby communities and authorities. Experimental deployment demonstrates the system's capability to track critical environmental changes and issue timely warnings under varying conditions. Owing to its modular architecture, affordability, and ease of deployment, the proposed solution can be adapted to different geographical regions, supporting proactive disaster preparedness and enhancing communitylevel safety.

title: Enhanced Weakly Supervised Learning for 3D Hand Pose Estimation
authors: E. M. P. J. De Saram, R.G.N. Meegama
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Accurate 3D hand pose estimation plays a vital role in applications such as augmented reality, virtual reality, robotics and human-computer interaction. Traditional methods rely heavily on fully supervised learning, which demands large-scale 3D annotations that are both expensive and time-consuming to obtain. This study investigates the effectiveness of weakly supervised learning techniques to reduce the reliance on extensive annotations while maintaining high accuracy. We initially implement a ResNet18-based regression model for 3D hand pose estimation and subsequently enhance performance using an optimized EfficientNet-B0 model. The transition to EfficientNet-B0 demonstrates significant improvements in both precision and computational efficiency. Experimental results show that weakly supervised learning not only alleviates the annotation burden but also yields reliable pose estimations suitable for real-time deployment. These findings underscore the feasibility of adopting lightweight, weakly supervised models in practical scenarios, paving the way for more accessible and scalable hand pose estimation systems across various interactive technologies.

title: Advancing Fake News Detection using Hybrid Deep Learning with FastText and Explainable AI
authors: L. Sri Lakshmi, B. Shynitha, A. Vaishnavi, N. Venkata Sireesha
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The dissemination of fake news across digital platforms has become a critical challenge, affecting public opinion, social stability, and decision-making processes. Various machine learning and deep learning approaches have been proposed in the detection of fake news, but most suffer from limited contextual understanding and interpretability, hindering their applicability in real-world applications. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning framework, CNN-LSTM, for effective capturing of both local linguistic patterns and longrange contextual dependencies in news articles. In addition, FastText word embeddings are used to enhance semantic robustness so that the model is capable of handling misspellings and rare word variations popularly encountered in deceptive content. Explainable AI is further implemented using LIME to obtain transparent and human-understandable justifications for model predictions. The proposed approach is evaluated on the WELFake dataset using standard metrics for classification. Experimental results depicted the hybrid model achieved a superior performance of 94.8 %, thus outperforming traditional and separate models of deep learning with a balanced precision and recall. The integration of explainability further ensures trustworthiness, making the proposed system suitable for deployment in high-stakes misinformation detection scenarios.

title: The Impact of Cryptocurrency Market on Stock Returns: Evidence from Bahrain
authors: Bayan Alkhabbaz, Daouia Chebab, Hussain Al Araibi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: This study explores the relationship between cryptocurrency returns, specifically Ethereum, and stock market performance in Bahrain, with an emphasis on the effects of macroeconomic variables such as interest rates, inflation, and oil prices. The research aims to: understand and examine the interaction between cryptocurrencies and stock returns in Bahrain, assess the impact of cryptocurrency trends on stock market behaviour, evaluate the effects of volatility and risk transmission from the crypto market to traditional financial markets, and identify any time-lag or lead-lag dynamics between the two. Monthly data were analysed using the Autoregressive Distributed Lag (ARDL) model, allowing for both short- and long-run relationships to be captured. The results indicate a statistically significant long-run impact of Ethereum returns and oil prices on stock returns, suggesting growing market integration. Short-run dynamics also reveal evidence of volatility spillover and delayed effects, confirming a lead-lag relationship. These findings underscore the increasing influence of digital assets on traditional markets and have practical implications for investors, policymakers, and regulators in emerging financial systems like Bahrain.

title: Closed Loop Bridge Type DC-DC Converter with High Voltage Gain and Lower Switching Stress
authors: Abhishek Raj Aman, Sagun Kumar, Awadhesh Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This work present, a closed-loop bridge-type DCDC converter that achieve a high voltage gain and also minimizing voltage stress on power switches. For high-demand applications such as electric vehicle charging, renewable energy systems (solar power) and high-voltage DC systems (HVDC) this converter is designed. To ensure reliable and consistent operation, a feedback loop based on a proportional-integral (PI) controller is used to provide steady and reliable output performance. This controller dynamically readjusts the switching patterns in response to changes in input voltage. The result is that this system remains within the operating range and can balance out sudden changes in either input voltage or load demand with a stable output. The closed-loop control improves transient performance, reduces ripple, and alleviates voltage overshoot and current spikes. MATLAB Simulations confirm the converter ability to maintains a constant output voltage under sudden changes in load and input voltage across a range of test scenarios. The feedback mechanism maintains high efficiency, permits smooth regulation, and reduces current spikes. Thereby improving operational reliability and reducing stress on switches. Overall, the converter offers a robust, efficient, and safe solution for high-performance DC-DC power conversion.

title: ESG and Corporate Governance as Predictors of Firm Profitability: A Machine Learning Perspective
authors: Medhi Meli, Zainab Alalwani, Mooza Al-Hadi, Fatima Kazarooni, Enas AlMajid
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: This study evaluates whether Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) indicators, together with corporate-governance attributes, can predict one-year-ahead profitability among S&P 500 firms. Using annual data from 2015–2024 obtained from the Refinitiv databases, the analysis develops three machine-learning model specifications that progressively adjust the role of governance information: (1) ESG pillars combined with financial indicators and explicit governance-structure variables, (2) ESG pillars combined with financial indicators only, and (3) Environmental and Social pillars combined with governance-structure variables and financial indicators. Five supervised learning algorithms, Ridge Regression, SVR, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, and LightGBM, were used. Across all specifications, LightGBM consistently delivers the strongest predictive accuracy. Comparative results show that adding detailed governance-structure variables produces only marginal improvements in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>, indicating that Refinitiv's governance metrics already embed most of the relevant board-oversight information. Overall, the findings confirm that ESG and governance indicators, when combined with financial fundamentals, provide stable forward-looking predictive information for firm profitability in mature equity markets.

title: Split Learning for Privacy-Preserving Distributed Intelligence: Balancing Communication Efficiency and Model Performance
authors: Yushu Du
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: This study focuses on a typical task in distributed sensing and privacy-sensitive applications, namely human activity recognition (HAR), and explores the application value of Split Learning in privacy protection and performance balance. The paper constructed three models on the UCI HAR dataset, including convolutional Neural Network (CNN), recurrent Neural Network (RNN), and Long Short-Term Memory Network (LSTM), and systematically analyzed the influences of the number of clients, batch size, and hyperparameter Settings on the model accuracy, communication efficiency, and stability. The experimental results show that the LSTM model performs best in a distributed environment. An appropriate number of clients and medium-sized batches can achieve a good balance between precision and communication overhead. Learning curve analysis also reveals a nonlinear relationship between model depth and convergence speed. The research results verified the feasibility of segmentation learning in HAR scenarios, and at the same time provided methodological references and guidance for future privacy-sensitive distributed intelligent applications.

title: Equipment Fault Location Model for Distribution Networks Based on Low-Voltage Topology Backtracking Analysis
authors: Lin Peng, Aihua Zhou, Zhonghao Qian, Min Xu, Sen Pan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of power consumption, the increasing number of low-voltage-level equipment has led to a rising probability of device failures. Improving the accuracy of fault location in distribution network equipment plays a crucial supporting role in enhancing power supply reliability. This paper proposes a low-voltage topology backtracking algorithm to search for fault locations in distribution network equipment, adopting a data-driven approach to identify fault intervals. By integrating a support vector machine (SVM)-based data-driven detection method, a data-knowledge hybrid-driven model with a guidance mechanism is constructed. Distortion coefficient, offset coefficient, and irregular distortion criteria are employed to quantitatively describe the distortion characteristics of topological sequences, thereby dynamically guiding the fault location model according to topological variation patterns to achieve adaptive matching between localization results and fault scenarios. The proposed method, based on probabilistic statistical approximation and highdimensional parameter fitting, directly establishes a mapping relationship between feature quantities and fault conditions from a data-driven perspective, strengthening the role of mechanistic analysis in the decision-making process. Additionally, by reducing the interference of abnormal data, the method adapts to fault location requirements under various scenarios, exhibiting strong noise immunity and higher accuracy compared to existing knowledge-driven approaches. Combining empirical knowledge with data-driven algorithms, the proposed method effectively reduces the probability of fault misjudgment.

title: Medi Voice: An AI-Powered Voice Recognition and Medical Record Automation System
authors: Logeshwari R, Akshaya R B, Ananthakumar L, Sathish D K, Praveena A, V Karunakaran
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Paper-based patient history recording. usually slackens out the hospital procedures and creates. inaccuracies, incomplete patient records are traceable. The gaps occasionally interfere with the levels of treatment and diminish patient. compliance rates. MediVoice has been designed to counter this. through transforming verbal communication between the doctor and the patient. registered into official electronic documents. The system employs speech to synthesize recognition and natural language processing. transcripts that are highly accurate and they are centrally stored. in a safe repository that is accessed through web and mobile. interfaces. Other than recording set up, MediVoice also. adds convenient features such as drug reminders, an inbuilt help giving medical questions, and safe. doctor-patient communication channels. The system will save the company the time wasted on manual data entry. enhances the quality of documentation, simplifies administration. burdens, and helps to establish a continuous healthcare. patient and provider experience.

title: Mamba Flow Head: Polar Constraint Module for Stereo Matching
authors: Xiaoshi Lin, Shuiping Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Iterative optimization-based stereo matching methods have demonstrated significant value in industrial applications, with the number of iterations providing a flexible trade-off between accuracy and computational cost. However, current research primarily focuses on improving the cost volume module, context feature extraction, or the iterative update mechanism within different network architectures, often neglecting a fundamental principle of stereo matching: the epipolar constraint. In this paper, we propose an enhanced module in iterative optimized model’s flow head by adding Mamba block. Our approach aims to constrain the module in theoretical level of expressive power rather than a complex loss function. The proposed module can be independently integrated into existing iterative models such as RAFT-Stereo, IGEV-Stereo, and Selective-Stereo. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of our method. The corresponding evaluation is on Scene Flow and KITTI 2015. Code is available at https://github.com/xslynin/stereo-mamba-flowhead.

title: Explainable Transformer-Integrated Model for Multimodal Human Activity Recognition in Smart Healthcare
authors: Kommerla Anusha, A. Prakash, Borusu Ramani, G. Ashwini Kumari, Pottapati Sathish Kumar Reddy, P Jaishree Wankhede
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR)using wearable sensors plays a critical role in smart healthcare systems, particularly for continuous patient monitoring and elderly care. Nevertheless, traditional machine learning models frequently have trouble with temporal dependency modeling, multimodal sensor fusion, and interpretability. This research suggests an explainable Transformer-integrated hybrid framework for multimodal human activity recognition that combines Extreme Learning Machine (ELM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and Attention Mechanism (AM). While a Transformer-based fusion module models long-range inter-sensor interactions, ELM facilitates quick and efficient feature transformation, GRU records temporal dependencies, and attention highlights informative time segments. The suggested model outperforms state-of-the-art techniques with an accuracy of 96.65% when tested on the KU-HAR benchmark dataset. The outcomes show enhanced interpretability, robustness, and appropriateness for intelligent healthcare applications.

title: Experiential Learning in the Teaching of Basic Sciences Through the Use of ICTs, a Critical Analysis with a Systematic Review
authors: Jasmin Cuba-Cisneros, Wilver Auccahuasi, Walter Vasquez-Mondragon
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Information and communication technologies are changing the way teaching is done; therefore, technological tools improve processes related to experiential learning. In this sense, we analyze how information and communication technologies help improve experimentation in experiential learning. In this research, we analyze related articles to demonstrate how experiential learning based on basic science courses is being used. We begin with an analysis of 153 articles, and using the PRISMA method, we identify 16 articles with the greatest contribution to the topic developed. We also present a critical analysis related to the technological tools used.

title: Design and Implementation of an IoT System and Logical Prototype Model Using the Circuitverse Open-Source Tool
authors: Meenakshi Kashyap, Pinky Yadav, Malarvizhi A, Sanjula S, Sonal Kumar, Kaustubh Kumar Shukla
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The key intention of this work is to create an innovative path to implement IoT based device and its logical prototype. An open-source tool named as CircuitVerse is used to achieve the targeted goal. Several models have been designed logically tested and validated. The foremost benefit of this unique work is one can be able to implement a complicated IoT based architecture into a simple logical prototype model. Since, the entire process and costing is very low comparatively so it is more useful for the students and researchers because of its accuracy specially. In this research work through this paper the prototype model is correctly simulated and logically verified as well as shown the output by making use of LEDs in CircuitVerse environment. It has been also noted that the modular & hierarchical modelling method accomplished the system understandable, reusable, and better at finding and fixing bugs. Additional advantage of this work is mapping of prototype based logical design with the real hardware. So, in many ways it has been found that CircuitVerse tool is much effective for the IoT system design and implementation since, before hardware implementation it is providing one path to test and have some idea. This work has a huge impact in education for modelling, testing, and validating intelligent connected systems. The markable point is even in coming future people may think on the integration of the real-time cloud stages, the growth of physical hardware prototypes, which has unlimited applications like smart cities, healthcare and several monitoring cum industrial automation. Thus, it's a good multidisciplinary work in that people from different background can get involved and contribute for a better outcome.

title: An AI-Enabled Non-Invasive Framework for Early Detection of Oral Cancer
authors: T. Fasiha Khanam, T. Baba
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The oral cancer is a significant health concern in the world bearing in mind that morbidity and mortality of the late diagnosis occurrence is indeed high. Alternative intervention is noteworthy in order to achieve patient and treatment survival. The assumption made by the researcher of the given paper is the possibility to create the system of oral cancer diagnosis on AI-basis and reproduce the deep learning models, e.g., Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), DenseNet or MobileNet. To enhance the model, a dataset of images of an oral lesion was trained in the assistance of the traditional preprocessing techniques (resizing, normalization, and data augmentation). The evaluation was confirmed by the 10-fold cross-validation and average accuracy of 97 per cent, the precision of 0.97/F1-score per-fold, and the F1-score of 0.97/ F1-score per-fold. It was also found out that denseNet was useful in sharing features in subtle lesion patterns, CNN learnt the hierarchical features and MobileNet was a fast network and could be implemented in real-time. The success of the provided system is represented in the fact that it allowed the comparative analysis of the past works. The framework offers a resource, that is efficient, fast and devoid of a particular invasive procedure and has the potential to help the clinicians to identify the presence of oral cancer at an early stage and the capacity to reduce the human factor and improve the performance of the patients. The next generation implementation of work will also be based on multimodal clinical information and mobility development to provide increased accessibility.

title: Analyzing Digital Circuit Design Using Quantum-Dot Cellular Automata Simulation and Evaluation
authors: Sneh Lata, Ravindranath C, Kavya Shri G., Pavithra G, Swapnil S. Ninawe, M.C.Prabavathi
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Quantum-dot Cellular Automata (QCA) introduces an emerging nanoscale approach aimed at reshaping digital circuit development by delivering quicker processing, greater integration levels, and extremely low power usage, thereby addressing many of the limitations found in conventional CMOS designs. In this work, QCA-based multiplier and squaring architectures are created and examined, highlighting their relevance in modern digital signal processing tasks. Instead of relying on current-driven transitions like CMOS logic, QCA carries out operations through electron placement patterns inside quantum dots, removing the need for traditional switching methods. The designed systems incorporate binary adders, AND structures, and MUX-oriented RAM elements, and their behavior is evaluated in terms of layout area, total cells, and power demand using QCADesigner tools.

title: Pocket Engineered Vertical TFET for Enhanced Drive Current
authors: Khushboo Singh, Ram Awadh Mishra, Kumari Nibha Priyadarshani
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: This paper provides a detailed investigation of pocket area engineering in a vertical TFET structure, with an emphasis on the effects of shifting the pocket towards the source region. The study shows that moving the pocket towards the source increases the band-to-band tunneling probability. This leads in improved ON-state current, steeper subthreshold slope (SS), and a drop in threshold voltage. Further improvement in transconductance and other analog parameters, implying applicability for low-power analog/RF systems. The proposed structure achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{O N}/I_{O F F}=1.73 \times 10^{13}$</tex> and sub-threshold slope (SS) as 13.46 mV/dec along with the fully supressed ambipolarity. Achieved ON current is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$344 \mu \mathrm{A}/\mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> which is better as compare to reported Ge pocket VTFET. These results show that source-side pocket is an important design parameter for high-performance vertical TFETs.

title: Integrating XAI and Blockchain to Enhance Security and Resilience in Industrial Operations
authors: Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Hamza Ibrahim, Cosmas Ifeanyi Nwakanma, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Industrial systems are increasingly exposed to cy-ber-physical threats as sensing, automation, and machine learning (ML)-driven control expand the operational attack surface. This work introduces an integrated XAI-Blockchain framework that combines an interpretable one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) and long short-term memory (LSTM) intrusion detection models with PureChain's tamper-resistant logging to secure end-to-end industrial data flows. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves real-time detection with low-latency explanation generation, maintaining a high explanation stability of 0.98. It supports reliable access control by blocking 100 % of unauthorized attempts and provides deterministic, near-linear blockchain logging suitable for industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) operations. The framework improves security transparency, operational continuity, and auditability, demonstrating its practical viability for resilient industrial environments despite the limitations, notably elevated false-positive rates and low tamper-detection performance. These are key areas for enhancement. Future work will refine the ML models and explanation mechanisms to reduce misclassification, improve interpretability fidelity, and strengthen security through enhanced authentication and encryption.

title: Pose360: Metric-Scale Visual Odometry by Grounding Learned Features with LiDAR Depth
authors: Kemal Mudie Tosora, Seher Kanwal, Seung–Ik Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Metric-scale state estimation is a cornerstone for autonomous systems, yet single-modality solutions often fail in challenging real-world environments. Visual odometry suffers from inherent scale ambiguity, while LiDAR odometry can be fragile in geometrically sparse scenes. To overcome these limitations, we propose Pose360, a novel Visual-LiDAR Odometry (V-LIO) system that robustly fuses a 360° panoramic camera and a 360° LiDAR. Our approach leverages learned features, SuperPoint and LightGlue, to establish strong 2D visual correspondences, which are then lifted into a sparse set of metric 3D-3D point correspondences using depth information from a synchronized LiDAR point cloud. The 6-DoF relative pose is then computed efficiently via a closed-form SVD-based solution. This focused fusion strategy directly resolves visual scale ambiguity without requiring complex non-linear optimization. We perform a rigorous quantitative evaluation on a challenging 450 m real-world dataset, demonstrating that our system achieves high global consistency with a translation Absolute Trajectory Error (ATE) of just 0.177 m RMSE against a high-fidelity LiDAR SLAM ground truth. We further validate its real-world applicability by successfully integrating Lidar360Pose as the core odometry engine in a full robotic navigation stack, proving it is an accurate and reliable solution for metric state estimation.

title: A Multi-Scale D-ImgNet Learning Approach for Detecting Diabetic Retinal Abnormalities
authors: Pontagani Suresh, Ratnababu Pilli
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Nowadays, many people are suffering from retinal diseases, and it becomes more complex for all age groups. There are various reasons people can develop retinal diseases. Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is one of the most common retinal diseases that can be caused by diabetes. It is very important to detect DR at an early stage. In this paper, Multi-Scale D-ImgNet (MSDI) is introduced to detect DR at early stages, thereby helping prevent permanent vision loss. The proposed MSDI mainly captures multi-scale patterns that refine high-definition features from the input data. The preprocessing technique is a coarse-level denoiser that removes high-frequency distortions but ensures the global structural configuration of the retina map. The other filter is a fine-image enhancer that eliminates residual noise and enhances lesion edges without losing clinical information. To refine the final output, the D-Filter Model was introduced, which contains dual filters that show a refined output image. Finally, based on these refined output images, the multi-class classification layer is used to classify the multiple DR stages, such as DR (Healthy, Mild Non-Proliferative DR (NPDR), Moderate Non-Proliferative DR, and Severe Non-Proliferative DR). The experiments are conducted using the EyePACS dataset, with results showing high classification performance.

title: Automation Software Evaluation Using Median Regressive Sentiment Analysis
authors: Anil Chandrasekaran, Aweidha Alqubaisi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: This study applies a Median Regressive Sentiment Analysis approach to evaluate twenty office automation software solutions. Sentiment is defined quantitatively using weighted feature presence, reflecting how well each software meets desired functional requirements. The overall sentiment score for each product is computed and then normalized around the median sentiment value to reduce bias from outliers. Based on this median benchmark, software scoring within 20 % of the median is classified as neutral, those below 20 % as negative, and those above 20 % as positive performers. A regression model is then applied to examine the influence of feature weights and popularity ratings on sentiment deviations from the median. This combined analytical framework provides a robust, data-driven assessment that integrates both technical capability and user perception, supporting transparent and evidence-based decision-making in the selection of office automation software.

title: Classification of Phonetic Syllables Using Stacked Autoencoder and Characterization via Centroid
authors: Francisco dos Santos Viana, Carlos Eduardo Nascimento Cajado, Samuel Magalhes Pereira, Alexandre Cesar Muniz de Oliveira, Carlos Soares, Areolino de Almeida Neto
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This work presents a new method for layer insertion in stacked autoencoder neural networks. In this approach, a branch of layers is inserted on the side of the last hidden layer, and the output layer after the training of an existing layer has stabilized. Later, the new branch is merged with the previous layers. This insertion type is called collaborative, as it introduces new knowledge to the network without reducing the knowledge already acquired by the previous layers. This approach enables a neural network to learn with reduced design time. It overcomes the typical problem of defining the number of layers and the number of neurons in each layer. This technique was applied in phonetic syllable classification, where the Fast Fourier Transform obtains the audio data. These audio data were processed using a vertical bar plot to compress the audio data by using centroids. This procedure provided data compression without losing characteristics. Thus, collaborative insertions were evaluated in terms of the degree of growth for a multi-class classification problem.

title: Generalized Root Raised Cosine Window-Based and Prony-Based Estimation Method for OTFS Sensing
authors: Liangchen Sun, Yutaka Jitsumatsu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: For channel analysis in high-mobility scenarios, accurate estimation of fractional delay-Doppler parameters is essential for reliable sensing and communication. To address this challenge, we explain two novel methods in this paper. First, we introduce a generalized root raised cosine (RRC) window that removes the conventional constraint of keeping window values within [0, 1], allowing negative and greater-than-one values for enhanced waveform flexibility. When combined with the corresponding interpolation algorithm, this method achieves improved sensing capability. In addition, we present a two-stage, Prony-based estimation method that can accurately recover up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N-1$</tex> delay-Doppler parameters under noiseless conditions. Numerical simulations validate the high estimation accuracy of these approaches and demonstrate their strong potential for future ISAC frameworks.

title: From Connectivity Assurance to Experience Assurance: Paradigm Evolution and Technology Outlook for Aerospace Audio-visual Transmission
authors: Jing Wen, Liming Wang, Jing Fu, Juan Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This paper reviews the evolution of aerospace audio-visual transmission technologies, tracing the transition from first-generation connectivity assurance enabled by physical-layer optimization, to second-generation quality assurance achieved through cross-layer adaptation. We propose a third-generation paradigm, experience assurance, which leverages semantic communication and deterministic networking to transmit meaning rather than mere bitstreams. This approach incorporates Time-Sensitive Networking (TSN) to ensure spatio-temporal determinism, effectively addressing bandwidth constraints and reliability challenges in aerospace environments. We analyze the evolutionary trajectories and enabling technologies of these paradigms while identifying current bottlenecks, such as limitations in on-board intelligence and standards integration. Finally, we highlight future trends, including the integration of communication, sensing and computing, and generative artificial intelligence (AI) reconstruction.

title: Optimization of AI Enabled Learning Path Model Based on Knowledge Graph Transition From Fragmentation to Systematization
authors: Zhijian Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In this paper, Breadth First search (BFS) is combined with Graph neural network (GNN), and the Breadth First-graph Networks (BF-GN) model is innovatively constructed, aiming at optimizing the learning path of AI enabled based on knowledge graph. The model uses the BFS algorithm to traverse the knowledge graph effectively and realize the systematic integration of fragmented knowledge. At the same time, the introduction of Transformer to extract feature graph neural network (GNN) can deeply explore the complex relationship between knowledge nodes and improve the accuracy and richness of knowledge representation.
