It is a useful tool to create high-quality digital shadows and steer data-driven crime prevention methods in smart cities by offering better generalization to this hybrid model.

title: Smart IoT enabled Remote Elderly Care Framework Leveraging Edge Computing
authors: Varsha K. S, Jashwanth Kumar. K, P. Aujita Babu, Shri Vishwas. V, Sakthi Santhosh. D, P.Naren Sunthar
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The growing demand for continuous health supervision of elderly individuals necessitates intelligent, real-time, and scalable healthcare solutions. The paper introduces a new Smart IoT-enabled Remote Elderly Care Framework based on the edge computing to provide low-latency analytics, anomaly detection, and personalized health monitoring. The system uses wearable IoT sensors to measure physiological indicators of heart rate, ECG, blood pressure, and motion information that are relayed through LoRaWAN to edge nodes, such as Raspberry Pi. Local processing and analysis of the data is done using a hybrid LSTM-XGBoost model in these nodes and it has an average accuracy of 95.1% when it comes to identifying health anomalies such as falls, arrhythmias and hypertension alerts. Insights are then safely sent to the cloud to be analyzed and visualized to be stored and analyzed as trends. The cloud has also been used to support the development of advanced AI models that are capable of making long-term predictions on health, and dashboards provide user-friendly interfaces to caregivers and physicians. Experimental performance of 10 simulated and real-time deployments showed over 60 % latency improvement over cloud-only systems, F1-scores over 92, and user satisfaction ratings over 8.5/10. This architecture will help overcome the major drawbacks of current elderly care systems including long response time, excessive false alarms, and inadequate flexibility. In addition, the architecture facilitates personalization that is sensitive to context and explicable AI decisions with SHAP, which increases transparency and trust in healthcare automation. The results confirm that the system can be used in assisted living facilities in reality, especially in the locations that have poor network coverage. This work provides the background to the further intelligent healthcare ecosystems to be optimized towards elderly populations.

title: ECVF: Efficient Convolution Vision Transformer Fusion for Real-Time Image Analysis
authors: Kummari Venkatesh, M D V Prasad, Geetha Kurikala, Lokesh Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The world population is increasing exponentially. To cater to the growing population, food grain yield needs to be enhanced. The plants are the prime foundation of food grain production, and production is declining due to nutrient deficiencies (ND). This ND identification (NDI) at an early stage is recommendable to prevent a decline in crop yield. To address this problem, this research paper introduces an efficient convolution vision transformer (ECVF)-based model for NDI. This ECVF model is trained on groundnut crop field images captured in the field, and it achieved 98.62% accuracy in identifying ND. It is 1.62% sophisticated than the current models. Furthermore, this ECVF accomplishes better across other concert metrics, such as precision, and achieves efficient performance across all matrices. As a result, the ECVF framework significantly increases the effectiveness of computerized ND recognition in groundnut crops under complex backgrounds while simultaneously improving global food security.

title: Practical Field of Bangladesh Modern Applications: Encrypting Parameters & Values without CSRF and Ensuring Validation Protection
authors: Md Nahid Akter Shafi, A.S.M. Rashel, Nazmul Islam Bhuiyan
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: The increasing reliance on web applications for sensitive transactions demands robust security mechanisms. Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) and insecure parameter handling remain common vulnerabilities in modern web systems. This paper proposes a security model that encrypts parameters and values in HTTP communications using stateless JWT-based authentication. By combining JSON Web Tokens (JWT) with symmetric key encryption (AES-256), the architecture eliminates the need for traditional CSRF tokens while ensuring data confidentiality and integrity. All parameters are encrypted at the application level and validated during decryption, forming a secure, efficient alternative to CSRF token-based methods. With comprehensive results presented, the analysis demonstrates that encrypting parameters and values is more effective in one application system compared to another.

title: Analysis of Analog Characteristics of Junctionless Nanowire Gate-All-Around MOSFETs based Food Analyte Biosensors
authors: Ravi Rastogi, Devendra Kumar Tripathi, Sarvesh Dubey
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: In the present simulation-based study, the performance of a Food Analyte Biosensor based on Junctionless Gate-All-Around (JL-GAA) MOSFETs has been investigated. The sensitivity of the suggested device using different biomolecules like Gluten, Zenine, Keratin, and Gelatine inside the cavity is the main focus of the study. The device offers promising subthreshold characteristics and above threshold characteristics in the presence of biomolecules. The results have been obtained by performing numerical simulation over TCAD device simulator.

title: Simplification of Detailed Battery Energy Storage Device Model for Power System Stability Calculations
authors: Andrey N. Belyaev, Alexandra N. Sipovich, Yaroslav E. Tihonov, Matvey S. Mukhorin, Gleb D. Demidov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: The study examines battery energy storage system (BESS), its impact on power system in various operating conditions and optimization parameters to improve small-signal and transient stability. Detailed model of storage device has been developed, effective control of which ensures correct changes in charge/discharge modes based on active power flow from network. Optimal parameters of PID controller are calculated, at which balance is maintained between speed and overcontrol. Single- and three-phase short circuit calculations at various nodes of power system show correct response of BESS in emergency situations. Effectiveness of BESS consists in reducing short-circuit currents, voltage and frequency stabilization as well as decreasing network current surges. Investigation results confirm that BESS is critical element of modern power systems, ensuring increased stability during integration of renewable energy sources and effective damping of transients. Practical recommendations for implementation of energy storage devices in distribution networks are proposed.

title: Designing and Analyzing Multi-Layered Security Model for Ransomware-Resilient Cloud Services
authors: Tarun Kumar Vashishth, Puneet Chauhan, Kamlesh Kumar Gautam, Sharad Kumar, Sanjeev Kulshrestha, Vikas Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Ransomware has emerged as a major cybersecurity threat, especially to cloud computing systems, where data confidentiality, integrity, and availability are important. The design and implementation of a multilayered security model to improve the resilience of cloud services to ransomware attacks are presented. The security model includes duplicate techniques for improving data confidentiality integrity and availability. Each of these is included as a second layer to provide increased levels of depth in defense so that the instantiation of diverse techniques effectively limits the potential for infection and provides for rapid mitigation that supports continued operations in case of an attack. Provisions exist for the use of artificial intelligence to infer abnormal behavioral characteristics of people and computers, for example, crypto security measures and decentralized storage of data to preclude unauthorized modification. The experimental verification indicates that the proposed techniques to reduce the rate and frequency of occurrence of ransomware are enhanced and the appropriate data recovery time periods are improved greatly when compared with available single-layered security techniques.

title: Fusion-Based Feature Extraction and Lightweight Deep Learning for Pigeon Pea Leaf Disease Detection with Compartative Classifier Analysis
authors: Anupam Patil, Virupakshappa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The pigeon pea (Cajanus cajan) is a significant pulse crop that is widely cultivated in tropical and subtropical countries the numerous fungi, viruses, bacteria, and mycoplasma-like organisms that attack pigeonpeas result in severe output losses if not identified and treated early. In this paper implemented algorithm precisely isolates only the diseased leaf regions by applying segmentation (more specifically, a U-Net-based semantic segmentation model), guaranteeing that feature extraction that follows concentrates only on the lesion-affected areas. By combining deep and handcrafted features, this study suggests a novel multi-stage framework for the efficient classification of leaf diseases in pigeon peas. To ensure that feature extraction only concentrates on lesion-affected areas, diseased regions in the leaf images are first segmented using a U-Net-based semantic segmentation model to eliminate complex field backgrounds like soil, stems, and overlapping foliage. The suggested MSF-FEX (Multiscale Segmentation and Fusion-based Feature Extraction) algorithm, which integrates color histogram descriptors and lesion geometry analysis, is used to extract important texture, color, and shape features from these segmented areas. Deep features are learned by a lightweight convolutional neural network (Lite-CNN), and for improved discrimination, handcrafted features are concatenated with deep representations using a feature fusion technique. Several models, such as SVM, Random Forest, and the suggested fusion-based CNN, are used for classification. The fused model outperforms traditional deep-only or handcrafted-only approaches in terms of accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(98.35 \%)$</tex>, precision, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> -score, according to comparative tests conducted on a custom pigeon pea disease dataset. The suggested approach can be modified for other crops with comparable visual disease symptoms and has great promise for real-time disease monitoring in smart agriculture.

title: Multimodal Fake News Detection Using OCR and Image Captioning with Semantic Similarity Assessment
authors: Swapna H, Soniya B.
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Detection of fake news is a basic issue to be addressed to control the spread of misleading information across the social media, in the form of both text and images. Traditional approaches were text oriented but now the misinformation greatly spreads in the form of images and memes which may have misleading messages. To address this issue in this paper we propose a multimodal approach where in the false content is identified based on both the textual and visual content. As it combines the visual and textual semantics it outperforms unimodal approaches which fail to identify misaligned imagetext pairs. This semantic score is an indication of whether the news is fake or not. First, the system uses OCR to pull out any text that is hidden in the image. Then, it makes an automatic caption that summarizes the meaning of the image. By measuring image—caption agreement (using SBERT model), we identify inconsistencies which is an indication of fake news. Otherwise, we semantically match these two texts against the content of real time news to find whether the news is real or fake. Experiments were carried out on the benchmark data set Fakeddit and three classifiers Random Forest, Support Vector Machine and Logistic Regression were used to assess the performance. Results from all the three classifiers suggest that image-caption consistency is a valuable signal for fake news detection.

title: A New Era of Artificial Intelligence to Examine Algorithmic Discrimination for Social, Healthcare and Legal System Among Transgender Individuals
authors: Jessy k Jayanth, Thendral.T, Ragul Raman S, Aarya.V, Nivedhitha.M, S. Hanushka Srinivasan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Algorithmic systems gain more and more impact on getting access to social services, to healthcare, and the outcomes of law. Transgender people have certain risks since there is bias in data, model design, and the manner in which these systems are implemented. Biological and personal factors, like not having a strong ability to handle stress or not having good skills to deal with tough situations, can affect how someone reacts to stress and mental health problems. For example, when a person feels ashamed or hates themselves because of the negative views and stereotypes society has about been LGBTQ+, this can cause mental health problems. However, being transgender is not always clearly recorded, or if it is, it might be mixed up with male or female categories, making it hard to find and fix in AI systems. This paper looks at how AI can hurt transgender people in areas like society, healthcare, and the law. It also suggests ways to check and measure these harms and fix them, without breaking privacy or taking away people's ability to make decisions. One way to reduce unfair treatment is by using bias-mitigation techniques, which can change how algorithms work before they are used. These methods might hide sensitive information, add more data, or change how important certain data is to make things fairer.

title: Real-Time Underground Cable Fault Detection and Localization System Development
authors: R. Premkumar, Prasanna Bhore, Omkar Thorat, Sushank K, Olidia Das, Yashaswini V
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: As modernity equips itself with new achievements, some fields of life get changes, just as an example of changing from overhead lines to underground cables for electricity transmission in urban areas. It saves space, and the reliability of power distribution is increased. The challenge comes with the issue of finding the position of the fault that occurs in the underground cables, as pinpointing the exact location becomes difficult during repairs. The current methods rely on the application of some specialized algorithms to locate a fault and identify fault locations. The goal of this project, therefore, is timely and definitive detection of the locality of faults in underground cable strokes. The proposed system effectively detects faults by monitoring variations in voltage and analyzing changes in resistances of cable segments. These cable segments are modeled using a number of resistors with switches at set distances simulating faults. When a short circuit occurs, the voltage drops across the series resistors varies. This data becomes the input to the analog-to-digital converter that feeds precise digital datum to the programmed Arduino microcontroller. The system subsequently determines and indicates the exact distances in kilometers after the base location to the defect on a 16X2 LCD display. There is a buzzer to inform maintenance personnel about such faults. The proposed approach offers an accurate, cost-effective, and quick result for sensing faults in an underground power network, especially in urban cities, airports, and defense applications.

title: Simulation Study on Highway Landslide Stability Based on FEM-DEM Coupling
authors: Xiaoyong Zhang, Jianxu Long
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: In order to explore the evolution mechanism of slope instability, taking the slope containing clay and gneiss along a highway as the research object, the continuous discrete coupling (FEM-DEM) numerical simulation method was used to construct the slope model and carry out numerical simulation. The results show that the FEM-DEM coupling method can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the single simulation method, and realize the efficient and accurate simulation of the whole process of slope instability; The results show that the slope instability is characterized by four stages, i.e., local initial sliding, internal extension of sliding plane, formation of landslide mass, and sustainable development of landslide mass. There are significant differences in slope displacement, shape of sliding surface, and response of gneiss at the bottom of each stage; At the meso level, the contact force chain is “loose at the upper part and dense at the lower part” and decays with time; Porosity and coordination number reverse evolution. The research results provide theoretical support and technical reference for rock slope stability evaluation and reinforcement scheme optimization.

title: Machine Learning Driven Prediction of Antenna Reflection Coefficients (S11) from Geometric Parameters
authors: Shreyas Rajendra Hole, Deepak Suresh Asudani, Gaurav Kumavat, Vinothkumar Kolluru, Neeraj Jain, Shreekant Salotagi
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The reflection coefficient (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>) of a microstrip patch antenna is a critical performance metric, quantifying how effectively the antenna radiates versus reflects input power. Traditional antenna design relies on electromagnetic simulation and physical prototyping, which are time-consuming and resource-intensive. We present a data-driven framework that predicts (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>) (dB) directly from geometric parameters-frequency (GHz), patch length and width (mm), slot length and width (mm)-using ensemble machine learning. Our pipeline processes a dataset of 1,266 antenna measurements, performs exploratory analysis, standardizes features, and compares multiple models: linear regression, random forest, stacking ensembles, and hyperparameter-tuned variants. The best random forest model achieves R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9957, MAE = 0.0694 dB, and MAPE = 2.61%. A stacking regressor combining ridge, random forest, and gradient boosting attains R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9899. We analyze feature importance, demonstrate model generalization via cross-validation (average CV R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> ¿ 0.96), and discuss trade-offs between interpretability and accuracy. This work highlights the feasibility of rapid, accurate (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>) estimation from simple measurements, enabling accelerated design loops and reduced reliance on full-wave electromagnetic simulations.

title: Hybrid RegNet-Swin Transformer for Precision Gastrointestinal Disease Classification A Deep Learning Approach Using the Kvasir Dataset
authors: Ajeeb Sagar, Ayden Xavier Alvito Joanes, Shreyas Rajendra Hole, Akshat Agarwal, Vinothkumar Kolluru, Gaurav Londhe
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Gastrointestinal (GI) diseases such as esophagitis, polyps, and ulcerative colitis require early and accurate diag- nosis to prevent complications and improve patient outcomes. Traditional endoscopic diagnosis relies on manual interpretation by medical experts, which is time-consuming and prone to vari- ability. Deep learning-based methods have emerged as promising alternatives for automated classification of GI diseases. This study presents a hybrid deep learning model combining RegNet and Swin Transformer to classify GI tract images from the Kvasir dataset, a benchmark dataset containing eight categories of endoscopic images. RegNet, a CNN-based architecture, is used for extracting localized spatial features, while Swin Transformer cap- tures long-range dependencies and global contextual relationships in images. The dataset was preprocessed using image resizing, normalization, and extensive data augmentation techniques to enhance model generalization. To address the issue of class imbalance, a Class-Balanced Loss function was applied, ensuring fair learning across all categories. The dataset was split into 70% training, 10% validation, and 20% test sets, and the model was trained using the AdamW optimizer with a learning rate of 0.001 for 25 epochs. Performance evaluation metrics included accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and Matthews Correlation Coefficient (MCC), with MCC chosen for its robustness in handling imbal- anced classification tasks. Experimental results demonstrated that the proposed RegNet-Swin hybrid model achieved a test accuracy of 89% and an MCC of 0.8721, outperforming standalone CNN architectures. The confusion matrix and classification report indicated high classification performance across most categories, with minor challenges in distinguishing closely related GI conditions such as ulcerative colitis and dyed-resection margins. The find- ings suggest that combining CNN-based and Transformer-based models significantly enhances feature extraction, classification accuracy, and generalization capability. This research highlights the potential of hybrid deep learning models in medical image analysis and AI- assisted diagnostics. Future work should focus on expanding the dataset, optimizing model interpretability, and deploying the system in real-world clinical environments to assist gastroenterologists in automated endoscopic disease detection..

title: Real-Time Multi-User Authentication Using Mouse Dynamics Behavioural Biometrics
authors: Aayush Rajpoot, Nikhil Sharma, Sourabh Sahu, Vivek Anand, Siddharth Bhalerao, Sameer Yadav
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: This research investigates the feasibility of utilising mouse dynamics as a passive, hardware-independent, and real-time biometric authentication method in multi-user environments. The primary objective of the study was to develop and evaluate a real-time authentication system by analysing unique behavioural profiles derived from a user's mouse movements, including pointer velocity, click rates, and directional patterns. A comparative analysis was conducted to assess the performance of four machine learning classifiers: Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbours, Support Vector Machine, and a Multi-Layer Perceptron. The evaluation was performed in a multi-user setting that incorporated scenarios with both varying degrees of behavioural overlap and increasing complexity. The findings indicate that the Random Forest model achieved the highest overall accuracy at 91.2% and exhibited superior performance in authenticating users with highly variable mouse movement patterns. Conversely, the Multi-Layer Perceptron classifier, with an accuracy of 89.5%, proved more effective at discerning subtle and complex behavioral nuances. These results collectively underscore the potential of mouse dynamics as a promising, non-intrusive authentication technique that maintains user workflow while offering a robust balance between predictive accuracy, model interpretability, and practical implementation constraints.

title: Sign Language to Voice and Display Conversion Using Deep Learning on Raspberry-Pi
authors: Ravi Kumar Sariki, Patala Venkata Sai Charishma, R. Sundar, K Swaroopa, Dilbar Urazbaeva, Mukhayya Djumaniyazova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning-based sign language recognition system designed to improve communication accessibility for people who have significant hearing loss or who experience challenges in hearing. The system employs computer vision and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to classify hand gestures representing sign language letters. Image preprocessing techniques such as resizing and normalization are used to improve recognition accuracy. The CNN architecture incorporates ReLU activation and max-pooling layers for efficient feature extraction, followed by a softmax layer for multi-class classification. Real-time gesture tracking using OpenCV ensures temporal awareness by analyzing finger joint positions. Recognized gestures are converted into text and further synthesized into audible speech using advanced text-to-speech (TTS) technology. A Tkinter-based graphical user interface (GUI) displays recognized signs and offers context-aware text prediction. Experimental results demonstrate Accurate and reliable real-time translation of sign language into text and speech.

title: Integrating RNA Secondary Structure with Graph Neural Networks for Accurate mRNA Subcellular Localization
authors: Tonta Ishverya, Nareddy Koushik Reddy, B.Pravallika
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: In order to predict the subcellular localization of messenger RNA (mRNA), a crucial component of post-transcriptional gene regulation that affects protein synthesis, cell division, and the course of disease, this paper proposes a unique structure-aware graph neural network (GNN) architecture. The secondary structural context present in RNA molecules is not taken advantage of by conventional computer models, which rely on shallow statistical learning and sequence-based properties. Our suggested model overcomes this constraint by combining deep sequence embeddings with RNA secondary structure, turning each RNA sequence into a graph with base-pairing interactions as edges and nucleotides as nodes. Then, attention-based GNN layers are used to capture long-range contextual linkages as well as local structural dependencies. Accurate classification across several localization categories is made possible by the final fused representation. Our model obtains an overall accuracy of 88.00%, with class-wise F1-scores of 93.94%, 87.18%, 84.85%, and 87.72% across four subcellular compartments, according to experimental evaluation on benchmark datasets. 88.61%, 88.55%, and 88.42% are the macro-averaged precision, recall, and F1-scores, respectively. These results outperform state-of-the-art techniques, demonstrating that structure-aware GNN architectures offer a high-performance and biologically interpretable solution for mRNA localization prediction.

title: An Overview of Blockchain Applications in Secure Voting Systems
authors: Paras Gupta, Harsh Dev, Priyanshu Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Secure, transparent, and accessible voting is an important facet of any democratic system, especially in elections with high stakes and high populations, like those in India. Conventional voting practices are challenged by things such as impersonation, marginal participation among remote populations, lag in results, and incompetence in both endto-end verification. Recently, e-voting systems that employ a blockchain framework have surfaced, generating an optimistic perspective on how the immutable, decentralized, and transparent nature of blockchain technology can help eliminate these barriers. This article provides an extensive review of existing research on blockchain voting from 2019 to 2025 with an emphasis on Indian examples and also other research around the world. The reviewed literature was systematically categorized by blockchain framework, authentication methods, application scale, and performance metrics. Subsequently, we identified significant research gaps, including scalability and performance limitations, lack of variability in remote connectivity, legal requirements and compliance issues, and integration constraints surrounding the current election industry infrastructure. We now propose a secure voting system that employs a private Ethereum blockchain, IPFS storage, is privacy preserving, and biometric authentication framework of voting, which are all built to be scalable and often maintain an offline-first approach of delivery. We aim to provide a system with high accuracy, verifiable results, and accessible proposals while also addressing the significant limitations proposed in the current literature.

title: Deep Learning Based Animal Detection with Integrated Behavioral and Danger Analysis
authors: Mousam Kumari, Anjali Patel, Niyatee Saha, J. P. Patra
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Accurate recognition of wildlife is essential to monitor animal behavior and promote conservation efforts. This project presents a deep learning wildlife detection and behavioral analysis system using the YOLOv8 object detection model and the Gemini API. The system detects animals from camera trap images, provides bounding boxes, confidence scores, and generates textual reports about the detected species, behavior, and danger level analysis using the Gemini AI Prompt. Evaluated on the Caltech Camera Trap dataset, the model achieved a mean average precision (mAP) at a 0.5 threshold of 84.8%, demonstrating a high precision for species detection.

title: Research on the Influence of Conscious and Subconscious Goals on Higher-Order Learning in Gamification in Higher Education
authors: Liyuan Zhao, Jinxing Hao, Lilinwei Chen
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: Goal setting is an inherent feature of gamification. While existing studies have insufficiently addressed the role of goal-setting mechanisms in gamification teaching, a critical gap remains regarding subconscious goal-setting. Focused on higher education, this study explores the impact of gamification on learning outcomes related to higher-order thinking skills (HOTS) and conducts a field experiment with 100 students in an ERPsim game to examine how conscious and subconscious goal setting influences HOTS in such contexts. Drawing on Goal Setting Theory and the subconscious automaticity model, a theoretical model was constructed. The results indicate that conscious and subconscious goal setting exert distinct effects on students' HOTS in real gamification classroom settings. Additionally, an interaction effect was identified between these two goal types: their positive influence on knowledge depth was attenuated when both types of goals were set simultaneously. This study confirms that both conscious and subconscious goal setting in gamification in higher education can effectively enhance HOTS achievement. These findings enrich and complement gamification theory, while providing practical guidance for instructors to design gamification that improve participants' higher-order thinking skills.

title: Explainable Deep Learning and Ensemble-Based Framework for Alzheimer’s Disease Diagnosis and Progression Prediction
authors: Fathima Minnah A, Rahel Abraham Thomas, C. R. Dhivyaa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Alzheimer’s Disease (AD) represents a steadily advancing neurodegenerative condition defined by progressive cognitive deterioration, where recognizing subtle onset patterns and anticipating disease trajectory remain central to therapeutic decision-making. This research outlines an interpretable hybrid ensemble learning construct that blends conventional and deep computational paradigms for multidimensional assessment of cognitive integrity and progression forecasting from Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) toward AD. The framework integrates Logistic Regression, XGBoost, and an Artificial Neural Network (ANN), trained on heterogeneous data derived from ADNI dataset that encompasses volumetric Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) indices and neurocognitive evaluations. It integrates the strengths of each model, producing the final classification. Additionally, SHAP analysis is also performed to understand the feature contributions for the AD prediction. Experimental results prove that the proposed ensemble model achieves high predictive accuracy.

title: Optimization of Selective Harmonic Elimination for Emerging Single-Phase Five-Level Inverter Using Genetic Algorithm
authors: Alla Eddine TOUBAL MAAMAR, Mourad NAIDJI, Touhami ABDELOUAHED, Aimad BOUDOUDA, Radu PORUMB, Saad MEKHILEF
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This study focuses on the analysis and experimental investigation of Selective Harmonic Elimination (SHE) for emerging single-phase multilevel inverter using Genetic Algorithm (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$G A$</tex>), among the best algorithms for optimization. The employed inverter, a widely adopted emerging topology, uses fewer switches than conventional designs while delivering a five-level output voltage. GA solves transcendental nonlinear equations to find SHE's optimal commutation angles. Simulation outcomes closely align with theoretical predictions, demonstrating the method's simplicity compared to analytical techniques, suitability for inverter control, and cost-effectiveness for real-time implementation on a low-cost Arduino ATmega2560 Microcontroller. Theoretical analysis is validated through MATLAB/Simulink simulations and a hardware prototype built with efficient electronic components. Both simulation and experimental findings validate the resilience and efficacy of the presented modulation technique for emerging single-phase five-level inverters.

title: Agentic AI in DevOps: Boosting Software Automation and Collaboration
authors: Mohammed Akour, Mamdouh Alenezi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: This paper explores the transformative impact Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) can bring into DevOps, mainly in automation, collaboration, and decision-making. We combine controlled experiments and real deployment in a fintech setting to show that the integration of Agentic AI into CI/CD pipelines drastically reduces deployment times, detects errors better, and encourages multi-disciplinary collaboration. Our results show a 36% reduction in test cycle time and a 65% decrease in rollback frequency. Deployment assurance was rated a high 4.6/5 from developers when using the AI assistant-giving developers' confidence in this assistant. This paper offers practical guidelines and architectural blueprints for organizations intending to implement Agentic AI, followed by discussions on factors of ethical, operational, and technical concern for building explainable and trustworthy AI systems.

title: Mitigating Backdoor Attacks via Attention Head Pruning
authors: Zhihong Liang, Kaitian Huang, Yuan Pan, Liang Xuan, Ximing Zhang, Wenqian Xu, Yiwei Yang, Yixin Jiang, Peiming Xu, Leyu Bi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: The proliferation of sophisticated neural architectures, particularly Vision Transformers (ViTs), has been paralleled by the evolution of advanced adversarial exploits, among which backdoor attacks remain a critical threat. These attacks instill hidden malicious behaviors, activated by specific triggers, into an otherwise high-performing model. While defenses have been proposed for convolutional networks by analyzing neuron-level statistics, these approaches do not directly translate to the component-specific architecture of Transformers. Concurrently, work in model interpretability has revealed that distinct functionalities, such as safety mechanisms, can be localized to individual attention heads. This paper bridges these two domains. We hypothesize that in a backdoored ViT, the malicious functionality is not diffuse but is sparsely encoded within a small subset of attention heads. We propose that these backdoor heads exhibit unique statistical signatures in their activation distributions. Building on this, we introduce two novel defense strategies: Entropy-Based Head Pruning (EHP), which identifies heads with low distributional entropy using a poisoned dataset, and Statistical Divergence Head Pruning (SDHP), a more practical method that identifies anomalous heads by comparing clean-sample statistics to the model's embedded Batch Normalization statistics. Our methods excise these malicious heads, effectively neutralizing the attack. Experiments on CIFAR-10 and Tiny-ImageNet demonstrate that our approach can reduce attack success rates to near-zero while preserving over 99% of the model's original clean accuracy, significantly outperforming existing neuron-based pruning defenses.

title: An Explainable Artificial Intelligence Framework for Breast Cancer Detection Using Optuna-Optimized Logistic Regression
authors: Muskan, B.K. Mishra, Pritpal Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Breast cancer ranks among the most prevalent and correct classification of malignant vs benign tumors which is important for the success of treatments and to reduce mortality rates. The following study presents an Explainable Artificial Intelligence-based machine learning model for the diagnosis of breast cancer on the Wisconsin Breast Cancer Diagnostic dataset. It uses Logistic Regression as the base classifier, optimizing its hyperparameters using Optuna Bayesian optimization to achieve better predictive performance and robustness for the model. Standardization of the dataset has been done. The optimized model reached an Accuracy of 98.25 %, with very high precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC which proved to be effective in tumor classification. In this regard, SHapley Additive exPlanations, and Local Interpretable Model-agnostic explanations have been integrated to provide insights into the model's global and local behavior. SHAP analysis shows radius, texture, perimeter, and area as the main important features responsible for malignancy, whereas LIME provides instance-level explanations and helps visualize each feature contribution to individual predictions. This integration between model optimization and explainability bridges the gap between performance and transparency, increasing the clinicians' trust in the AI-powered diagnostic system. The proposed framework will show how combining Optuna-tuned Logistic Regression with XAI techniques can provide reliable, interpretable, and accurate predictions capable of supporting medical practitioners to make informed and trustworthy decisions during early diagnosis in breast cancer detection.

title: Adaptive Energy Management for Grid-Connected EV Charging Stations Using Solar PV and Battery Storage
authors: Amanpreet Kaur, Rishabh Dev Shukla, Manish, Rupam Bhagawati, Sunny Vig
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Electric vehicle (EV) charging station operations and plans must manage their energy needs efficiently to minimize dependency on the grid, minimize operational costs, and maximize the benefit of renewable energy generation. This paper introduces an Enhanced Energy Management System (EEMS) for a Grid-Connected EV Charging Station, using solar photovoltaic (PV) generation and battery storage as a means to manage energy distribution. EEMS responds to real-time peak demand, minimizing grid procurement costs by managing consumption in light of solar generation priority and the charging and discharging schedule of the battery. A MATLAB-based model and simulation of the EV charging station is built to illustrate its energy distribution characteristics over a 24-hour horizon, where EV load, solar power generation, and battery state of charge (SOC) are the main design parameters. The EEMS produced encouraging results, with substantial grid energy procurement cost reductions, particularly during peak solar generation hours. The EEMS was capable of minimizing the impact of EV-connected loads while maximizing EV owner solar and battery resources but ensured that charging would still occur without interruption. An optimization model was developed to minimize grid energy costs through fmincon. The proposed optimization demonstrated, at the aggregate level, that solar and battery storage could provide a substantial share of the connected EV load, particularly during peak periods, relying less on the grid, and improving the overall energy efficiency of the organization.

title: Towards an Oracle for Binary Decomposition Under Compilation Variance
authors: Ang Jia, He Jiang, Zhilei Ren, Xiaochen Li, Zhipeng Yang, Yaxin Duan, Ming Fan, Ting Liu
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: Critical Challenges in Content Moderation for People Who Use Drugs (PWUD): Insights into Online Harm Reduction Practices from Moderators
authors: Kaixuan Wang, Loraine Clarke, Carl-Cyril J. Dreue, Guancheng Zhou, Jason T. Jacques
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Human-Computer Interaction
abstract: None

title: Mixed Reality Golf Putting: a Comparative Analysis of Golf Putting Performance in Real, Virtual, and Augmented Virtuality Environments
authors: Felix Steiner, Ke Li, Fabian Räthel, Frank Steinicke
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Although immersive technologies hold enormous potential for enhancing sports training, existing systems encounter notable limitations. While fully immersive virtual reality (VR) systems can address constraints of physical training setups such as limited space or equipment, full VR environments often lack rich, realistic, and multisensory feedback often required for effective sports training. To address this gap, this work investigates skill-based sport training, particularly, golf putting, across the reality–virtuality continuum. We implemented a mixed reality (MR) putting simulator that combines real equipment (putter and golf ball) with a virtual training environment. Three training conditions were realized: (i) real-world putting, constrained by limited physical space; (ii) a VR putting, in which a real putter and ball are used but represented virtually within the virtual environment (VE); (iii) an augmented virtuality (AV) putting, which reveals the real putter, ball, and users' limbs through a localized video-passthrough window, aiming to preserve users' sense of embodiment and presence while enabling simulation of an unlimited putting area. We conducted a pilot within-subjects experiment with 36 participants and found that the AV condition improved immediate putting performance, reduced task load, reduced perceived motion sickness, and increased user preference compared to the VR condition, while user's sense of presence remained comparable to VR training. These findings indicate that AV offers a pragmatic “sweet spot” on the virtuality continuum, balancing the trade-off between sensory fidelity and training flexibility, offering valuable design insights for immersive sports training systems.

title: Efficient LLM Adaptation to Low-Resource Languages via Cross-Lingual Semantic Anchoring
authors: Bekhzod Shukhratov, Shokhrukh Baydadaev, Jang Woo Kwon
abstract: Most Large Language Models (LLMs) remain heavily English-centric, resulting in high tokenizer fertility, where a single word in a low-resource language decomposes into multiple sub-tokens. This inefficiency inflates computational costs and weakens adaptation performance. We introduce bilingual exchange for optimized dense (BXOD) embeddings, a multi-stage framework for efficient cross-lingual adaptation based on cross-lingual semantic anchoring. BXOD employs a cold-start bilingual embedding initialization that anchors new vocabularies for low-resource languages within the semantic space of a high-resource model, guided by the multilingual LaBSE encoder. The pipeline expands the tokenizer to reduce fertility and initializes new embeddings through semantic alignment rather than sub-token composition. BXOD was evaluated using Llama 3.2 (1B, 3B) models adapted to Uzbek and Malay, and Gemma 2 (2B) models adapted to Uzbek, Malay, Korean, and Spanish. It achieved significant gains in low-resource settings, such as improving Malay news classification accuracy from 29.95% to 50.35% (+68.1%, p < 0.001) and English-to-Uzbek translation BLEU from 3.22 to 4.74 (+47.2%, p < 0.001), while preserving English measuring massive multitask language understanding (MMLU) performance and accelerating convergence. Results highlight BXOD’s strength for typologically distant, low-resource languages, while showing reduced benefits for lexically similar, high-resource languages such as Spanish. BXOD thus establishes an effective and computationally efficient paradigm for extending LLMs across linguistically diverse settings.

title: DMPT: An efficient Dual-Mamba Framework for Pedestrian Trajectory Prediction
authors: Kai Wu, Shiyi Tang, Haoyi Zhang, Yong Zhong, Weihua Li
abstract: Accurate and efficient pedestrian trajectory prediction is crucial for autonomous driving system, enabling safe and timely decision-making in complex urban environments. Despite much progress made in deep learning models, existing methods still struggle to simultaneously model intricate spatiotemporal interactions and inherent uncertainty in future trajectories, while maintaining computational efficiency. To address these limitations, we propose an efficient Dual-Mamba framework (DMPT), integrating intrinsic motion modeling and extrinsic social interactions into a unified encoder-decoder Mamba-based architecture. Specifically, we construct motion hypotheses for representative motion patterns from observed trajectories and employ a bidirectional Motion-Mamba encoder (BiMME) with learnable positional embeddings to thoroughly capture inter-pattern dependencies in both forward and backward directions. A classification head is then used to produce probabilities over motion patterns for selection and multimodal prediction. Conditioned on the selected motion hypotheses, a social Cross-Mamba decoder (SCroMD) incorporates neighbor information via a cross-selective scan mechanism, where a neighbor-dependent system matrix is aggregated with distance-based weights and used to decode the system states within the target motion hypotheses. Finally, a regression head generates multiple socially plausible future trajectories, each with a corresponding probability. This dual-head prediction allows the model not only to forecast diverse possible futures but also to provide an estimation for each prediction. Extensive experiments on three datasets demonstrate that our method has the best average minADE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">20</sub>/minFDE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">20</sub> of 0.19/0.30 on ETH/UCY and the lowest ADE of 6.72 on SDD, surpassing existing baseline models. Our method also demonstrates superior computational efficiency, achieving the highest inference speed while maintaining the lowest FLOPs of 1.11G and a competitive parameter size of 0.12M. This optimal balance between prediction accuracy, computation, and memory efficiency ensures reliable real-time trajectory prediction in autonomous driving.

title: AMTFusion: boosting 3D object detection by adaptive multi-modal temporal fusion and augmentation
authors: Wei Tian, Zhenglin Du, Xiaolong Zhao, Qiankun Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Accurate perception of surrounding 3D objects is indispensable for ensuring the safety of autonomous driving systems. Furthermore, the integration of multi-modal sensors has emerged as a pivotal research area within detection paradigms based on Bird’s Eye View. In this paper, we introduce a sparse query-based 3D object detection framework by adaptive multi-modal temporal fusion of camera and LiDAR sensor data. Initially, we leverage the spatial-aware cross-modal feature aggregation to adaptively fuse instance-level point cloud and image features. We further elaborate a sequential temporal modeling approach, which facilitates an enhanced multi-modal 3D object detection within constant spatial and temporal complexity. To mitigate the challenge posed by limited high-quality training data, we incorporate a fragment-wise multi-modal temporal data augmentation strategy, aimed at bolstering the model’s performance for detecting long-tail distributed objects. Our approach achieves an NDS detection score of 75.5% on the nuScenes test set (without test time augmentation and model ensembling). These results underscore the efficacy of our approach and highlight its superiority compared to prior state-of-the-art methods. We further show that our approach remarkably improves the accuracy of multi-object tracking on nuScenes dataset.

title: HGMRec: A Hypergraph-Based Medical Knowledge-Enhanced Literature Recommendation Model
authors: Hao Ding, Weiwei Zhu, Yuanyuan Shu
abstract: The rapid expansion of medical literature has intensified the challenge of providing researchers and clinicians with accurate and personalized knowledge recommendations. This paper introduces HGMRec, a hypergraph- and medical knowledge graph-enhanced recommendation model designed for heterogeneous and multimodal medical information. The model leverages UMLS (Unified Medical Language System)-based entity recognition and TransE embeddings to inject domain-specific knowledge, and constructs a heterogeneous hypergraph that jointly represents literature, entities, and images. A hypergraph attention mechanism captures higher-order relationships, while a contrastive alignment module ensures semantic consistency across modalities, and a user-side dynamic attention network adapts to evolving user interests. Experiments on two real-world datasets demonstrate that HGMRec significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. HGMRec achieved an NDCG@10 of 0.442, representing a 4.25% improvement over the strongest baseline, and an F1@10 of 0.402, surpassing others by at least 4.7%.

title: Dual-Verifiable Federated Learning with Vector Commitments Against Collusion Attacks_supp1-3674195.pdf
authors: Xia Feng
abstract: Collusion attacks, where the server and malicious clients collaborate to bypass gradient source confirmation or tamper with aggregation results, cause a fundamental damage to the training process in federated learning (FL). However, existing verifiable FL frameworks typically adopt a split-verification model-clients can independently validate the correctness of aggregation results, while the server is responsible for confirming the legitimacy of gradient sources. Thus, the collusion attack has emerged as a critical and intractable vulnerability, as it completely collapses this split-verification model, thereby invalidating such verification mechanisms. To tackle this fundamental issue, we propose an innovative dual-verifiable FL framework. Specifically, by leveraging vector commitments, our scheme first integrates both gradient source confirmation and aggregation result verification into a unified framework. Based on this unified design, our scheme implements two targeted strategies to defend against collusion attacks. To prevent collusion-enabled gradient source spoofing, our scheme introduces a semi-trusted verification cluster in place of unreliable server-side validation and embeds an anonymized identity-check strategy to collaboratively confirm gradient source legitimacy. To counter collusion-driven manipulation of gradient aggregation results, our scheme customizes auxiliary verification proofs with a computational one-wayness for client-uploaded gradients. This renders it infeasible for adversaries to tamper with the aggregation result through reverse engineering. Under experiments and security analyses, our scheme achieves reliable dual-verification and robust resistance to collusion attacks. Moreover, it reduces computation and communication overhead by at least 40.83% and 50.47%, respectively, compared to state-of-the-art verifiable FL schemes.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence in Software Engineering: Current Trends, Gaps, and Future Directions
authors: Adam Khan, Asad Ali, Muhammad Ismail Mohmand, Mahdi Zareei, Rajesh Roshan Biswal
abstract: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) has gained increasing attention for improving the transparency of artificial intelligence systems. However, there is a less clear understanding of its adoption and research patterns in the field of Software Engineering (SE).This systematic literature review focuses on the use of XAI within SE and the prospects of cross-domain learning. To achieve this, twenty-nine studies applying XAI in Software Engineering (SE), published between March 2020 and April 2024, were reviewed, and their findings were compared with XAI practices reported in healthcare and finance. The findings show that SE research mostly uses local explanation techniques: 86% of the studies employ instance-level methods, such as Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME), whereas global explanation techniques that provide an overall view of the model are used less frequently. In terms of application domains, XAI studies in SE are mainly focused on software defect prediction, while other domains, such as software testing, and effort estimation remain comparatively underexplored. Regarding implementation tools, scikit-learn is the most frequently used machine learning (ML) library in XAI-based SE studies, and less often, TensorFlow, PyTorch, and R based-frameworks are used. The current study is more comprehensive in terms of scope compared to the previous review, as it addresses four research questions, examines the types of explanations, and clearly mentions the utilization of ML libraries. The findings indicate that future SE studies should incorporate global explanation techniques, diversify XAI methods, and extend the research to underrepresented SE tasks. In addition, clear reporting of tools and libraries is essential to enhance reproducibility. Overall, although XAI in SE is progressing, the domain remains at an early stage, and insights from other fields may help improve interpretability, reliability, and trust in SE systems.

title: 3DVidar: A Single Mmwave Radar Based 3D Vibration Sensing Method Via Multi-Point Multi-Path Multi-Antenna Enhancement
authors: Yulong Zhang, Xuanheng Li, Yi Sun
abstract: Vibration sensing is crucial for machinery health monitoring, but traditional contact sensors face deployment challenges. Recently, millimeter wave (mmWave) radar has emerged as a promising contact-free alternative. However, since radar is mainly sensitive to the vibrations perpendicular to its antennas, existing works can only achieve 1D/2D vibration sensing based on single radar, or 3D sensing by multiple ones. In this paper, we propose 3DVidar, a single-radar 3D vibration sensing system without external reference target. Considering the insufficient information provided by single radar, we introduce a multi-point multi-path multi-antenna signal enhancement strategy to compensate for the lack of 3D vibration information. Furthermore, we develop two dedicated mechanisms to selectively filter the most informative radar signals for subsequent processing. Based on the enhanced signals, we design 3D-VRNet, a deep learning framework that incorporates positional priors and fuses multi-view signals through multi-scale convolutions and an attention mechanism. We implement 3DVidar on a commercial mmWave radar, and the results on two type of vibration targets show that it can accurately reconstruct 3D trajectory across various conditions.

title: Multi-Modal Longitudinal Framework for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis Using DPTPSAN and SHAP-Based Interpretability
authors: Virendra V. Bagade, Yashwant V. Dongre, Laxmi Bewoor
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: This paper is addressing AD diagnosis on pre-clinical stage by considering the longitudinal multimodal biomarker studies which reveal that the continuum of Alzheimer's disease (AD) includes a long latent phase, referred to as preclinical AD, which precedes the onset of symptoms by decades. Diagnosis treatment during the preclinical AD phase offers an optimal opportunity for slowing the progression of disease. Despite significant advancements in the field of Alzheimer's disease (AD) diagnosis, current diagnostic models still face several challenges that hinder their clinical applicability. Most existing methods focus either on neuroimaging or clinical data in isolation, without effectively leveraging the complementary strengths of multi-modal data. Moreover, many diagnostic frameworks rely on cross-sectional data, ignoring the temporal dynamics that are crucial for understanding the progression of AD. The integration of longitudinal clinical data with imaging information remains underexplored, and synthetic adjustment of such heterogeneous data is rarely addressed in the literature. Furthermore, issues like class imbalance, suboptimal feature fusion, limited interpretability, and lack of real-world deployment mechanisms-such as mobile-based tools-further exacerbate the challenge.

title: A Novel Multi-Factor Authentication Technique Using DNA Cryptography to Safeguard Oil Companies' Data
authors: Amal Khaleel Hamad, Mishall Al-Zubaidie
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: Cyber transaction protection is one of the largest concerns to oil firms due to the increasing security and privacy threats. Rivest Cipher 4 (RC4), and ElGamal cryptographies are only two among the many frameworks and measures that are already being implemented to secure transactions in the cyber world. Nevertheless, they possess their drawbacks like slow performance, breakability, obsolescence, and susceptibility to the whale-phishing attack. This paper introduces an efficient means of guarding Internet of Things (IoT) communications and oil industry cyber transactions depending on zero trust architecture (ZTA). It is an amalgamation of advanced practices such as tokenization (TK), data integration/DNA cryptography, GPS (global positioning system)-based authentication, password salting and hashing. This strategy will enhance the security of online transactions in the oil industry by enhancing the degree of privacy and reliability of cyber transactions through incorporation of extensive, robust, and effective verification frameworks that can withstand complex cybers attacks. The proposed framework is assessed theoretically to prove the security and performance of the system. In this study, we have been able to provide a theoretical security analysis against various threats and comparisons to show that our proposal would be better than the past research.

title: Agentic AI for Recon, Threat Analysis and Mitigation Reporting
authors: Kalaivani T, Charanya P, Abhindhira KP, Barath Kumar L, Santhosraj BS, Srinithi S
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Digital attacks in the ever-changing digital environment are becoming highly complex, more common, and dynamic, and this has become a major threat to organization of all sizes. In this paper, the researcher introduces the design of an autonomous and intelligent cybersecurity system that will carry out reconnaissance, threat reporting, and mitigation of websites and IP addresses. Based on an agentic AI architecture, the system uses dedicated AI agents to perform tasks through information collection, vulnerability scanning, and threat pattern matching and result interpreting, which do not require the training of new models. Instead, it runs on the top of existing Large Language Models (LLM), such as GPT and open-source ones, to comprehend and argue about complex security situations A User Interface is used to enter missions, an Orchestrator is used to sequence tasks, Agent modules are used to interact with industry-standard pentesting tools (such as Nmap, Nikto, and SQLmap), a Safety module precludes actions that are beyond its purview, and a Reporter module provides actionable insights. Its framework is focused on automation, adaptability, and scalability, and also limits the use of manual analysis and allows quicker and more precise threat detection and remedies. By means of continuous learning and effective management of various penetration testing situations, this approach becomes a viable way of enhancing cybersecurity measures in small organizations and learning institutions.

title: Real-Time Multi-Modal Deepfake Detection Using Spatiotemporal Attention Networks and Cross-Domain Adversarial Training
authors: A P. Gopu, R. Rakesh, P. Swetha, P. Tharun, S. Gopinath, K. Baby
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The CNN-LSTM structures implemented for deepfake detection approaches have displayed effectiveness, but their accuracy has been limited and cross domain success has been limited due to less than optimal spatiotemporal modeling and ability to detectdeepfakes in real time. To improve upon these limitations, our work proposes MS-STAN (Multi-Scale Spatiotemporal Attention Network), a spatiotemporal framework for deepfake detection that utilizes attention mechanisms to improve understanding of spatial features and temporal coherence. To accomplish this, the proposed model integrates EfficientNetV2 for enhanced extraction of spatial features, Bi-directional LSTMs to better exploit temporal dependencies, and self-attention to better aggregate temporal dependencies. To reinforce generalization by training across domains and without further delay, cross-domain adversarial training is also employed. Experimental findings on FaceForensics++, Celeb-DF, DFDCshow a significant improvement in accuracy and real-time detection at 45 FPS with an accuracy of 77%.In addition, our proposed spatiotemporal detection implements heatmaps to visualize spatiotemporal features that contributed to the decision in the classification process.

title: Deep Learning-Based Bird Species Classification Using ResNet-50
authors: Girish S, Yukesh S, Balaji Kannan K, Vigneshwar B, Vishnu Sekhar R
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Taxonomic classification of biological species plays a vital role in biodiversity monitoring, ecological research, and conservation efforts. Recent advances in deep learning have enabled automated species identification using visual data, reducing reliance on manual expertise. In this work, we investigate the effectiveness of transfer learning-based convolutional neural networks for bird species classification using a curated dataset of Indian birds. A systematic experimental study is conducted using ResNet architectures pretrained on ImageNet, focusing on the impact of feature extraction strategies, partial fine-tuning, and data augmentation. We compare a frozen backbone baseline against fine-tuned models with varying degrees of trainable layers and evaluate their performance across multiple training configurations. Additionally, a comparison between ResNet-50 and ResNet-152 is performed to analyze the trade-off between network depth and classification accuracy. To further assess the influence of dataset complexity, experiments are conducted on a reduced subset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex> visually distinctive bird species. The results demonstrate that partial fine-tuning of higher network layers combined with data augmentation significantly improves classification performance, achieving high validation accuracy while maintaining stable training behavior. This study highlights the effectiveness of controlled fine-tuning strategies for fine-grained taxonomic classification tasks.

title: Human Perception of AI-Generated Digital Avatars: Exploring Uncanny Valley and Ethical Dimensions
authors: Akshat Thakur, Mayon Rajpoot, Harminder Kaur
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The paper offers an in-depth, multidimensional analysis of human perceptions with regard to AI-generated digital avatars. Key themes involve the uncanny valley effect, perceived usefulness, ethical awareness, and cultural sensitivity. In all, 12 participants took part in the survey using a validated 23-item instrument whose reliability was excellent for all factors, Cronbach's α>0.78. Key findings show that ethical awareness coexists with, rather than opposes, technology acceptance. Regression modeling showed that age and experience with AI significantly predict perceptions of usefulness, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.42, at a probability level below 0.05. Cluster analysis uncovered two distinct user clusters, namely, tech enthusiasts exhibiting high levels of acceptance and cautious evaluators preoccupied with cultural sensitivity. The correlations among factors reveal the role of ethical awareness as an acceptance moderator. These findings provide empirical guidance into culturally sensitive deployment and ethical governance of synthetic resurrection technologies. The currently conducted study falls within the scope of AI ethics, human-computer interaction, and policy development, further combining the approaches of psychological, utilitarian, normative, and contextual frames regarding the perception of synthetic avatars.

title: Neural Network–Based Optimization of Base Station Placement for Local Positioning Systems
authors: Mikhail Vdovichev, Denis Bogdanov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: This paper deals with the problem of placing base stations of a local radio positioning system when the users are located inside a building and the infrastructure is deployed around it. In this situation, both the geometric configuration of the stations and the radio signal attenuation in the building must be taken into account. For a given floor plan, candidate layouts of three base stations are evaluated using a joint criterion based on the geometric factor and a coverage measure derived from a path-loss model with wall attenuation. The best layouts, found by exhaustive search, are then used as training data for a neural network that predicts near-optimal base station coordinates for new buildings.

title: An Efficient Deep Learning Approach for Face Detection Using Eigenfaces
authors: Singanamalla Vijayakumar, G. Abhinav, G. Sai Krishna, T. Mahender Reddy, Somula Ramasubbareddy
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The practical applications of real-time face recognition systems include their use in smartphone unlock functions and automated attendance systems as well as secure access control and airport identity checks together with surveillance operations and smart home automation systems. Real-time applications in this field demand identification methods which operate quickly while maintaining both scale and precision. This project applies Eigenfaces combined with Incremental Principal Component Analysis (IPCA) to build a face recognition system that stand out with both large datasets and real-time data processing. IPCA stands apart from traditional PCA because it performs data analysis through smaller batches which results in more efficient memory usage and better scalability. The continuous update of eigenfaces in IPCA enhances system performance by producing more accurate results and better stability which results in improved quality capabilities for dynamic environments. The system performs image preprocessing alongside IPCA feature extraction before implementing distance metrics for recognition which offers an effective solution for real-time face recognition tasks.

title: Content-Based Image Retrieval using Deep Learning for Medical Images
authors: Novy Jacob, Soumya George, Deepa Mathew K, Laxmi, Rooby M
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This project introduces an advanced framework incorporating GLCM, CNN, and Euclidean distance metrics for improved medical image retrieval. Focused on the efficient retrieval of medical images like Xrays, CT scans, MRIs, and histopathological slides, the application leverages deep learning techniques. By integrating GLCM for texture analysis and CNN for feature extraction, the system aims to enhance the accuracy of content-based image retrieval. Euclidean distance metrics will be utilized for similarity comparisons. This project aims to significantly improve medical professionals’ image search capabilities and contribute to enhanced healthcare outcomes.

title: Reliability Evaluation Method for Data Flow Control in Substation Interval Renovation Based on Multi-agent Algorithm
authors: Ning Chen, XiaoYong Sun, HongBin Wang, Hang Lv, Yu Yan, Bin Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: In digital distribution substations, the real-time sharing and fusion of multi-bay data can effectively improve the protection, measurement and control performance of the substation. Firstly, it is necessary to address the high real-time performance, stability and reliability of cross-bay data exchange in the substation process bus. From the perspective of the data sharing requirements of relay protection in distribution substations, this paper evaluates the communication characteristics when setting the process bus to transmit SAV and GOOSE messages in a “bay-oriented” and “location-oriented” manner, and analyzes and discusses the problems existing in “function-oriented” process bus communication settings as well as their solutions. The research shows that when the network traffic is within the operating range and the message length of each bay is the same, the maximum number of bays capable of data sharing is approximately inversely proportional to the signal sampling rate of a single bay; under a 100M network, the process layer networking modes oriented to bays and locations can basically meet the data sharing needs with a sampling rate below 400 points per cycle, and the main delay and data sharing limitations stem from the performance characteristics of switches.

title: The Concept of Constructing Normalized Scales for Diagnostic Results of Complex Objects
authors: Artem A. Geleta, Elena S. Suloeva
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: The report is devoted to issues of metrological assurance of diagnostics. Diagnostic methods aimed at assessing the state of complex objects are presented. Within the framework of the metrological approach, the problem of low informativeness of results reducible to a binary choice is investigated. A transition to the use of normalized scales for quantitative assessment is proposed as a solution. The result of the work is implementation variants for constructing scales based on the principles of metrological assurance, allowing for an increase in the informativeness of diagnostics.

title: Dualscan.AI: A Unified Platform for Brain Tumor and Pneumonia Recognition from Medical Images
authors: N. Bhuvaneswary, B. Hari Krishna, S. Vivek Kumar, P. Siva Prakash
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: In effective patient care, brain tumor and pneumonia must have an early and appropriate diagnosis. But interpreting chest X-ray and manual MRI images is frequently resource-intensive, time-intensive, and susceptible to variation inherent in clinical practice. Assisting physicians in making diagnoses, this project focuses on creating DualScan.ai, an fully integrated, AI-based medical image analysis platform that Automatically detects pneumonia on chest X-rays and brain malignancies from MR images. Convolutional neural networks DualScan.ai couples both Visual Analysis and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). Vision Transformers (ViT) have been carefully fine-tuned per application. imaging technique utilized alongside sophisticated preprocessing methods techniques like segmentation, noise reduction, and anatomical Verification of structure. A backend server based on Flask Trusted API service and a React front-end with an easily understandable To perform model inference, the platform uses TensorFlow 2.x. The system has JWT-based authentication to provide safe access to users, stringent image validation pipelines to ensure input integrity, and cloud deployment on Render with containerization on Docker for high availability and scalability. Full training and testing achieved encouraging performance which had good accuracy, recall and AUC score but lung at 95% of recall rate and brain tumor accepted only 92% accuracy. The software also includes confidence scoring for clinical interpretation and to record history analytics for Longitudinal monitoring. Leveraging the most advanced deep learning technology and cloud-based system architecture, DualScan.ai is an end-to-end solution, fulfilling the need for efficient, accurate and low-cost medical imaging Diagnostic tools. The study can contribute towards the execution of AI in healthcare and enhance diagnostic workflow.

title: Design and Experimental Investigation of an Enhanced Solar Dryer with Polycarbonate Sheet and Thermal Energy Storage
authors: I Rahamathullah, T Jayakumar, A Kumaravadivel, S. Sugumar
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Solar drying is a good and a sustainable method used in preserving agricultural produce though the traditional open sun drying method has issues based on low efficiency, lack of quality control and limited weather dependence. The present research offers the design, fabrication and experimental study of an improved type of cabinet-based solar dryer that is combined with a polycarbonate sheet and thermal energy store to dry brinjal (Solanum melongene). The suggested system integrates a polycarbonate sheet drying chamber, forced air circulation and each of the following thermal stability enhancements such as a combined latent sensible heat storage system which uses phase change material (PCM) and high heat capacity material (HHCM) which is incorporated to enhance the thermal stability. Similar experimental studies involved three drying setups, that is, open sun drying, cabinet drying without PCM, and cabinet drying with PCM-HHCM. The dryer was tested on the basis of temperature change, solar radiation, rate of removal of moisture and time taken in the course of drying. Findings reveal that PCM-HHCM integrated dryer has gained the higher and more constant drying temperature and the highest drying temperature of about 72° C in contrast with the other settings. The increased thermal retention was a great boost in the speed of moisture removal and the time taken to completely dry brinjal was only a day long in a daylight cycle. The paper shows that the integrated impact of the polycarbonate sheet refraction and thermal energy storage is effective in enhancing its efficiency in drying, including the quality of products and thus the proposed system is applicable in the small scale of farming. (by using polycarbonate sheet the UV rays are not enter into the dryer so it will maintain the vitamin content present in the food product why because the UV rays affect the vitamin content in the food). Using PCM-HHCM to increase the drying time it will reduce the drying time and give more efficiency compare to open sun drying and cabinet solar drying).

title: Detecting Sign Language Using LSTM-Based Algorithm for Inclusive Communication
authors: Vishal Singh Khati, Gaurav Sharma, Pawan Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This study presents an Indian Sign Language (ISL)-specific real-time sign language recognition system. The system uses OpenCV for skin segmentation, focusing on hand motions, to find and monitor Zones of Interest (ZOI). Hand landmarks are recorded using Media Pipe, and their important points are kept in a NumPy array. Model training is facilitated using TensorFlow, Keras, and LSTM. A live webcam feed is used for evaluating the model in real time. This creative method offers a way to communicate with the outside world, which is promising for the deaf and dumb community. Accuracy, precision, and recall metrics are used to assess the system’s efficacy using a dataset of frequently used Indian sign language motions. The LSTM model obtains a perfect accuracy of 1. The output emphasizes the usefulness of the technology by providing both text and audio representations of the identified sign gestures.

title: A Neural Network Approach for Speech to Text Translation of Low Resource Language
authors: Kumaresh Sheelavant, Preksha G P, Sakshi Shanbhag, Shreya B C, Sneha T, Shilpa Patil
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Developing speech translation systems for low-resource languages remains a major issue because lack of annotated datasets and linguistic resources. This work introduces a neural speech-to-text translation framework designed for low-resource Indian languages, with Konkani as a case study. The proposed system converts spoken Konkani into English text through Hindi as a pivot language, integrating Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) and Neural Machine Translation (NMT) models within a unified architecture. The methodology involves audio preprocessing, feature extraction, and pivot-based translation using pretrained models such as IndicConformer and Transformer-based NMT to ensure fluency and adaptability. By leveraging transfer learning and multilingual modeling, the approach effectively addresses issues of data scarcity, accent variation, and linguistic diversity.
