In comparison, our proposed pipeline attains 29.6% WER and 28.1 BLEU, demonstrating competitive performance despite operating in a low-resource setting. These results show that our model performs on par with, and in some cases surpasses, existing benchmarks for similar multilingual pipelines.

title: Integrating Transformer and Generative Models for Intergenre Text Reframing
authors: Alina Sungurova, Yaroslava Isaeva, Ekaterina Isaeva
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: In the context of rapidly increasing volumes of specialized information, the problem of adapting complex texts to the cognitive characteristics of different user groups is gaining particular importance. The effectiveness of information perception largely depends on the reader’s background knowledge, education level, and familiarity with domain-specific terminology. This paper proposes an architectural framework for a system designed to perform cognitive adaptation of specialized texts. The suggested model integrates several modules, including text analysis, audience cognitive profiling, adaptive text generation with semantic preservation, and quality evaluation. The use of transformer-based language models in combination with corpus-driven audience representations enables the generation of texts aligned with the cognitive load and comprehension level of the target group. To solve the problem, we consider Ch. Fillmore’s frame semantics as the cognitive linguistic foundation of the study, and develop the workflow, which compiles a two-genre corpus, simulates framing profiles in the genre subcorpora using the FrameSemanticTransformer model, and finally generates the reframed texts according to the expected framing profile using the gigachat-0.1.43 model. The proposed architecture can be applied in the development of intelligent educational platforms, information and analytical systems, and media services aimed at delivering complex materials to non-specialist audiences without loss of meaning or accuracy.

title: Hybrid Genetic Algorithm and Fuzzy Control Approach for Lunar Lander Soft Landing
authors: Vitaly S. Oleynikov, Guan Tianyi
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: Lunar landing requires precise control of the descent trajectory to ensure both safety and fuel efficiency. This paper develops a practical control solution by combining the global optimization capability of genetic algorithms with the adaptive nature of fuzzy logic control. The initial conditions are set based on the Chang'e-3 mission profile, with an initial altitude of 15 km and horizontal velocity of 1.7 km/s[1]. The genetic algorithm generates an optimal reference trajectory by considering fuel consumption and various operational constraints. Fuzzy control rules are then formulated based on this trajectory to handle the real-time control task during landing. The fuzzy controller continuously adjusts the thrust to maintain trajectory tracking while accommodating disturbances and model uncertainties. Simulation results indicate that the proposed method achieves consistent landing performance with good accuracy and robustness, making it suitable for autonomous lunar landing missions.

title: Automated Malaria Diagnosis Using a Fine-Tuned EfficientNetB3 Model on Blood Smear Images
authors: Mukesh Kumar, Sandeep Kumar, Gaurav Sharma, G Santhosh Kumar, Zaid Alsalami, Anurag Shrivastava, Deepak Banerjee
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Malaria is a major global health issue with great death especially in tropical and subtropical regions. Correct and quick diagnosis is essential for good prevention and control of diseases. Historically, the diagnosis of malaria has hinged on hand microscopic inspection of blood smears, a technique prone to human error as well as time-consuming and labour-intensive character. By building an automated malaria detection system using an upgraded EfficientNetB3 model, our work seeks to address these problems. Designed to split blood smear images into parasitised and uninfected groups, the EfficientNetB3 design celebrated their harmonic scaling of depth, width, and resolution. With an F1-score of 0.97 the model proved dependability and durability by showing amazing general accuracy of 97% for both classes. Particularly in environments with limited resources, these findings underline the promise of the customised EfficientNetB3 model to greatly increase malaria diagnosis, hence improving patient outcomes and supporting more efficient disease control.

title: Lattice-Based Functional Encryption for Next-generation Privacy-Preserving Computation
authors: S. Vimalalochana, M. Sakthivanitha, S. Cyciliya Pearline Christy, Vishwa Priya V, Vedavalli S, S. Janani
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: With the rise of cloud-based data processing and multi-party computations, the demand for secure, sturdy, and effective privacy-preserving tasks has risen. Homomorphic Encryption (HE) and Bilinear Functional Encryption (FE) users face computational and properties constraints in the form of high computation overheads, high ciphertext expansions, and lower functionality accuracy reducing usability. This research wishes to avoid such limitations by developing Lattice-Based Functional Encryption (FE) framework, which takes into consideration the neighborhood of security, efficiency and accuracy. Constructing a series of structured lattice based schemes for key generation and ciphertext formatting, allows for encrypted data evaluation without any need for decrypting, with worst-case hardness security properties. Experimental evaluations based on structured datasets, time of encryption, time of queries evaluation, ciphertext length, and accuracy of functions were observed. Lattice-Based FE achieved a total time of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.5 ~\text{ms} / 1 ~\text{KB}$</tex> encryption time, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$14.3 ~\text{ms} /$</tex> query evaluation time, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.1 ~\text{KB} / 1 ~\text{KB}$</tex> ciphertext size and resulted in 99.0 % functional accuracy as compared to HE and Bilinear FE methods. The findings show that the lattice-based FE was able to deliver scalable and effective privacy-preserving computations. This framework moreover will provide a promising approach for secure data processing tasks based on the IoT, finance and cloud interest, ushering an expansion into more cryptographic methods for years to come.

title: Modeling a Reduced Hybrid Quantum-Inspired Framework for Optimized Speckle Denoising Filters
authors: M Su-Ada, S Shailesh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: This paper examines a hybrid classical-quantum workflow for speckle-oriented image denoising, in which denoising is formulated as a discrete filter-parameter selection problem over image batches. Candidate convolution kernels are evaluated using batch-averaged peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) and structural similarity (SSIM), and the best-performing kernel is deployed within a variance-guided adaptive filtering stage. The selected kernel configuration is then encoded into a quantum register and marked using a Grover oracle, followed by amplitude amplification to increase the likelihood of retrieving the target configuration upon measurement. In this formulation, the quantum circuit serves to demonstrate parameter encoding, oraclebased marking, and probabilistic retrieval consistent with the classically selected optimum, rather than replacing classical fitness evaluation. Experiments conducted on speckle-contaminated image batches from multiple imaging modalities show that the optimized kernel provides stable denoising performance, while the quantum subroutine reproduces the target configuration with high probability under practical circuit-depth constraints.

title: Automated Control of Product Labeling System Based on Computer Vision
authors: Maxim Bogachev, Margarita Rudyy, Daniil Inechkin, Gleb Shvets
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: The increasing requirements for product traceability and digital labeling impose stricter demands on the correctness of applying unique identifiers within national and supranational regulatory systems (e.g., the "Chestny Znak" system in the Russian Federation, EUDAMED in the European Union for medical devices). Violations such as duplication, absence, or incorrect placement of labels complicate logistics processes and lead to risks of regulatory penalties, product batch returns, repeated re-sorting, and reduced trust from partners and consumers. This paper addresses these challenges by proposing an additional in-line control mechanism based on an automated machine vision system. The proposed solution enables continuous verification of labeling quality directly on the production line and transfers the responsibility for release decisions from human operators to an automated inspection system, thereby improving process reliability under conditions of high throughput and product variability.

title: Contextual Feature Extraction for Personality Prediction Using Transformer-Based Models
authors: Suman A. Patil, Dr.Shivaleela Patil
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Personality inference from written text has emerged as a prominent interdisciplinary area, connecting the fields of computational linguistics, psychology, and artificial intelligence. Its applications span personalized recommendation engines, mental health monitoring tools, and adaptive human-computer interfaces. Early approaches largely depended on self-reported surveys and simple textual features, including Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) metrics and n-gram representations. In recent years, the field has shifted toward deep learning solutions, utilizing architectures such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. This research investigates the capabilities of transformer-based models, particularly DistilBERT, to extract detailed contextual cues from social media content. By leveraging multi-head self-attention and contextualized embeddings, the approach captures intricate semantic and syntactic relationships, enabling a more sophisticated characterization of personality traits. The proposed methodology not only achieves superior predictive accuracy compared to conventional techniques but also provides practical benefits in terms of computational efficiency and scalability. Overall, these results highlight the significant impact of advanced natural language processing techniques on personality modeling, while also emphasizing critical concerns related to interpretability, ethical considerations, and potential biases in the data.

title: VOAPI2: a Vulnerability-Oriented Testing Framework for RESTful APIs
authors: Lübin Xu, Zhuolin Zeng, Zhen Tang, Biao Xiao, Dong Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the rapid proliferation of Web services, RESTful APIs have become a fundamental building block of modern software systems. As APIs increasingly expose critical business logic and sensitive data, ensuring their security has emerged as a pressing challenge. However, most existing RESTful API testing tools primarily emphasize functional correctness and robustness, and thus often fail to effectively uncover security vulnerabilities that do not manifest as obvious crashes or server errors.In this paper, we present VOAPI2, a vulnerability-oriented inspection framework for RESTful APIs that explicitly targets security flaws by leveraging the intrinsic relationship between API functionality and vulnerability types [1]. Unlike conventional API fuzzers that explore endpoints indiscriminately, VOAPI2 adopts a guided testing strategy that first identifies security-critical endpoints by analyzing semantic cues in API specifications. Based on these cues, the framework generates vulnerability-specific attack payloads and embeds them into state-aware request sequences that respect API execution dependencies.To accurately determine whether a vulnerability is successfully triggered, VOAPI2 further incorporates a feedback-driven verification mechanism that analyzes API responses and observable side effects rather than relying solely on generic server error codes. We implement a prototype of VOAPI2 on top of an existing stateful API testing engine and conduct a preliminary evaluation on real-world APIs. The results demonstrate that VOAPI2 is capable of discovering security vulnerabilities that are missed by state-of-the-art API testing tools, including previously unknown security issues that were later confirmed by developers. These findings indicate that vulnerability-oriented testing is a practical and effective direction for improving the coverage and efficiency of RESTful API security testing.

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)

title: Intelligent Early Warning Model for Financial Public Opinion Risk Based on Multimodal Feature-Level Fusion
authors: Yining Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: The core challenge of financial public opinion risk early warning is the difficulty of segmentation and coordination of multi-source heterogeneous data (text, image, video and other modalities) at the feature level, which leads to the one-sidedness and lag in risk signal identification. To this end, this paper proposes an intelligent early warning model for financial public opinion risk based on multimodal feature-level fusion. First, a dedicated module is designed to deeply extract the essential features of each modality. Then an adaptive feature-level fusion framework for the financial field is constructed. Finally, the gated fusion technology is used to deeply integrate the optimized aligned and weighted multimodal features at the feature level. Experimental results show that the proposed model has an accuracy of 92.5 %, an F 1 value of 90.7 %, and a recall rate of 91.8% on a real financial public opinion dataset. Cross-modal attention and weighted fusion mechanisms are proven to be the key to improving performance (the F1 value of the model drops to 80.1 % and 82.5 % respectively after removal). The model also shows good adaptability in different risk scenarios. These results verify the effectiveness of multimodal feature-level fusion methods in financial public opinion risk warning, which can significantly improve the recognition accuracy and adaptability of the model.

title: RMC-YOLO: A Small Object Detection Framework with Routing Attention and Multi-Scale Context Modulation
authors: Jimeng Chen, Xiaodong Wang, Lianjun Zhang, Yuli Liu, Min Luo
abstract: Detecting small objects in UAV images is challenging due to their limited size and the complexity of background. This work introduces an improved YOLOv8nbased approach designed to enhance performance in such environments. Specifically, a feature modeling framework, named RMC (Routing-aware Modulated Context) is designed, which integrates a routing-aware attention mechanism with a multi-scale context modulation strategy to better capture and distinguish small object features. The backbone of YOLOv8n is restructured by introducing the Omni-dimensional Dynamic Convolution (ODConv) module and the Routing Attention Spatial Aggregator (RASA) module, both aimed at improving the model's sensitivity to target regions. Meanwhile, the original SPPF module is replaced by the Multi-Scale Context Modulation (MSCM) module to strengthen the modeling of complex contextual information. Further improvements are made in the neck by introducing an extra feature layer and replacing the detection head with the Adaptive Spatial Feature Fusion (ASFF) module, which is incorporated to enable effective multi-scale feature fusion. Validation on the VisDrone2019 dataset demonstrates that the RMC-YOLO method reaches 38.9% <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP}@0.5$</tex>, representing a 4.9% improvement over the original YOLOv8n.

title: Lightweight Yet Powerful: Optimized Tightly Coupled Hyperdimensional Computing Accelerators
authors: Mohsen Asghari, Sebastien Le Beux, Ron Mankarious
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Hyperdimensional Computing (HDC) is a hardware-friendly machine learning paradigm that encodes data into high-dimensional binary hypervectors, enabling efficient computation through lightweight bitwise operations. Existing HDC accelerators, often implemented on FPGAs, typically rely on custom instruction sets and co-processor designs to optimize performance. However, these architectures face programmability challenges and incur significant control overhead when interacting with the main processor. To overcome these limitations, we present a methodology for designing and optimizing tightly coupled HDC accelerators. The proposed method produces accelerators enhanced with a controller capable of decoding instructions corresponding to key HDC kernels, thereby reducing communication overhead and improving throughput. To validate the approach, we implemented the accelerator at the RTL level and modeled the entire system in SystemVerilog. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to state-of-the-art architectures, the proposed method reduces software code size by up to 98% while achieving a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(3 \times$</tex>) speedup.

title: An Error-Resilient Area-Efficient Ultra-Low-Power 4-Bit Flash ADC Design with Multi-Vt Transistors
authors: Riyanka Banerjee, Jay Nangia, M. Santosh, Jai Gopal Pandey
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: In high-speed, low-resolution conversion applications, Flash analog-to-digital converters (ADCs) are most suitable. In the proposed work, a Flash ADC is designed using multithreshold transistors offered in sub-micron CMOS technology, effectively leveraging low-threshold voltage transistors (LVT), nominal-threshold voltage transistors (NVT), regular-threshold voltage transistors (RVT), and high-threshold voltage transistors (HVT) transistors to optimize performance and power efficiency. The designed Flash ADC architecture incorporates calibrated multi-threshold transistors based comparators to balance between gain-bandwidth trade-off, reduced area, desired figure of merit (FoM), and ensure bubble-free encoding. The proposed design achieves a sampling rate of 2 MS/s at a 1.2 V supply, consumes only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$17.48 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$478.6788 \mu \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> active area. Key performance metrics include a peak differential non-linearity (DNL) of 0.4 LSB, INL of 0.7 LSB a signal-to-noise-and-distortion ratio (SNDR) of 22.83 dB, an effective number of bits (ENOB) of 3.5, and a resulting FoM of 273.13 fJ/conversion-step.

title: Delay Tolerant Precaching Scheme Based on Proximal Policy Optimization in Content-Centric Vehicular Networks
authors: Jeongtak Na, Jongpil Youn, Irina Em, Euisin Lee, Youngju Nam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The surge in multimedia demand from autonomous driving and infotainment stresses RSUs; mobility and limited wireless capacity cause delays and QoS degradation. While CCVN precaching has been studied, delay-tolerant services remain underexplored. We present a PPO-based delay-tolerant precaching scheme featuring path-aware RSU discovery, proportional precaching decisions, and opportunistic transmission within deadlines. A tailored reward balances delivery success, backhaul reduction, and traffic efficiency. Simulation results in Large-scale SUMO experiments show that our scheme outperforms a heuristic scheme in content delivery success ratio and traffic overhead, demonstrating scalable and adaptive learning for delay-tolerant vehicular content delivery.

title: Clarity by Design: Comprehensive Checklists in Medical Communication: Kelly Schrank: [Book Review]
authors: Joanne M. DeVoir
abstract: Presents reviews for the following list of books, Clarity by Design: Comprehensive Checklists in Medical Communication.

title: Hybrid Beamforming for Subarray-Level Movable Antenna Enhanced MU-MIMO Communications
authors: Shanshan Zhang, Songjie Yang, Wenxuan Zhang, Youzhi Xiong, Siya Yao
abstract: This study investigates subarray-level movable antenna (MA) architecture for multi-user MIMO (MU-MIMO) systems. Unlike conventional systems with fixed-position antennas (FPAs), the proposed scheme harnesses the additional positional degrees of freedom (DoFs) of movable subarrays to enhance spatial multiplexing capabilities for both multi-user and multi-stream communications. Our objective is to maximize the overall system spectral efficiency by jointly optimizing the hybrid beamforming design and the positions of all subarrays. To tackle this challenging non-convex optimization problem, we first adopt a block diagonalization (BD) based digital precoder to effectively eliminate multi-user interference. Subsequently, the joint optimization of the analog beamformer and the subarray positions is efficiently solved using a sequential interference cancellation (SIC)-based algorithm. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed SIC-MA method significantly outperforms the benchmark SIC-FPA scheme where subarrays are fixed.

title: KidMesh: Computational Mesh Reconstruction for Pediatric Congenital Hydronephrosis Using Deep Neural Networks
authors: Haoran Sun, Zhanpeng Zhu, Anguo Zhang, Bo Liu, Zhaohua Lin, Liqin Huang, Mingjing Yang, Lei Liu, Shan Lin, Wangbin Ding
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Pediatric congenital hydronephrosis (CH) is a common urinary tract disorder, primarily caused by obstruction at the renal pelvis-ureter junction. Magnetic resonance urography (MRU) can visualize hydronephrosis, including renal pelvis and calyces, by utilizing the natural contrast provided by water. Existing voxel-based segmentation approaches can extract CH regions from MRU, facilitating disease diagnosis and prognosis. However, these segmentation methods predominantly focus on morphological features, such as size, shape, and structure. To enable functional assessments, such as urodynamic simulations, external complex post-processing steps are required to convert these results into mesh-level representations. To address this limitation, we propose an end-to-end method based on deep neural networks, namely KidMesh, which could automatically reconstruct CH meshes directly from MRU. Generally, KidMesh extracts feature maps from MRU images and converts them into feature vertices through grid sampling. It then deforms a template mesh according to these feature vertices to generate the specific CH meshes of MRU images. Meanwhile, we develop a novel schema to train KidMesh without relying on accurate mesh-level annotations, which are difficult to obtain due to the sparsely sampled MRU slices. Experimental results show that KidMesh reconstructs CH meshes in an average of 0.4 seconds, and achieve comparable performance to conventional methods without requiring post-processing. The reconstructed meshes exhibited no self-intersections, with only 3.7% and 0.2% of the vertices having error distances exceeding 3.2mm and 6.4mm, respectively. After rasterization, these meshes achieved a Dice score of 0.86 against manually delineated CH masks. Furthermore, these meshes could be used in renal urine flow simulations, providing valuable urodynamic information for clinical practice.

title: Monkey See, Monkey Break? Study of Rule-Breaking Imitation in Virtual Crowds
authors: Kexiang Huang, Tairan Yin, Jose Luis Ponton, Ruida Tang, Reiya Itatani, Gangyi Ding, Oscar Argudo, Nuria Pelechano
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Rule-breaking behaviors, such as jaywalking or skipping queues, are common in crowds but difficult to study in real-world settings due to limited control and observability. Virtual reality (VR) provides a controlled alternative, but its validity depends on whether VRelicits realistic rule-breaking behavior. We conducted a VR study with 65 participants navigating a virtual city with four scenarios differing in social norms: walking on grass, crossing outside a crosswalk, jaywalking at a red light, and skipping a line. In each scenario, the proportion of rule-breaking virtual characters was manipulated (0%, 25%, 50%). Participants' movements and gaze were recorded to assess behavior and attention. Results showed higher rule-breaking as the number of violators increased, except in the low-stakes crossing scenario. Rule-breakers attended more to violating characters, whereas rule-followers focused on compliant ones. Participants cited efficiency, safety, and social norms as key factors guiding their decisions. Overall, VR reproduced natural patterns of social compliance and noncompliance, supporting its use for studying rule-breaking and applications in crowd simulation, urban design, safety training, and immersive media.

title: Enhanced Disease Susceptible Variant Identification via Short Identity by Descent Segments
authors: Chonghao Wang, Werner Pieter Veldsman, Yufen Huang, Xiaodong Fang, Lu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Rare diseases affect millions of individuals worldwide, yet diagnostic yields for them still remain low. Among variant identification approaches, identity by descent (IBD) mapping is used to identify disease susceptible variants originating from a recent common ancestor among affected individuals, but existing IBD detection models struggle to identify these variants in short IBD segments. Here, we introduce SILO, a novel model to detect disease susceptible variants in both short and long IBD segments. SILO employs a two-stage procedure to detect IBD segments. In the first stage, SILO identifies long IBD segments based on common variants. In the second stage, SILO utilizes rare variants to detect short IBD segments using a seed-and-extend algorithm. We evaluated SILO in simulated data and real data from the 1000 Genomes Project. Our results demonstrate that SILO outperforms existing models in detecting disease susceptible variants within short IBD segments, and show comparable performance in detecting these variants within longer IBD segments. These findings highlight the potential of SILO to increase diagnostic yields for rare diseases by enhancing the identification of previously overlooked disease susceptible variants in short IBD segments. Nonetheless, we note that the detection of short IBD segments remains challenging due to limited precision, leaving room for future improvement.

title: Angular-Domain Control of Electromagnetic Waves Through Scattering Manipulation
authors: Ran Song, Tao Hong, Jiangcheng Ge, Zhexun Zhang, Wen Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article addresses two primordial questions concerning angularly dispersive periodic structures. First, how does the scattering response govern electromagnetic functionality at specific angles? Second, under what conditions can perfect transmission, reflection, or absorption be achieved at given angles? To this end, a unified framework is proposed to systematically integrate angular-selective transmission, reflection, and absorption. By decomposing the total scattered field into independently controllable antenna mode scattered field (AM-SF) and structural mode scattered field (SM-SF), the framework establishes a systematic link between scattering characteristics and electromagnetic functionality. This approach offers a clear physical foundation for angular manipulation of electromagnetic waves. The proposed framework is validated through simulations of three types of angle-selective surfaces (ASSs), namely, reflection–absorption (RA), transmission–reflection (TR), and transmission–absorption (TA). Experimental measurements are further carried out for TM-polarized TR-ASS and TA-ASS, along with a dual-polarized TA-ASS. These results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method and demonstrate a scalable design framework for diverse angular-domain filtering functionalities in RF front-ends.

title: FreqHDR: Frequency-Enhanced HDR Video Reconstruction
authors: Tianxin Huang, Yun Zhou, Tian He, Zhixin Zheng, Aidong Men, Zhuqing Jiang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Reconstructing high dynamic range (HDR) video from alternating exposure sequences remains highly challenging. Existing methods primarily rely on optical flow or attention mechanisms for low dynamic range (LDR) sequence alignment and ghosting artifact suppression. However, optical flow integration alone proves insufficient for consistent performance improvement in dynamic/static scenes, exhibiting limited noise suppression and suboptimal detail recovery. To address these limitations, a novel exposure fusion framework, FreqHDR, is proposed, incorporating a dedicated Spatial-Frequency Integration Block (SFIB). The encoder adopts a stage-wise cascaded architecture for progressive multi-scale feature extraction, with each stage enhanced by the SFIB module. The SFIB follows a dual-path design: the spatial path employs convolutions and channel attention to capture local structural details, while the frequency path utilizes Fourier transforms for global semantic context. Features from both domains are dynamically fused and refined via a gated residual connection, significantly strengthening representation learning. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior or competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches on benchmark datasets.

title: The Value and Methodology of Blue-and-White Porcelain Decorative Patterns: A Technical and Theoretical Framework for Purification, Classification, and Restoration
authors: Yujie Cheng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: The study of blue-and-white porcelain patterns has long been challenged by two core issues: the high adhesion between patterns and backgrounds, and the complexity of categorical systems. This paper proposes a computational framework for a complete research pipeline to systematically address these challenges. The framework consists of two implemented pillars and one forward-looking direction. First, the GrabCut algorithm is employed to achieve high-precision segmentation of complex patterns, effectively resolving feature confusion and providing clean data for subsequent analysis. Second, through a benchmark comparison of seven deep learning models,ResNet101 is established as the current optimal model for classification (with a test accuracy of $\mathbf{9 1 . 9 8 \%} \boldsymbol{)}$,providing a solid performance baseline for the intelligent recognition of patterns. Building on the achievements in segmentation and classification, this paper further outlines a prospective digital restoration path centered on generative models, thereby forming a complete research loop from “data purification” and “pattern recognition” to “content regeneration”. This work not only resolves key technical challenges in pattern segmentation and classification through empirical research but also lays a theoretical foundation and practical framework for an end-to-end computational analysis system for blue-and-white porcelain patterns, promoting a shift toward quantifiable and scalable research paradigms in the field.

title: Multi-Level Pelican Optimization with Fuzzy Thresholding and Deep Residual CNN for Enhanced Fatty Liver Classification in Abdominal CT Scans
authors: R Radha, Blessy Boaz
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Fatty liver disease is one of the most common liver problems in the world; however, it is frequently overlooked in its early stages since the variations in abdominal CT scans tend to be compact. This paper introduces a novel hybrid framework that integrates Multi-Level Pelican Optimization (MLPO), Fuzzy Thresholding, and a Deep Residual Convolutional Neural Network (ResCNN) to facilitate the identification and classification of fatty liver. The proposed framework uses MLPO in two different ways: first, to improve the quality of the image by choosing the best contrast parameters, and second, to find the most distinguishing characteristics after liver segmentation. Fuzzy thresholding is different from typical binary segmentation algorithms since it gives pixels membership values based on their intensity levels. This renders it easier to find soft boundaries, which makes it easier to delineate the liver region even in low-contrast or noisy images. The segmented liver area is achieved through a ResCNN architecture, utilizing deep feature extraction and skip connections to preserve spatial and contextual information. This approach assists the classifier in telling the difference between normal and fatty liver tissues, even when there is a lot of variation in classes and intensities overlap. Many tests have been performed on annotated abdominal CT datasets, and the results were based on accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, F1-score, and the Dice similarity coefficient. The results show that the new MLPO-Fuzzy-ResCNN framework does an excellent job of classifying things better than other methods. This study provides a strong, automated pipeline that can be used in clinical environments to help with early diagnosis and successful monitoring of fatty liver disease utilizing non-invasive imaging methods.

title: An Efficient Fingerprint Extraction and Matching Algorithm for Imaging Device
authors: Yikun Geng, Yushi Li, Xuefeng Zhang, Along Jin
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of how to efficiently extract and accurately match the fingerprint information collected by the imaging device, an efficient fingerprint extraction and matching algorithm is proposed. Firstly, the preprocessed fingerprint image is extracted using the trained convolutional neural network (CNN), and the extracted feature vectors are compared using the elastic deformation matching algorithm. The matching degree between the two fingerprint images is evaluated by calculating the similarity and distance between the feature points. The algorithm adjusts the position of the feature points within a certain range to achieve a more accurate matching effect. The simulation results show that under different imaging device types and fingerprint image quality conditions, the average matching accuracy of the proposed algorithm is $94.53 \%$, and the extraction speed is also faster.

title: Design of Non-Gaussian Receivers for Ultra-Low Frequency Channels
authors: Wenwei Ying, Xun Li, Xiaobo Wang, Zijie Niu, Anbo Zhang, Zhen Qiao
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: Conventional receivers experience severe performance degradation in ultra-low frequency channels due to non-Gaussian noise interference. To ensure reliable communication for underwater vehicles and to support mission-critical operations, a non-Gaussian receiver design is proposed. The proposed approach includes two main components. The first component develops a nonlinear processor that estimates the derivative behavior of the noise probability density function through a nonparametric method. A dedicated search algorithm simplifies the estimated curve into a three-segment linear model, enabling Gaussian mapping of non-Gaussian signals. The second component optimizes the Turbo decoder using a maximum a posteriori iterative decoding structure. The received signal is divided into information and parity bits, which are processed by two sub-decoders that exchange a priori and extrinsic information through multiple iterations. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed receiver achieves comparable performance to the conventional Gaussian receiver under Gaussian noise conditions, while providing significant improvement in communication reliability under non-Gaussian impulsive noise environments.

title: A Geometric-Analytical Algorithm for Hybrid AOA/TDOA Localization
authors: Jindian Lv, Feifei Pang, Haiyan Wang, Yong Liang
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: Safety management of low-altitude airspace is a critical prerequisite for fostering the high-quality development of the low-altitude economy. Accurate positioning technology has emerged as a central challenge in mitigating collision risks associated with the deployment of high-density aircraft. This paper proposes a collaborative positioning algorithm that integrates time-difference-of-arrival (TDOA) and angle-of-arrival (AOA) information. The proposed algorithm rigorously exploits the inherent geometric constraints present in the TDOA and AOA observation models. By incorporating AOA measurements into the nonlinear equations of TDOA, the algorithm effectively transforms the nonlinear localization model into a pseudo-linear formulation. Unlike conventional nonlinear Taylor-series expansion methods, the proposed approach derives a closed-form solution directly from the pseudo-linear equation, thereby completely eliminating the iterative process and its associated convergence issues. Simulation results demonstrate that maintaining a low Horizontal Dilution of Precision (HDOP) during sensor deployment leads to superior localization performance.

title: Achieving Secure and Reliable URLLC in Cell-Free Massive MIMO via Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Donggen Li, Shuo Sun, Xinxin Ma, Hengjie Ren, Chong Huang, Wenjiang Feng
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: The stringent requirements of ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC) contribute to an enhanced service experience for user equipments (UEs). However, it also poses a challenge to the design of security strategies when UEs require multiple businesses while the communication resources are constrained. This letter focuses on the security and reliability of URLLC for uplink transmission in cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) systems. We define a new metric, secure and reliable probability (SRP), to characterize the trade-off between secure and reliable services in the system. Furthermore, we propose a power control approach based on self-supervised learning, which adjusts the user's power allocation strategy to meet maximum security or maximum reliability requirements of different UEs and improve the performance of the whole system. Finally, extensive simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

authors: N/A

authors: N/A
