abstract: A common way to represent electric power quality is as an indicator of how well users can use electric power services. Power consumption is reduced when wave forms are inconsistent, voltage is not well controlled, flicker and harmonics are noticeable, or there are brief occurrences that cause the normally sinusoidal wave to be distorted. This is known as power quality decline. The use of based bespoke power devices in specialized power applications is growing. Non-sinusoidal load currents have become more prevalent because to the extensive usage of high-power semiconductor switches at the usage, distribution, and transmission levels. These days, power quality is undoubtedly a big concern, especially for electronic equipment like programmable logic controllers and speed-controlled motors. crucial in relation to the power supply. Because many electronic instruments used in modern manufacturing procedures are highly sensitive to disruptions, industrial loads are less forgiving of power quality issues. Based on ideas of instantaneous power that utilize the synchronous-rotating d-q reference frame, the authors of this work proposed a multi-level control strategy for the integrated D-STATCOM/BESS device. This scheme consists of an exterior, middle, and internal level. Because rotating reference frames are more accurate than stationary frame-based methods, they are used. In contrast to a PI controller, an ANN (Artificial Neural Network) method is employed to improve system performance. The outcomes of the suggested system demonstrate how successful it is in comparison to the PI controller.

title: Adaptive and Optimization-Based Model-Invariant Fault-Tolerant Control Strategies for Dual Three-Phase PMSM Drives Under Open-Phase Faults
authors: Saloni Bansal
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: This paper describes an adaptive fault-tolerant control strategy for dual three-phase permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drive systems in open-phase fault scenarios that was developer without the need for fault localization or reconfiguration of controllers. Conventional methods for developing fault-tolerant controllers rely on complex fault-detection schemes and a reconfigurable control structure that can complicate the fault-tolerant design, exacerbate transient performance after fault detection and can lead to misdiagnosis. The new method enables robust postfault operation through an adaptive process to synthesize reference currents directly from feedback information (at run-time), using advanced model reference adaptive systems (MRAS) for online parameter identification and real-time current adjustment, ensuring stable torque generation and torque ripple suppression across the various fault scenarios demonstrated. Experimental and simulation results show that the controller implemented smooth transitions from healthy operation to fault-tolerant operation, improved operational reliability, reduced controller complexity, and tested the dual three-phase PMSM fault-tolerant controller without any extra hardware (i.e. just enhanced the current levels), or even required identification of the faulted phase. The durability of the proposed method was demonstrate on a dual three-phase PMSM test platform whereby the tests were complete with additional mechanical fault-tolerance. Overall, the proposed scheme was demonstrate to have better durability and could be use on both single and multiple open-phase fault scenarios in high-reliability applications.

title: Domain-Specific Abstractive Text Summarization Using BERT and BiLSTM with Attention-Guided LSTM Decoding
authors: T. Anirudh, S. Prashmitha, T. Deekshitha, Alakuntla Raja Shekar, M. A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Manual information condensation is becoming ineffective and prone to error due to the quick expansion of domain-specific textual material in industries like technology, healthcare, and law, especially when terminology and contextual dependencies are complicated. Current generic summarizing techniques frequently fail to maintain factual consistency and domain semantics. In order to produce succinct and coherent summaries suited to specialized corpora, this paper suggests a domain-specific abstractive content summarization framework that integrates a contextual BERT encoder with a BiLSTM-based sequence model and an attention-guided LSTM decoder. The fundamental aims of this study are to boost domain-term recall, improve summary fluency, ensure semantic coherence, and exceed standard summarizing baselines. The model is trained using paired document–summary datasets using domain-adaptive pretraining and fine-tuning procedures. Performance is measured using typical automatic metrics including ROUGE-1, ROUGE-2, ROUGE-L, and BLEU. With a ROUGE-1 score of 48.7, ROUGE-L of 45.1, and BLEU of 21.9, experimental results show continuous improvements over Lead-3 and BERT-only baseline models, confirming the efficacy of the suggested framework in capturing long-range dependencies and domain-relevant semantics. These findings support the applicability of the suggested approach for accurate, scalable summary of specialized technical literature.

title: Compression and Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models
authors: Josephine Prem Kumar, R Abhiram, V Madhan Kumar, Vishnu Ramchandra Naik
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) such as GPT, LLaMA, and BERT deliver state-of-the-art performance but require high memory and computation, limiting use on resource-constrained devices. This paper presents a unified compression and fine-tuning pipeline integrating ultra-low-bit quantization (3-bit/4-bit) using BitsAndBytes, Activation-Wise Quantization (AWQ), and Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation (QLoRA). A 14B-parameter LLM is compressed to 8–10 GB, fine-tuned on downstream tasks, and evaluated on GSM8K, OpenBookQA, and SQuAD. The system achieves up to 75% model size reduction, 2× faster inference, and 2% accuracy drop. Stability limits in 3-bit quantization and fairness effects on StereoSet are also analyzed. The results show that hybrid quantization–fine-tuning enables efficient, high-performance LLM deployment on edge devices with minimal accuracy loss.

title: Enhancing Learning Efficiency Through AI-Driven Smart Classrooms: Insights From a Meta-Case Analysis
authors: Amanullah, Xu Meidan, Mariam Rahman
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This study uses secondary data analysis of previous empirical research to investigate the transformative potential of AI-driven smart classrooms in improving educational equity, collaborative learning, and learning efficiency. The study explores how artificial intelligence (AI) technologies, like intelligent tutoring, adaptive learning systems, and real-time analytics, might be integrated to overcome the shortcomings of conventional educational systems. The research demonstrates notable gains in important educational indicators. Findings reveal significant gains in student engagement (35%), teacher efficiency (30%), and collaboration (25%), by combining data from a meta-case study that involved 50 teachers and 200 students. The results highlight how AI may be used to automate administrative operations, personalize learning experiences, and promote data-driven decision-making. Additionally, the study discusses the challenges of AI adoption, such as privacy concerns and the digital divide, while proposing a connectivist theoretical framework to optimize smart classroom ecosystems. By showing how AI may produce more dynamic, inclusive, and effective learning environments that are suited to the needs of students in the twenty-first century, this study adds to the continuing conversation about educational technology.

title: GADA-FG-SBIR: Graph-Augmented Diffusion Alignment for Fine-Grained Sketch-Based Image Retrieval
authors: Mohammed A.S Al-Mohamadi, Prabhakar C J
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Fine-Grained Sketch-Based Image Retrieval (FG-SBIR) is a challenging cross-modal task requiring the recognition of subtle structural differences between sketches and photos. Existing CNN- and attention-based methods improve retrieval but still fail to jointly preserve fine-grained part relations and reduce the sketch–photo domain gap. This study proposes GADA-FG-SBIR (Graph-Augmented Diffusion Alignment), which combines graph reasoning and diffusion-based alignment. Graph Convolutional Networks enforce part-to-part consistency, while a denoising diffusion module progressively bridges the modality gap. Training is guided by a multi-loss objective integrating contrastive, graph consistency, and diffusion reconstruction losses. Experiments on four benchmarks demonstrate consistent state of the art improvements, achieving up to +6.8% mAP and +5.6% Acc@1 over strong baselines. Moreover, the framework maintains practical efficiency, with only a marginal inference overhead compared to prior baselines, ensuring scalability for real world deployment. Proposed framework not only improves retrieval accuracy but also highlights semantically meaningful object parts, enhancing interpretability for real-world applications such as e-commerce, forensic analysis, and design prototyping.

title: Gold Coated PCF-SPR Sensor With Dual Core For Refractive Index Sensor Application
authors: Sushma Sawraj, Anupam Sahu, Dharmendra Kumar, Sudhanshu Verma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: The article describes a PCF-SPR sensor, a type of photonic crystal fiber with dual cores that has circular symmetric air holes of varied sizes. The transducer is separated from the detecting solution by a gold (Au) surface on the outer surface of the underlying opto-chemical sensor. This allows the analyte to interact directly with the plasmonic interface, which makes the sensor more sensitive. The sensor functions optimally when its refractive index (RI) is between 1.31 as well as 1.37, which means that there is very little loss of confinement as the Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR) wavelength changes to synchronize fluctuations in RI, usually the analyte’s refractive index. The PCF-SPR sensor that was created has a wavelength sensitivity (WS) of 3000 nm/RIU and an amplitude sensitivity (AS) of 393 RIU-1 when operated in y-polarized mode. The $3.33 \times 10^{-5}$ RIU was the resolution it had. This sensor has potential applications in blood cell detection, particularly in glucose monitoring and cancer cell detection, demonstrating its versatility in sensing biological signals.

title: ADB IoT: A Method for Identifying Botnets in Internet of Things System
authors: Qamar Alam, Pallavi Singhal, Sangeeta Arora
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The volatile growth in Internet of Things/connected smart device has escalated protection threats, significantly botnet attacks, which can compromise networks and connected devices. This study recommends ADB IoT, a novel algorithm for diagnosing botnets in the IoT scenarios, Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory deployment a dominant, deep learning model to deal with these. By closely supervising network traffic and determining uncertain patterns, ADB IoT amplifies security by precisely correlating malicious activities in real time. Our algorithm uses the BiLSTM for the analysis of data and provide results with the help of past data. The proposed technique boosts the cyber security of IoT devices and generates them more adaptable to evolving attacks.

title: Empowering Educators Through AI-Assisted Code Generation for Life Skills Education
authors: Patchara Eamcharoen
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This study investigates how AI-assisted code generation can empower educators to rapidly design learning activities without programming expertise. Using ChatGPT, a webbased application was developed and deployed on WordPress to promote life skills awareness through scenario-based quizzes across adolescence, adulthood, and older age. The learning activity encouraged students to reflect on health-related choices and behaviors influencing longevity. Moreover, analysis of student reflections revealed meaningful engagement and reflective learning in life skills-particularly in raising awareness of healthy decision-making, strengthening problem-solving perspectives, and fostering personal responsibility for well-being. The quality of life skills content and the effectiveness of its delivery contributed to high overall student satisfaction. Student feedback indicated strong engagement, usability, and positive learning experiences with the web-based activity. The findings demonstrate that AIassisted code generation can democratize educational innovation, enabling educators to create meaningful, technology-enhanced learning experiences that strengthen life skills for lifelong wellbeing.

title: Multiclass Anemia Classification With Imbalanced Data: A Comparative Study of SMOTE, Tomek Link and ADASYN Using Hold-Out and Stratified Cross Validation
authors: Megha Verma, Tripti Swarnkar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Anemia and iron deficiency remain major public health issues in underdeveloped regions such as Bangladesh, India, Pakistan, and Africa. Conventional diagnosis relies on blood tests, but cost-effective, scalable alternatives are needed in resource-limited settings. This study applies machine learning for multi-class anemia classification (mild, moderate, severe) following WHO standards. To address dataset imbalance, SMOTE with Tomek Links and ADASYN were used. Six ML mod-els were evaluated using hold-out and stratified crossvalidation, with performance measured by accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and precision. Among them, SVM with ADASYN achieved the highest sensitivity (97.7%), emphasizing the importance of handling class imbalance for reliable anemia detection in low-resource environ-ments.

title: An Energy-Aware Hybrid Adaptive Preprocessing Framework for Attention-Guided Histopathological Cancer Detection
authors: Kanishk Agrawal, Javed Wasim, Raghav Mehra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The demand for histopathological cancer diagnosis to be reproducible and high-quality spurs the need for preprocessing frameworks that increase image interpretability and optimize computational cost. However, current algorithmic approaches are mainly limited by noise sensitivity, staining variability, and energy load. To that end, we present the Energy Aware Hybrid Adaptive Pre-processing Framework for cancer detection in histopathological images (HAPA-D) by using hybrid adaptive contrast, attention guided denoising, and staining normalization, and optimizing for energy efficiency while maintaining quality detection of cancer. As an early step, the input images for HAPA-D methods were collected from the CHAVI dataset. The input histopathological images are pre-processed as per the proposed HAPA-D pipeline to improve interpretability and computational costs through incorporating multiple preprocessing and optimization module into a framework. Adaptive Contrast Enhancement (ACE) enhances local tissue contrast, with Attention-Guided Denoising (AGD) selectively removing noise and retaining significant diagnostic regions. A Stain Normalization Module (SNM) addresses inter-slide discoloration by making sure datasets are as similar a color as possible. Energy-Aware Feature Optimization (EAFO) helps improve energy efficiency by removing redundant features and retaining clinically necessary diagnostic information. The features optimized using EAFO are then classified using the Efficient Focal Attention-Based Extreme Learning Classifier (EFA-ELC), which offers multi-class cancer diagnosis with accuracy and reliability. All techniques are implemented in a Python framework and are compared with the many existing methods based on their accuracy (99.4%) and energy consumption (45J). In conclusion, EFA-ELC provides accurate, reliable, and computationally sustainable multi-class cancer detection from histopathological images.

title: Tiny Poison, Big Effect: Comprehensive Multi-Vector Poisoning Assessment of Small Language Models
authors: Neelkamal Sharma, Upasna Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Large language models support an enormous range of modern applications, yet their training pipelines remain vulnerable to subtle data manipulation. Since the training and evaluation of large models is a resource-intensive process, many real-world deployments increasingly rely on Small Language Models (SLMs)—compact transformer architectures that are inexpensive to train and deploy. This makes SLMs a practical platform for reproducible security analysis. In this work, we present a systematic multi-vector poisoning evaluation on compact transformer architectures [1] (50–63M parameters), demonstrating that rigorous security analysis remains feasible at minimal computational cost. Across 60,000 controlled generations spanning five model configurations, we evaluate how distinct poisoning manifestations—backdoor triggers, verbatim memorization, domain contamination, and private data extraction—respond to architectural and inference based variations. Our empirical findings reveal attack-specific encoding: despite ultra-low contamination (as low as 0.0167%), backdoor activation reaches 100% success, cross-domain contamination reaches 70%, memorization 40%, and PII extraction 95%. Increasing capacity selectively mitigates extraction (95%→0%) but leaves backdoors intact, deviating from assumptions that scaling universally improves robustness. Random-sequence memorization exhibits 13-fold variation with model size, and, counter-intuitively, reducing poison concentration from 4% to 0.2% increases certain attack success rates by 66%, exposing a capacity saturation dynamic. Our reproducible pipeline and checkpoints enable systematic vulnerability measurement without billion-parameter infrastructure. These findings demonstrate that even minimal poisoning can critically compromise small language models and that robustness depends strongly on vulnerability manifestation rather than poisoning magnitude, highlighting the need for manifestation-specific defenses.

title: A Transformer Less Inverter Topology for Renewable Energy Application with Smart Protection Unit
authors: Ramchandra P. Hasabe, Amaraja Aniket Dhamangaonkar, Nikita Pramod Magdum
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: The global transition towards sustainable energy sources like solar photovoltaic (PV) and wind power relies critically on efficient and reliable power conversion. At the heart of grid-connected renewable energy systems lies the inverter. It does the essential job of converting the direct current (DC) electricity generated by sources like solar panels or wind turbines into the alternating current (AC) electricity. Which is required by the grid and most appliances. Traditionally, this conversion incorporated a low-frequency isolation transformer. This provided crucial galvanic separation between the DC source and the AC grid for safety and voltage matching. However, the quest for higher efficiency, reduced cost, smaller size, and lighter weight has driven the rapid evolution and widespread adoption of transformer less inverter technology, particularly dominating the residential and commercial solar PV market. This paper presents a transformer less inverter topology for PV systems. The proposed system is built in two stages. First stage stands for PWM controlled boost converter while the second stage is configured with SPWM controlled H- bridge inverter followed by smart protection unit. The hardware experimental results on $400 \mathrm{~W}, 230 \mathrm{~V}$ SPWM controlled inverter model are presented which indicates good voltage regulation across load variations. The harmonic analysis indicates 2.5% THD for the lamp load.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Land Registry Systems: A Study on Security and Architectural Design
authors: Atul Lal Shrivastava, Rajendra Kumar Dwivedi, Shiva Prakash, Garima Yadav
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: The systems of land registration are crucial for keeping records on who owns what, making deals connected with the land possible and non-fraudulent. But the current systems have problems like being opaque, slow and prone to fraud [1] [2]. This work aims at providing a solution that would use blockchain in developing a secured land registry system. Given decentralised, transparency and the ability to maintain an unchangeable record, blockchain can be seen as a ideal solution to enhance the credibility of the land records. The paper discusses the beneficial characteristics of blockchain that can improve the land registry system ranging from data immutability and decentralization to exclusion of middlemen. It also describes different countries experiences of developing the principle blockchain based land registry systems and benefits, as well as challenges met. The research indicates that digitisation of land records from block chain solutions can improve clarity, decrease paperwork and improve the security of records of land ownership. However, there is a set of challenges arising from the use of this technology, including accuracy of data, interfaces, and compatibility with other systems, and legal-institutional reform that is essential for the adoption of this technology.

title: SAM: A Lightweight Static Attention Module for Kidney Stone Detection in CT Images
authors: Shukai Ding, Nirattaya Khamsemanan, Cholwich Nattee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: Kidney stone disease is a common and painful condition that requires early and accurate diagnosis. Computed tomography (CT) is the preferred imaging tool, but the large image size and abundant background tissue make automatic detection challenging. Existing deep learning methods often rely on manual region-of-interest (ROI) cropping or complex multistep pipelines, which are labor-intensive and difficult to generalize. In this work, we propose the Static Attention Module (SAM), a plug-and-play component that learns dataset-level spatial priors at the input stage. SAM emphasizes kidney regions and suppresses irrelevant background without requiring expert annotations or incurring measurable computational cost. The experiments on the Yildirim CT dataset demonstrate that SAM consistently improves the accuracy of the four CNN backbones, with ResNet50 + SAM achieving 99.42%, thereby surpassing previously state-of-the-art methods. Further visualizations demonstrate that SAM directs networks toward clinically meaningful regions. These results indicate that SAM is an efficient and practical method for lightweight yet interpretable detection of kidney stones.

title: Hierarchical Feature Engineering with Hybrid Lstm-Dense Networks for Multi-Horizon Risk-Adjusted Stock Returns Prediction
authors: Ankush Goyal, Richa Golash, Sachin Choudhary, Kushagra Golash
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Financial time series (FTS) have a unique crossscale interaction characteristic. Traditional methods treat shortterm and long-term analysis separately and miss complex nonlinear interactions. This research addresses the limitations of deep models that operate at fixed scale time frames and often fail to interpret cross-scale feature interaction through building multi-horizon feature extraction(MHFE) pyramids from simple to complex and capturing stock movement patterns at different windows (5, 10, 15, 20, 30, 50, 100, 200) and finding cross-scale relationships among these windows. This approach generates features that build a context-aware base for our LSTM-Dense model. The proposed model combines temporal context (LSTM layer) with feature relationships (Dense layer). Through a hierarchical feature engineering pipeline that converts raw OHLCV data into 184 technical indicators over a variety of timeframes, along with three complementary multi-feature importance methods (FIM), demonstrates that the proposed pipeline isn't over-reliant on any single indicator type, contributing to its 0.91 AUC robustness, 96.6 % win rate, and 7.19 Sharpe. This research contributes to quantitative finance, demonstrating that carefully engineered features combined with appropriate deep learning architectures can achieve superior performance in financial market prediction tasks.

title: Integrated Healthcare Management and Patient Care Coordination System
authors: K. Jagadeesh, N. Vishnu Babaiah, J. Vineetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Early medical consultation is often delayed due to limited doctor availability, geographical barriers, and financial constraints. Many individuals also overlook initial symptoms, potentially leading to serious health complications. To address these challenges, this paper presents an AI-powered Healthcare Management System developed using React.js with a secure backend. The system enables users to perform quick symptom-based self-assessments and receive preliminary health insights. An intelligent algorithm analyzes user-provided symptoms and suggests probable conditions by comparing them against a comprehensive medical dataset. The backend, implemented using Firebase or Node.js, ensures robust performance, real-time data handling, and secure authentication. The platform features a clean, user-friendly interface, strong data protection mechanisms, device compatibility, and additional functionalities including AI-driven recommendations, telemedicine support, and chat assistance. This system aims to facilitate informed health decisions and promote early diagnosis through accessible digital healthcare solutions.

title: Artificial intelligence in plant disease detection: A systematic and comprehensive survey
authors: Ubaid Mubashir Wani, Sumit Kumar, Surbhi Vijh, Kartik Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Plant leaf disease detection has emerged as a critical application of artificial intelligence in precision agriculture, driven by the need for scalable, accurate, and real-time diagnostic systems. This study presents a comprehensive synthesis of recent deep learning approaches, encompassing CNNs, Vision Transformers (ViTs), attention mechanisms, ensemble learning, and self-supervised frameworks. Benchmarking across diverse datasets—including PlantVillage and hyperspectral sources—reveals classification accuracies ranging from 94.3 to 99.4 percent, with ViT and hybrid CNN-ViT models consistently outperforming traditional architectures. Innovations such as GAN-based augmentation, leaf vein segmentation via U-Net, and preprocessing techniques like CLAHE significantly enhance model robustness and generalization. Furthermore, deployment-oriented strategies including YOLOv5 for real-time detection, EfficientNet for transfer learning, and federated learning for privacy-preserving collaboration demonstrate practical viability. The integration of graph neural networks and multi-modal fusion further advances spatial reasoning and spectral sensitivity. This meta-analysis underscores the importance of reproducible benchmarking, adaptive feature refinement, and lightweight architectures for field deployment, offering a rigorous foundation for future research in AI-driven plant pathology.

title: Regression Analysis of Mechanical and Tribological Properties of Waste Tyre Rubber and Prosopis Juliflora Fiber Reinforced Epoxy Composites
authors: Sivakumar Sambath, Rajasekar Rajendran, Sabarish Rajagopalan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Sustainable polymer composites reinforced with natural fibers and industrial waste fillers have attracted significant attention for automotive and engineering applications due to their environmental and functional advantages. In this study, the mechanical and tribological properties of epoxy composites reinforced with waste tyre rubber particles (WTRP) and Prosopis juliflora fiber (PJF) were experimentally investigated and analyzed using regression-based modeling. Five composite formulations, including neat epoxy, single-reinforced, and hybrid- reinforced systems, were fabricated and evaluated for tensile strength, flexural strength, impact energy, density, wear rate, and coefficient of friction. The hybrid composite containing 10 wt% PJF and 15 wt% WTRP exhibited the best overall performance with a tensile strength of 68.7 MPa, flexural strength of 110.2 MPa, impact energy of 7.2 J, and the lowest wear rate of 0.85 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup> mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>/N•m. Regression models were developed to predict the mechanical and tribological responses with high accuracy, achieving coefficients of determination exceeding 0.95. The strong agreement between experimental and predicted results confirms the effectiveness of regression analysisas a reliable tool for the design and optimization of sustainable epoxy hybrid composites.

title: Terahertz-Assisted Multimodal Gesture Recognition for Secure Authentication
authors: Udayakumar Allimuthu, Shaik Rahil Basha, Madda Sai Teja
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: In this paper, we present a multimodal artificial intelligence system that utilizes three different sensing modalities to address significant real-world issues associated with secure authentication, human-computer interaction, and applied public safety. The multimodal system leverages the strengths of three different sensing modalities—eye-tracking-based user authentication, gesture-based sign language recognition, and terahertz (THz) imaging for threat detection. The three sensing modalities were chosen for integration because each sensing modality has a real-world application that can, to some extent, mitigate the limitations of individual unimodal systems. By integrating the different data sources with a single deep learning model, we were able to leverage the strengths of the different sensing modalities to address some of the challenges and limitations posed by environmental noise and user invariance attributes of unimodal systems. On the prototype implementation, we were able to achieve a system accuracy of 97.3%, with accuracies of 98% for eye-tracking authentication, 95% for gesture recognition, and 97% for threat detection. Our findings show that the multimodal sensing approach can be used to provide secure, contactless user authentication, improve human-computer interaction for the speech-impaired, and enhance safety monitoring for access control systems in sensitive environments such as airports and hospitals.

title: AutoSDLC-LC: A Low-Code AI-Driven Framework for Accelerating and Democratizing the Software Development Lifecycle
authors: C Jayamala, Ajathyan N E, Dharanya A G, Lakshitha Viswanathan, Vishnu Priya S, Anees Nachiya K
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The increasing demand for rapid software development, especially from non-technical stakeholders, has highlighted the need for intelligent automation within the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC). In this paper, we present AutoSDLC-LC a new low-code, AI-driven, paradigm-shifting software development framework that is radically changing software engineering through its ability to speed up application development with high precision and democratization. Combining the transformer-based Natural Language Processing (T5) to convert a set of requirements into a model, the AutoML to generate the backend model, and the OpenAI Codex to dynamically generate the code, the framework enables users to develop, run, and deploy full-stack applications with little manual programming. AutoSDLC-LC has a drag and drop workflow designer, smart schema-to-component map following meta-learning, and GPT as an aid in test case generation. The framework has a demonstrated 72.6% average time savings on development in ten varied case studies, which spanned inventory management to sales analytics, reached an average code coverage of 90.2%, and an AutoML model accuracy of 94.2%. Also, API binding precision was at 93.8% and defect density at 1.43 KLOC. Such head-to-toe combination serves more than just larger time-to-market by enabling citizen developers and business users to take part in an end-to-end delivery of software. Through the utilization of the low-code accessibility and AI-based intelligence, AutoSDLC-LC virtually eliminates the remains between conceptualization and actualization, leading to an evolutionary solution which is applicable in the current agile space. The results provided confirm its feasible usefulness in easing the effort, upsurge automation and sustaining quality software. The future extensions will be aimed at better explainability, adding natural language debugging, and incorporating federated deployment mechanisms.

title: Proactive Threat Detection in IoT Networks via Raspberry Pi-Based Honeypots
authors: Bhagavant K Deshpande, Rajani Kodagali, R Bharathy, Rajrupa Ray Chaudhuri, Z Justin, S. Aravind
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This article describes how to implement a honeypot system for Internet of Things (IoT) and cyberattack monitoring on a Raspberry Pi. The system uses a lightweight and cost-effective honeypot to block unauthorized access attempts and detect malicious behavior associated with IoT devices. The collected data is analyzed and visualized to identify trends in attack vectors, including the use of insecure communication protocols, open ports, and compromised credentials. The results highlight the growing security threats facing IoT systems and demonstrate the effectiveness of threat prediction. This article demonstrates the usefulness of honeypots in improving situational awareness, enhancing defense mechanisms, and providing a strategic response to growing cyber threats in IoT ecosystems.

title: Apulian Geo-Spatial Data Clustering and Analysis
authors: Antonio Bellanova, Cristina Cici, Michele Gargano, Michele Gramegna, Gaetano Grasso, Giuseppe Pirlo, Gianfranco Semeraro
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The analysis of spatial data has acquired a fundamental importance in contemporary decision-making in various fields: from governance to administration to emergency management. This paper highlights the efforts to analyse the geo-spatial environmental data of Apulia, a south-eastern Italian region, in collaboration with the technical working group of InnovaPuglia S.P.A., which provided the public regional geospatial data and the necessary computing power to manage the mass of data. The work presented is the last step in a long journey that has led the work to be based on the use of UMAP to reduce the dimensionality of the data and on the use of BIRCH to clustering the enormous amount of data. Indeed, the amount of data available was enormous considering the detail obtained with a grid of 100×100 metre cells. The results reveal distinct spatial clusters within the region, each characterised by unique environmental conditions, which were recognised as crucial by the experts' analysis. In addition, the work provides a cluster analysis through the evaluation of metrics such as Silhoutte Score, CHI and DBI.

title: Optimizing VGG-16 for Early Lung Cancer Detection: A Deep Learning Approach with Enhanced Interpretability
authors: Aadrika Awasthi, Shiv Kumar Verma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Lung cancer continues to be a major cause of death from cancer globally, and late presentation remains a cause for low survival. Screening is hence vital, but established methods of diagnosis, including biopsy and human-driven radiological interpretation, are slow, expensive, and vulnerable to human fault. Recent developments in deep learning and artificial intelligence hold promise for accurate and efficient computerized analysis of medical images.Here, a transfer learning technique from the VGG-16 convolutional neural network (CNN) is utilized for the classification of images from CT scans of the lungs as benign or malignant. Preprocessing was done for the dataset via resizing, normalization, and augmentation for enhanced generalizability. Pre-trained VGG-16 was fine-tuned for the target set of data with adjustment of the fully connected layers for binary classification. Training was carried out for optimization via the Adam optimizer along with regularization methods for minimizing overfitting.Model performance was measured via Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score and ROC-AUC with a marked improvement being realized after optimization. Pre- and post-optimization confusion matrices are readily observable to indicate the reduction of misclassifications, particularly false negatives, critical in detecting cancers. Findings indicate the prospect of VGG-16 transfer learning in the classification of medical images and as a supplement in diagnosis decision-making. Limits in data quantity and generalizability are highlighted, with future studies to focus on larger and diversified data and Clinical Verification.

title: Smart Manufacturing with Oracle Cloud: AI, Compliance and Global ERP Integration
authors: Rajeev Vinodkumar Rungta, Rashmi Bharathan, Manoj Srivastava
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: It is an empirical study about the opportunities of integrating machine learning (ML) into the Oracle Cloud-based smart manufacturing systems and its implications to the functioning efficiency, compliance with regulations, and interoperability of the global ERP. The research utilized a mixed-methodology because it analyzed the data of 4 multinational production firms that adopted the Oracle manufacturing cloud, Oracle integration cloud, and OCI machine learning services. The ML models that consist of predictive maintenance, demand forecasting and quality anomaly detection were trained on six months of historical production data to be tested within 6 months of operation. The results suggest that the time that had been saved was by a considerable margin (21%), forecast accuracy was better (17%), inventory optimization (26%), and accuracy was better in compliance reporting (98%). The distributed cloud system and embedded AI capabilities at Oracle served to offer the simple data integration of the ERP systems without compromising the ISO 9001 and GDPR-compliant data integration. The paper concludes with the conclusion that the AI-ML system of the Oracle Cloud can be employed to offer scalable real-time analytics, operations automation, and global compliance synchronization in Industry 4.0 systems. This analysis can be extended to future research to support reinforcement learning based adaptive manufacturing and cross industry benchmarking.

title: EcoCluster-ML: An Energy-Conscious Hybrid Metaheuristic + Learning Clustering Protocol for Prolonged Lifetime in Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Amit Kumar Gupta, Nishant Tripathi, Ruchi Rani Garg, Ankit Verma, Vipin Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: We propose EcoCluster-ML, a hybrid clustering protocol for wireless sensor networks that combines a lightweight online-learning estimator for residual energy trend with a multi-objective metaheuristic-based cluster-head (CH) selection to maximize network lifetime while preserving connectivity and QoS. EcoCluster-ML uses (1) a per-node energy-trend estimator to predict short-term residual energy, (2) a fitness function balancing residual energy, intra-cluster distance, and node centrality, and (3) a PSO–GA hybrid optimizer to select CHs and form clusters adaptively. Compared to classical protocols (LEACH, SEP, TEEN, DEEC) and recent metaheuristic-based schemes, EcoCluster-ML shows improvements in First Node Death (FND), Half Node Death (HND), Last Node Death (LND), average residual energy, packet delivery ratio (PDR), and throughput under homogeneous and heterogeneous deployments. Simulation results show up to ~25–40% extension in network lifetime metrics and better energy balance. We include statistical validation across 30 runs and ablation studies on the estimator and optimization blocks.

title: Detecting Student Depression Using Non-Clinical Measures with Explainable Predictive Modeling
authors: Akshat Kotnala, Nidhi Arora, Shilpa Srivastava
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Depression among students is a serious global mental health concern, affecting academic performance, emotional wellbeing, and long-term development. While traditional diagnostic tools like self-reported questionnaires and clinical interviews are useful, they often suffer from subjectivity, recall bias, and limited scalability. This study introduces a data driven, interpretable machine learning approach to predict student depression using both academic and nonacademic factors, without relying on clinical indicators. The dataset comprises student information on demographics, academic workload, lifestyle habits, social interactions, financial stress, and emotional state. Following thorough preprocessing including handling missing values, encoding variables, correlation-based feature reduction, and SMOTE to address class imbalance, ten supervised machine learning models were trained and assessed. Among them, a SMOTE enhanced XGBoost model achieved the highest test ROC AUC score of 0.95. To maintain transparency, SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) was employed to interpret the model’s predictions, highlighting key risk factors such as academic pressure, poor sleep quality, financial difficulties, and low social support. These findings can help guide early interventions and build trust with stakeholders. Future work may involve incorporating longitudinal and multimodal data, deploying real time solutions in educational settings, and addressing ethical considerations around privacy, fairness, and consent in AI based mental health systems.

title: Hybrid GAN–Diffusion and Lesion-Aware Generative Models for Crop Disease Classification in Precision Agriculture
authors: P. M. G. Jegathambal, J Madhavan, K M Monica, B Gayathri, Z. Justin, Deepa Simon
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Crop diseases are an ongoing threat to global food production, and advancements made in automated diagnosis are limited due to the public image datasets being sparse and disorganized. Applications of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to training set augmentation mostly neglect important lesion characteristics or produce images that focus on idealized laboratory conditions, and therefore, miss capturing conditions in the field. To address this gap, we created a hybrid framework that combines conditional GANs, lesion-aware synthesis, diffusion-based refinement, and Cycle GAN-driven domain adaptation. This framework was validated on Plant Village and the New Plant Disease dataset for tomato late blight and grape Esca. We evaluated image quality using SSIM, FID, and LPIPS metrics. Classification performance was evaluated using CNN and lightweight models, Mobile Net and Efficient Net. The pipeline showed a 7.2% increase in recall and 6.6% increase in F1-score compared to standard augmentation, specifically targeting the under sampled disease class, where augmentation impact was most significant. All figures are reproducible given the five independent iterations per dataset. These were executed on an NVIDIA RTX 3090 (24 GB) GPU, with PyTorch 2.1. The additional computational burden that diffusion refinement entails and the dependence on the quality of segmentation in lesion-aware synthesis do not overshadow the framework’s enhancement in reliability and robustness, nor the fully operational pathway toward edge-ready solutions in precision agriculture.

title: Mitigating Artificial Intelligence Hallucinations in Education: A Comparative Study of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Large Language Models
authors: Pei-Hua Chen, Yueh-Min Huang, Ting-Ting Wu, Hsin-Yu Lee
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into educational technologies promises to revolutionize personalized learning. However, their propensity for “hallucination,” the generation of factually incorrect or nonsensical information, poses a significant risk to knowledge integrity and student trust. To address this critical challenge, this paper investigates Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), an architectural approach that mitigates hallucinations by grounding the model's responses in factual data. Before generating an answer, the RAG framework retrieves relevant information from a verified knowledge base, ensuring the output is contextually accurate. We conducted a comparative study between a standard LLM and a RAG-powered system whose knowledge base was populated with specific curriculum materials. The generated answers to domain-specific questions were assessed through two distinct lenses: a quantitative evaluation of factual accuracy by subject matter experts and a qualitative analysis of student perceptions gathered through semi-structured interviews. Our preliminary findings indicate that the RAG model demonstrates a marked improvement in factual accuracy and a significant reduction in hallucinatory content. Furthermore, students perceived the RAG-generated responses as more trustworthy and useful for their learning. This research provides empirical evidence for the necessity of adopting RAG in educational AI, offering a pathway toward developing more reliable and effective digital learning tools.

title: Key-Bits Guided Scrambling Scheme for Papr Reduction in OFDM Systems
authors: Lingyin Wang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: As one of the primary defects of OFDM systems, the high PAPR has always hindered the practical application of this technology. The guided scrambling (GS), as a simple and easily implementable scheme, has the advantage of not requiring the side information. However, because only a few bits differ between the bit sequences corresponding to the candidate signals in conventional GS scheme, the resulting high correlation between candidate signals limits the optimization of PAPR performance. This paper presents a key-bits GS scheme. It only scrambles a part of bits, which can save the computational complexity of scrambling process. Additionally, the proposed scheme also designs the mapping constellations required in the mapping process of OFDM systems. This design of mapping constellations, combined with the key-bits scrambling, is mathematically equivalent to applying phase weighting to the mapped sequences, which reduces the correlation between candidate signals and greatly improves PAPR performance. Simulation results show that the proposed key-bits GS scheme not only significantly improves PAPR performance of OFDM systems but also reduces the computational complexity of scrambling process compared with conventional GS scheme.

title: AIoT-Based Traffic Flow Optimization in Smart Cities
authors: Avnish Mishra, Ayushi Singh, Suchitra, Vijay Kumar Dwivedi
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: One of the major issues that can affect the sustainability of smart cities is urban traffic congestion. In this paper, an AIoT-based framework for traffic flow optimization that utilizes a combination of various IoT sensors and deep learning models for traffic prediction and management is proposed. Unlike existing solutions, the proposed framework adopts an edge-cloud architecture that is scalable and supports low-latency processing and deployment. The performance of LSTM, GRU, and Transformer models is evaluated using the PeMS08 traffic dataset. The result of the experiment shows that the Transformer model has the best performance with an RMSE of 106.2, and the GRU model has a competitive performance with lower computational complexity, which is edge-deployable. The proposed approach also improves the performance of traffic efficiency metrics, including vehicle delay, throughput, and CO2 emissions, outper-forming the statistical model. Additionally, scalability, security, and deployment considerations are investigated to fill the gap between theoretical traffic prediction models and intelligent transportation systems.

title: Quantum Feature Encoding–Driven Early Warning System for Sepsis and Organ Failure Using Hybrid Quantum–Classical Learning in ICUs
authors: Tamilselvan Arjunan, Gopalakrishnan Arjunan, N. Jagadish Kumar, Gomathimeena M, Manju K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Sepsis and the development of multi-organ failure still constitute significant causes of death in the ICU, hence the necessity to develop predictive systems that could detect the fact of subtle physiological deviations before clinical deterioration. Despite the potential of classical machine learning models, including logistic regression, random forests and deep neural networks to predict sepsis, they frequently encounter challenges with large-dimensional ICU time-series data, nonlinear interaction of features and ability to predict sepsis at an early stage. These also models are sensitive to missing values and have limited cross-hospital generalizability and high rates of false-alarms which makes them difficult to implement in clinical settings, this paper proposes a Quantum-Assisted Early Warning System (QAEWS) based on quantum feature encoding and hybrid quantum-classical based learning models that provides better early sepsis and organ dysfunction detection. Amplitude and phase encoding the ICU physiological measures, lab measurements, and short-term vital sign traces are encoded into quantum-enhanced representations, which allow complexer modeling of complicated time-dependent relationships in a high-dimensional Hilbert space. The predictive architecture combines the Quantum Support Vector Machines (QSVM) and Quantum Neural Networks (QNN) and supplemented by the classical neural layers to improve classification accuracy and false alerts. Model performance was compared with the Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and LSTM models using the same feature sets in the MIMIC-IV and eICU datasets. The quantum-assisted framework achieved a 7–12% increase in AUROC, a 14–18% reduction in false alarms, and provided predictions 1.5–2.8 hours earlier than classical baselines. Sensitivity at 85% specificity improved by 10–15%. These results demonstrate that quantum-enhanced feature spaces offer superior discrimination of early sepsis patterns, establishing QAEWS as a robust and clinically valuable approach for real-time ICU early warning.

title: Phase-Shifting Transformers Application for Improving Efficiency of Different Voltage Grids Parallel Operation
authors: Sergey V. Smolovik, Artem I. Denisenko, Vladimir A. Smirnov, Vladimir S. Chudny
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: This article examines the use of phase-shifting transformers (PST) for optimal power flow distribution across parallel-operating electrical networks of different voltage classes and limiting current overloads on transmission lines. Considerations are presented for selecting the rated power of a PST in an equivalent circuit containing parallel-operating overhead transmission lines with voltages of 500 and 220 kV. Based on steady-state calculations, it is shown that voltage phase regulation on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$500 / 220 \text{kV}$</tex> autotransformers enables the redistribution of power flows between networks of corresponding voltages, which leads to a significant reduction in losses and justifies the costs of installing additional equipment. The necessary PST control parameters are determined.

title: Bias Detection and Mitigation in Datasets and Language Models
authors: Nikita Bhedasgaonkar, Kanan Shah, Shraddha Dolas, Ayush Chhetry, Dev Gupta, Anjali Deshpande
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Many Natural Language Processing (NLP) activities, including text generation and classification, have been greatly simplified by the development of language models. These models have shortcomings despite their increasing usefulness and popularity, with bias becoming among the most urgent issues. This study uses advanced techniques including Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Fairlearn, and AIF360 to investigate the existence of bias within AI systems, namely language models. It looks at transformer-based models like BERT, DistilBERT, RoBERTa, and XLM-RoBERTa as well as datasets. The study uses fairness criteria such as Equalized Odds Difference (EOD) and Demographic Parity Difference (DPD) to assess the performance of Fairlearn and AIF360 across various machine learning methods. Results show discernible biases, particularly genderrelated ones, and suggest a way to reduce these biases in encoderdriven language models. The study emphasizes how crucial it is to recognize and deal with bias in various NLP model designs in order to advance justice and moral AI development.

title: Hyperbolic Active Learning for Label-Efficient Action Segmentation
authors: Jingqiao Xiu, Wei Ji, Menglin Yang, Yufeng Chen, Hanbin Zhao, Fangfang Wang, Roger Zimmermann
publish: ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications, and Applications
abstract: None

title: Magnetic Core Material Evaluation for Energy Harvesting Sensors
authors: Khang Le, Daniel Monagle, Steven B. Leeb
abstract: Current transformer magnetic energy harvesters (CTMEHs) harvest and convert magnetic energy from ac conductors into electrical energy for low-power sensors. The CTMEH magnetic core is a critical design component as its material properties significantly influence harvested power. Previous works have mainly focused on modeling CTMEH behavior or improving power harvest given a specific core material. Some existing research has explored the effects of different core materials on power harvest in limited fashion; but a comprehensive, comparative study of high permeability, high saturation flux density CTMEHs had yet to be explored. This paper presents a thorough comparison of the effects of CTMEH core material on power harvest. State-of-the-art magnetic cores are evaluated at several ac transmission line currents and loading conditions. Results demonstrate that high effective initial permeability materials excel at lower-current applications, while high saturation flux density cores offer better performance at higher-current applications. Meanwhile, both high effective initial permeability and high saturation flux density are desirable for a magnetic energy harvester core. The comparative results of this paper offer energy harvesting sensor designers targeted guidance in CTMEH material choices.

title: Causal Effects of Flooding on Urban Transport Resilience: A Double Machine Learning Approach with Satellite-Derived Flood Maps
authors: Xinyi Fang, Linjun Lu, Poly Z.H. Sun
abstract: Existing flood resilience assessments face a fundamental trade-off: correlational analyses of real-world data suffer from confounding bias, while simulations of hypothetical scenarios lack empirical grounding. To address this identification gap, we propose a physically-grounded causal inference framework that derives unbiased causal parameters directly from physical observation. First, we generate high-fidelity flood maps from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery using a fine-tuned U-Net model adapted for dense urban environments. Second, we integrate these empirical maps into a microscopic traffic simulation model to quantify dynamic network disruptions. Third, we apply double machine learning (DML) to isolate the causal effect of flooding on transport resilience while rigorously controlling for high-dimensional urban form confounders. Applied to a flood event in a dense coastal megacity, our analysis reveals two key discoveries: (1) a nonlinear tipping point around 25% flood intensity, distinct from recent studies assuming linear degradation, beyond which system performance collapses chaotically; and (2) a ‘dual nature of vulnerability’, where low-density suburbs suffer a fragility of isolation (2.9× higher road closure rate) while dense urban cores exhibit a fragility of congestion (1.47× greater waiting time increase). Validated across 130 sensitivity tests, this framework provides a robust, data-driven blueprint for spatially targeted resilience investments, demonstrating that causal identification in disaster contexts requires physically-grounded observation, not synthetic assumptions.

title: SHADOW: Secure Hidden Authenticating Digital Objects in the Wild
authors: Daniel Riccio
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Biometric authentication is now widely adopted across both consumer and enterprise applications, offering an intuitive and seamless user experience. However, research in the field has historically focused more on improving recognition accuracy than on addressing fundamental security and privacy concerns. As biometric technologies become increasingly pervasive, their exposure to a broad range of threats, including template leakage, identity theft, and cross-matching, raises critical challenges. Existing protection schemes such as cancelable biometrics, biometric cryptosystems, and privacy-preserving protocols suffer from significant limitations: they are often vulnerable to inversion, prone to residual linkability across reissuances, or computationally unsuited for edge devices. To address these issues, this work introduces SHADOW (Secure Hidden Authenticating Digital Objects in the Wild), a framework for secure and privacy-preserving biometric authentication based on on-device generation of non-invertible and revocable representations. SHADOW achieves template-protection properties including unlinkability and renewability. It leverages analytically defined oscillatory functions to construct a structured authenticating object that binds a hidden certificate to the protected template and supports client-aware verification via nonce-driven masking. Trusted hardware can be used as optional hardening for endpoint secret protection; it is not required for SHADOW's template-protection guarantees, while client integrity assumes the confidentiality of the device-held secret. The proposed binding strategy achieves formal irreversibility and unlinkability by design, while supporting renewability and minimal computational overhead. Experiments conducted on public face, iris and voice benchmarks demonstrate that SHADOW achieves comparable recognition performance while ensuring robustness against major attack vectors, including inversion, bruteforce, and record multiplicity. The framework is compatible with modern embedding-based biometrics and exhibits properties that can facilitate deployment in decentralized and edge computing environments.

title: A Predict-Then-Optimize Framework for SMT Quality Enhancement Based on Causal Inference
authors: Lei Gao, Zhengkai Li, Jinyong Yu, Hao Sun, Xinghu Yu, Tong Wang, Huijun Gao
abstract: Within the surface mount assembly line, the placement process parameters of the mounter significantly influence assembly quality. This paper decomposes the placement process parameter optimization problem into two distinct subproblems: the displacement prediction problem (DPP) and the process parameter adjustment problem (PPAP). A causal deep displacement prediction model is proposed to address DPP, which is a critical factor in optimizing placement parameters to enhance surface mount technology assembly quality. Then, a deep reinforcement learning algorithm is introduced to solve PPAP. Given the multitude of factors affecting placement accuracy and their complex interactions, a model training methodology incorporating a causal inference-based attention mechanism and simulated interventional operations is further proposed. This methodology drives the network to capture underlying causal relationships rather than mere correlations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed prediction model significantly outperforms other state-of-the-art algorithms, while the employed parameter optimization method also surpasses alternative placement methods.

title: Influence of Avatar Appearance and Target Distance on Locomotion Method Selection in Virtual Reality
authors: Omar A. Khan, Junyeong Kum, Hyeongil Nam, Myungho Lee, Kangsoo Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Room-scale locomotion in VR can promote physical movement to combat the negative effects of a sedentary lifestyle. Therefore, it is useful to investigate factors which may motivate users to walk instead of teleport. In a within-subjects study with 24 participants, we investigated the effects of avatar appearance and target distance on users' locomotion behavior, when given the option to either walk or teleport. In particular, we manipulated three aspects of avatar appearance-body completeness (full-body vs upper-body only), body weight (normal vs obese), and clothing (casual vs superhero armor), and we provided three target distances (1.5 meters, 3.0 meters, and 4.5 meters). We found that avatar obesity decreased willingness to walk, but only for full-body avatars. We also found that close target distances encouraged more walking than teleporting, up to a certain threshold distance. Additionally, we found that users' perceived similarity to their avatar correlated with their willingness to walk. Our findings can be applied to motivate users to walk more in VR, such as in VR exergames.

title: Supporting Bi-Directional Collaboration with Unmodified Immersive Applications Using AccompliceVR
authors: Ruijun Sun, Anthony Steed
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: With the increasing popularity of virtual reality (VR) applications, allowing interaction with non-immersed spectators has become an important supporting functionality. To this end, a variety of VR streaming systems focus on allowing remote spectating experiences. However, such systems lack mechanisms that allow spectators to assist or explore inside VR applications alongside the VR players. To address, we explore AccompliceVR (AVR), a novel streaming and remote collaboration system. Using built-in OpenVR functionality, AVR introduces avatar representations of spectators overlaid into the normal stereo views of unmodified VR applications. AVR also enhances the remote spectator experience by stitching frames of video from the VR player to reconstruct a pseudo-panoramic view. This allows them to move around and point at the environment with an avatar. We first evaluated that AVR is compatible with a selection of the most popular VR games. The system is then validated through a paired user study with 30 participants using a mixed-design pattern. Participants played two well-known VR games (as 'pilot') and were assisted by their partner in the pair (as 'copilot'). The results support that AVR shows significant improvement in communication between pilots and copilots and enhances the pilot's understanding of instructions from their copilot.

title: Design of Magnetic Sensor Array-Based PUFs for IoT Security
authors: Shanshan Wang, Xiaowei Zhang, Pengjun Wang, Xiangyu Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper proposes a physically unclonable function (PUF) design method based on a magnetic sensor array, leveraging inherent process variations in sensor manufacturing as an entropy source to generate unique cryptographic keys without requiring additional hardware support. This approach reduces system complexity while enhancing security. A balancing algorithm and reconfigurable strategy are employed to optimize PUF responses and improve stability. Furthermore, a lightweight bidirectional authentication protocol based on the magnetic sensor array PUF is introduced, with formal analysis demonstrating its effectiveness in resisting common security threats, including man-in-the-middle attacks, replay attacks, and brute-force attacks. Experimental results show that the proposed PUF exhibits excellent uniqueness, randomness, and reliability, achieving a randomness of 98% and a reliability of 97.8%. Compared to conventional silicon-based PUFs, this approach offers advantages such as low cost, low power consumption, and high environmental adaptability, providing an efficient and secure identity authentication and data protection solution for resource-constrained IoT devices.

title: dVRM: Cross-Switch Memory Sharing and Self-adaptive Allocation in Distributed Data Plane
authors: Mimi Qian, Lin Cui, Fung Po Tso, Yuhui Deng, Zhen Zhang, Weijia Jia
abstract: Programmable switches have revolutionized networking by enabling a new spectrum of applications, such as network telemetry, in-network computation, and machine learning. These applications heavily utilize register memory but their performance is significantly constrained by the scarcity of on-chip resources, such as the 15 MB of SRAM available on a Tofino switch. To effectively accommodate increasingly memorydemanding applications, we aim to pool register resources across multiple switches, creating a larger unified register memory space. This resource pooling approach addresses the limitations of existing single-switch Virtual Register Memory (VRM) solutions, which cannot meet the demands of these applications in distributed environments. To achieve this, we propose dVRM, a distributed VRM deployment framework that enables crossswitch memory sharing and self-dynamic memory allocation on the data plane. dVRM introduces three innovations: (1) Grouped Multi-Switch Registers (GMRs), virtualizing distributed pipeline stages into a unified memory pool; (2) a self-adaptive, bit-width allocation mechanism driven by real-time data-plane feedback; and (3) lightweight heuristics for concurrent application deployment with distributed VRM, formulated as a mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) problem. We have implemented dVRM on both P4 hardware switches (with Intel Tofino ASIC) and BMv2. Experimental results show that dVRM significantly reduces hash unit consumption by up to 26% and achieves an improvement in accuracy (ARE) of up to 57.3% across various workloads.

title: A Gossiping-based Adaptive Kalman Filter for Frequency and ROCOF Local Data Fusion
authors: Guglielmo Frigo, David Macii, Dario Petri
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs) provide high-accuracy frequency and Rate of Change of Frequency (ROCOF) measurements under quasi-stationary operating conditions. However, their measurement uncertainty can increase by up to two orders of magnitude during fast transients, thus degrading the reliability of Wide Area Monitoring, Protection, and Control Systems. To address this issue, this paper proposes event-triggered Adaptive Kalman Filters (AKFs) that, installed at PMU-equipped buses, dynamically augment local ROCOF measurements with information received from adjacent PMUs when relevant transient conditions are detected. By leveraging spatial correlation in system dynamics, the proposed strategy mitigates the reduced tracking capability of classical model-based prediction during transient events with minimal communication overhead and with no scalability problems. Simulation results on modified IEEE 5-bus and 14-bus systems show a substantial reduction in ROCOF estimation uncertainty (up to one order of magnitude in very noisy environments and if the PMU estimates are performed over data records of no more than four nominal cycles each), together with a more moderate improvement in frequency estimation accuracy. Application to real-world PMU data further confirms the stable and consistent behavior of the algorithm.

title: Effective Defense Strategies Against Jailbreaking in Large Language Models
authors: Ashika Sameem Abdul Rasheed, Mohammad M. Masud
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across a range of natural language processing tasks. Yet, their vulnerability to prompt-based adversarial attacks, particularly jailbreaks and prompt injections, poses significant safety and ethical concerns. In this study, we present DualGuard, a novel two-stage defense framework designed to identify and suppress unsafe outputs during prompt-based evaluations of LLMs. DualGuard combines semantic-level self-evaluation, wherein a model reflects on its own response for safety violations, with policy-level verification conducted by a high-accuracy, policy-aligned external judge (e.g., GPT-4o). We benchmark DualGuard against both unprotected LLMs and those employing Semantic Smoothing, a defense technique based on semantically preserving input perturbations. Through systematic experimentation on five prominent LLMs and ten adversarial prompts crafted to bypass standard safeguards, we demonstrate that DualGuard significantly enhances resilience against jailbreak attempts while maintaining interpretability and model usability. Furthermore, we analyze multiple semantic judge variants and a customized hybrid judge, which combines two judge variants. Our results show that DualGuard outperforms existing defense mechanisms across all tested models, blocking up to 100% of harmful responses while maintaining a low false positive rate (6%) on benign prompts, demonstrating strong usability preservation for real-world deployment. We also evaluated DualGuard on a mixed benchmark derived from the HarmBench dataset, consisting of 150 prompts (120 unsafe and 30 safe) covering chemical and biological threats, cybercrime, misinformation, harassment, and copyright violations. On this larger and more diverse benchmark, DualGuard—applied with Llama Guard as external judge, reduces attack success to 0.0–7.5% across four open-weight models while maintaining a high safe answer rate, demonstrating its robustness and model-agnostic design. This work contributes to ongoing LLM safety research by introducing a practical and interpretable defense approach that can be applied across different model families and real-world applications.

title: Calculating and Visualising Feature Importance Values for Counterfactual Explanations
authors: Bjorge Meulemeester, Raphael Mazzine Barbosa De Oliveria, Yifan He, David Martens
abstract: Despite the success of machine learning algorithms, their opaque nature challenges responsible application. Counterfactual explanations have emerged as one potential solution to explain individual decision results, but their applicability is limited by (1) their isonomic view of feature changes, in which it is not possible to observe how much each modified feature influences the prediction, and (2) the lack of effective graphical resources to visualise the counterfactual explanation. We solve this by introducing Counterfactual Feature Importance (CFI) values. To calculate these values, we propose two methods: a greedy algorithm, and an adaptation of Shapley values to the feature changes of counterfactual explanations, which we term CounterShapley. We additionally introduce three chart types to visualise the counterfactual explanations: (a) the Greedy chart, which shows a greedy sequential path, (b) the CounterShapley chart, depicting its respective score in a simple one-dimensional chart, and (c) the Constellation chart, which shows all possible combinations of feature changes, and their impact on the model’s prediction score. For each of our proposed CFI methods and visualisation schemes, we demonstrate how they make counterfactual explanations more interpretable and actionable. To validate our approach, we surveyed 22 data scientists. 85.7% prefer at least one chart alongside counterfactual explanation. The majority of data scientists agree that the visualisations enhance the informativeness and usefulness of counterfactual explanations, and over 70% agree that visualisations increase their confidence in understanding counterfactual explanations. These findings suggest that counterfactual visualisations are an effective tool, highlighting their potential to support decision-making in data science contexts.

title: MGD-SAM2: Multi-view Guided Detail-enhanced Segment Anything Model 2 for High-Resolution Class-agnostic Segmentation
authors: Haoran Shen, Peixian Zhuang, Jiahao Kou, Yuxin Zeng, Haoying Xu, Jiangyun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Segment Anything Models (SAMs), as vision foundation models, have demonstrated remarkable performance across various image analysis tasks. Despite their strong generalization capabilities, SAMs encounter challenges in fine-grained detail segmentation for high-resolution class-independent segmentation (HRCS), due to the limitations in the direct processing of high-resolution inputs and low-resolution mask predictions, and the reliance on accurate manual prompts. To address these limitations, we propose MGD-SAM2, which integrates SAM2 with multi-view feature interaction between a global image and local patches to achieve precise segmentation. MGD-SAM2 incorporates the pre-trained SAM2 with four novel modules: the Multi-view Perception Adapter (MPAdapter), the Multi-view Complementary Enhancement Module (MCEM), the Hierarchical Multi-view Interaction Module (HMIM), and the Detail Refinement Module (DRM). Specifically, we first introduce MPAdapter to adapt the SAM2 encoder for enhanced local-global perception required by HRCS images. Then, MCEM and HMIM are proposed to further enrich the local texture and global semantics by aggregating multi-view features within and across multi-scales. Finally, DRM is designed to generate gradually restored high-resolution mask predictions by jointly leveraging mask features and original images, compensating for the detail loss caused by directly upsampling low-resolution prediction maps. Experimental results demonstrate the superior performance and strong generalization of our model on multiple high-resolution and normal-resolution datasets, while requiring only 8.8% trainable parameters. Code is available at https://github.com/sevenshr/MGD-SAM2.

title: Demagnetization analysis and optimization design of low-speed high torque permanent magnet synchronous motor
authors: Weiliang Wu, Xiping Liu
abstract: Permanent magnet synchronous motors are widely used in low-speed and high torque fields such as metallurgy and mining due to their advantages of high efficiency, high torque density, and high power density. This article proposes a dual stator magnetic field modulation low-speed high torque permanent magnet synchronous motor. The use of a dual stator structure can fully utilize the internal space of the motor, thereby improving the torque density of the motor. At the same time, a magnetic field modulation motor structure is adopted to achieve multi unit collaborative work through the modulation effect of air gap magnetic conduction harmonics, thereby further improving the torque density of the motor. This article focuses on the problem of local irreversible demagnetization of permanent magnets under high temperature working conditions, which leads to adverse effects on motor output performance. Firstly, demagnetization testing and analysis were conducted on the rotor of a dual stator magnetic field modulated low-speed high torque permanent magnet synchronous motor using different permanent magnet materials at different temperatures. Then, by optimizing the structural parameters of the rotor permanent magnet, the degree of local demagnetization of the permanent magnet was reduced, and the effectiveness of the optimized design was verified through simulation. Demagnetization analysis and optimization design provide technical support for permanent magnets in low-speed high-torque applications.
