abstract: Building change detection is crucial for urban planning and sustainable development but faces challenges due to building morphology diversity, environmental interferences, and dependency on labeled datasets. To address this, we propose a domain-knowledge self-embedded training method that integrates prior insights on planar and vertical building features without relying on pre-annotated data. The approach introduces a pre-processing multi-layer feature extraction module and a post-fusion edge feature analysis module into conventional change detection frameworks, emphasizing high texture contrast and morphological shadows. Specifically, it employs multi-feature fusion and a deep edge guidance loss to enhance discrimination between building and non-building areas, utilizing non-maximum suppression and dual-threshold processing for improved accuracy and robustness. Evaluated on several remote sensing datasets, the approach demonstrates superior performance in reducing false detections and maintaining edge integrity, achieving reliable results even in unsupervised settings.

title: Microservice Failure Propagation Simulator: Survey and Gaps
authors: Sarthak Kalyankar, Rishikesh Chaudhari, Siddhi Bodake, Tushar Rane
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: The adoption of microservice architectures has introduced unprecedented scalability and agility, but at the cost of increased operational complexity and susceptibility to cascading failures. A localized fault can propagate through complex dependency chains, leading to widespread outages. To address this, the industry uses two primary paradigms: reactive Root Cause Analysis (RCA) and proactive Chaos Engineering. This paper systematically surveys academic research and industry tools for analyzing and mitigating failure propagation in microservices. We categorize and evaluate approaches from dependency graph analysis and causal inference RCA to fault injection frameworks. Our analysis reveals a critical gap: the absence of tools for proactive, offline simulation of failure propagation, which contrasts with existing reactive and in-vivo proactive methods. Current solutions explain failures after they occur or test them in live environments but cannot predict the blast radius of potential failures at design time. This survey delineates this gap and proposes a research agenda for developing dedicated failure propagation simulators to enable predictive resilience engineering.

title: Market Transaction Method of Source-Network-Load-Storage Integration Project Based on Smart Contract
authors: Jihe Xu, Zongmin Jiang, Mingtao Shi, Ning Zhu, Yanyi Ye, Ben Chen, Jianlei Wang, Huiying Yu, Chunlin Xie, Yumin Zhang
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: With the rapid global transition to sustainable energy systems, the integration of Source-Grid-Load-Storage (SGLS) in electricity markets has become crucial for optimizing energy distribution and balancing supply and demand. This paper presents a market transaction method based on smart contracts for the SGLS integration project, aiming to address key issues such as transparency, efficiency, and automation in electricity trading. Blockchain technology enables a decentralized, transparent, and secure trading system, facilitating seamless interactions between electricity generation, transmission, storage, and consumption. We describe the process flow of a blockchainbased electricity trading system, including energy and green certificate trading, and propose a methodology to integrate load and storage systems efficiently. The implementation of smart contracts reduces intermediary costs, enhances trust and traceability, and provides a reliable mechanism for resource scheduling. Simulation results demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of this approach in optimizing energy market transactions.

title: A Random Forest Regressor Model for Forecasting Agricultural Yield Using Agronomic and Climatic Parameters
authors: Gayathri S, P. Alagu Manoharan, S. Durai
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: This project builds a data-based system to predict crop yield per hectare in Tamil Nadu, India. Crop yield prediction is difficult because it depends on many changing factors like rainfall, temperature, soil type, and nutrient levels. To handle this, the system uses a machine learning method called the Random Forest Regressor, which is strong and can find complex patterns in data. The model uses six main inputs rainfall, temperature, soil <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, nitrogen <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{N})$</tex>, phosphorus <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{P})$</tex>, potassium (K) content, district, and crop type. It is trained and tested using past data and synthetic data, and it performs well based on MAE(Mean Absolute Error), RMSE(Root Mean Square Error), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> scores. A MySQL database is used to manage data efficiently and support real-time updates, creating a full system from data collection to prediction. This system gives useful insights to farmers and policymakers, helping them choose crops wisely and use resources effectively. It supports sustainable farming and food security in Tamil Nadu. In the future, it can include real-time weather data, satellite images, and profit analysis to make predictions even better.

title: Hybrid EfficientVit Model for Plant Disease Classification Using Leaf Images
authors: A Adithyan, T.H. Abdulla, Aljasir Muhammed, Christy Babu, B Sivakarthik, Rajeev Rajan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Plant diseases represent a persistent challenge to global food security, causing substantial crop losses each year. While traditional machine learning approaches have shown promise in automated disease detection, they often struggle with computational efficiency and capturing complex disease patterns. In this work, we introduce EfficientViT, a next-generation vision transformer specifically designed for agricultural applications. Our approach combines the global reasoning capabilities of transformers with computational efficiency through a novel cascaded multi-scale attention mechanism. We demonstrate that this architecture not only achieves superior accuracy in classifying diseases across multiple plant species but does so with significantly lower computational demands. The model’s lightweight design makes it particularly suitable for deployment in resourceconstrained agricultural settings, potentially enabling real-time disease monitoring systems that could revolutionize crop management practices.

title: Intelligent Computer Vision Model for Brain Sub-Region Segmentation and Classification in Alzheimer's Disease Detection
authors: Akash Kumar Bhagat, Sumukh Pandey, Malepati Chandra Sekhar
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a neurodegenerative disorder that requires early and exact analysis to improve the patient. This research presents a computer vision methodology to classify brain MRI scans as Normal Control (NC), Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI), and AD stages. The method applies Adaptive Dragonfly Optimization (ADO) to determine the hyperparameters of a Back Propagation Neural Network (BPNN) with the aim to enhance the performance of classification. MRI scans are preprocessed with a Gaussian filter to eliminate noise, improving the image quality for analysis. Comparative outcomes illustrate that the novel ADO-BPNN model performs better compared to current algorithms, such as CAD, CNN, and RF, with 97.62% accuracy, precision of 98.79%, recall of 97.47%, and F1-score of 98.32%. Such results show the promise of the model for the finding of AD at a previous level and decision support in a clinical setting. The model was deployed using Python 3.8 on a Windows 10 operating system. Through combining swarm intelligence by the Dragonfly Algorithm and tuning a neural network for brain sub-region classification, the method provides a robust and efficient solution to Alzheimer's diagnosis. The research proves the effectiveness of the integration of Deep Learning (DL) methods and optimization algorithms for developing the accuracy and interpretability of AD finding systems.

title: Investigating Slow Multiplicative Noise and its Influence on ECG Signals
authors: Gokul Prakash, Libya Thomas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: The influence of slow multiplicative noise (MN) on signal quality has been studied mainly from a theoretical perspective. Earlier work derived closed-form expressions for the energy spectrum of the noise modulation function (NMF), showing that such noise introduces both discrete and continuous spectral components. In this paper, we build on that foundation and move toward practical validation. Using synthetic electrocardiogram (ECG) signals created with NeuroKit2, we simulate amplitude and phase distortions modeled as Gaussian processes. The time and frequency domain behavior of the ensuing noise-modulated signals are investigated with particular focus on energy spectra, pulse fluctuations, and phase changes. To emphasize the distinctive role played by MN, we also contrast its action with that of additive noise. The overall goal is to introduce insight into the way MN deforms biomedical signals like ECG, and why its action has to be taken into account in clinical and wearable monitoring scenarios.

title: Rat Swarm Optimization-based CSD for Design of Circular Symmetric 2D FIR Filter and Hardware Architecture Implementation
authors: Venkata Krishna Odugu, Merina Jaladi, Harish Babu Gade, Pinjala N Malleswari, S. Harivardhagini, Malla Reddy Amreddy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Using Rat Swarm Optimization (RSO) and Canonical Signed Digit (CSD) representation, this work proposes an optimized architecture for a circular symmetrical Two-Dimensional Finite Impulse Response (2DFIR) filter. The filter is developed in 45 nm CMOS technology and deployed on Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) also for real-time signal processing applications. A novel modified McClellan transformation approach is called $\mathbf{P 5}$ transformation is considered for the design of sharp circular symmetric filter. Minimizing the error between the desired and actual filter responses is achieved through the efficient optimization of the filter coefficients in the suggested design, which makes use of RSO. By converting the optimized coefficients to CSD form, we minimize the amount of non-zero terms, which in turn reduces the number of needed multiplications and, in turn, the hardware complexity. This architecture is perfect for adaptive filtering, communication systems, and image processing since the filter is implemented on FPGA, which guarantees high-speed parallel processing. Genus, a synthesis tool, is used to obtain the real area, power, and timing of the design. Using simulation and real-time FPGA testing, we were able to evaluate the design’s performance and see that it strikes a good balance between power efficiency, speed, and hardware resource utilization.

title: Cross-view UAV Geo-localization via Wavelet-based Local Feature Enhancement
authors: Chen Zhang, Rui Zhao, Zhiping Lin, Zhenguo Gao, Qing an Peng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Cross-view image matching serves as a pivotal technology for UAV autonomous localization and navigation. Yet, given the severe viewpoint disparities between UAV and satellite imagery, current approaches often fail to balance the extraction of global semantics with the preservation of fine-grained local details. To overcome this limitation, we present an enhanced feature model built upon a lightweight backbone. We design a fusion architecture that leverages the Unit Spatial Attention Module (USAM) from Lin et al. to target salient regions, followed by the innovative integration of the Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) to establish a local feature enhancement module. This module explicitly captures high-frequency components—such as edges and textures—thereby effectively mitigating spatial information loss during downsampling. On the University-1652 benchmark, our method delivers highly competitive performance, achieving 81.63% R@1 in the localization task with a lightweight ConvNeXt-Tiny backbone. This validates the efficacy of our strategy in synergizing frequency-domain details with spatial semantics.

title: Wideband Frequency-Coupling Impedance Identification Under All Operating Points of Grid-Connected Inverters Based on PSO-GRNN
authors: Zilin Zhang, Zhiwei Xie, Wenhua Wu, Peng Liu, Zhihao Dai, Jiawei Xu
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: The acquisition of wideband frequency-coupling impedance in grid-connected inverters serves as a critical prerequisite for analyzing wideband oscillation phenomena. This paper proposes a data-driven all-operating-point frequencycoupling impedance identification method. First, the wideband frequency-coupling impedance data of the grid-connected inverter is obtained through online impedance measurement. Then, a frequency-coupling impedance identification model under all operating points is identified based on the generalized regression neural network (GRNN) algorithm. The particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm is utilized to dynamically adjust the smoothing factor of GRNN, thereby building an optimal GRNN model for accurate identification of frequency-coupling impedance under all operating points. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified through simulation.

title: Structured Prompt Optimization for Few-Shot Text Classification via Semantic Alignment in Latent Space
authors: Jiasen Zheng, Zijun Zhou, Huajun Zhang, Junjiang Lin, Jingyun Jia, Qi Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This study addresses the issues of semantic entanglement, unclear label structure, and insufficient feature representation in few-shot text classification, and proposes an optimization framework based on structured prompts to enhance semantic understanding and task adaptation under low-resource conditions. The framework first uses a pretrained language model to encode the input text and obtain basic semantic representations. It then introduces structured prompts composed of multi-dimensional semantic factors and integrates them with text features through a learnable combination mechanism, which forms task-related representations with clear boundaries in the latent space. To further strengthen the consistency between text representations and label semantics, the method constructs a structured label embedding matrix and employs a cross-space alignment mechanism to ensure stable matching between textual features and label attributes. In addition, the model applies prompt orthogonality constraints and a joint optimization objective to maintain independence across different semantic factors in the prompts, allowing the structured prompts to provide transparent and controllable guidance for classification decisions. Three types of sensitivity experiments, including learning rate sensitivity, prompt length sensitivity, and data scale sensitivity, are designed to evaluate the stability and robustness of the framework under different conditions. Experimental results show that the proposed structured prompt optimization framework effectively alleviates semantic conflicts and label ambiguity in few-shot text classification. It significantly improves performance on accuracy, precision, recall, and AUC, and demonstrates strong cross-task applicability. Overall, the method achieves stable semantic modeling under limited data and offers an efficient and interpretable optimization pathway for few-shot text classification.

title: $H_{\infty}$ Dynamic Output Feedback Control for the IPT System with External Disturbances
authors: Shuangxin Zhu, Qinglong Feng, Yi Zhang, Yuanjun Hou, Jinbin Zhao, Zhixin Wang
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: Due to unpredictable variations in actual operating environments, it is necessary for inductive power transfer (IPT) systems to dynamically adjust their output signals based on realtime demands under different conditions. To address this issue, an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> dynamic output feedback (DOF) control strategy is proposed to guarantee accurate tracking of time-varying reference outputs while maintain robust performance against environmental disturbances in this paper. Firstly, the dynamic mathematical model of the IPT system with the series-series (SS) compensation network is built via the extended describing function (EDF) method. Then, a closed-loop tracking control scheme with external disturbances is employed to enhance the regulation adaptability of the output voltage under engineering operational conditions. Subsequently, the gains of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> DOF controller are determined via sufficient conditions that ensure the asymptotic stability of the augmented closed-loop tracking control system and disturbance suppression index. Finally, simulation results of the SS-IPT system verify that the effectiveness and practical feasibility of the proposed control approach.

title: A Novel Automatic Modulation Recognition Methodology based on Attention Mechanism
authors: Yue Hu, Yu Long, Jinchao Hu, Yongyang Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Automatic modulation recognition is a key technology in cognitive intelligence, which can be used to distinguish signal modulation modes, especially for non-cooperative signals. The development of deep learning empowers automatic modulation recognition to be more intelligent and demonstrates better performance than traditional methods. However, it should be noted that automatic modulation recognition based on deep learning still has shortcomings. Firstly, the existing methods still cannot fully extract signal features. Secondly, the extracted features are directly used for brute force classification in most methods, which ignores the deep processing of features. These issues limit the further improvement of recognition accuracy. To solve the problems mentioned above, a novel automatic modulation recognition methodology based on attention mechanism is proposed. Firstly, a feature extraction enhancement module is designed to optimize feature extraction of signal. Then, a feature cross fusion module is proposed to fuse the enhanced features extracted from In-phase signal and Quadrature-phase signal respectively. The experiment is conducted to prove the effectiveness of our proposed methodology based on the benchmark dataset RadioML2016.10A. Compared to the existing popular deep learning methods, our methodology exhibits better performance, with recognition accuracy increased by 1.82% to 14.40%.

title: Quantum Cryptography Simulation
authors: Goutam R, Shwetha S, T R Loukya, Sowjanya M S, Sandhya S
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: By utilizing concepts from quantum physics like entanglement and superposition, quantum cryptography is revolutionizing data security. This study focuses on the application of classical computational models for simulation-based analysis of Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) protocols, namely BB84 and E91. By taking use of quantum features, QKD makes secure key exchange possible and guarantees that any effort at eavesdropping may be identified because of the disruption of quantum states. In order to assess how quantum communication channels behave under different noise and attack scenarios, the study models and simulates these channels. The study evaluates the efficacy and robustness of BB84 and E91 protocols in practical network settings by contrasting their performance and error tolerance. In order to determine if integrating quantum-secured systems with current classical infrastructures is feasible, scalability issues are also investigated. These simulations’ outcomes shed light on feasible security levels, resource needs, and workable deployment tactics. In the end, this research highlights how quantum cryptography may advance the basis for next-generation secure information networks by bridging the gap between theoretical quantum security models and real-world communication systems with the use of strong simulation frameworks.

title: ADLOB: An attention based deep learning model for predicting midprice in Limit Order Books
authors: Pulyapudi Aditya, Yalavarthi Roshan Sai, D Saicharan, Chevula Sai Anivesh, Anup Aprem
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: Predicting price movements in Limit Order Books (LOB) has historically been a challenging task due to the high frequency, complexity, and non-linear nature of the LOB data. This paper proposes ADLOB, a deep learning model incorporating convolutional neural network, an inception module, a LSTM layer and a custom weighted attention layer - the convolutional neural network extracts hierarchical and spatial features in LOB data, the LSTM layer extracts long term temporal dependence and the weighted attention layer selects the appropriate context among the hierarchical features extracted by both convolutional and LSTM layers. Numerical experiments on the FI-2010 benchmark dataset shows an accuracy improvement of 8% compared to the state-of-art model for LOB, along with similar improvements in precision, recall and f1-score. Backtesting results demonstrate improved performance in all metrics such as the cumulative return, maximum drawdown, Sharpe ratio, profit factor and win rate, compared to the state-of-art models for LOB.

title: Emergency Communication System for Hikers Using LoRa
authors: Sravani Nalluri, G. Gayatri, G. Abhinav, A. Sai Nagendar, Somula Ramasubbareddy
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Difficulties in communication in forest, hilly, and isolated areas are also a major danger to the hikers and small rescue groups since the cellular networks are either unreliable or even non-existent. To address this shortcoming, we have developed a small, inexpensive handheld tool that enables off-grid text messaging and SOS-assisted location sharing via LoRa technology, eliminating the need for towers, gateways, or internet connections. The proposed system will comprise an ESP32 microcontroller, LoRa RA-02 transceiver, GPS receiver, and user interface based on a minimal 16x2 LCD, backed up by custom low-power firmware and a store-and-forward communication model. Assessment was done in varied environments such as open fields, semi-urban areas, and thick forest hill areas, totaling more than 120 hours of tests. Findings have shown a consistent range of communication of 8-10km with a packet success rate of more than 93% and battery life of 18-72 hours, which depends on duty cycle, and the ability to propagate emergency alerts in network denials.

title: A Two-Stage Robust Operation Method for Microgrid Considering Charging and Discharging Control of Electric Vehicles
authors: Runru Zhai, Zhiyuan Tang, Youbo Liu, Hongjun Gao, Weiting Xu, Zhengwei Chang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs), due to their energy storage capabilities, can serve as flexible resources to provide various control services. The large-scale integration of EVs into microgrid creates new opportunities for optimal operation. This paper proposes a two-stage robust optimal operation method for microgrid considering EVs charging and discharging control. In the proposed method, the feasible operation region (FOR) of EV charging station (EVCS) is firstly estimated. Then, based on the FOR estimation, a two-stage robust optimization problem is constructed to calculate the optimal dispatch signals for EVCSs in microgrid. Finally, a pseudo-perturbation-based broadcast control (PBC) strategy is employed for each EVCS to allocate the obtained dispatch signal to EVs. Case studies demonstrate that the proposed method can maintain microgrid voltage within safe limits while ensuring economic operation of the system.

title: Adaptive IVMD-LSTM Model for Ultra-Short-Term Wind-Solar Power Forecasting
authors: Zhou Can, Li Jian, Luo Bin, Li Xiaopeng, Shi Peng, Nie Zhuang, Zeng Xueyang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: To fully leverage meteorological elements for enhancing wind and solar power forecasting, an ultra-short-term prediction model integrating Improved Variational Mode Decomposition (IVMD) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) artificial neural network is proposed. First, considering the volatility of renewable power and meteorological input sequences, VMD is employed to decompose the sequences into different frequency components. To avoid mode mixing and improve decomposition efficiency, the VMD process is optimized using a center frequency-based method, resulting in IVMD. Second, differential correlation analysis is applied to optimize the vectorization process of different modal components of the meteorological sequences. Each modal component is processed via sliding window vectorization and used as input to the LSTM model, so as to explore temporal correlations between meteorological modes and power sequences. Finally, to improve the adaptability and generalization of the model, an adaptive parameter optimization framework is introduced to match the best optimization algorithm for different power stations. Simulation analyses were conducted using real-world data from photovoltaic and wind farms in Southwest China. The results show that at the Ganbazi wind farm, the forecasting error (MAPE) was reduced by $13.7 \%$ and $1.6 \%$ compared to the LSTM and IVMD-LSTM models, respectively. Similarly, the Yimen solar farm saw improvements of $7.4 \%$ and $1.1 \%$, respectively.

title: Evaluating the Adversarial Stability of Classical Fake News Classifiers Using Transfomer Embeddings
authors: Nakul Chhimwal, Shivani Tufchi, Arpit Gola Bennett, Kartik Bhat
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The spread of fake news has become a significant concern in today’s digital landscape, calling for reliable and robust detection methods. This study investigates the adversarial robustness of classical machine learning models for fake news detection, emphasizing simplicity, interpretability, and resistance to manipulation. A balanced dataset of real and fake news articles was sourced from Kaggle’s Fake and True News dataset [8]. After preprocessing the text through tokenization, lemmatization, and TF-IDF vectorization, a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier was employed for classification. To enhance semantic representation, contextual embeddings were generated using DistilBERT to capture deeper contextual meaning in the news content.To evaluate robustness, a WordNet-based adversarial attack was applied by replacing words with contextually similar synonyms. The model achieved an accuracy of 99.25% on both clean and adversarially perturbed data. This consistency reflects the model’s robustness against minor lexical variations, such as synonym substitutions, without sacrificing classification quality. While these results are promising, further evaluation with stronger adversarial techniques is recommended to fully assess real-world applicability.

title: Realtime Sign Language to Text Conversion with AI Sentence Completion
authors: R. P. Narmadha, V. Ajay, S. K. Dharaneesh, S. Hareini
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: This paper is going to present a real time sign language translation system it can convert hand gestures into text using a webcam-based interface. User show a hand gesture and hold it steady for two seconds, which the system automatically recognizes the gesture and outputs the respective alphabet character. The proposed system is designed to enhance communication for individuals with speech and hearing impairments. Tracking and Hand detection are performed using the default MediaPipe hand landmark model, which enables accurate visual capture from live video streams. The extracted hand landmark features are processed using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) trained to classify sign language gestures at the character level. To enhance user experience and communication speed, a word and sentence prediction module is interfaced using the LLaMA 3.1 language model via API access on the Groq platform. The system is developed using Tensorflow and Python. This helps the system run directly in a web browser and provides real-time visual feedback through a simple user interface. Overall, unlike other sign language convertors, this paper presents on prediction of the words based on the sentence context which reduces a significant amount of time for the conversion of long sentences compared to traditional sign language conversion.

title: Market Bidding and Optimal Control of Distribution Transformer Areas Based on Virtual Power Plant Risk Management Model
authors: Bing Wang, Zhihui Wang, Lei Chen, Fei Xu, Ling Hao, Aoqian Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: As market bidding and optimal control participants, virtual power plants (VPPs) employ commonly centralized uniform clearing bidding involving price and quantity processes. Existing literature overlooks price bidding risks, focusing only on quantity-related revenue risks. This study addresses this gap by constructing a risk management model-based bidding risk-return function, quantifying price risks via historical clearing price probability matrix with conditional value-at-risk (CVaR). A two-dimensional risk framework merges price and quantity metrics into unified assessment for distribution transformer areas. Methods for market bidding and optimal control in energy and ancillary markets evaluate profit potential and control regulation capability. Cases show VPPs should adapt strategies to market traits and risk preferences. Profit analysis confirms the multi-market coordinated approach enhances profitability: total system revenue rises by 22.1% vs. single-energy market maximum; conservative VPP4’s risk loss ratio drops from 4.50% to 1.83%. The framework improves system coordination efficiency and supports VPPs in balancing risk and return under uncertainty.

title: A Modified Transformation for Solving Complex Ordinary Differential Equations
authors: Amal Khalaf Haydar, Radhi A. Zaboon, Shatha S. Alhily
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: This paper suggests a complex differential transformation in the one-dimensional space by modifying the real differential transformation. Thus the modified complex differential transformation is defined for complex valued functions of complex variable. A table of proposed transformation for many important complex functions has been included. In addition, solution of complex differential equations and also solution of the complex hypergeometric differential equation by the modified complex transformation are presented directly without converting to real space.

title: Automatic Parameter Identification for Electric Drives: Ensuring High Accuracy and Adaptive Control Systems
authors: Alexander Korolev, Andrew Senkevich
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: Achieving high accuracy, stability, and adaptability in electric drive control systems requires precise knowledge of the drive’s dynamic parameters. This paper presents an automatic frequency-domain identification algorithm for real-time parameter estimation without interrupting the technological process. The method employs a linear second-order transfer function model that represents either a DC drive or a vector-controlled induction motor. By injecting low-amplitude sinusoidal test signals into the control loop across a broad frequency range (0.1–5000 Hz) and recording the amplitude and phase response of the output signal, the algorithm extracts key parameters—system gain and inertial time constants—with a typical accuracy of ±1–2%. This approach eliminates the need for dedicated offline testing, thereby increasing system availability and enabling continuous performance monitoring and adaptive controller tuning. Experimental validation confirms the algorithm’s robustness in industrial environments, establishing a reliable foundation for optimized control design, early fault detection, and predictive maintenance strategies

title: Explainable Machine Learning Framework for Behavioral Sentiment Analysis Using ASD-Related Data
authors: S. M. Jeya Ramani, J. Ida Christy
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Behavioral data analysis plays a vital role in understanding patterns within structured human response data. Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD)-related datasets contain rich behavioral attributes that can be explored from an analytics-driven perspective without clinical or diagnostic intent. This paper presents an explainable machine learning framework for behavioral sentiment analysis using ASD-related questionnaire data. Structured behavioral response scores are aggregated and mapped into sentiment-oriented categories, namely positive, neutral, and negative, enabling sentiment classification of behavioral patterns. The proposed framework employs Random Forest, XGBoost, and Neural Network models to evaluate classification performance using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC metrics. To enhance transparency, Explainable Artificial Intelligence techniques, including SHAP and LIME, are integrated to provide global and instance-level interpretability of model predictions. Experimental results demonstrate that ensemble-based models, particularly XGBoost, achieve superior classification performance. Explainability analysis highlights the influence of key behavioral attributes related to social interaction and communication. The study emphasizes the importance of interpretable machine learning for behavioral sentiment analysis and demonstrates the potential of explainable analytics frameworks for structured ASD-related data without engaging in clinical diagnosis. This study focuses exclusively on analytics-driven interpretation of behavioral data and does not perform clinical diagnosis or medical decision-making.

title: An Adaptive Ensemble-Based Multi-Focus Image Fusion Framework Using Classical Methods and Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: M S Santhi Krishna, D Harikrishnan, C R Shiyas
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: Multi-Focus Image Fusion is a computational imaging approach to fuse multiple depth focused images into an all-in-focus representation. Classical fusion methods like Laplacian, Wavelet and PCA-based fusion can capture low level features efficiently but high level semantic features cannot be preserved. Deep learning methods (such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)) can be used to learn complicated spatial features but are more likely to ignore fine-grained texture details. Inspired by the success of classical fusion as well as deep learning technique, we develop a novel composite ensemble based architecture to better solve this problem. This model produces pixel-wise adaptive weights to fuse the pixel wise output of multiple fusion strategies. Comprehensive experiments on the benchmark MFIF datasets show that this framework has a significant and steady superiority over their classical and CNN counterparts in standard metrics such as SSIM, PSNR, Entropy, Standard Deviation and Spatial Frequency. The experimental results show that the method can obtain high-quality all-in-focus images so that the proposed method is capable for high-level image fusion.

title: PSense: Paper Material Sensing With MIMO Mmwave Radar Via Dual-Model Transfer Learning
authors: Fahim Niaz, Jian Zhang, Muhammad Younas, Muhammad Khalid, Ashfaq Niaz, Umer Zukaib
abstract: Accurate authentication of paper-based materials is crucial for secure document verification, counterfeit currency detection, and intelligent packaging across industries such as finance, logistics, and security. Differentiating between subtle variations, such as glossy, recycled, coated, or counterfeit paper, remains a significant challenge due to minimal material-level differences. We introduce PSense, the first millimeter-wave MIMO radar-based sensing framework for paper material analysis, offering a fully contactless solution that relies solely on reflected signals, eliminating the need for transmission-based or dual-sided setups. To characterize intrinsic paper properties, we propose a novel material-sensitive feature called the Energy Reflection Ratio (ERR), which encodes key physical interactions including surface reflectivity, internal attenuation, and dispersion, uniquely capturing the electromagnetic signature of each paper type. We further present Trans-PapNet, a dual-model transfer learning framework designed to enhance classification robustness and generalization. One model extracts spatial and spectral patterns from radar signal scalograms using ResNet-50, while the other processes ERR-based physical features. These complementary representations are fused via a shared attention mechanism, enabling effective domain adaptation across paper types. Our method achieves an overall classification accuracy of 97.85%, with strong performance in three knowledge-based transfer scenarios (94.8%, 93.0%, and 90.0%) and under both seen and unseen material conditions (95.3%). These results underscore PSense's robustness and scalability, paving the way for real-world deployment in mobile, secure, and intelligent paper-based authentication systems.

title: Design and Performance of 220 and 270 GHz Bandpass Filters for BICEP Array
authors: A. Steiger, P. A. R. Ade, Z. Ahmed, M. Amiri, D. Barkats, R. Basu Thakur, C. A. Bischoff, D. Beck, J. J. Bock, H. Boenish, V. Buza, K. Carter, J. R. Cheshire IV, J. Connors, J. Cornelison, L. Corrigan, M. Crumrine, S. Crystian, A. J. Cukierman, E. Denison, L. Duband, M. Echter, M. Eiben, B. D. Elwood, S. Fatigoni, J. P. Filippini, A. Fortes, M. Gao, C. Giannakopoulos, N. Goeckner-Wald, D. C. Goldfinger, J. A. Grayson, A. Greathouse, P. K. Grimes, G. Hall, G. Halal, M. Halpern, E. Hand, S. A. Harrison, S. Henderson, J. Hubmayr, H. Hui, K. D. Irwin, J. H. Kang, K. S. Karkare, S. Kefeli, J. M. Kovac, C. Kuo, K. Lau, M. Lautzenhiser, A. Lennox, T. Liu, K. G. Megerian, M. Miller, L. Minutolo, L. Moncelsi, Y. Nakato, H. T. Nguyen, R. O'Brient, S. Paine, A. Patel, M. A. Petroff, A. R. Polish, T. Prouve, C. Pryke, C. D. Reintsema, T. Romand, D. Santalucia, A. Schillaci, B. Schmitt, E. Sheffield, B. Singari, K. Sjoberg, A. Soliman, T. St Germaine, B. Steinbach, R. Sudiwala, K. L. Thompson, C. Tsai, C. Tucker, A. D. Turner, C. Vergés, A. G. Vieregg, A. Wandui, A. C. Weber, J. Willmert, W. L. K. Wu, H. Yang, C. Yu, L. Zeng, C. Zhang, S. Zhang
abstract: The BICEP Array (BA) is the latest in the BICEP/Keck series of experiments that aim to measure the polarization of the cosmic microwave background (CMB) with small aperture polarimeters located at the South Pole. To constrain the frequency response of these receivers, each detector is serially coupled to a band-pass filter (BPF). The electric circuits of these BPFs utilize series and shunt capacitors as well as series inductors, but critically do not include shunt inductors which simplifies fabrication. The filters are designed and simulated with Sonnet, and optimized for noise by considering loading from the atmosphere and the CMB. Multiple 220 GHz detector modules have had their frequency response measured at the South Pole. The 270 GHz detector modules have recently begun testing in a lab setting, and their performance in a BA receiver will be measured this winter.

title: Robust Self-Localization of Wireless Acoustic Sensor Networks in the Presence of TDoA Outliers_supp1-3676429.pdf
authors: De Hu
abstract: Today, we are surrounded by numerous intelligent terminals (e.g., smartphones, tablets, and laptops) that can collectively form wireless acoustic sensor networks (WASNs), which are expected to be the next-generation platform for sound processing. Since the geometric structure of WASNs is critical for tasks like source localization and acoustic beamforming, automatic self-localization of WASNs (SL-WASNs) is indispensable. Although several SL-WASN methods have been developed, most of them fail to account for the impact of time-difference-of-arrival (TDoA) outliers induced by noise and reverberation. To address this issue, we first propose a centralized-robust self-localization (CRSL) method for WASNs with a centralized network topology. Specifically, we develop a novel probabilistic model leveraging the zero-sum property of TDoAs to derive the probability of a TDoA measurement being an inlier (or outlier). Then, we construct a weighted cost function based on TDoA measurements, in which the weights are controlled by the computed inlier probabilities. Afterward, the Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno (BFGS) algorithm is employed to simultaneously estimate the positions of nodes and sources, as well as the inter-node time offsets. In addition, we extend the CRSL to a distributed framework, termed distributed-robust self-localization (DRSL), which incorporates distributed computation of inlier probabilities, distributed cost function construction, and distributed optimization. Furthermore, the computational complexities of both CRSL and DRSL are analyzed in detail. The main contributions of this work can be summarized as follows: a novel TDoA outlier recognition model, the CRSL method, the DRSL method, and their complexity analysis. In real-world experiments with M = 10 nodes and N = 8 sources, the proposed methods achieved over 8% higher localization accuracy than state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Design of Power Allocation and Device Grouping in PD-NOMA Communication Systems_supp1-3678581.pdf
authors: Min Hua
abstract: Power domain non-orthogonal multiple access (PD-NOMA) is considered an effective means of enabling massive connectivity and improving spectral efficiency in next-generation (6G) wireless communication networks, particularly for the massive Internet of Things (mIoT). This paper optimizes power allocation and device grouping to address energy efficiency and resource scarcity challenges. We investigate optimal power allocation under two distinct successive interference cancellation (SIC) decoding orders at the receiver, incorporating constraints on limited transmit power, quality of service (QoS), and SIC decoding requirements. Using Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions, we derive rigorous criteria for grouping IoT devices on shared subchannels and determine their optimal power coefficients. Analytical results prove that channel-gain-ascending SIC maximizes downlink subchannel capacity, while channel-gain-descending SIC optimizes uplink performance. Furthermore, we propose a resource-efficient device grouping scheme that reduces the required number of orthogonal subchannels compared to conventional orthogonal multiple access (OMA). Simulations demonstrate that the proposed scheme not only conserves the number of orthogonal subchannels compared to the OMA system, but also exhibits superior overall system capacity. This work directly contributes to energy-efficient resource management in sustainable IoT communications by minimizing spectral resources and enhancing network longevity.

title: Cycle Time Optimization in Scheduling Automated Bench Cleaning Tool for Semiconductor Manufacturing
authors: Luetao Li, Siwei Zhang, Yan Qiao, Naiqi Wu, Jie Li, Bai Yang
abstract: In the semiconductor wafer manufacturing process, wafer cleaning operation is responsible for removing the residues on the surface of wafers after processing. The cleaning time accounts for 30% of the wafer fabrication work, which is significant as other wafer production processes. Wafer cleaning equipment consists of a single-arm robot for transportation and multiple processing tanks that are organized in a linear topology structure. Unlike cluster tools with its processing modules being arranged in a radial structure, the moving time of the robot between the tanks in a cleaning tool cannot be treated as the same, making its scheduling problem more difficult. The purpose of this paper is to solve the scheduling problem of automatic bench cleaning tools to make the wafer residency time same among the tanks for a processing step while maximizing the tool productivity. In order to achieve this goal, a backward strategy for wafer cleaning is proposed and its characteristics under steady state are studied. Based on the analysis results, scheduling algorithms aiming at minimizing the wafer cleaning cycle are established. Finally, the practicability of the algorithm is proved by case analysis.

title: Adaptive Game-Based Learning for Artistic Anatomy: Effects on Learning Achievement and Creative Self-Efficacy among First-Year University Art Students
authors: Guo Xinming, Chau Kien Tsong, Wan Ahmad Jaafar Wan Yahaya
abstract: This study investigates whether interpretable adaptive difficulty regulation in game-based learning enhances learning achievement and creative self-efficacy in artistic anatomy education. An Adaptive Game-Based Learning (AGBL) system was developed using the ADDIE model and grounded in constructivist theory, the adaptive learning framework, and social cognitive theory. The system implements a rule-based, transparent difficulty adjustment mechanism driven by a performance index derived from accuracy and response time. A quasi-experimental design was conducted with 109 first-year university art students, who were assigned to either the AGBL condition or a Non-Adaptive Game-Based Learning (N-AGBL) condition. Analysis of covariance (ANCOVA) controlling for pretest scores revealed that the AGBL group significantly outperformed the N-AGBL group in both learning achievement (F(1,106) = 42.56, p < .001, ηp² = .286) and creative self-efficacy (F(1,106) = 56.90, p < .001, ηp² = .349). Furthermore, creative self-efficacy was positively correlated with sketching test scores (r = .587), and the association remained significant after controlling for group assignment (partial r = .370), providing convergent validity evidence. Offline benchmarking against fixed, random, and Bayesian knowledge tracing scheduling strategies demonstrated that the rule-based mechanism achieved more stable difficulty trajectories while maintaining comparable accuracy. The findings suggest that structured and interpretable adaptive control is associated with improvements in cognitive mastery and motivational belief. This study contributes empirical evidence for rule-based adaptive game-based learning in visual-spatial domains and offers replicable design principles for integrating adaptive regulation into creative learning environments.

title: Decoupling Energy Stability and Thermal Response in Thermoelectric Atmospheric Water Generators
authors: María C. Salgado-Gutiérrez, José L. López-Moreno, Perla Y. Sevilla-Camacho, Gilbert F. Pérez-García, Rubén Martínez-Revuelta, Horacio I. Solís-Cisneros
abstract: This work experimentally evaluates the dynamic reproducibility of a thermoelectric atmospheric water generation (AWG) prototype operated under six control strategies. Three independent trials were conducted per strategy, and time-series data of cold-side temperature, hot-side temperature, energy consumption, and coefficient of performance were analyzed. Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) was used to quantify temporal similarity and capture non-linear deviations across repetitions. Results show that energy consumption remains highly reproducible, with average DTW distances below 1.15 Wh in most cases. In contrast, thermal variables exhibit significant dispersion, with DTW values reaching up to 113.53 for the Thermal Lift Coefficient. These findings demonstrate a clear dynamic decoupling between energy stability and thermal response, indicating that stable electrical input does not guarantee consistent thermal behavior. The observed variability is attributed to the intrinsic thermal inertia and non-linear dynamics of the Peltier effect, which amplify minor operational fluctuations. The results highlight the limitations of average performance metrics and emphasize the need for time-resolved analysis and advanced control strategies to ensure reliable operation of thermoelectric AWG systems.

title: EnCANet: A Network with Entropy-Guided Saliency and Cross-Spatial Attention for Urban Building Change Detection
authors: Tiankai Ge, Xianglong Qiao, Yanyan Zhang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Remote sensing change detection (RSCD) is essential for urban building monitoring, yet accurate building change extraction is often limited by the structural complexity of buildings and spatial heterogeneity. To address these challenges, we propose EnCANet, a supervised RSCD framework for urban building change detection that explicitly models spatial information density and cross-spatial dependencies. The framework comprises three key components: (1) an Entropy-guided Saliency Prior (ESP) module that computes local channel-wise entropy to quantify spatial information density, generating adaptive weights to guide early-stage feature modulation; (2) an Entropy-Focused Cross-Spatial Attention (EnFocSA) mechanism that integrates entropy priors into attention calculation to suppress pseudo-change responses while enhancing cross-region contextual discrimination; and (3) a Uni-FiRE decoder that employs a unified fusion-and-refinement strategy to restore multi-scale details and ensure structural consistency. Extensive experiments on the LEVIR-CD and WHU-CD building change detection datasets demonstrate that EnCANet achieves F1 scores of 92.25% and 94.27%, surpassing the closest state-of-the-art competitors by 0.46% and 1.01%, respectively. These results quantitatively demonstrate the effectiveness of integrating entropy-driven spatial priors and cross-spatial attention for accurate and robust urban building change detection in complex heterogeneous environments. Our code is available at https://github.com/SkyeGe04/EnCANet.

title: Fine-Grained Features in Any-Shot Learning: A Survey
authors: Qiangchang Wang, Wenhao Li, Jinglin Zhang, Zheyun Qin, Linhao Li
abstract: Deep learning has achieved remarkable success across a wide range of domains and applications over the past few decades. However, the high performance of most deep learning models is heavily dependent on large volumes of labeled data, which is often labor-intensive, time-consuming, and sometimes impractical to collect. To address this challenge, anyshot learning (ASL)—such as few-shot and zero-shot learning—has been proposed to recognize novel classes with only a few labeled examples or class descriptions. The human visual system excels at identifying discriminative regions in complex scenes, and inspired by this capability, fine-grained features are extracted from input data to emphasize key regions, advancing the field of ASL. Despite the success of these methods, there is a lack of comprehensive surveys on the use of fine-grained features in ASL. This paper provides a detailed survey of recent research on fine-grained features in few-shot and zero-shot learning, offering summaries of various approaches, discussing their strengths and limitations, and identifying key challenges, future directions, and research opportunities to drive further advancements in the field. We also explore the potential of ASL in semi-supervised learning, weakly supervised learning, and domain adaptation and highlight its application

title: Wireless Channel Randomness Integrated Spread Spectrum Sequence Generation
authors: Dongming Li, Yuting Lai, Dong Wei, Meng Zhang, Dawei Wang, Xianglin Fan, Linchao Yang, Lin Bai
abstract: Spread spectrum communication plays a vital role in safeguarding the Internet of Things systems, due to its inherently low probability of interception and anti-jamming capability. However, conventional spread spectrum systems based on pseudorandom sequences are disadvantageous in limited sequence length and deterministic periodicity, making them vulnerable to brute-force attacks. To address these limitations and enhance the randomness of the spread spectrum sequences, a novel wireless channel randomness integrated spread spectrum sequence generation method is proposed in this work. Taking advantages of the intrinsic randomness, temporal variations, and unpredictability of wireless channel fadings, the proposed approach converts the extracted channel features into ordered sequences, which are then used to control the selection of irreducible generating polynomials for spread spectrum sequence generation. The proposed method improves the randomness and secrecy of the integrated spread spectrum sequence. Theoretical analysis and simulation results demonstrate that the proposed sequences not only achieve higher randomness entropy compared to the traditional m-sequence, but also pass the National Institute of Standards and Technology randomness tests. Furthermore, performance evaluations under various signal-to-interference ratio conditions show improved autocorrelation properties and largely lower bit error rates, validating the effectiveness of the proposed method in improving the anti-jamming capability.

title: Exploring Continuous Modes-Based Nonlinear Embedding Models for GaN HEMT Power Amplifiers
authors: Ahmad Zakaria Ahmad, Mohammad Abdul Shukoor, Patrick Roblin, Karun Rawat
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This work presents a nonlinear embedding model approach for designing continuous class E (CCE) power amplifiers (PAs) based on gallium nitride (GaN)-HEMT packaged devices with harmonics above 6 GHz. This approach extends the embedding model for broadband PAs by enabling direct access to the current generator plane (CP), as well as the accurate projection of loads at the package plane (PP). By exploiting the embedding model’s ability to enforce Foster behavior at PP, this method circumvents non-Foster load challenges and simplifies the design of wideband passive matching networks. CCE design parameter (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\phi $</tex-math> </inline-formula>) possibility is investigated over the operating frequency range to achieve the wideband Foster behavior. For validation, two CCE PA designs with CGHV1F025S (plastic DFN) and CGH40025F (ceramic) devices are implemented using the advancements in the extracted embedding model. These PAs deliver 43–44.5 dBm of output power and 51.3%–69.1% drain efficiency (DE) in the S-band. This work provides comprehensive, systematic guidelines for parameter extraction, and the results establish embedding model-assisted CCE design as a scalable solution for high-efficiency broadband RF transmitters.

title: Reliability-Based Design and Control of Crystallization Systems Under Parametric and Process Uncertainties_supp1-3670402.pdf
authors: Zoltan Nagy
abstract: Population balance model-based approaches are widely employed in crystallization process design to meet the industry-specific critical quality attributes (CQAs). However, their reliability is often limited by parameter uncertainties arising from noisy experimental data, which if, unaccounted for, can lead to suboptimal or failed designs. This study introduces a novel reliability-based design optimization (RBDO) framework for open-loop design of crystallization systems under uncertainty. The framework formulates CQA constraints as probabilistic constraints with user-defined reliability thresholds, enabling quantified, risk-informed process design. To solve the resulting RBDO problems efficiently, a surrogate modeling-based, nested simulation-optimization workflow is developed. The proposed methodology is applied to both batch and continuous pharmaceutical crystallization systems, demonstrating its ability to generate robust design trajectories with significantly higher probabilities of meeting CQAs under both parametric and operational uncertainties compared to the deterministic designs. The computational efficiency and accuracy of the approach are further validated through benchmarking against back-mapping-based solutions of the underlying chance constrained formulations.

title: Background-Noise-Driven Detection of Diffusion-Generated Images_supp1-3678476.pdf
authors: ou weihua
abstract: Diffusion-based image generation has proliferated, making robust detection of synthetic imagery critical. We propose a background-noise-driven detector motivated by the observation that real camera images preserve physical noise traces, whereas model-generated images tend to exhibit algorithm-induced residual statistics. Starting from sRGB, we apply a deterministic inverse ISP to obtain an approximate Bayer RAW representation, train a lightweight RAW-domain denoiser, and extract a noise residual by subtracting the denoised reconstruction from the inverse-ISP signal. A detector trained only on ADM residuals generalizes in a zero-shot manner to a wide range of unseen diffusion generators. By emphasizing noise-domain cues rather than semantic content, the proposed method offers a simple and practical approach to diffusion-generated image detection.

title: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems--I: Regular Papers Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers

title: Dynamic Quarantine Slicing for DDoS Mitigation in Healthcare SDN Environments
authors: Marshal S, Jaspher W. Kathrine, Shajin Prince, Samson Arun Raj A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Software-Defined Networking is increasingly integrated into healthcare networks to ensure effective network traffic control. SDN is highly vulnerable to Distributed Denial-of-Service attacks that exploit OpenFlow, critical medical workings such as tele-surgery and patient monitoring. The existing systems such as reactive filtering and static VLAN slicing, produce high latency up to 100ms and may not be effective against low-rate stealthy attacks that mimic the legitimate medical network traffic. This paper proposes a framework Dynamic Quarantine Slicing, a framework for realtime DDoS mitigation in healthcare networks. It combines a hybrid GRU-LSTM deep learning model with Ryu controllerbased firewall and switching mechanisms in adaptive order to apply policies of flow isolation. Results done on the Mininet healthcare testbed shows a 96% classification accuracy, it outperforms existing system's logistic regression 85 %.DQS can quarantine malicious flows within a latency of 5ms, ensuring rapid recovery within 60 seconds to ensure continuity in critical medical communications.

title: GCET 2025 Commentary
authors: N/A

title: I5CPS 2026 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)

title: Biomedical Fragmented Attention Spectral Transformer Network for Infrared spectra analysis
authors: Durgesh Ameta, Hardik Sharma, Praful Hambarde, Laxmidhar Behera
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Rapid and non-invasive molecular characterization of biological samples using Fourier Transform Infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy is increasingly important in clinical diagnostics, tissue analysis, and microbiology. However, existing approaches rely on conventional chemometric and machine learning pipelines that require extensive preprocessing and lack real-time capability. In this work, we propose BioFASTNet (Biomedical Fragmented Attention Spectral Transformer Network), an end-to-end deep learning framework for real-time spectral analysis. The model integrates a Multi-resolution Convolutional Feature Extractor (MCFE) to capture multi-scale spectral features and a chemistry-informed Fragment-wise Attention Module (FAM) to model localized, functional group–specific regions. This design enables effective learning of both global spectral patterns and localized vibrational signatures. BioFASTNet is tailored for imbalanced multilabel classification, addressing challenges arising from overlapping molecular attributes and skewed data distributions. Experimental results demonstrate superior performance on a functional group prediction dataset, achieving 97.81% accuracy and 91.97% F1-score, outperforming state-of-the-art (SOTA) models such as CIRSNet and AggMapNet. On an additional odor prediction dataset, the model achieves a SOTA F1-score of 36.68%. Furthermore, interpretability analyses using fragment-wise attention and Grad-CAM++ reveal that the model focuses on chemically meaningful spectral regions. These results highlight BioFASTNet as an efficient, accurate, and interpretable solution for real-time FTIR-based biomedical analysis. Source code and pre-trained models are available at: https://github.com/durgesgameta/biofastnet

title: Gain Assessment of Environmental-Informed Subarray Processing: The ALMA 2017 Example
authors: Alexandre L’Her, Angélique Drémeau, Florent Le Courtois, Gaultier Real, Xavier Cristol, Yann Stéphan
abstract: Underwater acoustic propagation can be affected by ocean fluctuations. In particular, internal waves can reduce the spatial coherence of the acoustic signal on SONAR arrays, causing performance degradation. Subarray processing is a popular method, used to mitigate the performance loss caused by environmental fluctuations. The problem lies in the correct parameterization of the length of the subarrays, itself linked to the acoustic coherence length, with the latter influenced by the fluctuations of the environment. In this work, we propose the use of a linear model, derived from canonical correlation analysis (CCA), to configure the length of the subarrays according to statistics of acoustic and environmental measurements, in the context of direction-of-arrival estimation. To compensate for the loss of angular resolution inherent to the combination of subarrays using conventional beamforming, we propose to consider a sparse regularization, solved using SoBaP, a Bayesian algorithm. We show on at-sea data from the 2017 ALMA campaign that regularization compensates for some of the resolution loss due to the use of the subarrays. In addition, detection performance is increased when using the regularized subarray scheme, particularly when acoustic coherence is low. Finally, using the CCA-informed scheme shows better detection performance than the noninformed schemes for low coherence and low signal-to-noise ratio, demonstrating the benefits of informing array-processing algorithms with environmental statistics.

title: Mem-ADPE: A Footprint-based Memory Prediction Method for Anomaly Detection and Prefetching in Consumer Electronics
authors: Jinting Ren, Hengyi Ren, Runyu Zhang, Chaoshu Yang, Thippa Reddy Gadekallu, Mohammad Shabaz, Zhaoyang Han
abstract: The rise of smart technologies has subjected consumer electronic devices to dual pressures: the need for optimized performance and robust security. Traditional memory access models often fail to accurately capture complex spatio-temporal memory locality, which simultaneously limits the efficiency of data prefetching and the reliability of security baselines. This paper proposes Mem-ADPE (A Footprint-based Memory Prediction Method for Anomaly Detection and Prefetching in Consumer Electronics), a unified framework designed to enhance both the security and efficiency of consumer electronic systems. Mem-ADPE innovatively represents memory access history as a 2D “footprint” image, utilizing a lightweight Micro-UNet deep convolutional network to achieve high-fidelity prediction of future cache line accesses. By establishing this single prediction result as the shared baseline for normal behavior, Mem-ADPE can effectively perform Anomaly Detection (by flagging significant structural deviations from the predicted footprint) and Data Prefetching (by accurately capturing complex spatial locality). This “one-image, dual-purpose” approach provides proactive security defense and optimized performance management for next-generation intelligent consumer electronic systems.

title: KPGS: Towards Real-World Complex Dynamic Scene Rendering With Keyframe-Driven Predictable Gaussian Splatting
authors: Yiqian Chang, Haoran Xu, Jianing Li, Xuan Wang, Yonghong Tian, Peixi Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Rendering complex dynamic scenes offers the advantage of observing and understanding the real world. However, existing Dynamic Scene Rendering (DSR) methods remain challenged by suboptimal reconstruction fidelity. These limitations stem from relying on a single, unified deformation model, which struggles to capture complex motions involving multiple sub-motions and abrupt geometric transitions. While temporal decomposition methods could alleviate such shortcomings, they introduce the additional challenge of ignoring motion correlations and increasing storage requirements. To address these issues, we introduce Keyframe-driven Predictable Gaussian Splatting (KPGS)—an efficient framework for high-fidelity complex dynamic scene rendering. Firstly, we present a patch- wise HSV clustering for extracting keyframes. Secondly, a prediction network based on the Transformer is utilized to calculate the deformable Gaussians at discrete keyframe times via voxelization. Thirdly, we propose an inter-frame deformation network and a mutual supervision between adjacent segments to maintain the temporal continuity. Extensive experiments on our newly built dataset (MotionGS), as well as public benchmarks HyperNeRF and Neu3D, demonstrate that KPGS could achieve a higher average view synthesis performance than SOTA approaches, while maintaining a balance between storage cost and performance. More details of the demo and dataset are available at KPGS Supplementary.

title: Overhead-Aware Adaptive RIS Subarray Grouping for Uplink Random Access in Cellular Networks
authors: Yizhu Wang, Zhou Zhang, Saman Atapattu, Marco Di Renzo
abstract: This paper investigates uplink reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted massive random access (RA) in multiuser cellular networks with heterogeneous user distances. The goal is to enhance media access control (MAC)-layer throughput under practical signaling and channel constraints. Conventional RIS configurations using full-array probing incur excessive overhead, especially with large numbers of reflective elements (REs). To address this, we propose an overhead-aware, multi-phase uplink RA framework that integrates adaptive RIS subarray grouping with scalable multiuser scheduling for dynamic user activity. The access optimization is formulated as a sequential decision process, from which an optimal threshold-based policy with closed-form structure is derived. Building on this, an opportunistic RA algorithm is developed to jointly optimize user scheduling, subarray configuration, and access timing with low online complexity that scales with RIS resolution, subchannel count, and user density. Numerical simulations, supported by analytical results, demonstrate significant throughput gains over existing schemes—including full-array, fixed-grouping, and both orthogonal and non-orthogonal multiple access methods—across varying coherence times, RIS sizes, and user distributions. The robustness of the proposed strategy is further verified under imperfect CSI conditions, confirming its suitability for large-scale, dynamic cellular deployments. The proposed framework is further shown to remain effective under practical impairments such as spatial correlation and finite-resolution RIS phase control.

title: CVSM: Contrastive Vocal Similarity Modeling
authors: Christos Garoufis, Athanasia Zlatintsi, Petros Maragos
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: The availability of large, unlabeled datasets across various domains has contributed to the development of a plethora of methods that learn representations for multiple target (downstream) tasks through self-supervised pre-training. In this work, we introduce CVSM (Contrastive Vocal Similarity Modeling), a contrastive self-supervised procedure for music signal representation learning in the audio domain that can be utilized for musical and vocal similarity modeling. Our method operates under a contrastive framework, maximizing the similarity between vocal excerpts and musical mixtures containing the same vocals; we devise both a label-informed protocol, leveraging artist identity information to sample the contrastive pairs, and a label-agnostic scheme, involving artificial mixture creation from randomly sampled vocal and accompaniment excerpts, which are paired with vocals from the same audio segment. We evaluate our proposed method in measuring vocal similarity both objectively, through linear probing on a suite of appropriate downstream tasks, and subjectively, via conducting a user study consisting of pairwise comparisons between different models in a recommendation-by-query setting. Our results indicate that the representations learned through CVSM are effective in musical and vocal similarity modeling, outperforming numerous baselines across both isolated vocals and complete musical mixtures. Moreover, while the availability of artist identity labels during pre-training leads to overall more consistent performance both in the evaluated downstream tasks and the user study, a label-agnostic CVSM variant incorporating hybrid pre-training with real and artificial mixtures achieves comparable performance to the label-informed one in artist identification and perceived vocal similarity.

title: Perceptual Decoupling with a New Dataset and the HiFi-Net Blind Image Quality Assessment Model
authors: Nanlin Xu, Ming Ronnier Luo
abstract: Traditional Image Quality Assessment (IQA) often treats perceptual quality as a monolithic concept, overlooking the independent contributions of its color and spatial dimensions. To address this gap, this research first introduces a large-scale subjective study to investigate the relationship between total image quality (IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub>) and its constituent color (IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</sub>) and spatial (IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</sub>) components. The results provide strong empirical evidence that IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub> can be effectively decoupled and accurately represented by a weighted linear combination of IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</sub> and IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</sub> (Pearson Correlation Coefficient of 0.96), while IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</sub> and IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</sub> themselves are largely independent (correlation of 0.092). Theoretically, this perceptual decoupling aligns with the functional specialization of the Human Visual System (HVS), which processes chromatic and structural attributes through segregated neural pathways. Moreover, we propose a novel Blind IQA (BIQA) model, the Hierarchical Fusion Network (HiFi-Net). This model leverages a pre-trained Vision Transformer (ViT) backbone to extract rich, multi-level features and introduces an innovative layer-independent perceptual attention mechanism (comprising intra-layer and inter-layer attention). This mechanism adaptively captures layer-specific quality-aware features and aggregates them across depths, extracting critical information from complex distortions for final score regression. Extensive experiments on nine public benchmark datasets demonstrate that HiFi-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance on both synthetic and authentic distortions, validating its effectiveness and strong generalization capability.

title: Copyrigh Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)

title: Observer-based Fault Estimation and Compensation Control for Nonlinear Multi-delays Systems With Multi-Source Faults
authors: Ming-Yang Qiao, Xiao-Heng Chang, Ju H. Park
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper addresses the fault estimation and compensation control problem for nonlinear stochastic multi-delays systems with multi-source faults. The T–S fuzzy model is used to describe the nonlinearity of the system. By assuming that each stochastic delay is independent and follows the Bernoulli distribution to describe the stochastic multi-delays possibly caused by sensor/actuator faults, the targeted fault observers with historical information are introduced to estimate the sensor/actuator faults respectively, so that the designed fault-tolerant compensation control (FTCC) method can ensure the stochastic stability and H∞ control performance for the closed-loop FTCC system. The sufficient design conditions for the fuzzy observer and the fault-tolerant compensation controller are obtained through the application of Lyapunov-Krasovskii stability theory and techniques. Finally, the effectiveness of the designed algorithm is verified through a common automation control system.

title: UPDet: Learning-based darting-out pedestrian detection by ultrasonic sensors
authors: Yingying Wang, Chenyu Jin, Hu Cheng
abstract: Ultrasonic sensors are among the critical components in vehicular applications, primarily due to their cost-effectiveness, which is also the main constraint limiting the performance of vehicle-embedded ultrasonic sensors, particularly in detecting dynamic objects. This paper introduces UPDet, a benchmark system for detecting and localizing pedestrians darting out in front of vehicles using only low-cost ultrasonic sensors. The system embeds a novel prototype system for simultaneous signal collection of raw ultrasonic echoes and automatic labeling sensor data, including RGB images and 3D LiDAR point clouds. During the offline signal processing phase, the 3D positions of pedestrians, detected from the fusion of RGB and LiDAR data, are used to annotate the echo data captured by the ultrasonic sensors. Various models that are constructed by 2D and 1D layers with specially designed attention mechanisms are trained and evaluated. More than 13 hours of synchronized sensor data in urban environments are collected. The trained model achieved an 82.28% detection accuracy on the split test set. The high detection performance of the UPDet system, when embedded in the NVIDIA Xavier platform, further demonstrates the substantial potential of ultrasonic-based sensory perception for enhancing automotive safety.

title: Reconfigurable Varifocal Lens Antenna Based on Plasma Technology
authors: Fatemeh Abbasian, Mohammad-Ali Shameli, Tavakol Pakizeh, Antonio-Daniele Capobianco, Mirko Magarotto
abstract: The growing demand for higher capacity in wireless communication networks has created a critical need for smart devices with dynamic focusing and beam steering capabilities in the 5G mid-band spectrum. Conventional focusing techniques and metasurface-based flat lenses often suffer from static operation and mechanical complexity, restricting their practical use in adaptive platforms. In this work, we propose a reconfigurable reflective lens based on plasma-filled glass tubes designed to operate at 5 GHz. Through full-wave numerical simulations, we demonstrate that selective modulation of plasma density permits continuous focal length tuning from 1.5λ to 3.5λ. Furthermore, by increasing the number of plasma tubes, we achieve dynamic lateral shifts of the focal spot exceeding 2λ. Compared with prior reconfigurable lens approaches in the microwave and millimeter-wave range, the proposed design offers a broader tuning range and introduces lateral reconfigurability, which has not been previously reported. These results highlight the potential of plasma-based flat lenses as a compact, fast, and energy-efficient solution for integration into next-generation wireless communication systems.

title: A Cubic High-Gain DC-DC Converter for Improved Maximum Power Extraction in Partially Shaded PV Systems
authors: Vikash Kumar Raushan, Sankar Peddapati, S.V.K. Naresh
abstract: This paper presents a high-gain cubic DC–DC converter designed for three-phase two-level grid-connected photovoltaic (PV) systems, offering enhanced performance under partial shading conditions. The converter achieves a cubic voltage gain using a single active switch and fewer passive components, with lower input current ripple and reduced voltage stress on semiconductor devices. A comprehensive steady-state and power loss analysis is presented to derive the converter’s high gain and efficiency. Here, a grid-current-based maximum power injection to the grid (MPIG) strategy is used instead of a traditional PV-side maximum power point tracking (MPPT), eliminating the need for PV-side voltage and current sensors. Further, the particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm is applied to track the global peak under partial shading conditions. The effectiveness of the converter and control strategy is demonstrated through experiments on a three-phase PV-grid prototype, tested with different array configurations and shading patterns.
