Experimental results show the proposed converter’s improved peak tracking, stable grid operation, and better performance under partial shading than the conventional boost converter, validating the proposed converter’s suitability for practical PV-grid integration.

title: A Single-Layer Printed Cross-Dipole Antenna With Small Footprint and Ultra-Wide Axial-Ratio Beamwidth
authors: Zhi Yang, Mei Li, Yi Tan, Ming-Chun Tang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A single-layer circularly polarized (CP) printed cross-dipole antenna featuring a compact size and an ultrawide 3-dB axial ratio (AR) beamwidth is presented. To address the severe input resistance reduction in the fundamental series resonant mode induced by ground-plane image currents, the parallel resonant mode of the low-profile printed dipole is utilized. For miniaturization, shorting pins are loaded at the dipole's equivalent electric wall, transforming the parallel resonance into a half-mode operation, and substantially reducing the resonant frequency. Subsequently, near-field resonant parasitic (NFRP) technique is employed to effectively reduce the associated high impedance, facilitating source matching. Owing to the inherent high polarization purity of the printed dipole, CP radiation is readily generated by implementing a fan-shaped cross-dipole radiator with a dimension perturbation on one arm. Benefitting from its specific symmetrical structure, this single-layer antenna achieves an exceptionally wide AR beamwidth. The final design attains a compact footprint of 0.0419λ0<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>×0.042λ0 while providing 3-dB AR beamwidths of 219° and 192° in the xz- and yz-planes, respectively.

title: Mode Sensitive Topological Interface State Dual-Mode Absorber in Hyperbolic Metamaterials Based One-Dimensional Photonic Crystals With Graphene and Gold Layers
authors: Gulzar Ali J, Sanjay Kumar Mohanty, Chittaranjan Nayak
abstract: Dual resonance absorption modes realized by a graphene in one-dimensional photonic crystal heterostructure of all-hyperbolic metamaterial (HMM)-based unit cells terminated with a gold- dielectric bilayer is examined. Resonance frequencies exhibit a distinct angular dependence: they shift upward under transverse electric (TE) polarization and downward under transverse magnetic (TM) polarization as the incidence angle increases from normal incidence up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$89^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula>. This angular response appears almost linear in both TE and TM modes, as visualized through contour plots. The effect is primarily demonstrated in the terahertz (THz) regime using Indium Phosphide (InP) and Tellurium (Te) as the constituent materials of the HMM, which exhibit metallic behavior within this frequency range. The same phenomenon is further verified separately with Indium Antimonide (InSb)/Te and Gallium Arsenide (GaAs)/Te combinations, confirming the robustness of the observed response. These findings emphasize the tunable and polarization-sensitive nature of resonances in hypercrystal systems, underscoring their potential for advanced THz photonic applications.

title: A Novel Nanobiosensor Based on Carbon Nanotube Tunnel Field-Effect Transistor for Low-Power and Label-Free Detection: Design and Quantum Simulation Analysis
authors: Khalil Tamersit, Hocine Bourouba, Abdellah Kouzou
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: In this paper, a novel nanoscale biosensor based on a coaxial-gate carbon nanotube tunneling field-effect transistor (CG-CNTTFET) is proposed and analyzed using quantum simulations. The simulation framework relies on the selfconsistent solution of the Poisson equation coupled with the nonequilibrium Green's function (NEGF) formalism. The nanobiosensor features an open lateral biosensing region located on the drain side. The performance of the proposed ballistic biosensor is evaluated through its transfer characteristics, band diagrams, sensitivity, and subthreshold swing. The results reveal that the biosensor exhibits high sensitivity within a dielectricmodulated TFET configuration featuring open lateral biosensing regions. Moreover, the biomolecule-induced electrostatic variations are shown to produce enhanced sensitivity when the device operates in a steep subthreshold swing regime. Optimizing the CNTTFET subthreshold characteristics can therefore pave the way for the development of ultra-high-sensitivity biosensors with unprecedented detection performance.

title: Redefining Cyber Ecosystems with AI-Driven Threat Detection and Secure Blockchain Infrastructure
authors: Manju D, Rajesh Kumar Varma, Shanu Kuttan Rakesh, S.R. Anand Kumar, Padmini Debbarma, Poorvi Gandham
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Rapid evolution on the side of cyber threats calls for more sophisticated solutions based on the artificial intelligence and blockchain technology. Current security systems are however not scalable, do not provide for real time threat detection and information integrity. The proposed structure of this research is a hybrid AI Blockchain framework that will be used to improve threat detection and data security. The cyber threats are investigated with specialized algorithms like Decision Tree Classifier and all data are securely and tamper proof stored using blockchain technology. The techniques involve smart contracts, user entity and behaviour analysis and its respective anomaly detection. It is this combination which enables the enhancement in the detection accuracy, reduction in the response time as well as the data integrity. The model is validated through real experiments and results show better accuracy with less false positives. The system provides a blockchain emulator called Ganache that can be used to run the local Ethereum environment.

title: Static Hand Gesture Recognition using Score Fusion of CNN based RGB-D Net
authors: Dayananda Kumar N.C, K.V Suresh, Dinesh R
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Hand Gesture Recognition (HGR) involves detection of various hand postures and signs to convey information in non-verbal communication. It is widely used in human computer interaction for machine interface and sign language recognition to aid people with verbal and speech impairments. HGR systems with multimodal data fusion have proved to be more efficient as compared to unimodal systems for real time applications. In this work, RGB and depth multimodal data based score fusion using dilated convolutional neural networks is proposed for static hand gesture recognition. Experiments are conducted on OUHANDS dataset and achieved the accuracy of 88.91% in complex real time scenarios.

title: Coordinated Planning of Distributed Photovoltaic—Energy Storage Systems in Residential Communities Under the Dual-Carbon Targets: A Genetic Algorithm—Based Topology and Capacity Optimization Approach
authors: Zixin Guo, Peng Cui
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Urban residential energy systems face increasing challenges in integrating distributed photovoltaic generation while managing peak demand and grid dependency costs. Existing approaches suffer from three critical limitations: (1) they treat energy storage capacity sizing and network topology design as separate optimization problems, failing to capture their inherent interdependencies; (2) they overlook the spatial economics of shared storage systems, where connection distance penalties can offset cooperative benefits at larger scales; and (3) they employ simplified cost models that neglect temporal price variations and the nonlinear relationship between PV capacity and long-term economic returns. To address these challenges, we propose GenEnergy, a genetic algorithm-based framework for joint optimization of residential energy networks with distributed photovoltaic generation and shared storage infrastructure. Our approach introduces three key innovations corresponding to the identified limitations: (1) a unified chromosome encoding that simultaneously represents network topology and storage capacities, enabling coordinated optimization through specialized crossover and mutation operators; (2) a spatially-aware cost function incorporating distance-dependent connection penalties that reveals optimal deployment scales for shared storage (10-30 households in compact configurations); and (3) a comprehensive long-term cost model integrating time-varying electricity prices, PV installation economics, and storage lifecycle costs. Extensive experiments on the Ausgrid dataset comprising 300 Australian households demonstrate that GenEnergy achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5. 4}-\mathbf{6. 0} {\%}$</tex> cost reductions compared to conventional strategies while discovering interpretable deployment insights: shared storage benefits diminish beyond 30-40 households due to geometric dispersion, and optimal PV sizing (1-1.5 kW per household) balances installation costs against self-consumption gains.

title: Document Routing and Tracking System using Workflow Management Algorithm
authors: Ryan Cuarez, Apolinar Bongcawil, Carolino Tubo, Donna Joy Guiritan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: This study presents the design and development of a Web-Based Document Routing and Tracking System (DRTS) integrated with a workflow management algorithm to enhance document processing efficiency within the Local Government Unit (LGU) of Magallanes, Philippines. The system was developed to automate routing decisions, provide real-time tracking, and prioritize tasks based on predefined workflows. A needs analysis was conducted through consultations and interviews with LGU personnel to assess existing procedures, identify bottlenecks, and gather system requirements. These findings informed the development of workflow models and database structures that ensure accurate document flow and data integrity. By incorporating a workflow management algorithm, the system enables dynamic task routing, strengthens interdepartmental coordination, and enhances monitoring capabilities. The system’s performance was evaluated using established software quality standards, including functional suitability, usability, reliability, and efficiency. Results indicate a High Acceptable rating, demonstrating its effectiveness in improving document management, reducing processing time, and fostering greater accountability within the LGU.

title: An Intelligent Soil Moisture Measurement System Integrating Multi-Antenna Transmission Data
authors: Yuanhua Liu, Yibo Su, Xinliang Niu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Soil moisture monitoring is a critical task in agriculture and ecology, yet traditional single-point measurements struggle to address the challenge of spatially uneven soil moisture distribution. This paper designs an intelligent soil moisture measurement system integrating multi-antenna transmission data, proposing a multi-antenna transmission-reception architecture and data fusion algorithm to achieve large-area, high-precision soil moisture monitoring. The study innovatively employs a multi-source data fusion model combining weighted D-S evidence theory with LSTM, significantly enhancing measurement accuracy in non-uniform scenarios. The system demonstrates excellent environmental adaptability and stability, offering a novel technical solution for precision agriculture and water resource management.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in Financial Fraud Research: A Bibliometric Analysis of Trends and Collaborations
authors: Vandana Mehndiratta, Varuna Gupta, Shilpa Srivastava, Meenu Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: This study conducts a bibliometric analysis of the financial fraud research domain, synthesizing publication metadata to uncover collaboration structures, thematic hotspots, and temporal trends. The research utilizes the bibliometric tool Vos viewer to retrieve the insights on the various aspects of financial fraud detection using the emerging technologies. The study analyses the integration of AI and machine learning techniques on the detection of financial fraud. Using co-authorship networks, we identify influential authors and collaboration clusters, while keyword frequency and co-occurrence patterns reveal core topics and emerging fronts such as anomaly detection, machine learning, insider trading, and regulatory analytics. Temporal profiling of publications highlights growth phases and shifts in emphasis across years and venues. The analysis provides an integrated view of the field’s intellectual structure, enabling scholars and practitioners to locate key contributors, benchmark thematic coverage, and identify gaps for future inquiry. Results offer actionable insights for forming research collaborations, prioritizing topics, and designing evidence- based strategies against financial fraud.

title: CVAF-TGON: Cross-View Attention Fusion and Temporal Graph Occupancy Networks for Vision-Based Smart Parking
authors: Nareddy Koushik Reddy, Majjari Sudhakar, R. Karthikeyan, Prakash Kumar Sarangi, M. A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Intelligent transportation systems depend on effective parking management, but current solutions based on per-slot magnetic or ultrasonic sensors are still expensive, rigid, and unable to offer semantic or predictive insights. This research presents CVAF-TGON, a novel vision-driven architecture that combines a Temporal Graph Occupancy Network (TGON) with Cross-View Attention Fusion (CVAF) to overcome these drawbacks. While the TGON models spatiotemporal connections between slots and vehicles to enable robust prediction and anomaly detection, the CVAF module combines multi-camera detections into a single bird’s-eye view, eliminating occlusions and improving slot-level precision. The framework ensures scalability and privacy by operating on edge devices with federated learning and sim-to-real domain adaptation. In comparison to sensor-based baselines, experimental evaluation on multi-lot datasets shows that occupancy accuracy surpasses 95%, five-minute forecast accuracy surpasses 88%, and anomaly detection AUC surpasses 0.90, all while lowering hardware and maintenance costs.

title: Assessing the Impact of the Tamil Nadu Shops and Establishments Act, 1947 on Retail Workers' Rights in Hypermarkets
authors: R Revathy, Petikam Sailaja, B. Jeyaprabha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The Tamil Nadu Shops and businesses Act, 1947 is the basis on which the working conditions of workers in commercial business, including the hypermarket, are controlled. In this study, the impact of the Act on the rights of the retailers is considered critically in the context of a fast-developing industry of hypermarkets in Tamil Nadu. The research looks at the effectiveness of the Act in defending the rights of employees in terms of working hours, breaks, weekly holidays, overtime payment, and safeguarding of women and young employees. It achieves this through field interviews, case studies and legal analysis. The results have shown significant lapses in enforcement and awareness of legislation particularly in contractual and part-time workers despite the progressive aim of the legislation. Hypermarkets are prone to bypass or loosen the compliance norms due to the desire to make profit and operate with efficiency, which leads to overworking, job insecurity, and non-grievance redress. The paper also highlights the low power of trade unions within the organised retail industry and the failures in inspections. The Act needs to change to capture the current labour relations in the retail sector, there must be increased awareness of workers, and it needs to have more regulation. The research contributes to the discussion about social justice and labour rights within the developing service economy in India through the evaluation of the interaction between laws and workplace reality.

title: Data-Driven Learning in Physical Education: The Role of Arm Muscle Strength and Digital Feedback in Handball Jump Shot Performance
authors: Amran, Wawan Sundawan Suherman, Ali Satia Graha, Asmuddin, Ade Gusti Ningsih
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This study investigates how data-driven learning and digital feedback can enhance the understanding and improvement of sport-specific skills in physical education, focusing on the relationship between arm muscle strength and jump-shot performance in handball. A quantitative correlational approach was employed with 32 male university athletes from Halu Oleo University. Arm muscle strength was assessed using the 30 -second push-up test, and jump-shot performance was measured through a 60 -second accuracy test, with digital video recordings analyzed using motion-analysis software to provide real-time feedback. The results revealed a strong, significant correlation between arm muscle strength and jump-shot success (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.67>r$</tex>-table=0.349, p<0.05), indicating that 45% of performance variance is explained by upper-body strength. Conceptually, this research highlights how digital performance data and feedback loops can support technology-enhanced learning in sports education by enabling more precise skill evaluation and personalized training.

title: A Novel AI-Driven Framework for Robust Channel Estimation in Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface- Aided Wireless Networks
authors: Keerthna G, B. Lavaraju, C. Jayamala
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) are emerging as a transformative paradigm in next-generation wireless networks, enabling programmable control of electromagnetic waves for enhanced signal propagation. Channel estimation in RIS-aided networks is essential but difficult because the cascaded channels are high dimensional and RIS elements are passive. In this paper, RISNet-CE, a new AI-assisted channel estimation framework, has been proposed, which has the capability of incorporating the Transformer network and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network so as to obtain the spatial and sequential dependency of the RIS-based system. It is a supervised learning model that maximizes a custom designed loss function (Normalized Mean Squared Error (NMSE) to be more specific) and refined through attention-based feature integration and residual learning to ensure optimal robustness. RISNet-CE model demonstrates better performance when compared with previous and traditional approaches used in deep learning. It achieves 0.0845 at 35 dB SNR, which is better than MMSE (0.2409), RIS-SRNet (0.1012), and DeepCE (0.1609). Also, a reinforcement learning agent based on PPO is incorporated to enable dynamic reconfiguration of RIS phases, which offers, up to 9.01 dB of SNR gains in mmWave LoS conditions. The framework keeps its MAPE under 17.1% with 160 RIS components and can run on low-latency inference and memory-footprint without needing high-performance computing. RISNet-CE shows promising efficiency, versatility and scaling, which has been confirmed in extensive simulations of different propagation settings, and is a large step toward automated and smart RIS-based wireless systems.

title: CA-AMFA: Context-Aware Adaptive Multi-Factor Authentication for IoT-Based Household Security Systems
authors: Vatsal Jha, Yokesh Babu S
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) devices in home environments present unique security challenges that traditional authentication methods fail to address effectively. This paper presents Context-Aware Adaptive Multi-Factor Authentication (CA-AMFA), an innovative edge-based security system that dynamically adjusts authentication demands based on real-time risk analysis. The proposed approach uses an integrated risk model and leverages three contextual bandit algorithms—Upper Confidence Bound (UCB), Thompson Sampling, and Epsilon-Greedy—to dynamically update risk factor weights through continuous learning mechanisms. The hardware implementation on Raspberry Pi demonstrates the system's relevance to resourceconstrained environments. Through rigorous evaluation across a variety of scenarios, it is demonstrated that the bandit-based approaches significantly outperform fixed-weight schemes, lowering false acceptance rates by up to 44.75% and lowering unnecessary authentication friction by up to 20.18%, while maintaining strong security guarantees. The UCB algorithm demonstrated the best performance, achieving perfect accuracy in high-risk scenarios where traditional approaches failed. The opensource deployment and reproducible evaluation platform make CA-AMFA a computationally efficient solution that well balances security and usability for next-generation IoT authentication systems.

title: Speech Emotion Recognition: A Human-Centric Framework with Enhanced Data Augmentation and Lightweight CNN
authors: Shreyansh Shakya, Aloukik Das, Preetam Suman, Sasmita Padhy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) has significant potential to enhance human-computer interaction, particularly in real-time applications. However, deploying SER models on resource-constrained edge devices necessitates efficient and compact solutions. This paper proposes a lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) framework enhanced with targeted audio augmentation techniques and optimized feature representation. The preprocessing pipeline incorporates controlled pitch shifting, time stretching, and Gaussian noise injection to improve data diversity. Thirteen Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs), along with their first and second-order derivatives, are extracted and subsequently reduced using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to form 32 by 32 feature maps. Trained on the Ryerson Audio-Visual Database of Emotional Speech and Song (RAVDESS) dataset, the proposed model achieves 72 percent of classification accuracy, demonstrating a favorable trade-off between performance and computational efficiency. Comparative evaluations against hybrid and attention-based architectures highlight the model's suitability for edge deployment. Future work includes integrating lightweight attention mechanisms and extending validation across more diverse emotion datasets.

title: Exploring Gen Z's Adoption of AI-Based Learning Tools: Evidence from Can Tho, Vietnam
authors: Thai Anh Vu Nguyen
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: The study looks at how students in Vietnam decide whether to use AI tools in their learning. Data were collected from 470 university students in Can Tho through a structured survey. The model was based on well-known technology adoption theories, focusing on five possible influences: effort expectancy, performance expectancy, perceived usefulness, social influence, and perceived trust. Analysis with SmartPLS 3.0 shows that usefulness, ease of use, and expected performance are the main drivers of willingness to adopt AI. In contrast, trust and social influence were not found to play an important role. These results suggest that students pay closer attention to whether AI tools save time, improve their study outcomes, and are easy to handle. The findings provide practical insights for universities and educators seeking to promote the adoption of AI in higher education in a sustainable way.

title: Enhancing the Hail Resistance of Photovoltaic Modules Through Glass Thickness Optimization and Nanocoatings
authors: Yuanbiao Deng, Vadim Davydov, Aleksandr Bobyl
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: Hailimpact is one of the main problems that reduces the performance and lifetime of photovoltaic (PV) modules, especially in cold or severe climates. This study examines how hail causes damage and how the damage can be reduced by using thicker glass and protective nanocoatings. Our field study in Saint Petersburg and review of existing research show that small cracks in the front glass can lower power output by up to 20%, while further damage from dust, water, and ice can reduce it by more than 50%. Tests also show that PV modules with 4 mm glass are much stronger and can keep normal operation after hail impact. In addition, hydrophobic coatings help prevent snow accumulation and make the panel easier to clean, though they lose their effect after long outdoor use. The results show that combining stronger glass with improved surface coatings can make PV modules more reliable and longer lasting in hail-prone and cold regions.

title: A Machine Learning Approach for Web Service Autoscaling: EMD-LSTM and Attention Mechanism in Cloud Computing
authors: Lendy Pradhana Hartono, Royyana Muslim Ijtihadie, Tohari Ahmad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Traditional reactive autoscalers respond only after resource exhaustion occurs, causing SLA violations and oscillating scaling behaviour. This paper proposes a proactive autoscaling system that combines Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD), LSTM networks, and attention mechanisms to predict web service traffic and provision resources. The approach decomposes non-stationary RPS patterns into 7 Intrinsic Mode Functions, predicts each component using independent LSTM models, and weights predictions using multi-head attention to emphasise informative signals. Evaluation on real e-commerce traffic demonstrates that EMD-LSTM achieves an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.991, compared with 0.223 for the baseline LSTM. Production deployment in Kubernetes environments shows the system processed 7.67× more requests while consuming only 72.7% fewer resources, achieving a 28x improvement in resource efficiency. The work shows that combining accurate signal decomposition, temporal learning, and attention-based weighting simultaneously achieves both SLA compliance and cost efficiency, while reducing the lost request ratio from 87.17% to 1.63%.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Skin Disease Classification Using Adaptive Segmentation and Dual-Stage EfficientNet
authors: Susmita Panda, Debashish Kar, Sambandh Bhusan Dhal, Krishna Chaitanya Gadepally, Stavros Kalafatis
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Skin lesion classification from dermoscopic images remains challenging due to severe class imbalance and confounding artifacts. We present a comprehensive deep learning pipeline that synergistically combines morphological segmentation, partial oversampling, and two-phase transfer learning to address these challenges. Our approach employs adaptive threshold-based preprocessing to isolate lesions from hair and background artifacts, strategic partial oversampling to balance minority classes without overfitting, and a dual-stage EfficientNet-B3 training strategy-first freezing the backbone for stability, then finetuning with focal loss for enhanced minority class detection. Evaluated on HAM10000 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0, 0 1 5}$</tex> images, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7}$</tex> classes) using patientlevel data splitting, our method achieves 82.3% accuracy, 65.0% balanced accuracy, and 66.9% macro F1-score. A comprehensive ablation study reveals that two-phase training contributes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{+} \mathbf{3. 6} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> accuracy individually, while the complete pipeline improves balanced accuracy by 13.5 percentage points over baseline. Notably, melanoma F1-score improves from 0.509 to 0.586, demonstrating significant enhancement in detecting this critical malignancy. Our approach offers a practical, reproducible solution for automated dermoscopic screening with strong performance on minority classes.

title: IoT Based Flood Monitoring and Control System
authors: Thakur Ankit Krishna Kumar, Abhishek Kumar, Ankit Singh, Gaurav Pal, Manish Pal, Mahima Tyagi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This paper presents an IoT-based flood monitoring and control system designed to provide real-time flood detection, early warning systems, and management of flood-prone areas. This system is used by a network of sensors deployed to location strategically to measure parameters such as water levels, rainfall, and soil moisture. These sensors are connected to the center of the IoT platform, which continuously monitors the data and processes it for a potential flood risk. When disapproved thresholds are met, the system triggers alerts to the local authorities and the public via SMS or mobile applications. In addition, the system can be activated to automate flood control measures, such as opening floodgates or diverting water to prevent further damage. The architecture is designed for scalability, reliability, and cost-effectiveness, making it suitable for both urban and rural areas flood management applications. The system operates with minimal human intervention and does not rely on complex AI algorithms, focusing instead on straightforward data collection, threshold-based alerting, and simple control mechanisms. This makes it highly accessible and easy to implement, providing a practical solution for flood risk mitigation

title: A Reputation-Aware Price Adjustment Mechanism for Load Balancing in Cloud Computing
authors: Lilia Chourou Gherir, Ahmed Elleuch, Mohamed Jemni
publish: 2025 7th International Communication Engineering and Cloud Computing Conference (CECCC)
abstract: Dynamic price adjustment plays a central role in regulating supply and demand in cloud computing environments. We introduce a pricing mechanism based on a trial-and-error model, supervised by an auctioneer, and initially relying on the assumption of honest agents. However, in practice, some agents adopt opportunistic behaviors that may distort price formation and compromise the efficiency of resource allocation. To increase the resilience of the system against such manipulations, we integrate a reputation mechanism designed to penalize malicious actors. Experimental results show that this approach significantly improves the robustness of the pricing process, by limiting imbalances and protecting honest agents from both malicious suppliers and customers. This coupling between pricing and reputation thus promotes a fairer and more efficient allocation of resources.

title: Permissioned Blockchain-Enabled Decentralized Streaming for Adaptive QoS in 6G Networks
authors: J. Chandra Priya, G. Nanthakumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: With the emergence of 6G networks, there is a need for a highly reliable and secure infrastructure to support the demand for fast and seamless communication services. In this paper, we propose a customized blockchain architecture as a solution, taking advantage of its enhanced security features, traceability, and pseudo-transparency. Our proposed architecture aims to improve Quality of Service (QoS) in 6G networks by leveraging a permissioned blockchain. It ensures security and scalability to meet the future demands of high-speed and low-latency communication. The salient feature of Decentralized Adaptive Quality Streaming (DAQs)is the use of a permissioned blockchain, which provides inherent accessibility by adding an additional layer to multimedia streaming, thereby preventing unauthorized tampering. The chaincodes enforce service level agreements (SLAs) between content providers, network operators, and end-users. These self-executing contracts automatically enforce predefined QoS parameters, ensuring that the agreed-upon service standards are met. This improves the integrity and authenticity of transmitted content, enhancing the overall user experience. In our experiments, we observed that this architecture achieved an impressive 88% improvement in overall response time compared to the standard architecture. The architecture provides a novel and robust solution for improving QoS in 6G networks through its customized blockchain infrastructure.

title: Capacitive Energy Harvesting from the Electric Field of Overhead Power Lines
authors: Jussi Keskikuru, Tuomas Rauta, Joel Haverinen, Qudrat Ullah, Pekka Ruuskanen
abstract: This article introduces a capacitive energy harvester that harvests energy from the electric field of an overhead power line through a low-potential electrode. In this method, the electric field between the power line and the ground generates a displacement current, which is converted into usable DC energy after rectification and storage stages. Experiments were conducted at line-to-ground voltages of $20 \mathrm{kV}, 33 \mathrm{kV}$, and 63.5 kV, which showed that the charging speed of the output capacitor directly depends on the line voltage. The results confirm the trend that as the line voltage increases, the electric field strength and energy harvesting ability also improve. This research demonstrates that capacitive energy harvesting from the electric field of power lines is a reliable and cost-effective method that can effectively provide power to monitoring devices, wireless sensors, and IoT devices in smart grids applications.

title: PSO Optimized Wireless Solar Charger for Electric Vehicles
authors: Snigdha Chaturvedi, Aditya Kumar, Ankush Yadav, Aditya Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The shift toward electric vehicles (EVs) is essential for reducing carbon emissions and addressing climate change. Nonetheless, the broad adoption of EVs encounters obstacles, especially in creating an effective and sustainable charging network. This paper introduces an innovative solution that combines renewable energy with wireless technology to transform EV charging. Our strategy focuses on integrating solar-powered wireless charging technology, representing a significant shift from traditional charging methods. Solar panels capture sunlight and convert it into electrical energy, which is then stored in batteries for use when needed. This renewable energy source promotes sustainability and lessens reliance on non-renewable power grids. The key innovation is the wireless power transmission system, which employs magnetic induction between transmitter and receiver coils. This approach removes the necessity for physical connectors or cables, providing a smooth and convenient experience. Our system utilizes wireless technology to improve access to charging stations and minimize the wear and tear that comes with traditional plug-in methods. Furthermore, our solution tackles significant issues within the EV charging infrastructure. By harnessing solar energy, we greatly lessen the environmental impact linked to conventional charging techniques, thereby supporting a cleaner and more sustainable transportation ecosystem. Additionally, our system's reliability is bolstered as it functions independently of the grid, ensuring continuous charging services even in remote or off-grid areas. We are confident that our approach has great potential to accelerate the adoption of EVs and promote a more environmentally aware future.

title: Smart Farming Techniques in Modern Agriculture Using Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
authors: Sara Jamil, Devansh Paurya, Geetanjali Pandey
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The burning evolution of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) has changed the agriculture systems and offered the opportunity to employ data-driven and automated solutions in the sphere of agriculture. Smart Agricultural practices rely on IoT devices, computer vision and predictive analytics which can be applied to enhance productivity of crops, its resources as well as sustainability. The most popular AI models that have been applied in the detection of crop diseases, soil quality and yield prediction include Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Decision Trees. The collaboration of the AI and the Internet of Things (IoT) technologies may lead to the real-time monitoring of such environmental characteristics as humidity, temperature, and soil moisture, developing an effective irrigation and fertilizer regulation. These AI-based systems have been found to have high impact concerning reducing wastages of resources, electricity maximization and early warning of stressful plant conditions [1]-[5]. Moreover, they are also implementing federated and edge learning systems to ensure the privacy of information in large-scale agriculture and scalability [7], [13] also. Automation, data analytics and smart sensing may all empower farmers to possess the appropriate instruments to achieve resiliency and sustainable farming practices in their decisions. In the paper, the author is going to review and analyze the current trends, technologies, and challenges associated with the application of AI and ML in smart agriculture in the light of its efficiency in enhancing productivity, sustainability, and flexibility in the rural setting.

title: GNN-XGBoost Fusion: Improving Chronic Disease Risk Prediction through Graph-Structured Clinical Representations
authors: Killada Sai Kumar, Vanmathi C, Mangayarkarasi R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This experimented study proposes a hybrid graph-based classification framework that integrates a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) with an Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGB) classifier for multiple disease prediction. A similarity graph is constructed from a set of clinical, lifestyle, and cognitive features obtained from a public repository, Each sample represented as a node and edges represents neighborhood relationships through a normalized adjacency matrix. The GCN uses the graph structure to learn low-dimensional node embeddings that jointly capture individual features and inter-sample relationships, especially those related with stage-wise similarities. These learned embeddings are subsequently used as input to an XGBoost classifier to predict the target labels. The dataset is divided into training and testing sets using stratified sampling to preserve class distributions. Model performance is evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed GCN-XGBoost framework effectively models complex feature interactions and relational dependencies, providing an efficient and robust approach for stage-aware disease classification.

title: Secure Access Control System Using Combinational Logic and Arduino-Based Monitoring
authors: Archana Sharma, Anurag Singh, Nita Sahu, Leesha Chandrakar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: This work introduces an innovative password protection system that combines microcontroller-based control, transistor power supply, and logic gate-based verification. The 4-bit password input is accepted using physical switches, with the valid password kept in the Arduino. A user-designed XOR, NOT, and AND gate-based logic circuit compares the input from the switch to the stored password. Comparison of the input result is fed into the Arduino, which manages password attempts and system access. When the password is entered correctly, the Arduino activates a transistor to enable voltage flow to a connected load such as a temperature sensor. In the case of incorrect password entry, the system goes into lockout mode, deactivating the transistor and preventing voltage flow to the device indefinitely. This hybrid solution offers a practical, tamper-resistant embedded solution that merges high-speed logic-level password verification, programmable control, and physical device protection via transistor switching. We have also incorporated a voice feedback feature in the system with Arduino and PyCharm to improve its functionality.

title: Improving Fast Project Delivery using Decision Support Vector Machine with ACO approaches in SAP
authors: Sravan Katragadda
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: One of the most critical issues concerning enterprise resource planning systems like SAP is fast project delivery, where the correct risk forecasting, optimal scheduling, and resource allocation support the timely execution. The earlier methods are generally not effective in selecting features, and in prediction and judgment making are unscalable, so pathetic. This research proposes a hybrid model combining the Decision Support Vector Machine (DSVM) with Ant Colony Optimization (ACO)-based Feature Selection (ACO-FS) to improve the quality of project delivery. The workflow starts with gathering project data from SAP modules, including resource consumption, budget variances, vendor lead times, and risk logs. The resulting data undergoes Z-Score Normalizationto achieve a standard scale across all attributes. Afterward, ACO-FS is used to trim down the most valuable and non-redundant features, effectively downsizing the dimensions without losing information important to project delivery outcomes. The refined feature set is then categorized using DSVM, which classifies the project’s risk categories. Correspondingly, the ACO algorithm is conducive to optimal resource allocation and task scheduling to reduce bottlenecks. The usefulness of the suggested framework is evaluated based on performance measures such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and computational efficiency. Generally, the integration of DSVM and ACO-FS provides a smart and reasonable decision support system capable of achieving significant improvements in the speed of project execution and asset use, and in business agility in dynamic business structures.

title: Explainable Machine Learning Models for Alzheimer’s Diagnosis: Integrating Neuroimaging, Biomarkers, and Clinical Features for Improved Trustworthiness
authors: Kajol Kathuria, Nirmal Kumar Agarwal, Vikas Singhal, Urvashi Gupta, Smriti Sachan, Birendra Kumar Saraswat
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Many types of dementia exist, but Alzheimer’s Disease (AD) is biggest contributor to dementia worldwide. As such, it is essential that we accurately identify AD as soon as possible as possible in order to properly treat and plan for care. At present, traditional methods of diagnosing patients for AD primarily include neuroimaging and clinical assessments, both of which often use subjective judgement to reach a diagnosis and are likely to be low in sensitivity during early AD. DL methods may be an accurate way to predict early onset AD however, due to their lack of interpretability, they have not received much clinical success. This work addresses this issue by providing an explainable multimodal DL framework for predicting the early onset of AD using MRIs, PET scans, and clinical biomarkers. The authors use CNN with an attention fusion mechanism to extract and combine complementary information from multiple modalities. SHAP and Grad CAM techniques were used to increase the interpretability of the multiple DL networks, which allow the transparency of feature and brain region identification. Experimental results using benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework produces comparable classification accuracy to existing methods and provides meaningful clinical insights. Furthermore, the paradigm that has been suggested strikes a compromise between generating predictive performance and interpretability; thus, the proposed framework supports trustworthy and evidence-based decisions, indicating its potential for reaching deployment in clinical settings.

title: IoT and AI-Based Predictive Maintenance for Hybrid Vehicles in Urban Transport Systems
authors: M. Suseela Grace Padma, Karthick R, Srinivasulu Sirisala, Eswara Prasath Natarajan, Dilip Moyal, Palanivel Kandappagoundar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Approaches for Guaranteeing Timely and Reliable Autonomous Transport from Heterogeneous Electric Vehicle Fleets on Urban Roads with Hybrid and Non-hybrid Capabilities. The rapid integration of hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs) in urban public transport leads to new challenges related to their availability, safety, and efficient operation. In this article, a three-layered IoT and AI-based framework for predictive maintenance of hybrid vehicles is presented by combining on-board perception sensors and delay-sensitive communication protocols with cloud-based deep learning models. In this work, a stacked Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) autoencoder is used to detect anomalies with the time series sensor data while fault categories such as battery overheating, brake system degradation, and inverter failure are identified by a Random Forest classifier. The anomaly detection trigger was also updated to a dynamic threshold using Extreme Value Theory (EVT) to be more robust and reduce false alarms. In addition, the fuzzy logic decision engine offers actionable information such as Remaining Useful Life (RUL) and maintenance urgency. Experimental validation with 50 hybrid buses and a one-year real-world dataset shows that our solution can achieve up to 37% of unplanned downtime reduction, leading to a cost saving of more than 18%, validating the scalability and applicability of the proposed approach on a large scale for intelligent urban transport systems.

title: Smart Grids in the Era of Digital Transformation: A Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Nikita Bhati, Ruchi Jain Garg
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Technology has brought with it countless dynamics of attaining the power to change in accordance with the ever-changing environment in this era, and no organization in the world is immune to this force. Digital transformation along with smart grid technology constitute a revolution to create efficient, resilient, and sustainable energy systems that power every society. This article applies bibliometric analysis to investigate the evolution of literature on smart grid and digital transformation from the information published in the major databases. The research analyses existing researches using performance measures, co-authorship networks, and keyword networks. The findings allow the reader to follow the overlap between digital innovation and grid modernization in key aspects such as the Internet of Things (IoT), artificial intelligence, and integration of renewable energy sources. Furthermore, the paper contributes to the deepening of our knowledge on the increased pace of smart grids research development in the framework of the digital transformation and provides the systemic description of the knowledge development. The paper will introduce a new approach to the role of digital technologies to the sustainable energy system by uniting two previously distinct areas, which has been demonstrated by bibliometric mapping.

title: Interpretability-Driven Dual-Disease Detection System for Cardiovascular and Vector-Borne Illnesses using Machine Learning
authors: Saloni Sikriwal, Nkenglefack Anyike Megan Williams, Kadam Pathak, Avinash Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Timely and reliable diagnosis of serious illnesses is a critical need in modern healthcare, particularly in regions where medical resources are limited and the number of patients is high. Delays or errors in diagnosis can lead to severe health complications, increased mortality rates, and greater strain on healthcare systems. To address these challenges, this paper presents a machine learning framework that emphasizes interpretability, designed to predict three significant medical conditions: Dengue fever, Pneumonia, and the risk of a Heart Attack. For the prediction of Dengue and Heart Attack, structured data from clinical records is used, and a detailed preprocessing method is applied. This method includes managing missing data, converting categorical variables into numerical form, and normalizing the features. Multiple models, such as Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machines, Random Forest, and XGBoost, were trained and evaluated, with ensemble methods proving to be the most effective. In the case of Pneumonia detection, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is employed to analyze chest X-ray images. The images are enhanced using various data augmentation techniques, such as rotation, flipping, and zooming, to make the model more resilient to variations in imaging conditions. The results from real-world datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. Random Forest achieved an accuracy of 95.4% in detecting Dengue, an augmented CNN achieved 94.3% accuracy in detecting Pneumonia, and Random Forest achieved 90.8% accuracy in predicting Heart Attacks. These findings confirm that ensemble learning is effective for structured data, while deep learning models are more suitable for image-based diagnoses.

title: Enhancing Adversarial Malware Generation with GANs and Large Language Models
authors: Devnath N S, B. Hemanth Kumar, Adarsh Sasikumar, Devi Rajeev
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Malware evasion is an important field of study since it helps in designing malware detectors that resist real-world attacks and analyzing weakness in malware defenses. Most of the time malware authors have no direct access to the structure and parameters to the model parameters, so they perform black box attacks. To evade detection, adversaries often leverage deep learning techniques like Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to evade malware detection. This paper proposes a GAN based framework for generating adversarial malware, and also utilizes large language models (LLMs) to test its potential to influence evasion. Initially, the malware generated by the baseline GAN reduced the detection rate from 79.6% to 7.1% for the random-forest model. By integrating LLMs, the detection rate dropped to 0.8%. This indicates how LLMs can enhance adversarial malware generation, and therefore more defensive implications must be taken into consideration when constructing malware detectors.

title: Energy and Migration Cost Minimization in Virtual Network Embedding for Disaggregated Cloud Data Centers
authors: Jiahe Xu, Chao Guo, Xinyu Wang, Moshe Zukerman
publish: 2025 7th International Communication Engineering and Cloud Computing Conference (CECCC)
abstract: Cloud data centers are evolving toward a disaggregated architecture to achieve higher efficiency in handling unprecedented large-scale workloads, particularly acceleratorassisted workloads such as AI training and inference. The core idea of this architecture is to decouple resources-including CPUs, memory, NICs, and accelerators-that were traditionally integrated within standalone servers, and reorganize them into shared resource pools. In this work, we study the virtual network embedding problem, which has been extensively explored in traditional server-based data centers, within the context of disaggregated data centers. Specifically, we address the consolidation of ongoing virtual network requests by migrating them to fewer nodes and links, thereby improving overall resource efficiency. We explicitly account for migration costs in the optimization process and aim to minimize them. Finally, we present Pareto fronts that characterize the trade-offs between power consumption and migration cost.

title: Jester-Informed Synthetic Data Generation for TPU-Accelerated Binary Gesture Recognition
authors: Sanjana T G, Spandana S, Shibasish Banerjee, Priyanka Desai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This work presents a binary gesture recognition system that overcomes the usual real-world data requirements by training only on synthetic videos based on the Jester dataset. Instead of relying on large annotated video datasets, the motion patterns of the 20BN-Jester dataset are analyzed to produce parametrically controlled swipe-left gesture sequences based on real-world human motion statistics. A specially designed 3D convolutional neural network with 2.16 million parameters is trained on the synthetic data using Google TPU v3-8 hardware. The synthetic data generation process simulates hand trajectories, temporal patterns, and real-world movement variations as seen in real Jester videos, allowing for a strong correspondence between synthetic and real-world gesture behaviors. On synthetic validation tasks, the network converges in the first training iteration and reaches full validation accuracy. On a held-out set of 23 real Jester swipe-left videos, the same network demonstrates strong synthetic-to-real transfer with excellent generalization performance. The proposed system overcomes privacy concerns in data collection, supports balanced training data, and improves computational efficiency compared to traditional real-data systems. A lightweight supplementary implementation further verifies strong convergence performance in resource-constrained hardware settings.

title: Residual Attention UNet with Pyramid Pooling for Landslide Mapping and Comparative Study of UNet Models on Multispectral Data
authors: Kathiroli Raja, Selvam R, Sandeep Raman R, Kavinkumar N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Landslide detection in an expedited and accurate way is crucial in disaster management. RAUNeP (Residual Attention U-Net with Pyramid Pooling) is a hybrid deep-learning approach for accurate remote landslide detection and mapping. RAUNeP is a U-Net architecture with residual blocks integrated with attention mechanism and pyramid pooling in the pooling layer. The dataset used for training is multi-spectral and terrain composite data from Landslide4Sense dataset. The results indicate that the proposed RAUNeP significantly improves the detection of areas affected by landslides by reducing false negatives. This improvement is attributed to the ability of the attention mechanism to focus on landslide sites and the improved global-local feature extraction by the pyramid pooling along with the focal Tversky loss. While the U-Net and the Residual Attention U-Net model with attention nodes scored the F1 score of 0.72 and 0.74 and the recall of 0.70 and 0.75, the proposed RAUNeP scores a recall and F1-score of 0.808 and 0.75 respectively. We also infer the inverse relation between vegetation (NDVI) and the occurrence of landslides

title: The Dual Role of Artificial Intelligence and LLM in Cybersecurity
authors: Leandros Maglaras, Vassilis C. Gerogiannis, Mohamed Amine Ferrag, Alexios Lekidis, Abderrahmane Lakas, Naghmeh Moradpoor
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Large Language Models (LLMs) are transforming the cybersecurity landscape, taking a dual role as both enablers of defense. This paper explores this duality by distinguishing between AI for Security, where intelligent models enhance threat analysis, intrusion detection, and automated mitigation, and Security for AI, which focuses on securing models themselves against adversarial manipulation, data leakage, and prompt-based exploitation. To empirically evaluate the capabilities of modern LLMs in cybersecurity, we conducted extensive experiments using the newly developed CyberMetric dataset, which assesses cybersecurity knowledge across multiple domains such as cryptography, network defense, and software vulnerabilities. Our evaluation of twenty state-of-the-art LLMs—including GPT-5 Chat, Grok 4 Fast, Qwen3 235B, Gemini 2.5 Flash, and DeepSeek V3.2—demonstrates that the 2025 generation of models achieves near-human performance, with GPT-5 Chat reaching 98.75% accuracy and several open-source models performing within a 2-3% margin. These results highlight the growing maturity of open LLM ecosystems for cybersecurity applications and confirm that modern LLMs are becoming reliable instruments for cyber defense, capable of supporting automated threat analysis, vulnerability assessment, and compliance verification, while also emphasizing the need to ensure their safety, transparency, and resilience against misuse.

title: Deep Learning in Sign Language: A Comprehensive Systematic Review of Recent Progress
authors: Ashwini Nikhil Shinde, Rajeswari Kannan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Advances in recent past years studies focusing on the language of sign recognition and translation have grown, which in turn has improved access to interaction with people who have hearing impairments. We present a survey of recent work in the field and we also report current research trends and the methods are primarily used. We did a systematic literature review which included 32 research articles published between 2019 and 2023 which we looked at in detail. The review is mainly of approaches based on deep neural networks used for SL recognition and translation. We look at what the main models, data sets and support technologies are which we also look at in terms of their pros and cons. We found out that video-based sign to text translation is a key element in the enablement of continuous and effective communication.

title: Enhancing Pre-Eclampsia Screening Through Machine Learning and Multi-Source Data Integration
authors: Sweety Ashok Wanave, Gauri M. Dhopavkar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Pre-eclampsia remains one of the most severe pregnancy complications, tormenting the maternal and neonatal outcomes all over the world. In order to mitigate this risk, clinicians need risk-stratification instruments that are not only rapid but also evidence-based. This paper presents a predictive framework in modules that would combine clinical, biochemical, and biosensor data into a single and clear risk estimate. It consists of five modules which are inter-relational as follows: (1) acquisition and harmonization of multi-source data, (2) preprocessing and feature construction, (3) supervised machine-learning modelling, (4) clinical risk stratification and decision support, and (5) independent validation and performance monitoring. The Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting (XG Boost) models are crossvalidated and hyper-optimized to ensure strong discrimination and calibration. Individual risk type categories are thus created and incorporated to the clinician dashboards effortlessly and the patients are given brief educational information. The proven system helps to detect high-risk pregnancies at early stages and implement timely interventions, including low-dose aspirin and enhanced monitoring. Demonstrating that machine learning can be directly introduced into obstetric practice, the framework shows that a data-driven approach can offer a practical mechanism to improve maternal safety and the initial diagnosis of pre-eclampsia through the synthesis of clinical records, laboratory data, and biosensor inputs.

title: Design and Implementation of a Deep Learning Embedded Cryptosystem for IoT Applications
authors: Muhammad Ismail, Daniel Tadros, Mahdi Madani, El-Bay Bourennane
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)
abstract: Securing communications in the Internet of Things (IoT) is a critical challenge, particularly for resource-constrained devices operating on local networks. This paper presents a comprehensive cryptosystem for text-based message transmission between an IoT device and a connected server. We propose a deep learning embedded cryptosystem that integrates several advanced security techniques. Our system leverages the DiffieHellman (DH) key exchange to securely establish a shared secret between the server and devices, which is then used with Advanced Encryption Standard in Galois/Counter Mode (AESGCM) encryption scheme to safeguard messages. JSON Web Token (JWT)-based authentication ensures that only devices with valid credentials can access the system. At the same time, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based machine learning model further analyzes device-specific logs to classify devices as trusted or malicious. Qt-based applications on both the IoT device and the server facilitate real-time interaction, monitoring, and control. Experimental results demonstrate key exchange times of 150 to 200 ms, encryption/decryption latencies of approximately 50 ms per message, and JWT verification within 20 ms, while the CNN model achieves over 90 % classification accuracy, with precision, recall, and F 1 -scores of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$95.2 \%, 94.7 \%$</tex>, and 94.9 %, respectively. This integrated solution not only provides robust security but also lays the groundwork for future real-time anomaly detection and scalable IoT deployments.

title: Ray Tracing and Full-Wave Simulation Integrated Channel Simulation Method
authors: Yixiao Li, Fei Wang, Le Zhang
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: The traditional statistical channel model has significant limitations, as it can only handle regular tunnels, fails to consider the details of tunnel walls, and does not account for the impact of antenna deployment environments. On the other hand, the physics-based channel model based on ray tracing still has limitations, such as ignoring the influence of scatterers when the antenna is located in the induction field or near-field region, and not supporting high-precision simulations that consider the shielding effect of the train car. This paper proposes a simulation method that integrates ray tracing with full-wave simulation. For the antenna installation environment and the interior of the train, a full-wave simulation method is employed. For the tunnel environment outside the train, the ray tracing method is used. This approach allows for precise full-process evaluation and enhances the end-to-end transmission performance within the channel. Test results have verified the feasibility and accuracy of this method.

title: Comparative Analysis of Benchmarks in Machine Learning and Deep Learning
authors: Anderson G. Araujo, Lucas B. B. Santana, Edward D. Moreno
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: This article presents a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) on benchmarks in Machine Learning and Deep Learning, analyzing their characteristics, applications, limitations, and challenges. The methodology followed Kitchenham's guidelines, and five research questions (RQs) were formulated to guide the identification and analysis of studies. These RQs focused on the most used benchmarks, their influence on model generalization and performance, practical purpose, categorization criteria, and employed frameworks. The search was conducted across databases such as Scopus, IEEE Xplore, Web of Science, and ACM Digital Library. This process resulted in the selection of 76 articles, 12 of which were analyzed in depth. The results detail specific benchmarks for embedded systems (EEMBC, MiBench), mobile devices (3DMark, AnTuTu), and AI (AIBench, MLPerf). The findings highlight their importance in hardware and software evaluation, bottleneck identification, and reproducibility promotion.

title: Federated Learning: State-of-the-art, Challenges and Research Opportunities
authors: Ton That Tam Dinh, Manh Cuong Ho, Ayalneh Bitew Wondmagegn, Juyoung Kim, Sungrae Cho
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is an emerging paradigm in machine learning that enables collaborative model training without centralizing data, thereby addressing privacy and security concerns. Unlike traditional centralized approaches, FL keeps data on local devices and only exchanges model updates, making it highly relevant in the era of strict data governance regulations. This survey provides a comprehensive overview of FL, covering its foundational concepts, related works, and major categories including horizontal, vertical, and transfer learning. We highlight practical applications across healthcare, finance, mobile devices, IoT, autonomous systems, and wireless communications. Furthermore, we examine the challenges faced by FL, such as data heterogeneity, system scalability, communication efficiency, and security vulnerabilities. We also explore solutions proposed in recent literature, ranging from differential privacy and secure aggregation to personalization and hierarchical architectures. The survey discusses future research directions that integrate FL with edge computing, blockchain, and next-generation wireless networks. By synthesizing these insights, we emphasize the transformative potential of FL in shaping decentralized, privacy-preserving artificial intelligence.

title: Adaptive Split Federated Learning for Network-Driven Cut Selection in Vehicular Edge Intelligence
authors: Youngjoon Yang, Sunghyun Kim, Chaehyeon Kim, Dongkyun Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Vehicular Edge Intelligence (VEI) enables Roadside Units (RSUs) to locally process roadway images, train objectrecognition models, and trigger collision-warning functions. RSUs placed in different road environments observe different scene distributions, which leads to Non-Independent and Identically Distributed (Non-IID) local datasets. As a result, each RSU tends to overfit frequently observed classes while learning limited representations for rare but safety-critical objects, and this imbalance makes global convergence in distributed training unstable. To overcome the limitations of pure local learning, RSU-server collaborative training has been investigated as a more suitable approach, and Split Federated Learning (SFL) provides a practical architecture by combining split execution with parameter aggregation. However, vehicular communication channels vary over time in terms of bandwidth, distance, and interference, so a fixed cut layer in SFL cannot balance activation transmission cost and clientside feature expressiveness across RSUs. This paper proposes a channel-driven Adaptive Split Federated Learning (ASFL) scheme that selects the split layer dynamically according to real-time RSU-server link quality and available bandwidth. RSUs with weak channels are assigned shallower cuts, whereas RSUs with favorable links use deeper cuts to exploit richer feature extraction. Comparative experiments under different adaptive cut-selection configurations demonstrate that the proposed ASFL framework achieves improved model accuracy and reduced training latency by effectively balancing computation and communication overheads. These results indicate that network-aware adaptive partitioning is a promising strategy for distributed learning in VEI settings.

title: Benchmarking the Symmetry of MOSFET Compact Models with Emphasis on ACM2
authors: Deni Germano Alves Neto, Márcio Cherem Schneider, Manuel J. Barragan, Sylvain Bourdel, Carlos Galup-Montoro
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: The symmetrically built MOS transistors of integrated circuits exhibit symmetric electrical behavior if the source and drain terminals are interchanged. Additionally, a series association of transistors is electrically “equivalent” to a single transistor. However, some of the compact MOSFET models do not comply with the requirements of symmetry and transistor equivalence. This paper reports tests of symmetry and of series association of transistors of some compact models available in circuit simulators. We show that the ACM2, a chargebased model in which the terminal voltages are referred to the substrate, is fully compliant with the transistor symmetry, but that some popular models are not. To test the symmetry property, we show examples of transistor current-voltage characteristics and derivatives up to the fifth order, and capacitance-voltage characteristics, all tests around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{D S}=0$</tex>. A MOSFET binary current divider is employed to test the consistency of the model applied to a series association of transistors.

title: Conditional Implicit Neural Representations via Cross-Attention for Multivariate Time-Series Imputation
authors: Eunho Shin, Janghun Hyeon, Yunho Jeon
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Existing Transformer-based approaches for time-series imputation typically rely on full-sequence reconstruction, which leads to computational inefficiency. To address this, we propose Conditional Implicit Neural Representation via Cross-Attention (CINR-CA), a novel framework designed for high-efficiency imputation. We formulate this task as a Conditional Implicit Neural Representation (INR) problem. By adopting a coordinate-based query mechanism, our model selectively computes outputs only for missing timestamps. This strategy significantly reduces inference costs by avoiding unnecessary computations on observed data. Experiments on the ETTh1 dataset demonstrate that CINR-CA reduces computational complexity (MFLOPs) by approximately 43 % (Large settings) compared to the state-of-the-art SAITS model, offering a practical solution for resource-constrained environments where efficiency is important.

title: Adversarial Robustness Analysis of Deep Learning-Based Automatic Modulation Classification in Wireless Communication
authors: Sunjun Hwang, Eunho Choi, Dohyun Hwang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Deep learning-based Automatic Modulation Classification (AMC) has emerged as a critical technology for spectrum sensing and cognitive radio applications. However, the vulnerability of these models to adversarial attacks poses significant security concerns in wireless communication systems. This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of adversarial robustness for a VTCNN2-based AMC model using the RadioML2016.10A dataset. We systematically analyze three representative adversarial attacks-FGSM, DeepFool, and C&W-and evaluate two defense mechanisms: adversarial training and Denoising Autoencoder (DAE). Our experimental results demonstrate that the baseline model is highly susceptible to adversarial perturbations, with accuracy dropping from 54.02% to as low as 10.80% under DeepFool attack. FGSM-based adversarial training improves robustness across multiple attacks, increasing accuracy under FGSM from 17.59% to 33.47% and improving robustness under DeepFool <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(10.80 \% \rightarrow 15.36 \%)$</tex> and C&W <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(29.41 \% \rightarrow 40.67 \%)$</tex>, with a modest clean-accuracy drop <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{5 4. 0 2} \boldsymbol{\rightarrow} \mathbf{5 1. 4 8} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex>). In contrast, DAE preprocessing preserves clean accuracy(53.94%) but provides negligible improvement under FGSM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(17.59 \rightarrow 17.48)$</tex> and only limited gains under iterative L2 attacks such as DeepFool <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(10.80 \% \rightarrow 21.16 \%)$</tex> and C&W <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(29.41 \% \rightarrow 32.81 \%)$</tex>. These findings highlight the urgent need for robust defense mechanisms in deep learning-based wireless communication systems.

title: Data-Level Parameter Sampling for Channel-Adaptive Semantic Communications
authors: Chan Hyung Kim, Geonhui Lee, Minju Chae, Seung-Chan Lim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Although semantic communication leverages deep learning-based joint source-channel coding to achieve robust transmission, transceiver models trained at a fixed signal-tonoise ratio (SNR)often fail to generalize under varying channel conditions. To address this limitation, we propose a data-level parameter sampling (DLPS)-based training framework. The proposed framework employs an equivalent parallel additive white Gaussian noise channel model to enable stochastic sampling of channel parameters during end-to-end training. In the proposed DLPS strategy, a single SNR value is assigned to each training data sample, providing structured exposure to diverse channel conditions and improving channel adaptability. The simulation results show that the proposed framework maintains strong reconstruction performance across a wide SNR range, thus outperforming alternative sampling strategies and achieving competitive performance with SNR-specific dedicated training using a single unified model.
