title: Semi-Supervised Learning and Blockchain Integration for Transparent Smart Contract Security
authors: Muhammad Sannan Khaliq, Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: With the rapid proliferation of blockchain technology and smart contracts in consumer IoT systems, ensuring digital trust and security remains a persistent challenge due to vulnerability diversity, data scarcity, and limited on-chain auditability. To address these issues, this paper presents PureChain, a scalable trust framework that integrates semi-supervised deep learning for smart contract vulnerability detection with blockchain-based audit logging for transparent security assurance. The framework employs a lightweight Conv1D neural architecture trained via iterative pseudo-labeling and a Mean Teacher strategy, effectively leveraging tens of thousands of unlabeled contracts to minimize annotation requirements. Experimental evaluation shows that PureChain achieves macro F1-scores exceeding 99% and significant improvements in recall for rare vulnerabilities, outperforming both supervised and state-of-the-art baselines. The integration of blockchain ensures all detection events are immutably recorded, enabling accountable and verifiable device operation. These results demonstrate that adaptive semi-supervised learning, combined with on-chain transparency, provides a robust and efficient foundation for secure smart contract monitoring across IoT and edge environments, with future work targeting more adaptive pseudo-labeling, semi supervised learning (SSL) techniques and cross-chain generalization.

title: Consolidated Competence Assessment of Seafarers Using Eye-Tracking, Speech Analysis, and EEG-Based Stress Evaluation
authors: Kan Hon Wong, Yisi Liu, Vishakha Lall, Jia Da Lim, Chee Onn Tham, Ashwin Madhav Khandke
abstract: Seafarer competence in visual attention, communication, and stress management is critical to maritime safety. While assessment methods for these individual areas have evolved with simulators, sensor data, and artificial intelligence, there remains no established approach to systematically integrate these outputs into a practical competence measure to support more efficient and standardised debriefing and to enable reliable comparison between trainees. In this paper, we present a multimodal assessment report that fuses analysed results from eyetracking, audio recordings, and Electroencephalogram (EEG) signals collected during full-mission bridge simulator exercises. The report provides an integrated, instructor-friendly summary of each trainee's performance across key competence pillars, along with an executive summary. This approach offers maritime instructors a more comprehensive and objective tool to identify individual strengths, weaknesses, and development needs.

title: One-Shot Training for Reduction of Performance Variance in LLM Rotation Quantization
authors: Sangki Park, Chanhoon Kim, Ki-Seok Chung
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Rotation-based quantization techniques have emerged as effective methods for compressing Large Language Models by mitigating the impact of activation outliers. However, existing approaches face significant challenges: random rotation methods like QuaRot exhibit substantial performance variability across different random seeds, while learned rotation methods like SpinQuant require hundreds of calibration samples, increasing the training cost. To address these limitations, we propose OneRot, a novel one-shot rotation-based quantization technique that achieves stable performance with only a single calibration sample. OneRot introduces random perturbations to layer activations during training, which amplify gradients and enable effective rotation matrix learning in a single attempt while simulating diverse activation distributions. Experimental results on Llama-2 7B, Llama-3.2 3B, and Llama-3.2 1B models with 4-bit quantization demonstrate that OneRot reduces perplexity by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.25,2.88$</tex>, and 21.48, respectively, compared to QuaRot, and reduces standard deviation by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.01,0.24$</tex>, and 1.41, respectively, while achieving comparable performance to SpinQuant using 800 times fewer samples. OneRot provides a practical solution for efficient LLM deployment by combining the stability of learned rotations with the computational efficiency of random approaches.

title: Axial-iFormer: Robust and Efficient Wafer Map Defect Classification via Modulated Axial Attention
authors: Jaeho Song, Juhyeon Noh, Jihoon Lee, Yonggwan Won, Jaehyung Park, Jinsul Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Reliable classification of wafer map defect patterns helps process engineers identify yield-limiting issues in semiconductor fabrication plants. Hybrid CNN-Transformer models such as iFormer, which combine local convolutions with global self-attention, have recently achieved strong results on this task. However, iFormer's Single-Head Modulation Attention (SHMA) still performs expensive global attention over all spatial tokens and provides little inductive bias for thin, line-shaped defects such as scratches. We introduce Axial-iFormer and a lightweight companion model that replace SHMA with a Modulated Axial Attention block. The new block factors 2D attention into row-wise and column-wise operations and modulates the resulting context through a gating branch, preserving long-range dependencies while lowering computational cost. On the WM-811K wafer map benchmark, the performance-oriented Axial-iFormer-S increases Scratch Recall to 82.85%, a gain of 3.35 percentage points over the iFormer-S baseline. The efficiency-oriented Axial-iFormer-S-Lite reduces the parameter count by 29% yet still surpasses the baseline on overall defect detection, indicating that Axial-iFormer is well suited to yield monitoring scenarios with tight computational budgets.

title: Research and Analysis of Intelligent Traffic Flow Prediction Based on Deep Learning and Multi-Source Data Fusion
authors: Junming Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Accuracy prediction of traffic flow plays a vital role in intelligent transportation system, which directly affects the performances of traffic signal control and route planning. In this article, we propose a traffic flow prediction model based on deep learning with multi-source data fusion. It learns to predict short- and medium-term traffic flow by integrating historical traffic flow, weather information, time cycles, and spatial topology features. The proposed model adopts a weighted ensemble approach to exploit the strengths of Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), Transformer, and graph neural network models. The experimentation for the work is validated using the Kaggle and METR-LA datasets. The MAE of the fusion model is decreased by about 12% compared to that of the best single model, and the RMSE is dropped by about 15%. Further analysis of feature importance reveals spatial and weather features contribute the most to the performance improvement. The proposed approach demonstrates superior accuracy and robustness, which can allow engineers to better optimize signals and ease traffic jams in smart transportation.

title: A Low-Cost, SDR-Based Testbed for Multi-Domain Characterization of 5G NR Amplifier Chains
authors: Rodrigo Apaza Huanca, Renan Garcia Escarzo, Juan Carlos Paredes Condori, Esteban Gironda Lagrava, Karel Walter Gomez Orellana, Hugo Orlando Condori Quispe
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: The characterization of 5G New Radio (NR) power amplifiers is critical but traditionally relies on cost-prohibitive equipment. This paper presents a low-cost, reconfigurable testbed using a single Software-Defined Radio (SDR) for both 5G compliant waveform generation and analysis. We introduce a multi-domain methodology integrating frequency (ACPR), time (power envelope), and modulation (EVM) analysis. Validated on an amplifier chain, the testbed captures the EVM “bathtub” curve, determining the linear dynamic range for a 256-QAM 5G NR signal. This work establishes the platform as an accessible tool for practical RF engineering, education, and research.

title: LSTM - Greedy Algorithm Integration Framework for Timing Prediction and Multi-Objective Optimisation Research
authors: Zezheng Wei, Hongyu Du
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In this study, the integration framework of Long Short-Term Memory Network (LSTM) and greedy algorithm is constructed for the problem of time-series data prediction and multi-objective optimisation. Firstly, the historical data and predictive features are analysed by time-series modelling, and the LSTM model is constructed to mine the time-dependent features of the data to achieve fine-grained prediction of the future time period, which provides the basic data support for the subsequent optimisation. Secondly, the greedy algorithm is introduced to dynamically group the time series, and the constraints control the group size and the sum of sequence values, so that the optimisation objective takes into account multi-dimensional indicators and maximises the utilisation rate. Further, capacity adjustment variables are introduced to solve the mixed-integer programming problem by enumeration method to find the optimal solution between processing time and capacity constraints; for the prediction uncertainty, a robust optimisation model is constructed, and the worst-case sequence values are used as the constraints to validate the stability of the model within the perturbation range. The framework significantly improves the accuracy of prediction and the robustness of scheduling strategies through multi-level data processing and model synergy, and demonstrates excellent performance in time-series prediction and multi-factor scheduling problems, providing innovative analysis methods and technical paths for dynamic management scenarios.

title: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin American Symposium on Circuits and Systems (LASCAS)

title: A Conditional Variational Autoencoder-Enhanced Virtual IMU Contrastive Learning for Wearable Human Activity Recognition
authors: Qingrui Wang, Chengshuo Xia
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) with Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs) sensor is often constrained by the high cost of labeled data and the domain gap between virtual and real signals. To address this, we propose a virtual-to-real domain adaptation framework that integrates a Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE) with contrastive learning. The CVAE generates label-conditioned virtual IMU samples, while contrastive pretraining learns transferable representations, compatible with various self-supervised frameworks. We evaluate on a custom anaerobic exercise dataset with six activities, using three-axis accelerometer signals. Results show that our method improves accuracy from 56.8% to 81.7% with only 40 seconds of labeled data per class, demonstrating its effectiveness in few-shot cross-domain HAR.

title: Acknowledgements
authors: N/A

title: Multimodal Clustering-Driven Accurate Recommendation and Optimization System for Smart Health and Elderly Care Service
authors: Fang Guo, Xiaoli Gong, Min Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: With the accelerating aging population, traditional health care services face challenges such as mismatched supply and demand and insufficient personalization. This study proposes a smart health care service system driven by multimodal clustering to achieve precise service matching and dynamic optimization. The system integrates physiological, behavioral, and semantic data through a hierarchical architecture, including modules for multimodal data collection, clustering analysis, precise matching, and feedback optimization. Key technologies involve improved multimodal clustering algorithms (e.g., adaptive weight multimodal clustering, AWMC) that embed geriatric domain knowledge, coupled with a dual-loop feedback mechanism for real-time adjustment of matching strategies. Experimental verification in community and institutional care scenarios demonstrates that the system significantly enhances matching accuracy (92.3% precision vs. 78.5% in the control group) and reduces response time by 52%, compared to traditional rulebased methods. User satisfaction reaches 89.7%, reflecting the effectiveness of personalized service packages generated by multimodal profiling. The research provides a novel framework for integrating data-driven intelligence into health care services, offering practical insights for optimizing resource allocation and improving elderly care experiences.

title: Short-Horizon Load Forecasting for Peak-Shaving Opportunities
authors: Niño Louie R. Boloron, Louine Jacob P. Butalid, Arman T. Gascon, Chyre Jane C. Camaso, Cleifford S. Alfarero
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Power Engineering (AIPE)
abstract: We develop a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex> - and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0}$</tex>-minute persistence-anchored forecasting pipeline with train-only percentile triggers for peak capping in an anonymized executive government office. The dataset spans roughly three weeks of 15-minute active power measurements with limited features, reflecting realistic constraints in public buildings. Using expanding walk-forward validation, persistence is competitive with and often more accurate than ridge regression and gradient boosting for this short, feature-poor series. Peak flags are derived from train-only percentiles (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$85 \text{th} / 90 \text{th} / 95 \text{th}$</tex>) of active power and translated into actions via an inventory-bounded curtailment budget that assumes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> deployable shares of climate and lighting loads. Under leakage-safe evaluation and pragmatic data cleaning, the pipeline reduces monthly maxima when flags coincide with peaks and yields kWh savings across flagged intervals while bounding operator burden (actions per month) and false-action rates. We interpret persistence as sufficient under current data and infrastructure constraints, while acknowledging that richer datasets and exogenous variables can justify more complex models. The method is building-management-system (BMS) friendly, reproducible, and aligned with SDG 7 by improving operational energy efficiency under realistic conditions.

title: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
authors: N/A

title: Cross-Modal Consistent Augmentation Bridging 2D–3D Transformations
authors: Donguk Kim, Soonmin Hwang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: LiDAR-camera fusion models are essential for robust 3D scene understanding in autonomous driving. However, existing multi-modal augmentation methods are typically developed independently for the LiDAR and image domains, which often leads to misalignment between 3D geometric transformations and corresponding 2D visual information. In this paper, we present a geometry-aware framework bridging 2D-3D transformations. With a diffusion inpainting model conditioned on transformed 3D bounding boxes and object appearance, augmented images are generated to preserve LiDAR object geometry and align with the surrounding scene context. Experiments on the nuScenes dataset with BEVFusion demonstrate that our augmentation improves geometric alignment and yields consistent gains in 3D detection performance over both object sampling and object paste baselines. Our findings highlight the importance of geometry-aware, multi-modal augmentation for advancing LiDAR-Camera fusion models.

title: SHAP-Based Data-Driven Analysis of an Offset-Canceling Sense Amplifier in DRAMs
authors: Hyerin Lee, Geon Kim, Dongyeong Kim, Suyeon Kim, Jewon Park, Shinwook Kim, Ryun Kang, Sowon Kim, Chaehyuk Lim, Hyeona Seo, Jeonghyeon Yun, Juwon Lee, Ujin Choi, Eojin Kim, Minwoo Jeong, Myoungjin Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper presents an optimization method for the Offset-Canceling Sense Amplifier (OCSA) using a Support Vector Regression (SVR) model. The proposed approach quantitatively analyzes the influence of key design parameters through SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) and interprets the trade-off relationships among sensing accuracy, sensing speed, and power efficiency. The analysis results show that structural parameters mainly affect sensing sensitivity and speed, while timing parameters have a dominant impact on stability and power efficiency. Through this quantitative and data-driven analysis, the complex performance characteristics of the offset cancelling circuit are clarified, and a design framework is proposed that enables optimal performance balancing during the design stage.

title: Intelligent flame monitoring system on the robotic dog platform
authors: Yao Wang, Zengwen Zhang, Yiyi Zhou, Baorong Zhan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Fire is a major hidden danger that threatens building safety. There are about 300000 fire disasters in China every year, among which high-rise buildings have particularly serious consequences due to the difficulty of extinguishing fires and the rapid spread of fire. The traditional fixed fire monitoring system relies on a single sensor, which has bottlenecks such as limited monitoring range, poor environmental adaptability, lagging early warning, and insufficient reliability of wired deployment. To overcome the above limitations and combine the development trends of the Internet of Things and robotics technology, an intelligent flame monitoring system based on quadruped robotic dogs is proposed. The system takes the robot dog as the mobile carrier, integrates the multi-mode perception module and edge computing unit composed of infrared flame sensor, multispectral imaging device, temperature and humidity sensor and combustible gas sensor, and relies on the robot dog's excellent ability to surmount obstacles and extreme environmental adaptability to achieve all-round dynamic monitoring of building fire hazards. By adopting the technology path of "multi-sensor fusion+deep learning algorithm", a fire risk assessment model is constructed by collecting real-time data such as flame spectral characteristics, temperature distribution, and gas concentration, to achieve accurate identification and grading warning in the early stage of fire smoldering. Experimental verification shows that the system achieves a flame recognition accuracy of 98.3% in complex building environments, with an alarm response time of less than 2 seconds; Compared to traditional fixed monitoring systems, the monitoring coverage has increased by 40%, and it has outstanding environmental adaptability and dynamic operation performance such as smoke penetration resistance and high temperature resistance. This study provides a scientifically feasible intelligent solution for building fire inspection and complex scene fire warning. Its technical framework can be further extended to fields such as forest fires and industrial site fires, and has important scientific value and engineering application significance.

title: A Review of Proximal Policy Optimization for Uplink Multi-User SIMO-RSMA Systems
authors: Huy Dang Mac, Kiet Nguyen Tuan Tran, Dongwook Won, Sungrae Cho
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Rate-Splitting Multiple Access (RSMA) has emerged as a powerful multiple access technique for interference management in next-generation wireless networks. In uplink multi-user single-input multiple-output (SIMO) systems, RSMA enables a base station to decode multi-user transmissions more flexibly by splitting messages and partially decoding interference. Optimizing such RSMA systems is challenging due to the complex resource allocation and decoding order decisions required, especially under dynamic channel conditions. Recently, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) approaches, particularly Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), have been applied to efficiently learn near-optimal policies for uplink RSMA resource allocation. This paper provides a comprehensive review of PPO-based techniques for uplink multi-user SIMO-RSMA systems. We outline the fundamentals of SIMO-RSMA, describe the PPO approach for solving the sum-rate and fairness optimization problems in these systems, and survey state-of-the-art PPO-based solutions from 2022 to 2025. A comparative analysis of recent studies is presented, and open challenges as well as future research directions are discussed.

title: Joint Hardware–Waveform Co-Design of Semantic RF Transceivers for 6G Communications
authors: Francois Rivet, Mohamed Amine Hamoura, Sami Ouabrk, Nour Hello, Emilio Calvanese Strinati
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: The transition to sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks represents a paradigm shift from bit-centric transmission toward semantic and goal-oriented communication. Traditional modulation schemes, such as Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM), are designed to maximize bit-level reliability but overlook the semantic relevance of transmitted information. With the advent of Artificial Intelligence (AI) as native component of future communication systems, this limitation creates inefficiencies for emerging 6G AI assisted applications. This paper introduces a joint hardware–waveform co-design framework for semantic communication (SemCom) transceivers. At its core lies a novel semantic-aware waveform, termed Orthogonal Semantic Sequency Division Multiplexing (OSSDM), which adaptively shapes physical signals carrying semantic messages. OSSDM is designed to maximize communication effectiveness while ensuring spectral compliance with RF signal masks imposed by standards. The experimental validation with an USRP X310 exhibits the associated trade-offs of SemCom compression with resilience to impairments due to quantization and synchronization, and compliance with emission masks. These results highlight OSSDM-enabled semantic RF transceivers as a promising candidate for future 6G networks.

title: A Novel Data-Driven State of Charge Estimation for Li-Ion Batteries
authors: Basant Kumar Sethi, Nivedika Kher, Debottam Mukherjee
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: An efficient state of charge (SoC) estimation is a crucial prerequisite for a reliable operation of the battery management systems (BMS). This study proposes a data-driven SoC estimation technique based on the 2-RC equivalent circuit model (ECM) utilising gradient descent-based optimisers. A detailed comparative analysis is performed between data-driven optimisers like the least squares (LS), conventional gradient descent (GD), and the gradient descent with momentum (GDM) to assess the convergence characteristics, accuracy, and robustness of the proposed SoC estimator across various learning rates. The results demonstrate that GDM not only achieves a superior estimation accuracy over LS and GD but also converges significantly faster, thus showing a lower computational burden. The proposed estimator demonstrates a robust performance under the hybrid pulse power characterisation (HPPC) tests and is capable of handling small-scale noises. Simulation results demonstrate that momentum-based SoC estimators can be implemented in real time and may lead to the development of advanced BMS for electric vehicles.

title: CNN-BiLSTM Based Channel Estimation in RIS-NOMA System for 5G and Beyond
authors: Aman Kanwar, Aryan Mittal, Akash, Sandeep Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) is one of the key enabler along with Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) for beyond 5G (B5G) and 6G networks, offering enhanced spectral efficiency and massive connectivity. The estimation of RIS-NOMA channels is particularly challenging due to the cascaded nature of the BS-RIS-user transmission path combined with non-linear distortion introduced by NOMA-based signal superposition. In this study, a composite deep learning approach is designed that leverages convolutional neural processing for spatial feature learning and bidirectional recurrent memory structures to effectively characterize temporal correlations in the observed signals. A synthetic RIS-NOMA dataset is generated to capture user mobility, power imbalance, and varying RIS configurations. The model predicts the amplitude and phase of cascaded channels and is evaluated under multiple SNR levels using NRMSE and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> score. Simulation results show that the proposed CNN-BiLSTM model outperforms a baseline CNNLSTM, particularly in low-SNR and asymmetric power scenarios, demonstrating its potential for efficient channel estimation in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 G}$</tex> RIS-NOMA networks.

title: Optimizing Real Estate Investment Allocation in the UAE Market: A Spatial Machine Learning Framework
authors: Zahra Radhi A. Salman
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: The real estate market in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) presents significant complexity due to rapid expansion, speculative influences, and geographic dependencies. Traditional investment methodologies often rely on basic heuristics that fail to capture spatial relationships and market dynamics. This study presents a spatial machine learning framework for real estate investment allocation, analyzing 22,676 property listings across UAE emirates. Spatial autocorrelation analysis was conducted using Moran's I statistic, confirming significant geographic dependencies (Moran's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{I}=\mathbf{0. 1 7 8}, p<0.001$</tex>). Ensemble methods including Random Forest (RF) and eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) were assessed through 5-fold cross-validation. The Random Forest model achieved coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex>) of 0.930 with Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 183,744 AED, representing 882.9% improvement over area median baseline (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2} =0.095$</tex>, MAE =2,949,763 AED). Feature importance analysis using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) revealed location encoding (35.0%) and price normalization (35.0%) as dominant predictive factors. An investment scoring framework integrating rental yield, capital appreciation, property quality, and location attributes was developed and applied to identify 4,276 highpotential investment opportunities with projected 66.5% fiveyear Return on Investment (ROI). Geographic allocation analysis identified Jumeirah Village Circle as the optimal investment location, offering 9.79% rental yield, 76.58% five-year ROI, and 603 opportunities at median price of 910,000 AED. ROI decomposition analysis revealed rental income comprises 54.2% of total returns, challenging speculation-focused investment narratives in emerging markets.

title: Dynamic Response Analysis Based on Neural Network and Genetic Algorithm
authors: Tingkai Wang, Dongxuan Han
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: In this paper, a framework of algorithms for response modeling and structure optimization of multivariate systems is proposed, focusing on state prediction under highdimensional input conditions, modeling of time-series evolutionary processes, and mapping relationships between parameter structures and system performances. First, a neural network model is constructed to jointly predict multiple continuous response variables, which improves the accuracy and stability of high-dimensional output-consistent modeling. Second, a discrete-time evolution model is introduced to simulate the dynamic changes of the system state under external intervention, spontaneous adjustment and neighborhood propagation mechanisms, and the regulation parameters are optimized by genetic algorithms to maximize the response effect under cost constraints. Finally, combining weighted correlation analysis and hierarchical aggregation methods, the multiindicator structural feature vector is constructed, which is used to portray the coupling relationship between input factors and system performance. The model integrates deep learning, propagation modeling and intelligent optimization methods with high adaptability, strong scalability and good interpretability, and is suitable for response prediction and strategy regulation scenarios of multi-stage and multi-variable systems.

title: ScaleGS: Scalable Distributed Framework for Large-Scale 3D Gaussian Splatting with Edge Communication
authors: Yong Kou, Jinlong He, Xia Yuan, Dening Luo, Yanci Zhang

title: When Q-Learning fails: unstable behavior for infinite state spaces

title: TMF-Net: A Transformer-Based Multi-Modal Fusion Framework Integrating MRI and Clinical Data for Robust Brain Tumor Detection
authors: Sri Lakshmi M, Deepthi Kothapeta, Vijaya Kumar Koppula
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Diagnosis of brain tumors is a critical clinical exercise, and the selective differentiation between the benign tumors and the malignant ones is inseparable in the maximization of treatment regimens. In Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI), there is a lot of space and structural information as relates to tumor morphology; however, the imaging data alone might not be sufficient to cover patient-specific biological variables that alter tumor dynamics. In this framework, we present TMF-Net, a Transformer based multimodal fusion model which integrates MRI imaging to structured clinical genetic data during brain tumor classification. The given architecture makes use of modality-specific Transformer encoders to extract salient representation of both MRI and clinical modalities, and then combines them through cross-attention fusion mechanism that captures inter-modal relationships. Full analyses of the BraTS MRI dataset with and without clinical glioma grading data show that TMF-Net significantly outperforms both unimodal baselines and traditional fusion methods with an overall classification accuracy of 98.8%. In addition, Grad-CAM visualizations provide interpretability through outlining clinically relevant tumor areas, thus supporting the accuracy of the framework in clinical decision support implementation.

title: Capacity Analysis of Frequency Reuse Schemes for Steerable Beam in LEO Satellite Systems
authors: Weilong Ren
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: The construction of low earth orbit (LEO) satellite system is in great demand in the world today, where the interference due to the limited bandwidth is one of the difficulties. In the paper, we analyze the steerable beam capacity of different frequency reuse schemes in LEO satellite systems, including two traditional frequency reuse schemes (reuse factor =1,3) and soft frequency reuse (SFR). Besides, the influence of static inter-cell interference coordination (ICIC) technology on capacity is simulated at the same time, where we set up two scenarios: one LEO satellite forming one beam, and one LEO satellite forming three beams. Through the simulation and analysis, we get visual results and make a suggestion on the application of frequency reuse and ICIC in LEO satellite systems with steerable beam.

title: Evaluating GRAD-CAM Explainability Across Deep Learning Models
authors: Akhil Shaju, Akshay A C, Madhav Murali V, Nandan Krishna, Manoj V Thomas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence(AI) and Machine Learning(ML) have modified every field of modern life. However, due to the black-box nature, the lack of interpretability in deep learning models, particularly in Convolutional Neural Networks, limits real-world adoption. Explainable AI(XAI), on the other hand, improves the black-box nature of deep learning models by enhancing the transparency, making AI-driven predictions more reliable, especially in the fields of healthcare, education, etc. This research project integrates Grad-CAM(Gradient Weighted Class Activation Mapping) Lightweight CNNs and their architectures will enhance interpretability in classifications, generating visual explanations, aiding professionals in understanding AI predictions. The study evaluates various real-world datasets that leverage Grad-CAM for better visualizations. While applied in the efficient CNN the Grad- CAM can improve the model by increasing its interpretation. Experimental results demonstrate a high accuracy of $95 \%$ and above while ensuring efficient computation and transparent decision making. This work systematically evaluates Grad-CAM’s applicability in AI-driven fields.

title: Enhanced Age and Gender Classification in Dental Radiographs Through a Synergistic Deep Learning Framework Integrating Convolutional and Recurrent Neural Networks
authors: V. S. Chithra, K. S. Charishma, A. K. Hemanth, P Tejas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Forensic dentistry plays an important role in human identification, especially anthropology. Audit of dental features is time-consuming, subjective, and prone to bias. To overcome this, we would like to propose a hybrid deep learning framework that is novel in that it synergizes ResNet50, MobileNetV2, and VGG16 with Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) layers and improves the spatial feature extraction analysis of dental structures. Our method differs from previous studies that either used conventional CNNbased feature extraction or classifiers, as we benefit from GRUs in our work and exploit brightness transitions in the images over time for improving classification accuracy. Yet we have made efforts in improving model interpretability through explainable AI (X-AI) techniques, covering the aspects of reliability and transparency in predictions, which are often neglected by forensic AI models. The proposed model had been trained on a dataset divided into two gender classes and four age groups, yielding results of 90% and 92.5% accuracy in gender and age classification against existing methodologies, respectively. By increasing precision in the automation of forensic identification, this research not only opens new horizons in forensic dentistry but also paves the way for a scalable, interpretable, and efficient AI solution for post-disaster victim identification and anthropological studies.

title: AI-Enhanced Breast Cancer Screening with Deep Transfer Learning
authors: Mohini Kumbhar, Mayuresh Gulame, Renuka Wakhare, Punam Sawale, Priya Khune, Trupti Sonkusare
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Malignancy is a deadly ailment that distresses many advanced and rising nations globally. Explicitly, the commonness of breast melanoma in womenfolk is intensifying daily, in part because of a shortage of attentiveness and early discovery. Only by precisely pinpointing and categorizing breast cancer in its very primary stages can a suitable initial treatment be administered. By cultivating and supporting less skilled medical specialists, the application of computer-assisted diagnosis (CAD) and medical image exploration practices may add to the automation and acceleration of cancer recognition and categorization. Convolutional neural networks are crucial for accurately identifying and categorizing cancer in big databases of medical images. Using the publicly accessible BreakHis dataset and a variety of deep neural networks (DNN) (ResNet 18, Shuffle-Net, and Inception-V3Net), this research offerings a distinctive computer-aided opinion approach for breast malignancy categorization. Proposed method proved to be the utmost perfect and proficient classifier, with the highest average accuracy, sensitivity and specificity are 99.64%, 98.80%, 97.12 individually.

title: Real-Time Traffic Sign Detection and Recognition for Smart Vehicles
authors: Zahraa Shihab Al Hakeem, Hiba J. Aleqabie
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: This paper proposes a real-time traffic sign detection and recognition framework tailored for smart vehicles in dynamic road environments. The system integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for deep feature extraction with support vector machines (SVMs) for reliable classification. A robust preprocessing pipeline—comprising normalization, noise filtering, and color space transformation—ensures effective operation under variable lighting and weather conditions. Evaluated on a hybrid dataset of $\mathbf{1 8, 8 2 6}$ annotated traffic signs and real-time video frames, the system achieved an accuracy of $99.06 \%$, with a precision of $98.94 \%$ and a recall of $98.81 \%$. The false alarm rate remained low at $1.3 \%$, validating its practical reliability. Comparative assessments against state-of-the-art models such as YOLOv4-SPP and MobileViT demonstrate that the proposed system offers superior accuracy while maintaining real-time performance on modest hardware. Minor degradation was noted under conditions such as occlusion and low-light scenarios. Future enhancements will explore multi-frame tracking, sensor fusion (e.g., LiDAR and infrared), and lightweight model optimization through pruning and quantization. Overall, the proposed system provides an efficient, scalable, and high-performance solution for real-world deployment in intelligent transportation systems and autonomous driving platforms.

title: Automatic Generation Method of Secondary Work Tickets for Substations Based on SDD Model and Self-learning Fuzzy Reasoning
authors: Zutao Shen, Dan Luo, Peng Xu, Zeyin Song, Yuteng Wu, Hongling Wu
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: A substation secondary work ticket automatic generation method based on SDD model and self-learning fuzzy reasoning is proposed to address the problems of low efficiency and possible missing items in manual work ticket preparation. Firstly, the overall architecture of the automatic generation system for secondary work tickets in substations is constructed, including the model management layer, operation support layer, and production management layer. Then, combining CAD mapping technology and substation secondary circuit information model, a secondary circuit digital description model of the entire station is constructed. Finally, a secondary operation library is generated using an expert system, and a fuzzy matrix is established between the work symptom vector and the work reason vector. The secondary equipment maintenance work ticket is automatically generated through fuzzy learning inference. Based on experimental verification of the proposed method at a certain 220 kV substation, the results show that the average accuracy of the secondary work ticket generation is about 98.5%, and the average reliability coefficient is 0.942, which is superior to other comparative methods.

title: Automatic Publication Summarization using a BERT-based Natural Language Processing Model
authors: H K Susheelamma, R Madhavi Salai, Utkarsh Singh, Udaybhaskar Konagalla, H T Shabareesh
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Faculty members in academic institutions invest significant time manually creating and updating research publication summaries for profile building, grant applications, and institutional reporting. This manual process is labor-intensive, inconsistent, and increasingly difficult as publication counts grow. This paper presents an automated Publication Summary Generator system that employs Natural Language Processing techniques to extract publication data from multiple sources and generate concise summaries for faculty profiles. The proposed system integrates with Google Scholar, institutional repositories, and PDF documents to automatically collect publication information. It implements text extraction, preprocessing, and summarization algorithms using BERT-based transformer models to create meaningful summaries. The system provides a web-based interface built with React and Flask where faculty members can upload publications, customize summary parameters, and export formatted summaries in PDF, Word, and HTML formats. Evaluation with faculty members across different departments demonstrates that the system reduces profile building time by seventy-eight percent while maintaining summary quality comparable to manually written abstracts. The system successfully generates publication summaries that are grammatically correct, factually accurate, and suitable for professional academic profiles. This work contributes a practical solution for academic profile management and demonstrates effective application of modern NLP techniques in higher education administration.

title: Development of CAM-System for Laser Equipment
authors: Andrew Senkevich, Alexander Korolev
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)
abstract: In this article described process of developing CAM-system for high accurate laser machines. These systems provides ability of creating receipts for automatic MCU-based systems and in some cases provides operator control process execution. This software is necessarily for high accurate systems, especially in laser industry. This software development includes the variety of mathematical methods like optimization algorithms and high level mathematical models. All this methods should help to fill the need in this kind of software for high accurate laser machines and automation systems that includes these machines. In result were developed high level program for simulating process execution and tracking machine state in real time.

title: Design and Analysis of Natural Gas Pressure Energy Recovery System of Underground Gas Storage Coupled with Organic Rankine Cycle and Proton Exchange Membrane Electrolyzer
authors: Qianshan Jiang, Wei Yang, Kang’Kang Wang, An’An Zhang, Wei Wang, Peiyu Yin
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: To address the challenges of utilizing natural gas pressure energy in underground gas storage and insufficient utilization of cold energy after natural gas (NG) expansion power generation, an organic rankine cycle-proton exchange membrane electrolyzer-natural gas pressure recovery (ORC-PEME-NGPR) hybrid system was designed for underground gas storage scenarios under the ‘dual-carbon’ context. First, based on the principle of energy cascade utilization and considering the production characteristics of the underground gas storage, an ORC-PEME-NGPR integrated system structure was designed. Subsequently, guided by engineering thermodynamics theory, a coupled simulation model of the integrated system was constructed, and key indicators for, thermal analysis, economic analysis, and environmental analysis were established. Finally, a case study was conducted on an underground gas storage in south-west China to validate the feasibility and economic viability of the system. The influence of NG flow rate and pressure on system efficiency and economic performance was analyzed. The study demonstrated that the integrated system can effectively recover NG cold energy while utilizing its pressure energy, achieving energy efficiency and exergy efficiency of $82.53 \%$ and $55.30 \%$, respectively.

title: Deep Learning-Based Multi-Class Road Surface Classification Using CNN and Random Forest
authors: Parveen Sharma, Bhavna Thakur, Gaurav Sharma, Amandeep Kaur, Deepak Banerjee, Gourav Verma
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: In this paper, a road surface classification model based on a deep-learning approach is presented that classifies four types of roads ( Dry, Wet, Snowy, and Muddy roads) accurately. It has a data set of 9,880 images where the model was trained and tested and it has an outstanding performance on the four categories. In the case of Dry Road, it presents Precision (98.46%), Recall (98.65%), and F1 Score (98.55%); Wet Road (98.68%, 98.49%, 98.58%); Snowy Road (98.42%, 98.61, 98.52); and Muddy Road (98.35%, 98.11, 98.23). The support of each class includes 2,585 Dry, 2,650 Wet, 2,525 Snowy, and 2,120 Muddy road pictures that comprise 26, 27, 26 and 21 percent of the entire dataset respectively. The model is robust and consistent in the sense that the model has a uniform accuracy of 99 percent and an overall accuracy of 98.48 percent. Therefore, the findings demonstrate that the model works well in the correct identification and categorization of the type of road surface and can be applied to a big extent in autonomous vehicles navigation, intelligent transportation and automated road surface surveillance.

title: Current Status and Prospects of Noise Reduction Materials for Power Transformers
authors: Fengju Shang, Shixiang Liu, Jiaqing Zhang, Yi Guo, Fei Tang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: The development of ultra-high voltage transmission is an important measure to optimize the allocation of energy resources, ensure national energy security and realize the national dual carbon strategic goal. The use of noise reduction materials in substations (converter stations) is of great significance for meeting the noise standards of substations and achieving the goals of the dual-carbon strategy. This article analyzes the principles and classifications of sound-absorbing and sound-insulating materials used in power transformers in substations, and elaborates on the current development status, advantages and disadvantages of commonly used noise reduction materials. By combining the operating environment characteristics of substations, it is pointed out that the noise reduction materials for power transformers should have wide-frequency sound absorption (insulation) characteristics, excellent weather resistance, reliable mechanical properties, and ease of construction. At the same time, it is pointed out that to further improve the noise reduction performance, weather resistance, mechanical performance and environmental protection performance of noise reduction materials for power transformers, research on the preparation of noise reduction materials using natural sand particles or solid waste particles treated for environmental protection should be strengthened. These materials possess the functions of noise reduction under normal operating conditions and high-temperature melting in fire conditions, and also have the characteristics of low price, easy processing, environmental protection and dust resistance. They are ideal materials for noise reduction treatment in substations (converter stations), but their resistance to rain exposure and aging under high and low temperatures still need to be further verified through engineering practice.

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)

title: Implementation of YOLO-Based Deep Learning Models in the Development of Autonomous Traffic Accident Identification and Communication System
authors: Janice C. Sombillo, Jonardo R. Asor
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The main objective is to develop an accident detection system that integrates seamlessly with the existing network of closed-circuit television (CCTV) to automatically identify and notify authorities of traffic incidents as they occur. This approach leverages the current infrastructure of the Municipality of Los Banos, making the system both practical and cost-efficient for local government implementation. The dataset used for model training is sourced from RoboFlow, with additional non-accident data obtained from municipal CCTV footage. The system employs YOLO11, to detect traffic accidents in real-time. The model was trained using a curated dataset of annotated images covering a variety of traffic conditions and accident scenarios. Data augmentation techniques enhanced the model's adaptability to lighting and environmental factors. To ensure smooth streaming and timely detection, system-level optimizations were implemented to reduce latency, frame drops, and CPU load. A 20% portion of the dataset was reserved for testing, where the model achieved a precision, recall and Fl-score of 94.0%, indicating high accuracy and reliability.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)

title: Research on Numerical Recognition of Mine Gas Sensors Based on PP-OCRv5
authors: Chuan Luo, Lei You, Yanchi Liu, Xiaoyu Yang, Tao Li, Xinglin Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Gas inspection in mines is a critical task for ensuring safe coal mine production. Manual meter reading by gas inspectors may involve negligence or concealment, leading to inconsistencies between recorded sensor readings and actual values, which seriously affects safety supervision. To address this problem, this paper proposes a numerical recognition method for mine gas sensors based on PP-OCRv5. The core algorithm adopts PP-OCRv5, and the model is fine-tuned using a dataset of gas sensor images collected from real mine environments. The trained model is then converted using the TPU-MLIR tool to generate a format suitable for deployment on BM1684X edge computing devices. The system is validated on a test dataset. Experimental results show that when deployed on a single BM1684X device, the numerical recognition algorithm achieves a maximum average processing frame rate of 12.6 frames per second with a recognition accuracy of up to 97.5%. When multiple algorithm instances are deployed simultaneously, the average frame rate remains at 1.8 frames per second or higher. Overall, the proposed method meets the requirements of low power consumption, high speed, and high accuracy for real-time monitoring in underground mine environments, and provides practical evidence for the application of character recognition systems in edge computing scenarios.

title: Transformer-Based Single-Channel EEG Modeling for Mental State Classification in Wearable BCIs
authors: Ravichander Janapati, Usha Desai, Sanjeevakumar M. Hatture, Viswaprakash Babu, Rashmi P. Karchi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Mental state recognition with single-channel electroencephalogram (EEG)-based brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) plays significance in lightweight and wearable devices. However, a majority of traditional deep learning models are based on convolutional or recurrent networks that do not inherently feature long-range temporal and global attention patterns of an EEG signal. This paper provides a transformerbased single-channel EEG modeling model to classify the mental state in wearable BCIs. The proposed approach utilizes a temporal self-attention process to acquire contextual connections in whole sequences of EEGs, which allows better capturing of cognitive and emotional differences. EEG signals from the MetaWave Single-Channel EEG Mental State Dataset were first filtered with bandpass <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.5-40 \text{Hz})$</tex> and notch <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(50 \text{Hz})$</tex> filters to eliminate physiological and environmental noise. Short-time Fourier Transform (STFT) characteristics were obtained and converted to time-frequency representations as an input to transformer. This model was applied for inter-sequence correlations using a multi-head self-attention encoder, to predict mental states, including focus, relaxation, and stress, using a feed-forward classifier. The experimental findings show that the proposed transformer model attained 94.7% classification accuracy and an area under curve (AUC) of 0.97, which effective than the state-of-the-art models. The results confirm the efficiency of attention-driven temporal modeling towards a costeffective and efficient EEG decoding, which offers a pathway to the third-generation portable BCIs that will be able to assess mental state in real-time scenarios at low costs in daily setups.

title: Optimizing Quantum Decision Trees (QDT) Circuits Using Hybrid Golden Eagle Optimization with Adaptive Gradient Descent
authors: L. Maria Michael Visuwasam, Sudha B, G. Kalpana, Viswanathan Ramasamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: This research centers on enhancing quantum circuits for Quantum Decision Trees (QDT) a highly promising algorithm in the realm of quantum machine learning aimed at classification and pattern recognition-via a hybrid metaheuristic optimization framework that integrates Golden Eagle Optimization (GEO) with Adaptive Gradient Descent (AGD). Although QDTs provide interpretability and quantum-enhanced classification capabilities for high-dimensional data sets, their effectiveness is frequently limited by subpar circuit configurations, excessive gate depth, and susceptibility to quantum noise. The suggested hybrid approach utilizes the global search capability of GEO and the local convergence accuracy of AGD to optimize QDT parameters such as entanglement structure, placement of quantum gates, and decision thresholds. Experimental findings indicate that the proposed technique achieves quicker convergence (38 iterations), superior classification accuracy (97.8 %), decreased quantum circuit complexity (39 quantum gates), and enhanced noise resilience (fidelity: 0.986), surpassing traditional methods including Genetic Algorithms (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Simulated Annealing (SA). These results affirm the promise of hybrid metaheuristic optimizers in improving quantum circuit efficiency for real-world quantum machine learning applications.

title: A SiC MOSFET Short-Circuit Protection And Fault Type Identification Circuit
authors: Qiyuan Zhang, Tailai Xiong, Xuan Lin, Taiqiang Cao
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Silicon Carbide Metal Oxide Semiconductor Field Effect Transistor (SiC MOSFET) offers high power density but poor short-circuit withstand capability, so the design of fast and reliable short-circuit protection is crucial for the application of SiC MOSFET. Traditional short-circuit protection circuits based on di/dt detection suffer from weak noise immunity, poor detection flexibility, and high short-circuit current peaks during load faults, making it difficult to meet the practical short-circuit protection requirements for SiC MOSFET. Focusing on two types of short-circuit faults—Hard Switching Fault (HSF) and Fault Under Load (FUL), this paper proposes a fault identification circuit based on dv/dt and source current detection. The circuit can accomplish both short-circuit protection and fault type identification within $2~\mu$ s, providing important guidance for fault root cause analysis.

title: Transfer Learning for Early Diabetic Retinopathy Detection Using Efficientnet with Explainable AI
authors: Rahul Burkul, Sandhya Waghere
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: This research proposes a novel approach for early diabetic retinopathy detection using transfer learning techniques on EfficientNet combined with explainable AI methods. The novelty lies in optimizing EfficientNet architectures specifically for early-stage DR detection while providing clinically interpretable visualizations through Grad-CAM integration. It employs compound scaling to balance network depth, width, and resolution, achieving superior performance with fewer parameters compared to conventional CNN architectures [13]. Diabetic retinopathy accounts for significant vision loss in diabetic patients and is better managed if detected in the early stages. Our model uses feature extraction on pre-trained EfficientNet architectures which are fine-tuned on retinal image datasets, integrating explainability schemes to provide interpretable outcomes for clinicians. Among the tested architectures (B0-B5), EfficientNet-B4 achieved the best performance, reaching 83.86% testing accuracy, showing stronger generalization ability compared to the smaller variants The explainability components enhance confidence in the automated detection system, facilitating its integration into clinical workflows for early intervention and treatment planning.

title: Sugarcane Disease Detection Using Advanced Machine Learning and Image Processing Techniques
authors: Kishori Abhijeet Sawardekar Budhale, Bhaskar Vijayrao Patil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Sugarcane is a vital crop in tropical and subtropical regions, with India among the largest producers. Its yield and quality are often reduced by diseases such as Red Rot, Smut, Wilt, Mosaic Virus, Rust, and Yellow Leaf Disease. Traditional detection methods are labor-intensive, subjective, and unsuitable for large-scale monitoring. This study presents a hybrid deep learning framework for automated sugarcane disease detection using image processing techniques. Highresolution leaf images were collected from field surveys and online repositories, then preprocessed with Adaptive Histogram Equalization and k-means clustering to enhance segmentation and contrast. Statistical features were extracted using Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM) and Principal Component Analysis (PCA). A convolutional neural network (CNN), enhanced with transfer learning, served as the primary classifier, while a Support Vector Machine (SVM) provided secondary validation. Experimental results show that the hybrid CNN-SVM model achieved strong performance across multiple disease categories, with Red Rot detection particularly accurate. Comparative evaluation confirmed that the hybrid approach outperformed CNN-only models, demonstrating the complementary strengths of deep learning feature extraction and SVM decision boundaries. Integration into mobile-based platforms enabled real-time diagnostic feedback, reducing delays and supporting timely farmer interventions. Future directions include expanding datasets, incorporating climatic variables, and applying Explainable AI (XAI) and multimodal learning frameworks to improve transparency, scalability, and generalization. This research establishes a foundation for intelligent, field-ready disease detection systems that advance precision agriculture and promote sustainable sugarcane production.

title: FindMe.AI: A Multi-Modal Framework for Real-Time Missing Person Detection Using YOLOv5 and BERT
authors: P S Neha, Athira K Jagadeesh, Nandhana Lalu, Tn NavaneethKrishna, Abhinav Gosh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: FindMe.AI is a novel, real-time AI system for missing person identification. Our framework offers a truly end-to-end multi-modal fusion pipeline, in contrast to previous works that treat text and vision as distinct modalities. Three essential modules are synergistically integrated into FindMe.AI: (3) a predictive movement model that uses spatiotemporal data to predict likely trajectories; (2) BERT-based natural language processing to extract geo-temporal clues from unstructured text; and (3) YOLOv5-based real-time object detection for facial and vehicle recognition from video streams. These modules contribute to a single dashboard that gives law enforcement access to realtime, actionable intelligence. We demonstrate that our system achieves a 92% precision rate in facial recognition and an 89% F1-score for location extraction, outperforming traditional investigative methods with a 68% reduction in average search time during field deployments. Our system showcases a significant real-world benefit for public safety by transitioning investigations from a reactive to a proactive, data-driven paradigm while adhering to strict privacy and ethical guidelines.

title: A Review of Multi-Resource Frequency Regulation Control Strategies in New Power Systems
authors: Cong Wang, Wenying Wu, Wei Cui, Wei Wang, Chongtao Li, Chao Duan
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: With the rapid growth of renewable energy, new power systems are experiencing an increasing share of wind and solar generation. This shift reduces the system’s overall inertia and introduces greater variability, posing significant challenges for maintaining frequency stability. Traditional synchronous generators offer strong but sluggish frequency support, while renewables and energy storage systems respond more rapidly but often with constrained regulation capacity. These differences can lead to serious frequency fluctuations, especially under high renewable penetration. To address these challenges, this paper comprehensively reviews the frequency response characteristics of multiple energy resources, including thermal, hydro, wind, photovoltaic, load, and energy storage units. Moreover, the paper explores key control strategies such as deadband tuning and coordinated regulation, and discusses optimization approaches for system-level frequency support. The objective is to propose effective technical paths and parameter settings that support stable operation of power systems with high renewable integration. The findings aim to guide the development of frequency regulation frameworks for future renew-able-dominated power systems.

title: BanINT: A Bengali Dataset for Multiclass Intent Classification with BanglaSeqNet
authors: Md. Tanvir Ahammed Shawon, Fariha Haq, Md. Ayon Mia, Muhammad Ibrahim Khan, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin, M. M. Musharaf Hussain
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Detecting user intent in Bengali social media text is a critical challenge, particularly given the linguistic ambiguity, semantic overlap, and low-resource nature of Bengali NLP. To address this, we introduce BanINT, an innovative dataset comprising 4,817 manually annotated comments and descriptions collected from Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube. The dataset spans six intent categories: Emotion, Query, Request, Complaint, Statement, and Suggestion, reflecting a broad spectrum of real-world communication. We perform a comprehensive evaluation of transformer-based models, including BanglaBERT, mBERT, and XLMRoBERTa, under standard fine-tuning protocols. Furthermore, we propose BanglaSeqNet, a transformerbased architecture that integrates multi-head attention and multi pooling strategies to enhance representation learning. BanglaSeqNet, leveraging XLM-RoBERTa, achieves a promising 84.5 % accuracy with a macro F1-score of 0.844, demonstrating its effectiveness for multiclass intent detection. Our findings highlight key challenges in distinguishing semantically overlapping intent categories and underline the need for more context-aware modeling. This work establishes a strong foundation for advancing intent understanding in Bengali and other low-resource, linguistically complex languages.

title: A Method for Locating Single-phase Ground Fault Sections in Distribution Networks Based on a Waveform Similarity Algorithm Using Hausdorff Distance
authors: Yushan Zhou, Mengru Zhang, Yubo Song, Haibo Zhang, Wanying Qi, Jinrui Tang
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Fault section location constitutes the critical component in single-phase grounding fault diagnosis for distribution networks. Addressing the issues of low accuracy, slow speed and poor adaptability which existing fault section localization methods when dealing with single-phase ground faults in complex distribution networks with multiple nodes, this paper proposes an enhanced approach for fault section localization with the Hausdorff distance as fundation algorithm. Initially, the principle of the waveform similarity algorithm with the Hausdorff distance algorithm as the foundation is analyzed; Second, by leveraging the correlation of the three zero-sequence currents near each node of the distribution line, the relationship between the fault location and the node positions of the distribution line is determined. Further, by means of the Hausdorff distance algorithm, the fault position is identified via analyzing the similarity of current waveforms between the upstream and downstream areas of the fault point. A fault localization method with the Hausdorff distance algorithm as the foundation is established; Finally, a simulation model of a 110kV/10kV non-effectively grounding system was established in MATLAB software. Through theoretical analysis and experimental verification, it was demonstrated that the proposed method can efficiently and reliably complete fault section localization in complex distribution networks with multiple nodes and single-phase ground faults. The proposed methodology demonstrates efficient and reliable fault section identification with computationally efficient operations, strong adaptability to varying network topologies and straightforward engineering implementation.

title: Section 2. Automation & Control Systems
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Electrical Engineering, Automation &amp; Control Systems (ElCon-EE)

title: Al-Driven Leaf Health Assessment, Damage Quantification, and Leaf Area Index Calculation
authors: Parth Shinde, Prathamesh Jagadale, Harshita Chandekar, Mohammad Khatib Shaikh, Priyanka More
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The given paper suggests a new method of plant leaf analysis that will combine disease detection and sophisticated biometric measurements. The proposed solution is a multidimensional pipeline with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to classify diseases, U-Net to implement semantic segmentation, and Generative Adversarial Networking (GAN) which are not very heavy. We do not follow the traditional method, which considers the disease identification in the process of evaluating the damage only, but we also employ the innovative and intact leaf reconstruction to determine the LAI of diseased leaves. The methodology can assist in practical applications in agriculture since it can offer a full-scale diagnostic solution that is compatible with edge devices.

title: From PBFT to Blockchain: A Comprehensive Survey of Byzantine Fault-Tolerant Consensus
authors: Akaash Vishal Hazarika, Joshit Mohanty
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Byzantine Fault Tolerance (BFT) has developed from a theoretical concept in distributed database reliability to the basic structure of today's decentralized finance and global infrastructure. This paper provides a holistic overview of BFT approach, delineating imperative strides from classical synchronous resolutions to the most recent blockchain protocols. We give a formal treatment of the transition of the architecture from quadratic complexity (O(n2)) in PBFT to linear scalability <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(O(n))$</tex> in HotStuff and probabilistic guarantees, as compared with Nakamoto Consensus. Moreover, we present a critical review of some recent developments in 2024 and 2025 related to Machine Learning-accelerated adaptive consensus, probabilistic relaxation for high volume trading, lightweight protocols for IoT. By combining theoretical limits with a range of practical issues such as state transfer and cryptographic overhead, we hope that the survey provides a structured roadmap for researchers tackling the scalability-security trade-offs in future distributed systems.

title: DeepVerify: Advanced AI Framework for Deepfake Detection and Media Authenticity Validation
authors: SV Shivani, Surabi C S, J. Merlin Mary Jenitha, L.Mary Gladence
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Deepfakes are becoming more realistic in nature. As a result, the authenticity associated with digital media is being questioned. In fact, most current detection systems are based on single-modal or loosely-coupled multi-modal analysis models. As a result, the generalization capability is poor in realistic scenarios. Moreover, there is also a lack of interpretability in the role of different modality components. In this paper, an end-to-end fully automated deepfake detection method is proposed. It is based on the fusion of deep learning-based face analysis and audio verification systems. The analysis of the video frames is conducted using TensorFlow-based CNN models. The models are designed to detect the visual manipulations in the form of texture artifacts, peculiar eye blinking signals, and inconsistencies in the face structure. The audio signals are analyzed using Librosa- based feature extraction models that consist of RNN- based models. Experimental analysis carried out using public deepfake databases confirms that the multimodal framework outperforms both audio and video-based models in accuracy and robustness. Also, with regard to the practical applicability and challenges faced while deploying such models, the proposed architecture considers delays and scalability. The system is built on the Django web framework, which is a communication platform for providing online access (via an online forms) and issuing instantaneous forensic analysis to verify the effectiveness of multimodal deep learning to assist in preventing the proliferation of deepfake technology.

title: Intelligent Educational Robots Aided Integration of Design Fundamentals and Specialized Courses Path Optimization and Software Implementation
authors: Zhi Fu, Tiancheng Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: With the booming of intelligent educational technology, educational robots have become key tools for curriculum reform. Design science, an interdisciplinary field blending art and engineering, faces a common issue in its basic courses: disjointed general knowledge and professional skills, failing to meet compound talent cultivation needs in the new era. This study uses intelligent educational robots as a carrier to explore optimizing the integration of general and professional content in design basic courses and proposes a software implementation scheme. It first analyzes the course's knowledge system and teaching features to identify integration pain points, then constructs an optimization model with strategies for course content reconstruction, modular design, and evaluation mechanisms. Finally, it develops educational robot teaching software with intelligent interaction, personalized recommendation, and real-time feedback, verifying its feasibility via teaching cases. Results show the integration path and software enhance teaching efficiency, boost interdisciplinary collaboration, and stimulate creativity, offering new ideas and technical support for intelligent reform of design courses.

title: Improving the Classification Accuracy of Breast Cancer Through Ensemble Learning Methods
authors: Saba Tahseen, Apurva Chaudhari, Nidhi Joshi Parsai, Anaswara Venunadh
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Strong diagnostic methods must be developed because breast cancer is still a main health concern. The effectiveness of ensemble learning techniques in categorizing breast cancer according to unlike clinical characteristics is examined in this work. With an emphasis on characteristics like average appearance, average diameter, and average perimeter, mean area, and mean smoothness, Breast Cancer dataset is used. This data set was separated between training and evaluation categories sets to guarantee a comprehensive evaluation process. To develop a performance baseline, two separate classifiers-Random Forest and LightGBM were first used. The Voting Classifier approach with soft voting was then used to create an ensemble model integrating these classifiers. For checking each model's performance, Accuracy, classification reports, and matrix of confusion are used. The results obtained revealed that the ensemble model outperformed the individual classifiers, achieving higher accuracy and more balanced classification metrics. The work emphasizes how ensemble approaches may improve the diagnostic precision of breast cancer classification, which may lead to better patient care and therapeutic approaches.

title: Dual-Transformer-Based Zero-Voltage Switching of Dual Active Bridge Converter
authors: Anuj Kumar, Mayank Kumar, Madhusudan Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: The dual active bridge (DAB) topology is increasingly used as an isolated and bidirectional dc-dc converter topology for medium to high voltage and medium to high power applications. A dual-transformer-based (DT-DAB) with active H-bridge with H6 structure (i.e., three-semiconductor switches in each leg) for the primary side and active H-bridge with H4 structure (i.e., two-semiconductor switches in each leg) for the secondary side is proposed in this article with bidirectional power transfer capability. The proposed dual-transformer-based zero-voltage switching of dual active bridge (DT-ZVSDAB) topology reduces current stress, better power distribution, and improved efficiency. A strategy of double frequency switching for auxiliary switches is presented. The proposed converter operated on different charging state threshold point as compared to conventional DAB. Simulation results with single-phase shift modulation technique are presented for characterization of the proposed converter at a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 ~\mathrm{V} / 100 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> system under the buck mode of operation.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)

title: Static Code Vulnerability Detection Framework for Secure Compiler Assisted Software Development Pipelines
authors: Arivazhagan P, Sunitha. P, M. Sakthivanitha, M. Vijaya Maheswari, S.K. Ramani, T. Thirumalaikumari
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: With the growing use of software systems in critical areas, securing the code while it is being developed has become a top priority. Traditional manual code reviews and testing can't often find subtler vulnerabilities for a significant security potential risk. This study fills the gaps of automated and accurate detection of static code vulnerabilities in secure compiler-aided software development pipelines. The goal is to have a strong structure that combines the static code analysis, the anomaly detection using machine learning, and the compiler-based instrumentation in order to detect potential vulnerabilities early in the development. The proposed framework uses the combinations of hybrid techniques of pattern-based vulnerability scanning, control-flow and data-flow analysis, and reinforcement learning models for prioritizing critical issues. Experiments were performed on commonly used benchmark datasets, such as the Juliet Test Suite, among several programming languages, and the detection accuracy of 96.3%, precision of 94.8%, recall of 95.1%, and F1-score of 94.9% were obtained, which were <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 - 12 \%$</tex> better than existing static analysis tools on all metrics. The results show the effectiveness of making compiler-level insights work along with machine learning models in proactive vulnerability detection. Conclusively it does not only improve the security posture of software development pipeline but also lowers the overhead of remediation of vulnerability post deployment which is a practical solution for secure software engineering.

title: Message from the Vice Chancellor
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)

title: Design, Control, and Experimental Validation of a Regulated Flyback Converter for Automotive Auxiliary Applications
authors: Om Prakash Bairwa, Naveen Yalla, Narendrababu A
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Flyback converters are widely used in automotive systems to provide isolated, regulated 12 V power to auxiliary loads such as infotainment, sensors, and dashboard electronics. These applications demand reliable operation across a wide input voltage range. This paper presents the design and closed-loop control of a 100 W flyback converter delivering 12 V output from a variable DC input. The topology offers simplicity, galvanic isolation, and cost-effectiveness—well-suited for automotive needs.
