A voltagemode PI controller ensures tight regulation under dynamic input and load conditions. Simulation and experimental results validate the converter's performance, demonstrating effective voltage regulation, transient response, and efficiency for automotive auxiliary applications.

title: Design of RSSI-Only UAV Path Planning for Search and Rescue: Greedy, Prudent, and DRQN-Based Algorithms in GPS-Denied Mountains
authors: Jiwoong Jeon, Hyerim Jeon, Jonghyeon Bae, Juyeol Park, Hoki Baek
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper proposes three algorithms for received signal strength indicator (RSSI)-only UAV path planning in SAR for GPS-denied mountainous environments. RSSI is attractive for early path planning due to low energy cost, implementation simplicity, and ubiquity, but severe shadowing causes large fluctuations and unstable localization. To cope with this, we design: (i) a Greedy method that chooses the initial heading via the strongest RSSI and proceeds while adjusting using prior branch comparisons; (ii) a Prudent method that performs an initial cross-shaped scan and fork-shaped local searches at each decision point for reliability under heavy shadowing; and (iii) a deep recurrent Q-network (DRQN)-based method that adaptively selects between Greedy and Prudent at each step according to changing shadowing. Simulations show the DRQN approach balances both strategies, matching Prudent's high success rate while maintaining decision-step counts close to Greedy, yielding strong adaptability and robustness in challenging shadowing conditions.

title: GNN-Based Multi-Agent RL for Dynamic AP-CPU Assignment in Cell-Free Massive MIMO
authors: Mahnoor Ajmal, Joohwan Park, Dongkyun Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The deployment of cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) systems at scale requires distributed processing across multiple central processing units (CPUs). The assignment of access points (APs) to CPUs directly impacts computational load distribution and network performance. Existing optimization methods rely on global channel knowledge and iterative solvers, limiting real-time applicability. This paper presents a graph neural network (GNN) framework with centralized training and decentralized execution (CTDE) for AP-CPU assignment. The network topology is represented as a graph where APs form nodes and edges capture interference relationships. Graph attention layers generate embeddings that enable each AP to select its CPU assignment through a sequential coordination mechanism. Simulation results for a network with 50 APs and 20 users demonstrate 78% reduction in CPU load imbalance compared to distance-based assignment, while maintaining equivalent throughput. The learned policy achieves the highest minimum user rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10.82 \text{bps} / \text{Hz}$</tex> among all methods. Additional scalability experiments across 30 to 70 APs confirm consistent improvements of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3}-\mathbf{8 2 \%}$</tex>, validating the effectiveness of learning-based approaches for backhaul resource management.

title: Harnessing Technology for SME Expansion and Market Reach: Evidence from Bahrain
authors: Waleed A. Aziz
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: This study examines Bahrain Development Bank's (BDB) innovation-driven digital transformation through the development of the Tijara Online Banking Platform. Designed to enhance SME financing in Bahrain, Tijara emerged as a strategic response to unmet market needs, accelerated by the COVID-19 crisis. Using qualitative methods, including interviews with key managers and thematic analysis of internal reports, the research explores the platform's journey from inception to implementation. Tijara's development was guided by agile project management, cross-functional collaboration, and a hybrid model combining internal and external resources. Leadership alignment, staff training, and cultural transformation were critical to success. The platform achieved strong adoption, improved loan processing, and earned external recognition. However, challenges remain, including SME digital literacy, system limitations, and regulatory adaptation. Recommendations include forming a dedicated innovation unit, enhancing stakeholder engagement, and integrating AI and open banking tools. The study suggests scaling Tijara across the GCC through regional partnerships and whitelabeling strategies.

title: Cyber-Resilient Load Frequency Control in PV-Thermal Hybrid System via 1+FD ADRC
authors: Raj Kumar Yadav, Ajit Kumar, Rajdeep Kumar, Chetna Sagar
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: The dynamic performance of power supplies for pulsed power loads necessitates rapid and precise output voltage and frequency adjustment. The model independence and parameter design of active disturbance rejection control (ADRC) with a linear extended state observer (LESO) make it special. Although the LESO's crossover frequency restricts its tracking speed, it effectively records total disturbances with minimal system information. The Quasi opposition whale optimiser (QOWOA) approach is used to attain the best tuning 1+FD ADRC settings. A comparison study of reported controllers in the recent literature, PIR and FOPIR, taking into account the impact of loading conditions, resonance cyber-attack (RsA), and system parameter uncertainty, validates the effectiveness of the created <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1} \boldsymbol{+} \mathbf{F D}$</tex> ADRC. When compared to alternative approaches, such as the PIR and FOPIR controller, the performance test results show that the 1+FD ADRC demonstrates improved dynamic response and resilience under investigated circumstances.

title: Distribution Network Energy Loss Cost Minimization by Network Reconfiguration
authors: Prashant Singh, Ajay Singh, Subho Paul
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Minimizing energy losses in distribution systems is a critical objective for enhancing operational efficiency and economic viability. This paper proposes a comprehensive mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) framework for Distribution System Reconfiguration (DSR), integrating YALMIP as the modeling interface and Gurobi as the optimization solver. By incorporating time-varying load profiles and detailed network constraints, the proposed model accurately captures real-world distribution system behavior. The method is valid on IEEE 33-bus and 136-bus test systems, Displaying meaningful energy loss cost reductions and operational improvements.

title: Research on the Prediction Algorithm of Fusion Feature of Breast Cancer Mri Molecular Typing Based on Deep Learning
authors: Li Xu, Liang Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Accurate molecular typing of breast cancer is the key to formulating effective treatment plans and improving cure rates. This study proposes a multi-scale attention fusion CNN algorithm to fuse breast cancer MRI multi-sequence features to achieve molecular typing prediction. The experiment used MRI image data of 312 breast cancer patients, which were divided into training set, validation set and test set according to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7: 1: 2$</tex> after preprocessing. The experimental simulation results show that the prediction accuracy of the algorithm for luminal A type, luminal B type, HER2 overexpression type and triple negative breast cancer reached 85.6 %, the precision rate was 84.2 %, the recall rate was 83.8 %, and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> value was 84.0 %. Compared with the support vector machine (SVM), the accuracy rate was increased by 12.3 %, compared with the ordinary CNN algorithm and the CNN algorithm based on a single sequence feature, it has obvious advantages in all indicators. The algorithm provides efficient and accurate technical support for the early diagnosis of breast cancer and has important clinical application value.

title: Consensus for linear multi-agent systems with robustly handle uncertainties in dynamic event-driven framework
authors: Yihang Weng, Xingzhan Liao, Gengxin Chen, Tingting Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to robustly handle the linear multi-agent systems’ parameter uncertainties within a dynamic event-driven framework, ensuring consensus is maintained even under varying conditions. Uncertainties are paradigm bounded, embodied in dynamically changing state models, exacerbating complexity. The proposed method leverages an event-driven control strategy that reacts to state changes rather than operating on a fixed time schedule, where the state changes are dependent on set triggers being met. The triggers, characterized by dynamic variability in conjunction with the comparative state of adjacent agents at event spans, consistently maintain a value greater than 0 to effectively preclude any occurrence of Zeno behavior. By rigorous proof, it is shown that a solution necessarily exists and a method for solving it is given. The final arithmetic example proves the validity of the conclusion.

title: Hate Speech Detection in Romanized Sinhala Text Using Multilingual Transformer Models
authors: Sakun Chamikara, Deshan Sumanathilaka
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Social media has become a key platform for communication, but it has also amplified the spread of hate speech, particularly in languages and dialects that lack digital resources and support. One such case is Romanized Sinhala, an informal writing style commonly used by Sri Lankans. Its irregular spelling, use of phonetic forms, and mixing with English present serious challenges for automatic hate speech detection. This project explores a deep learning-based solution to detect hate speech written in Romanized Sinhala, with the aim of strengthening online safety in low resource language settings. To build the system, the SOLD (Sinhala Offensive Language Dataset) was transliterated into Romanized Sinhala using a custom pipeline. Several state-of-the-art multilingual transformer models, including XLM-RoBERTa, IndicBERT, and distil-mBERT, were evaluated to identify the most effective approach. Among them, multilingual BERT (mBERT) achieved the best performance, with an accuracy of 81.28 %, F1-score of 0.78, and recall of 0.81 after fine-tuning on the Romanized dataset. These findings highlight that pre-trained multilingual models, when adapted appropriately, can be effective even for languages with limited digital infrastructure. Beyond technical outcomes, this project has broader social relevance. It provides a foundation for developing tools that can reduce harmful content in Sinhala-speaking online communities, support moderation efforts, and promote healthier digital spaces in Sri Lanka.

title: Deep Neural Networks-Powered Medical Image Classification Of X-Rays
authors: Kannaram Meghana, Vajrapu Sai Anurag, Parinitha Chilakala, Dr.M Purushotham Reddy
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Medical imaging is essential to healthcare because it makes internal body structures visible without causing harm, which aids in diagnosis and treatment. Because chest X-rays are affordable, easily accessible, and rapidly provide crucial information for identifying respiratory illnesses like COVID-19, pneumonia, and tuberculosis, they are frequently used among imaging techniques. But it’s difficult to interpret chest X-rays correctly; it takes specific knowledge. Human error and delays in diagnosis can result from even slight image variations. This study uses the Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture to propose an automated chest X-ray classification system in order to address this problem. In contrast to conventional convolutional neural networks (CNNs), which primarily concentrate on local image features, To understand global relationships across the image, ViTs segment images into patches and use self-attention mechanisms. Better pattern recognition is made possible by this. To increase model robustness, the approach involves preprocessing a labeled chest X-ray dataset and applying data augmentation techniques. The system seeks to improve diagnostic accuracy, decrease reliance on manual interpretation, minimize human error, and expedite decision-making by leveraging the ViT model. In turn, this enables medical professionals to treat patients in clinical settings more quickly and consistently.

title: An Algorithmic Approach to Optimizing Network Routing Using Dijkstra's Algorithm
authors: Siva Sibi M, Hepzibah Christinal A, Anitha J
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The high speed, increasing complexity of the modern computer networks have set up a dire requirement of the effective and dependable route systems to ensure the optimum levels of data flow. Conventional types of routing are usually unable to react to dynamically changing conditions of the network like congestion, fluctuating bandwidth capabilities, and node failures. The current paper proposes an algorithm- based approach to network routing optimization using binary variation of Dijkstra Algorithm which is a method that is dynamically calculate shortest and most stable path between nodes in real time. Three types of network parameters proposed, e.g., traffic load, link reliability, and latency, are implemented into the path- cost calculation in the proposed model which increases the adaptability in dynamic environments. To see the efficiency of the proposed approach with respect to the traditional shortest path routing algorithms, a simulated network topology was developed. Through experimental results, it is shown that the average data latency and the packet loss rate have been considerably reduced and throughput efficiency is increased. The results prove that the modified Dijkstra based algorithm has been an efficient and scalable computationally approach that is applicable to the routing of large scale and adaptive routing in the next generation network infrastructures.

title: Explainable AI Models for Predicting Major Complications in Thalassemia Patients
authors: Sithumi Bhagya, Thosini Kumarika, Shiromi Perera, Anuja Premawardhena
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Thalassemia is an inherited blood disorder in which hemoglobin is synthesized abnormally and requires lifelong blood transfusions and continuous clinical monitoring. Secondary complications such as diabetes and leg ulcers are common, significantly reducing quality of life and increasing healthcare costs. Early detection of these complications is crucial for early intervention. However, the black-box nature of most machine learning algorithms hinders their application in clinical practice. In this study, predictive models for diabetes and leg ulcer complications in thalassemia patients were developed using a clinical dataset with 357 real patient data including demographics, laboratory values, and treatment histories. The models tested were Random Forest, XGBoost, and a Neural Network implemented in PyTorch. The neural network performed best for both complications, with excellent F1-scores and AUC scores. To ensure interpretation, Explainable AI methods were used using SHAP values to identify important clinical risk factors, LIME to produce case-specific explanations, and adversarial reasoning to test operational “what-if” scenarios. The results identified hypogonadism, serum ferritin, hemoglobin, ALT and AST as the best predictors of diabetes and leg ulcers. Through the application of explainable high-performance neural networks, this study demonstrates the potential of Explainable AI systems in improving personalized risk stratification, risk identification, and clinical decision-making for thalassemia management.

title: Empowering Women in the Digital Economy: Mobile Wallets, Digital Cards, and the Moderating Role of Digital Financial Literacy
authors: Amanda Perera, Neranjana Ekanayake, Kaushalya Silva
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: The rapid expansion of digital payment systems has created new opportunities for advancing financial inclusion, particularly among working women in urban areas. This study examines the impact of mobile wallet usage and digital card usage on the financial inclusion of working women in the Colombo District of Sri Lanka, while investigating the moderating role of digital financial literacy. A quantitative research design was employed using a structured online questionnaire administered to employed women residing in the Colombo District. Data were collected through professional email networks and widely used social media platforms, with screening criteria ensuring that respondents were active users of digital payment tools. After data screening and cleaning, 389 valid responses were retained for analysis. The results show that both mobile wallet usage and digital card usage have a significant positive effect on financial inclusion by improving access to formal financial services and supporting convenient transactions. Digital financial literacy significantly moderates the relationship between mobile wallet usage and financial inclusion, indicating that higher digital competence strengthens the effectiveness of mobile-based financial tools. However, no significant moderating effect was observed between digital financial literacy and digital card usage, suggesting that cardbased payment systems are less sensitive to differences in literacy levels among urban working women. This study contributes to the digital finance and financial inclusion literature by empirically demonstrating the combined and moderating effects of digital payment usage and digital financial literacy within an urban, developing-country context. The findings offer policy-relevant insights for financial institutions, regulators, and technology providers seeking to enhance the financial inclusion of working women in Sri Lanka. These insights support digital finance initiatives for working women.

title: Smart Nutrient Management for Enhancing Sugarcane and Paddy Production
authors: Ms I NITHYA ME, LAKSHANA S, VARUNIKA R
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Fertilizers need to be utilized in the most efficient way possible to ensure that the farming process is more productive and less damaging to the environment. Traditional systems of fertilizer recommendations often focus on human experience and fixed recommendations, which cannot consider changing environmental and soil situations. The proposed system concerning the fertilizer recommendations applied with the use of machine learning is much more efficient in the reduction of fresh fertilizer application and thus more environmentally friendly. Based on a database of 9,999 farming records, comprising eight key parameters: temperature, humidity, moisture, crop type, soil type, nitrogen, potassium, and phosphorus, the presented system selects the most efficient among the seven possible types of fertilizers. The three models constructed and tested by the researchers were the Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM), the Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) and an ensemble of Decision Tree, Random Forest and LightGBM classifiers. The study of feature importance has shown that soil nutrients and climatic conditions were the most important factors in predicting fertilizers with an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4. 2 \%}$</tex>. LightGBM has been found to be more effective than XGBoost (89.4) and the ensemble model (84.9) with the greater accuracy. This is because the results indicate that application of boosting strategies may significantly improve making of decisions regarding the use of fertilizers using sustainable management. The developed system, by offering a scalable, intelligent, and easy-to-use framework, which can be incorporated with the real agricultural systems, allows the farmers the power to apply the fertilizers accurately.

title: Dual-Horizon Bandit Learning for UAV Path Planning with Trajectory Prediction
authors: Juner Qiu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: Finding routes for drones in complex, dynamic environments is the main problem for autonomous navigation systems. Traditional algorithms like A* and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) have some limitations. These limitations show in real-time response speed, ability to adapt to new environments, and computational speed. This article proposes a path planning framework. It is based on Dual Horizon Multi-Armed Bandit (DH-MAB) technology. This framework also includes trajectory prediction technology. It is designed to deal with dynamic obstacles and uncertain environments. This framework has two decision-making perspectives. One is short-term returns, and the other is long-term value estimation. With these two perspectives, the framework can balance decision-making. It also combines a trajectory prediction module with an encoder-decoder structure. This can help avoid obstacles and enable online learning in advance. Researchers did experiments. They built a dynamic simulation environment using real meteorological data. The experimental results show that the DH-MAB algorithm works better. In areas like success rate (95.2%), path cost (98.3%), and computational speed, it does better than traditional static algorithms and standard Multi-Armed Bandit (MAB) methods. This shows it is really useful and practical in dynamic path planning.

title: Organizing Committee: ICRSS 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)

title: Ceylonmate - Smart Tourism Companion for Foreign Travelers in Sri Lanka
authors: Thathsarani K K T, Pathiraja J.W.P. D, Moragaspitiya M.R.K. A, DE Soysa J C, Buddika Harshanath, N.C.P. Kumarapperuma
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: We present an AI Travel Agent by an ecosystem which is more than a set of static, digital or physical catalogues. It consists of four innovative modules: context-aware dynamic itinerary planning, intelligent safety management, community cultural experience marketplace, and spatio-semantic AR discovery. The planner will use Hybrid Filtering and MultiObjective Genetic Algorithms to optimize the itineraries according to current weather and user fatigue. It is secured using a Spatio-Temporal Risk Analysis Engine and offline-first kinematic anomaly detection. The platform connects tourists with villagers through a 360° VR “Virtual Experience Viewer” and an AI “Moments” social content engine to support rural empowerment. Lastly, the system integrates with a Visual Positioning System (VPS) for markerless AR reconstruction by providing significant implications in terms of personalization, rural safety and historical engagement.

title: Digital Beamforming Method Based on Space-based Distributed Array
authors: Tianlin Xu, Wupeng Xie, Ruilin Pei, Zhuang Li, Zhijie Qu, Xinzhu Liu, Yanan Guan, Yunfei Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Beamforming technology has been widely applied in radar systems and related fields. To overcome the limitations of traditional fixed arrays, such as restricted electrical aperture and high vulnerability to enemy detection, a digital beamforming method based on a space-based distributed array is proposed. A joint optimization cost function is established for the array element positions and the antenna array weight vectors, aiming to achieve a unit response in the target direction and null responses in the jamming directions while simultaneously minimizing the sidelobe level. The Lawson criterion is employed to transform the original minimax problem into a tractable weighted optimization form. Based on this formulation, an alternating iterative optimization strategy is adopted, in which the antenna array weight vector and the array element positions are updated sequentially and repeatedly until convergence. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reduces the peak sidelobe level (PSLL), suppresses strong jamming, and forms a high-performance beam pattern, indicating that the detection performance of space-based distributed arrays can be significantly enhanced.

title: Hybrid YOLOv8-VGG16-ResNet50 Architecture for Enhanced Multi-Class Rice Pest Detection on the IP102 Dataset
authors: Rajasekaran Arunachalam, J Mohana
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Rice pest infestation significantly reduces crop yield and poses a major challenge to sustainable agriculture. To address limitations in existing detection methods, this study proposes a hybrid YOLOv8-VGG16-ResNet50 architecture optimized using Adaptive Energy-based Harris Hawks Optimization (AE-HHO) for multi-class rice pest detection on the IP102 dataset. YOLOv8 is employed for accurate pest localization, while VGG16 and ResNet50 extract complementary texture and semantic features through an adaptive fusion strategy. Experimental evaluation demonstrates superior performance, achieving 97.2% classification accuracy, 94.6% F1-score, and 95.1% mAP@0.5, outperforming state-of-the-art approaches. Comparative analysis with a lightweight detector further highlights the effectiveness of the proposed framework. The results indicate that the proposed approach provides a robust and efficient solution for intelligent pest monitoring, contributing toward precision agriculture and sustainable crop management.

title: Optimizing DistilBERT for Low-Resource Sentiment Analysis: A Study on Global Unstructured L1-Norm Pruning and Accuracy-Efficiency Trade-Offs
authors: W.M.N.W.B. Manike, R.A. Wijesinghe, A.M.T.N Adasuriya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Transformer models are not always deployed to low-resource devices due to the computational intensity of the model. Although some distilled models, such as DistilBERT, provide a minimum level of efficiency, additional compression is required to apply to any edge application. This paper offers a systematic evaluation of L1-norm unstructured pruning globally on a DistilBERT model that has been fine-tuned on binary sentiment analysis on the Stanford Sentiment Treebank (SST-2) dataset. The approach consists of pruning the model sequentially by 10 % to 60 % to check its performance using the accuracy of a stratified validation set and inference latency on a CPU. These findings indicate that model accuracy is very robust, and it still has more than 99% of the baseline performance until 40% sparsity. Only at this stage major degradation has taken place, and the accuracy retention was found to be 97.25 % and 94.22% at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0 \%}$</tex> sparsity, respectively. An important conclusion here is that this unstructured sparsity fails to bring inference speedups in conventional CPU hardware, highlighting a lack of a connection between theory and practical efficiency improvements. As a result, the paper has found the best operational point to be <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> sparsity, which is an evidence-based guide to developers. The research also concludes that L1-norm unstructured pruning is a very effective method of maximizing model compression without significant accuracy impairment and thus can be used to achieve efficient deployment of NLP models in resource-constrained settings.

title: Emotion Detection from Facial Expressions Using Frame-Based Deep Networks
authors: Adalarasu B, R. A. Ayyapparajan, M. Umamaheswari, R. Prasanna, Sundaresh S
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Expressions are applied in personal health and social interactions, as expressions give information about the manner in which our emotional life is shared. There are different expressions that are developed in the human body, such as facial expressions, body expressions, vocal expressions, linguistic expressions, eye expressions, and behavioral expressions. Identifying expressions is the secret to success in both personal and business life. Knowledge of facial expressions is very important in the process of identifying the emotions of a person. Facial expressions are the most important aspect of the relationship of nonverbal communication that exists between individuals. The emotions are the point of focus of the concept, and the introduction of the concept of cognitive emotions AI, an AI system that is utilized for the recognition of the emotions present in the facial expressions of individuals in real time. In this case, the point of focus is only on some universal emotions, which include happiness, anger, sadness, love, surprise, fear, and indifference. There are different AI systems that are utilized in this process for the identification of the emotions that are recognized. The process of preprocessing, extracting the information, and the final prediction of the emotions of the subject occur as the system receives the input image.

title: Safe Vision: AI Surveillance and Alert System using YOLOv8 for Robbery Prevention
authors: Satheesh Kumar M, Pula Akash, Guntupalli Balashowreelu, Rajendran Thanikachalam, R. Saranya, Shahul Hameed C
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Robbery and gun related violence create serious public safety issues that need quick detection and action. Traditional CCTV systems are common, but they depend a lot on human operators. This reliance can cause problems like fatigue, distraction, and delays in response. To address these issues, this study introduces a smart, real time surveillance system that uses the YOLOv8 deep learning architecture. Unlike older methods that focus only on static object detection, this new approach combines object identification specifically for firearms, knives, and facial disguises with behavior assessment to identify high risk situations accurately. The model is trained on a well-organized, unified dataset, which helps it work well under different conditions, such as changing light, severe obstruction, and varying crowd sizes. When the system detects a possible threat, it sends an automatic alert to security personnel or law enforcement right away to reduce response time. Tested for use in crucial places like banks, ATMs, and retail stores, this solution changes surveillance from merely collecting evidence to actively enhancing public safety. By greatly cutting the time between detection and response, this framework provides a practical technological solution for preventing and reducing violent crime in cities.

title: Bell Slotted Circular Patch Antenna for 5G and Beyond
authors: Anslin Star R, Anitha G
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: A new circular microstrip patch antenna featuring a unique bell-shaped slot specifically for millimeter wave usage. The antenna is fabricated on a Rogers RT/duroid 5870 substrate with circular patch radius of 0.933 mm. The design resonates at 59.75 GHz that achievs excellent impedance matching. A return loss of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{6 0. 5 ~ d B}$</tex> and a peak gain of 7.66 dBi is achieved when the bell-shaped slot is incorporated to the patch. A 1.4 GHz bandwidth is obtained which provides the reliable operation across the target spectrum. The bell slot geometry allows the antenna to remain compact without sacrificing performance making it an ideal fit for the next generation of 5 G and beyond wireless communication technologies.

title: Fuzzy-DRL: Cost and Energy Efficient Task Scheduling in Cloud-Fog Computing Environment
authors: G Sreenivasan, Pasupuleti Prathima, Johnson Kolluri, Kanneboina Ashok, Raveendrababu Vempati, Santhosh Kumar Medishetti
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Task Scheduling (TS) is a key feature of Cloud-Fog Computing (CFC) that ensures the efficient execution of latency-restrictive and computation-intensive applications and ensures the efficient utilization of the system and its sustainability. In this paper, a hybrid Fuzzy Logic and Deep Reinforcement Learning (FL-DRL)-based task scheduling model that co-optimizes makespan, energy cost, and execution cost will be proposed to operate in heterogeneous cloud-fog systems. The utilization of fuzzy logic is used to deal with uncertainty and dynamically prioritize tasks depending on the nature of workload and resource conditions and a deep reinforcement learning agent is used to learn the best policies of task-to-resource allocation through ongoing interaction with the environment. The suggested framework is tested on Alibaba GPUs workload traces and analysed in the SimPy simulation environment and evaluated in terms of its performance in comparison with ACO, pure DRL, and EEOA algorithms. Experimental outcomes show that FL-DRL can achieve as much as 60 % of makespan reduction, 57 % energy savings, and 45 % baseline method execution costs reduction with different task loads. These significant improvements confirm that integrating uncertainty-aware fuzzy reasoning with learning-driven decision-making enables robust and scalable multi-objective scheduling. The suggested FL-DRL model is an efficient framework that can be applied to energy-efficient, low-cost, and high-performance tasks scheduling in the next-generation cloud-fog computing systems.

title: Integrating Data Science and Meerkat Clan Optimization for Predictive Cloud Task Scheduling
authors: Devanga Dharani Lakshmi, Raveendrababu Vempati, Maheswari Bandi, A Chandrakala, C Veena, Santhosh Kumar Medishetti
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: An intelligent predictive task scheduling framework for cloud computing is presented that integrates data science techniques with the Meerkat Clan Algorithm (MCA) to enhance scheduling efficiency, reliability, and scalability. The framework employs predictive modelling to estimate task execution time, virtual machine (VM) failure probability, and expected throughput, enabling proactive and informed scheduling decisions in dynamic cloud environments. These predictive insights are incorporated into an MCA-based multiobjective optimization strategy that simultaneously minimizes makespan and VM failure rate while maximizing throughput. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is evaluated using realistic Alibaba GPU workload traces and implemented within the SimPy discrete-event simulation environment to accurately capture cloud system dynamics. Comparative experimental results against Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL), and Enterprise Development Optimizer Algorithm (EEOA) demonstrate substantial performance improvements. On average, the proposed scheduler achieves makespan reductions of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 5. 4 \%}$</tex>, lowers VM failure rate by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 1. 5 \%}$</tex>, and improves throughput by 34.4 % across varying workload scales. These results confirm that combining predictive analytics with clanbased meta-heuristic optimization provides a robust and scalable solution for next-generation, data-intensive cloud computing task scheduling.

title: A Decentralized Cloud Computing Enhancement Framework Using Blockchain for Resource Allocation
authors: Ganji Ramanjaiah, Balajee Maram, Tummala Srinivasa Ravi Kiran, Komanduri Venkata Sesha Sai Ramakrishna
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: We can use computing power as and when required from a common pool because of cloud technology. Even so, older clouds cannot work effectively. Leaving space underutilized increases the vulnerability of the hardware hub, in addition, they are not necessarily dispersed. A recent system uses block chain technology for increased trustworthiness, transparency, and autonomy of cloud resource sharing. A block chain refers to the digital ledger that is unchangeable and works offsetting on the cloud and other servers. A total of 50 computer network is recreated for running 1000 jobs. Paraphrase this (11 words). New configuration makes the resources work at 85% efficiency. The tasks are divided fairly among the participants who also contribute to the scoreboard. Everything is a mess but 98% is basically done for sure. Response times shrink versus older setups. Future networks refer to the 5G and beyond communications. Cloud services can prove beneficial in this area.

title: Proofreading and Editing in Student and Research Publication Contexts: International Perspectives: Nigel Harwood: [Book Review]
authors: Cooper Smitherman
abstract: Presents reviews for the following list of books, Proofreading and Editing in Student and Research Publication Contexts: International Perspectives.

title: Experimental Analysis of a Self-Coherent M-QAM Receiver by Means of Recurrent Optical Spectrum Slicing and Direct Detection
authors: Kostas Sozos, Francesco Da Ros, Metodi Yankov, Stavros Deligiannidis, George Sarantoglou, Charis Mesaritakis, Adonis Bogris
abstract: High order modulation formats constitute the most prominent way for increasing spectral efficiency in transmission systems. Coherent transceivers that support such higher order formats require heavy digital signal processing (DSP), which increases the power consumption of coherent pluggables, well above the intensity modulation and direct detection (IM/DD) counterparts. Self-coherent or phase retrieval methods have emerged as potential solutions, trying to combine the merits of coherent technology with the simplicity of direct detection. In this work, we experimentally demonstrate the reception of quadrature amplitude modulation (QAM) modulation formats based on direct detection aided by the recurrent optical spectrum slicing (ROSS) photonic accelerator, utilizing minimal DSP and low modulator driving voltages. We experimentally demonstrate 32 Gbaud QAM-4/16 for 25 km, 50 km and 75 km in the C-band aided by a linear digital equalization and the use of programmable photonics as recurrent optical spectrum slicers. We showcase successful detection with driving swings below Vπ/3 in contrast to the full swing required by conventional coherent transceivers. We further improve the system performance utilizing geometric constellation shaping. Finally, we explore the potential power consumption improvement for the next-generation 1.6T pluggables, showcasing over 40% reduction with respect to the most lightweight state of the art coherent solutions reported in literature.

title: ELA-FPM: An Energy-Efficient LUT-based Approximate Floating-Point Multiplier for Neural Network Acceleration
authors: Mahla Salehi Sheikhali Kelayeh, Asma Naserirad, Sahand Divsalar, Omid Salehinia, Shaghayegh Vahdat
abstract: As artificial intelligence applications grow in practicality and complexity, efficient hardware implementation of these algorithms turns into a critical issue. Floating-point multiplication is one of the most fundamental, yet power-hungry, operations in modern artificial intelligence algorithms. This paper proposes a novel floating-point approximate multiplier, called ELA-FPM, which uses a combination of static segmentation of input operands and an energy-efficient lookup table-based method to significantly alleviate the overhead of mantissa multiplication, which is the most power-consuming operation in a floating-point multiplier. The superiority of ELA-FPM manifests itself as delay, power, and area improvements of up to 68.3%, 95.3%, and 92.1%, compared to an exact floating-point multiplier and improvements of up to 65.6%, 76%, and 71%, compared to other state-of-the-art approximate multipliers. Furthermore, ELA-FPM is tested on some of the most famous convolutional neural network models, demonstrating a maximum accuracy drop of 0.67% compared to a model that uses exact multipliers.

title: Active Coupling Control-Based Transient Frequency–Voltage Coordinated Support for Grid-Following Inverters
authors: Jingrong Yu, Linyi Ju, Yijun Wang, Yan Li, Jiaqi Yu
abstract: The modern power systems with high proportion of renewable energy features “weak frequency” and “weak voltage” characteristics, imposing stringent requirement on the transient frequency and voltage support of grid-following (GFL) inverters in renewable energy generation systems. Existing grid support methods for GFL inverters are unable to simultaneously address both rate of change of frequency (ROCOF) and rate of change of voltage (ROCOV) under grid disturbances such as voltage sags. To overcome this limitation, the proposed method investigates the coupling mechanism between transient frequency and voltage when the grid impedance is of resistive-inductive characteristics. On the basis of this coupling mechanism, a frequency–voltage coordinated support method is proposed by employ an active coupling gain between the active power and reactive power. Compared with the existing method, the proposed method employs the active coupling rather than decouple ideas to enable the joint optimization of transient rate of change of ROCOF and ROCOV, yielding better grid compatibility with wide variation range of short-circuit ratio (SCR) and resistance-to-inductance(X/R) ratio, and smaller extrema of ROCOF and ROCOV. Both simulation and experimental results verified the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed method.

title: Enhancing Energy Efficiency in STAR-RIS-Assisted Massive MIMO-RSMA Networks
authors: Ridho Hendra Yoga Perdana, Thong-Nhat Tran, Wonseok Lee, Young Jeon, Taejoon Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper investigates energy-efficiency maximization (EEM) for downlink simultaneously transmitting and reflecting (STAR-RIS)–assisted massive MIMO (mMIMO) networks employing rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA). The energy efficiency formulation problem is a non-convex mixed-integer program, driven by (i) an upper bound on the allocable common SE, (ii) the transmit-power budget at the base station (BS), and (iii) the discrete phase shifts at the STAR-RIS, which together preclude direct optimal solutions. To address this, we first relax the problem and decouple it into two subproblems—phase-shift design and beamforming—that are solved in an alternating fashion. The phase-shift subproblem is handled via a bisection search, while the beamforming subproblem is tackled using an inner-approximation (IA) procedure that converts the original non-convex constraints into tractable surrogates. For real-time operation, we design a deep-learning (DL) framework that infers optimal phase shifts and precoding matrices under varying system parameters. Simulations demonstrate that the proposed scheme yields notable EE gains, and we further quantify the impact of the number of STAR-RIS and STAR-RIS elements on the overall system performance.

title: Finite-Time Disturbance Observer-Based Adaptive Second-Order Sliding Mode Control for Parallel Connected DC–DC Buck Converters With Nonideal Switches
authors: Cong Wang, Li Li, Minghui Yao, Yan Niu, Qiliang Wu
abstract: Parallel connected DC–DC buck converters (PCBCs) are inevitably subjected to the disturbances caused by nonideal active switches and uncertain passive components, which poses a significant challenge for achieving both precise current sharing and robust voltage regulation. To address the coupling between inductor current and output voltage, a multilayer control framework equipped with a slow voltage controller and fast current controllers is developed, which can automatically deal with the load variations, and be easily extended to PCBC with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N$</tex-math> </inline-formula> modules. Subsequently, the nonideal characteristics in PCBC are modeled as disturbances. The constructed finite-time disturbance observer (FTDO) can effectively estimate the disturbances, and then a feedforward compensation is made by using the obtained estimates. To mitigate chattering with the further aim of enhancing control accuracy of output voltage, a finite-time stable adaptive second-order sliding mode (SOSM) control is proposed, in which the adaptation algorithm achieves rapid reaching of sliding manifold, and avoids excessive control action. The presented control strategy can obtain fast and accurate tracking of output voltage, as well as balanced current sharing. Experimental results are presented to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Sparse SAR Separated Iterative Imaging for Stationary and Moving Targets
authors: Yufan Song, Fuxuan Cai, Cuijun Liu, Jingjing Zhang, Liping Wang, Wen Hong, Hui Bi
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imaging, moving targets often appear along with the stationary scene in the surveillance regions. The imaging results of moving targets are defocused by using traditional imaging methods since the velocities of them are unknown. Most ground moving target imaging (GMTIm) methods ignore the stationary targets when analyzing the signal of moving target. Therefore, effective imaging methods for both stationary and moving targets focusing in the same surveillance region are worth investigating. In this paper, the stationary and moving targets sparse SAR separated iterative imaging model and methods are proposed and used for the real data processing. Firstly, the two-dimensional (2-D) stationary and moving targets separated imaging model is constructed based on approximate observation. Then a novel sparse iterative reweighted algorithm (SIRA) is proposed to solve the constructed model and achieve the focusing of stationary and moving targets simultaneously. Furthermore, on the basis of SIRA, we improve the constraints on the stationary targets to enhance the recovery accuracy about them, and use alternating direction multiplier method (ADMM) to solve the novel optimization problem. Finally, the normalized relative speed estimation (NRSE)-based imaging method for moving target is introduced. Compared with traditional matched filtering (MF)-based and sparse-driven imaging methods, the proposed methods achieve separated reconstruction of moving and stationary targets, respectively while improving the imaging quality. Experiments based on simulated and real Fucheng-1 SAR dataset verify the proposed methods.

title: Design of a Multimodal Fusion Architecture for Anomaly Detection in Smart Greenhouse Environments
authors: JooWon Jeong, KiWoong Song, Hyun Yoe
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: This paper presents a multimodal fusion architecture for anomaly detection in smart greenhouse environments. The framework integrates image streams and environmental sensor time-series to address the limitations of single-modality systems. The design includes an image encoder, sensor encoder, and fusion layer, enabling scalable and real-time operation. A simulation-based evaluation, derived from structural module properties, suggests improved detection sensitivity and reduced false-alarm rates. Although theoretical, the findings indicate the potential of the proposed architecture as a foundation for future implementation and validation in next-generation smart agriculture monitoring systems.

title: 2025 3rd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Power Engineering (AIPE)
authors: N/A

title: LISK: A High-Performance In-Memory Learned Index for Variable-Length String Keys
authors: Zhaole Chu, Yigui Yuan, Junli Wang, Xiaoliang Wang, Peiquan Jin
abstract: Learned index has emerged as a new indexing technique that leverages machine learning to accelerate in-memory data processing. However, current learned indexes are primarily designed to index numeric keys and lack robust support for variable-length string keys. In this paper, we propose a novel in-memory learned index called LISK (<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</u>earned <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">I</u>ndex for <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</u>tring <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">K</u>eys) to support string keys. The novelty of LISK is two-fold. First, we propose a trie-like structure to address the limitations of linear models in fitting string keys. Each trie node indexes 8-byte key slices, which are organized as learned sub-indexes or B+-trees. Second, we present a new structure for learned sub-indexes, namely TLS (<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</u>wo-layer <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</u>earned <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</u>ubindex), which is tailored to handle the complex distribution of string keys. TLS utilizes three key designs to improve the overall performance: (1) a two-phase hybrid index construction, (2) a second-derivative-based data partitioning, and (3) a cachefriendly overflow node design. We conduct extensive experiments on five datasets and six workloads to compare LISK with seven existing indexes, including five trie-based indexes and the state-of-the-art learned index LITS. The experimental results show that LISK achieves an average 1.99× (up to 7.87×) higher throughput across the six workloads on real-world datasets. Specifically, compared with LITS, LISK achieves an average 1.42× (up to 1.91× ) higher throughput. The source code of LISK is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/suibianll/LISK/tree/master</uri>.

title: Virtual Reality Laboratory for Teaching Power Systems in Electrical Engineering Undergraduate Courses
authors: Daniel Rebouças Jaguaribe, Gustavo Alves, Fernando Lessa Tofoli, Ruth Pastôra Saraiva Leão, Lucas Silveira Melo, Raimundo Furtado Sampaio, Antônio José Melo Leite Júnior, Giovanni Cordeiro Barroso
abstract: The study of power substations is fundamental to the training of electrical engineers, as these facilities are crucial to power system operation, ensuring the reliability and efficiency of energy distribution. However, traditional teaching methods often face limitations in offering practical and immersive experiences. Given this gap, this work presents a Virtual Reality Substation Laboratory (VRSL), developed based on a 69/13.8-kV substation at the Federal University of Ceará, Fortaleza, Brazil. The VRSL provides a free-to-use educational platform that allows teachers and students to explore the structural and operational aspects of power substations, including equipment operation and fault simulations. Its integration into two substation-related courses over a three-year period engaged more than 120 students by combining virtual reality (VR) with conventional lectures. The triangulation of quantitative and qualitative analyses demonstrates substantial improvements in academic performance, student engagement, and comprehension of complex substation concepts. By bridging theoretical knowledge and practical simulation, the VRSL enhances engineering education and prepares students for real-world challenges in power systems.

title: Identification of Critical Frequencies in Wideband Near-Field Spatial Scans
authors: Tomas Monopoli, Xinglong Wu, Cheng Yang, Christian Schuster, Sergio Amedeo Pignari, Johannes Wolf, Flavia Grassi
abstract: Identifying critical frequencies of a device under test (DUT) from its near-field scans is an essential step in integrating near-field scanning into electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) testing. Indeed, real electronic devices may exhibit radiated emissions at multiple frequencies, which are unknown a priori. To this end, three computational methods are introduced to identify the frequencies at which the DUT is radiating: the space-filled spectrogram analysis, the grey level co-occurrence matrix approach, and spatial fast Fourier transform approach. Although a single general solution is not provided, each method leverages different properties of the measurements to extract significant features and identify frequencies of interest. Therefore, the choice of which method to apply depends on the characteristics of the DUT and on the requirements of the specific measurement campaign. The proposed methods are evaluated on a common dataset, assessing their computational efficiency, sensitivity to patterns, and robustness against noise. Case studies demonstrate their successful application in real-world scenarios and their comparative analysis provides insights into selecting the most appropriate approach for a specific application.

title: Towards Deploying VLA Without Fine-Tuning: Plug-and-Play Inference-Time VLA Policy Steering Via Embodied Evolutionary Diffusion_supp1-3678455.mp4
authors: Zhuo Li
abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have demonstrated significant potential in real-world robotic manipulation. However, pre-trained VLA policies still suffer from substantial performance degradation during downstream deployment. Although fine-tuning can mitigate this issue, its reliance on costly demonstration collection and intensive computation makes it impractical in real-world settings. In this work, we introduce VLA-Pilot, a plug-and-play inference-time policy steering method for zero-shot deployment of pre-trained VLA without any additional fine-tuning or data collection. We evaluate VLA-Pilot on both simulation and real-world experiments across distinct robotic embodiments. Experimental results demonstrate that VLA-Pilot substantially boosts the success rates of off-the-shelf pre-trained VLA policies, enabling robust zero-shot generalization to diverse downstream tasks and embodiments. Experimental videos and code are available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://rip4kobe.github.io/vla-pilot/</uri>.

title: BANet: Bidirectional Feature Aggregation and Adaptive Multi-Scene Perception-Based Lane Detection for Autonomous Driving
authors: Yunzuo Zhang, Zhiwei Tu, Weiqi Lian, Yubo Hu, Shibo Sun, Yaoge Xiao, Yu Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Lane detection is a hot topic in the field of autonomous driving, providing essential assistance to vehicles. Due to the lack of effective integration among hierarchical features and insufficient adaptability to variations of lanes under diverse background conditions, accurate lane detection remains challenging. To address the aforementioned issues, we propose BANet, an efficient lane detection network based on bidirectional feature aggregation and adaptive multi-scene perception, aiming to improve the lane detection accuracy under different backgrounds. Firstly, we propose a Bidirectional Feature Aggregation Module (BFAM) that, via the proposed composition built from PG2f, effectively integrates complementary information across scales, improving anchor localization. Secondly, we propose an Adaptive Multi-scene Perception Module (AMPM) that learns scene-aware spatial information to enhance lane-relevant cues, addressing the no-visual-clue problem. Finally, we propose an Edge Refinement Attention (ERA), which models spatial and channel information in parallel to refine lane representations. The experimental results on the CULane, CurveLanes, and TuSimple datasets show that the proposed network outperforms existing methods and exhibits excellent performance in the most challenging scenes.

title: Enhancing Electric Vehicles Charging Service Through Mixed Charging: A Two-Stage Distributionally Robust Optimization Model
authors: Weiwen Zhou, Yiyang Peng, Zhuowei Wang, Anthony Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In response to the need for sustainable transportation, this study addresses the challenges of providing the charging service to fluctuating and geographically dispersed electric vehicles (EVs) charging demands. This work proposed a mixed EV charging system that incorporates mobile charging services to complement fixed charging stations. The overall coverage rate of charging services is enhanced under this charging system. To cope with the complexities of estimating the EV charging demands due to evolving adoption of EVs and uncertain external factors (e.g.,driver’s car use behaviors, fluctuations in electricity rates), a two-stage distributionally robust optimization (DRO) model is developed to determine the placement and capacity of fixed charging stations, the location of mobile depots, and the number of mobile trucks to deploy. Acase study on HongKong’s North District is conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed model by solving it using a decomposition algorithm, through which the computational complexity is significantly reduced by iteratively solving the first andsecond stage problems. This study demonstrates that the mixed charging strategy significantly improves coverage, even with limited data, and highlights how investment levels affect performance. The mixed system improved coverage by 20% compared to fixed only infrastructure under the budget of 90million HKD. Additionally, the case study shows an increase of average coverage rate as budget level increases, which offers insights into the investment levels required to achieve a specific coverage rate. The contributions include a novel mixed charging strategy and a two-stage DRO model, providing a robust and scalable framework to support EV charging infrastructure planning under uncertainty.

title: Noncommutative Quantum Dynamics for Feature Representation Learning
authors: Hirokazu Shimauchi
abstract: We propose an explicit feature representation for supervised learning derived from short quantum dynamical trajectories composed of generally noncommuting operations, conceptually inspired by reservoir computing in that fixed, untrained dynamics are coupled to a simple trained readout. For each input, a fixed-depth circuit generates a sequence of prefix states, and features are formed by concatenating expectation values of a predetermined set of single- and two-qubit Pauli observables recorded across depths; multiple randomized but fixed circuit instantiations are aggregated to improve stability. A classical l2- regularized logistic-regression readout is trained on the resulting explicit features. To isolate representational effects, all methods are evaluated under identical preprocessing, repeated stratified holdout splits, and a unified model-selection protocol on a controlled subset of 11 tabular classification datasets, and are compared against classical baselines, including random Fourier features and second-degree polynomial expansions, as well as reservoir-style quantum baselines, with feature dimensionality matched when feasible. We further examine controlled variants targeting commutativity, entangling structure, history concatenation, and observable choice. A staged evaluation contrasts an idealized exact-expectation regime with finite-shot estimation under fixed and larger measurement budgets, thereby assessing sensitivity to sampling noise after re-tuning regularization. Beyond predictive performance, we analyze the induced geometry via empirical kernel spectra and effective-dimension diagnostics to contextualize the representations from a regularization-aware capacity perspective. Empirically, the strongest support concerns noncommutativity and history concatenation, whereas the contribution of the entangling block is mixed; the trajectory-based features induce empirically distinct kernel geometries, while finite-shot robustness remains strongly task-and configuration-dependent.

title: Improving the Restore Performance of Fine-Grained Deduplication on Docker Container Image Storage
authors: Haoliang Tan, Wenhao Ou, Xiangyu Zou, Lisha Qin, Cai Deng, Zhaoquan Gu, Wen Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: The rapid growth of container images leads to heavy storage pressures on image registries. Fine-grained deduplication, such as at the file and chunk levels, is a promising technique for reducing the storage space in image registries, compared to Docker's native coarse-grained image layer deduplication. However, fine-grained deduplication often incurs significant image restoration latency due to fragmented I/Os, resulting in up to 8× restoration I/O slowdowns. Moreover, existing restore-optimized deduplication techniques are not tailored to the characteristics of the container image, resulting in low deduplication ratios of images. Consequently, restoring performance remains a critical barrier to the practical adoption of fine-grained deduplication in container image registries. To address this challenge, we propose MiDedup, a restore-friendly fine-grained deduplication approach tailored for container image registries. MiDedup is built on an underexplored observation: the redundancy across layers of images follows a non-uniform distribution. Based on this insight, MiDedup introduces three core techniques: ① Across-layer-aware reorganization, which reorganizes deduplicated data into a compact, sequential layout, significantly reduces fragmented I/Os during image restore. ② Popularity-aware rewriting, which selectively rewrites hot image layers to further improve the balance between deduplication ratio and restore performance. ③ Hybrid-granularity deduplication, which combines file-level and chunk-level deduplication to reduce metadata and fragmented data. Experiments on the real-world Docker image dataset show that MiDedup reduces the 50–80% restore I/O overhead of fine-grained deduplication, which is comparable to the restore performance of native layer-level deduplication, while achieving 1.2–7.3× higher deduplication ratios.

title: 5G-Enabled Cyber-Physical Infrastructure for Intelligent Automotive Battery Management
authors: Eduardo Hidalgo-Fort, Pedro Blanco-Carmona, Manuel Martín-Romero, Antonio Torralba, Fernando Muñoz-Chavero, Ramón González-Carvajal
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This paper presents a prototype of an architecture of a cyber-physical system (CPS) for extending the lifetime of electric vehicle batteries. The proposed solution features a secure, AUTOSAR-compliant architecture with wireless connection designed to facilitate advanced optimization techniques for battery usage. Central to this architecture is the integration of a cloud-based complex Digital Twin - built upon high-precision behavioural models - and the deployment on the vehicle of corresponding simplified models for edge computing. This enables real-time estimation of battery status and optimal usage parameters locally, without relying on continuous cloud connectivity. To address performance degradation over time, the system incorporates a methodology for remote, wireless retraining of edge models using the cloud Digital Twin via 5G communication. The proposed solution has been fully implemented and validated in laboratory using a real battery yielding State-of-Charge (SoC) predictions with an error margin below 3.5%. Additionally, behavioural models were also developed for State-of-Health (SoH), Remaining Useful Life (RUL), State-of-Security (SoS) and State-of-Power (SoP). These models also provide, in a second iteration analysis, values for the maximum current to demand from the battery and the optimal operating temperature. Collectively, the proposed CPS architecture enables advanced battery management with a positive impact in the battery lifetime.

title: Quantum-Enhanced Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces with Hierarchical AI Orchestration for Secure 6G Networks
authors: Ali Ali, Demos Serghiou, Anton Tishchenko, Pei Xiao, Mohsen Khalily
abstract: The forthcoming 6G wireless systems must simultaneously achieve extreme capacity, ultra-low latency, and quantum-grade security. While classical physical-layer security techniques provide probabilistic protection, free-space quantum key distribution (QKD) suffers degradation under mobile and turbulent conditions. This paper introduces a Quantum-Enhanced Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (QE-RIS) framework that synergistically integrates quantum photonics with classical electromagnetic control via hierarchical AI orchestration. Our design embeds quantum-capable devices into RIS unit cells operating at 12.5GHz (RF) and 850nm (optical) wavelengths and employs a dual-loop control mechanism, a fast loop at 5 ms and a mid loop at 50 ms, to dynamically select classical, quantum, or hybrid modes based on real-time signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and quantum bit error rate (QBER). Monte Carlo simulations with 20 independent channel realisations demonstrate SNR gains up to 29.3 dB for RIS with N = 512 elements and 4-bit phase quantisation, QBER reductions ranging from 20% to 35% compared to free-space QKD, control latencies below 10 ms on edge-class hardware, and secure key rates exceeding 1 Mbit s−1 under moderate turbulence. In this paper, we detail implementation assumptions, control information elements (IEs), and key performance indicators (KPIs) aligned with emerging standardisation efforts. The proposed framework offers a rigorous and reproducible foundation for advancing quantum-enhanced wireless communications.

title: Miniaturized Wideband Filtering Antenna on Glass Substrate for mm-Wave AiP
authors: Sha Xu, Hongsheng Cai, Mankai Jiang, Qiongsen Wu
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents a miniaturized wideband millimeter-wave (mm-wave) filtering antenna with high out-of-band suppression, implemented on a glass substrate using through-glass via (TGV) technology. The antenna topology integrates a primary radiating patch with parasitic elements and a set of planar inverted-F antennas (PIFAs). The PIFA structures, enabled by metallized vias, are key to achieving both a wide impedance bandwidth and a sharp gain roll-off in the lower stopband. Concurrently, two sets of slots etched onto the main patch introduce two additional radiation nulls to ensure strong suppression in the upper stopband. The single antenna element maintains a compact footprint of 0.7 λ0 × 0.56 λ0 × 0.065 λ0 (@ 24 GHz). For experimental validation, a 1 4 linear array was fabricated and measured. The measured results demonstrate a 10-dB bandwidth of 21.8 - 30 GHz ( 31.7%) and a peak gain of 10.7 dBi. The three well-defined radiation nulls collectively provide over 13 dB of out-of-band suppression. The combination of wideband filtering performance, compact footprint, and a silicon-compatible glass-based platform make the proposed antenna an excellent candidate for highly integrated Antenna-in-Package (AiP) solutions for 5G and beyond mm-wave communication systems.

title: Event-based Facial Keypoint Alignment via Cross-Modal Fusion Attention and Self-Supervised Multi-Event Representation Learning
authors: Donghwa Kang, Junho Kim, Dongwoo Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Event cameras offer unique advantages for facial keypoint alignment under challenging conditions, such as low light and rapid motion, due to their high temporal resolution and robustness to varying illumination. However, existing RGB facial keypoint alignment methods do not perform well on event data, and training solely on event data often leads to suboptimal performance because of its limited spatial information. Moreover, the lack of comprehensive labeled event datasets further hinders progress in this area. To address these issues, we propose a novel framework based on cross-modal fusion attention (CMFA) and self-supervised multi-event representation learning (SSMER) for event-based facial keypoint alignment. Our framework employs CMFA to integrate corresponding RGB data, guiding the model to extract robust facial features from event input images. In parallel, SSMER enables effective feature learning from unlabeled event data, overcoming spatial limitations. Extensive experiments on our real‑event E‑SIE dataset and a synthetic‑event version of the public WFLW‑V benchmark show that our approach consistently surpasses state‑of‑the‑art methods across multiple evaluation metrics.

title: Bearing-Only Formation Control of Nonholonomic Unicycles: A Conditional Disturbance Utilization Method
authors: Qin Wang, Hanyu Yin, Yanmeng Zhang, Yang Yi, Wenbing Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Maintaining a stable formation among nonholonomic unicycles presents a significant challenge, particularly when subjected to real-world disturbances such as wheel slipping and motor noise. To tackle this issue, we propose a conditional disturbance utilization (CDU) framework that selectively uses favorable disturbances rather than simply rejecting all. First, the disturbance observer (DO) is employed to establish the informational foundation for subsequent conditional utilization decisions. And, the CDU mechanism dynamically decides whether the disturbance aligns with the desired control objective. Moreover, incorporating bearing rigidity achieves global stability with fast convergence. Specifically, the proposed potential function, constructed from bearing information, mitigates disturbance driven collision risks and ensures safety. Finally, simulations and experiments validate the proposed CDU-based formation control. The results demonstrate substantial improvements in responsiveness, and efficiency when compared to disturbance suppression method, highlighting the superiority of the proposed framework.

title: Towards Generalized and Explainable Face Anti-Spoofing via Decomposition–Fusion Networks
authors: Dhimas Arief Dharmawan, Anto Satriyo Nugroho, Seviko Attalarik Pramayudha Handoko
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biometrics, Behavior, and Identity Science
abstract: This paper presents DFNet, a decomposition–fusion network for generalized and interpretable face anti-spoofing. DFNet comprises two explicitly designed branches—a decomposition branch and a fusion branch—that jointly enhance robustness and interpretability in presentation attack detection. The decomposition branch extracts multi-level representations through a two-stage process, where coarse features and saliency maps are first computed to emphasize prominent regions, followed by the suppression of these regions to facilitate fine-grained feature extraction from less dominant areas. The fusion branch integrates the coarse and fine-grained streams using a residual architecture augmented with convolutional block attention modules, while a dynamic weighting mechanism adaptively balances their contributions during feature fusion. DFNet is extensively evaluated on multiple public benchmarks under both intra- and cross-dataset protocols, and its robustness is further assessed under strong Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) adversarial attacks. Experimental results demonstrate that DFNet achieves competitive or superior performance across diverse datasets and attack scenarios, while maintaining improved resilience to adversarial perturbations. Moreover, the modular, attention-driven design of DFNet provides saliency-based insights into the learned spoof traces, supporting its interpretability. Overall, this work high-lights the effectiveness of explicitly incorporating generalization, robustness, and interpretability into architectural design for real-world face anti-spoofing, with DFNet offering a concrete step toward this goal. For reproducibility, the source code is publicly available at https://github.com/dhimasarief/DFNet.

title: The Virtue of Hallucination: When AI Mistakes Make Software Safer
authors: Antonio Mastropaolo
abstract: “LLMorpheus: Mutation Testing Using Large Language Models,” published in IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering in 2025, proposes a large language model-driven mutation testing framework that moves beyond fixed operator catalogs by using context-aware code infilling.

title: Proficient implementation of Resource Allocation in New Radio based Vertical Network Slicing
authors: Kalpana Naidu, Narasimha Rao Banavathu, Vanlin Sathya, Kota Nageswara Rao, Sunkaraboina Sreenu
abstract: This paper addresses the channel and power allocation (CAP) problem in a downlink vertical network slicing (VNS) system. In this downlink, a Terrestrial Base Station and an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) cooperatively serve multiple ground users. Besides, UAV exploits both licensed and unlicensed spectrum resources to enhance the network capacity. Thus, an optimization framework for CAP is developed to maximize the aggregate downlink throughput under interference and minimum-data rate constraints. In order to resolve CAP, conventional iterative algorithms incur high computational complexity. In contrast, the proposed analytical approach provides predominantly closed-form solutions for the CAP problem. Further, the proposed analysis establishes the uniqueness of the obtained solution by deriving closed-form expressions for the required number of channels (based on channel conditions). Moreover, simulation results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed framework. Besides, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves lower computational complexity, while maintaining performance comparable to that of iterative algorithms.

title: MC-ORAM: A Concurrent ORAM Scheme for Multi-User Shared Storage
authors: Chuang Li, Duo Hu, Gang Liu, Yanhua Wen, Jianhao Wei, Zhuo Tang
abstract: The expansion of cloud-based shared storage increases data privacy concerns. While data encryption technologies can safeguard data content, they cannot prevent the leakage of data access patterns. By re-encrypting data and changing storage location after each access, Oblivious Random Access Machine (ORAM) can effectively avoid information leakage from memory access patterns. While ORAM was initially designed for single-user applications, most existing multi-user ORAM solutions have drawbacks, such as dependence on a trusted proxy, high client storage overhead, and low throughput. To address the issues of multi-user ORAM systems, this paper explores the design of proxyless ORAM solutions in shared storage scenarios and proposes MC-ORAM, a new multi-user oblivious data storage framework. It ensures data consistency through client collaboration in a proxyless architecture, achieves higher throughput by differentiating request processing based on privacy protection requirements, and uses recursion for optimization. We implemented MC-ORAM and analyzed its performance using a variety of indicators, as well as conducting comparative evaluations against alternative schemes. The results show that, on average, MC-ORAM reduces response latency by 18.1% and improves throughput by 23.8% compared to TaoStore.

title: S pectral- T rustworthy A ugmentation H armonizer towards Automated Lung Auscultation under Pathological Sample-scarcity Scenario
authors: Ying Wang, Fan Wang, Guoheng Huang, Xiaobin Zheng, Baiying Lei, Xiaochen Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: Despite the limited availability of pathological samples, automated lung auscultation continues to strive to establish a reliable diagnostic model. Due to this scarcity, existing automated classification methods focus primarily on feature augmentation and transfer learning, while overlooking the challenges of data quality control during the augmentation process and domain adaptation. To address these challenges, we propose a Spectral-Trustworthy Augmentation Harmonizer (STAH) framework comprising three synergistic components. First, SpecDiver (SD) generates a balanced and expanded data distribution via multi-level resampling and quantization augmentation. Based on this expanded spectral domain, TrustworthyAugFilter (TAF) then employs a dual-branch propagation mechanism with confidence-guided filtering to retain beneficial augmented samples while eliminating harmful ones that could destabilize training. Linking the SD and TAF components, HarmonicBridge (HaB) transfers representational knowledge from natural images to the spectral domain through innovative frequency decomposition, recombination, and reconstruction processes. Tests on the SPRSound 2022 dataset show that STAH gets scores of 91.02%, 79.38%, and 69.87% for tasks 1-1, 2-1, and 2-2. In the 2023 version of the dataset, our STAH reaches 82.47%, 80.46%, and 73.02% for these same tasks. We also tested our method using the ICBHI 2017 dataset. It achieves an Average Score of 73.52% for the binary task and 66.11% for the four-class task. The code is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/wangying1586/STAH_Automated-Lung-Auscultation</uri>.

title: Multimodal Multi-Scale Temporal Enhancement Network for Audio-Visual Scene Classification
authors: Ming Yin, Tianwei Liang, Xianxian Zeng, Daming Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
