abstract: Audio-visual scene classification (AVSC) aims at classifying a video recording into one of the predefined scene categories, using both audio and visual modalities, which is a fundamental yet challenging problem in scene understanding. Although some impressive results for AVSC have been achieved recently, the modeling of object semantics and long-term dependencies from various modalities remains fully unexplored in the literature. To this end, in this paper, a novel Multimodal Multi-scale Temporal Enhancement Network, namely M2TE-Net, is proposed for audio-visual scene classification. In particular, to handle the temporal information of audio-visual modalities, the Multi-Scale Temporal Enhancement Modules(MSTEM) are devised from global and local perspectives. Then, a U-shape Multimodal Interactive Module (UMIM) is designed to mine the interaction between two modalities, where the shared semantic information from different modalities is fused by exploiting the consistency and complementary ones. Experimental results on two benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed M2TE-Net outperforms state-of-the-art models, with competitive performance. The code is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/TinwaiLeung/M2TE-Net</uri>.

title: Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)

title: Cloud Computing for Solar Energy Optimization
authors: Farrokhzad Fardin, Sabbaghan Aref
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: Large-scale photovoltaic (PV) farms are rapidly expanding worldwide, but their operation is challenged by variability in solar resource, distributed asset complexity, and stringent grid-integration requirements. Cloud computing combined with edge devices, IoT telemetry, and modern machine learning provides scalable data processing, real-time analytics, and orchestration capabilities that materially improve power forecasting, performance monitoring, and operational efficiency of utility-scale solar installations. This article reviews cloud-centric architectures and algorithms for solar farm optimization, presents key analytical formulas used for performance assessment, and quantifies potential operational and environmental benefits. Illustrative Excelready datasets are provided for generating figures on task distribution (edge/cloud/application), forecasting improvements, and O&M cost reduction. Real, recent peerreviewed studies and industry reports are cited to support the analysis and recommendations.

title: Smart Solutions for Sustainable Cotton Farming: Integrating Sentinel Data and Machine Learning for Enhanced Water Stress Detection
authors: Neetish Kumar Chandrakar, Aakanksha Sharaff
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Cotton is an important part of the Indian cultures and history, it is the backbone of Indian textiles industry and symbol of craftsmanship. Cotton needs water but can't overdo it. Currently farmers face several irrigation difficulties in waterstress areas. Water stress is not only a problem that affects the lives of farmers but also affects the well-being of the whole community and economy. The big data analytics of satellites provides a separate platform to identify the absence of water in cotton cultivation. The analysis of satellite imagery helps farmers monitor the water stress and drought conditions, making informed irrigation decisions and eliminating unnecessary wastage of water. In this paper, the comparative study of different Machine Learning algorithms: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, Multi-layer Perceptron classifier (MLPClassifier), XGBoost, Logistic Regression, and k - nearest neighbor (kNN) will be evaluated to predict water stress in the agricultural fields using Sentinel-1 (S1) and Sentinel-2 (S2) satellite data. This research paper provides the practical information on the monitoring of crop health, which encourages the development of sustainable farming methods, through the comparison, based on the accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The findings demonstrate the role of village-level data of water stress in enabling farmers to emphasize on water scarce regions, enhancing effective water use and sustainable food production. The research will assist farmers to make superior irrigation choices and encourage sustainable farming.

title: Fusing Invariant Moments and Deep Residual Attention for Robust Fingerprint Classification and Anti-Spoofing
authors: Dharmendra Kaushik, Alok Kumar Singh Kushwaha, Vinay Kumar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: This study introduces an innovative hybrid framework for fingerprint classification and spoof detection that merges handcrafted invariant moment features with a deep residual attention network. This approach enables the clarity of global shape descriptors and the discriminative power of deep learning to be consolidated, leading to high accuracy and resilience among different fingerprint datasets and types of spoofing. Hu and Zernike invariant moments characterize the global motifs that are not affected by rotational and scale transformation, while residual network with attention mechanism is tailored to local ridge patterns. To combine these complementary features, a feature-level fusion scheme was applied and then a duty-decision heads for classification and spoof detection were adopted. As per the experimental results, our model is able to obtain a classification accuracy of 96.8% and spoof detection accuracy as high as 97.3%, which are clearly higher than other top performing models. The framework demonstrates remarkable generalization, particularly under adverse environments, such as low-quality or partial or spoofing of finger-vein patterns and it is very suitable for real-world biometric applications.

title: Synthesis of Energy Efficient and Performance Oriented Feed Forward Artificial Neural Network on Kintex-7 Low Voltage
authors: Pankaj Singh, Sandeep Budhani, Shilpi Bisht
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: In this paper we have proposed the synthesis of an energy efficient feed forward artificial neural network (FFANN) on Kintex-7 low voltage FPGA. The implementation of FFANN on FPGA is achieved by using a number of configuration such as varying capacitance, voltages and I/O standards. LVCMOS 15 and LVCMOS 18 are used as I/O standards. The maximum power efficiency is achieved with LVCMOS 15 I/O standard when voltage is varied from 0.970 to 0.870 and capacitance is varied from 10000 pF to 1 pF. By using varying voltage and capacitance, a reduction of 99.84% in I/O power, 15.55% in static power and 83.35% in total power consumption is observed. The reduction in I/O, static and total power consumption doesn't affect the performance of the FFANN. For the implementation of FFANN on FPGA we trained it using TensorFlow and then implemented the learned weights on FPGA. We have used 16-bit fixed point representation for data and Sigmoid is used as activation function. The model is tested on MNIST dataset. The implementation of FFANN requires a fraction of the available resources on the FPGA. The FFANN operates at a frequency of 216.5 MHz with a total negative slack of 0.004 ns which provides promising performance.

title: An Intelligent AI-Powered System for Comprehensive Career Progress Tracking and Interview Readiness
authors: Prabhu T N, Gokulnathan T, Pavithra P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Navigating the transition from academic life to the workforce remains quite difficult for new graduates. They frequently do not receive organized guidance for behavioral and technical interviews. Existing platforms tend to create disjointed learning experiences by concentrating on isolated components such as coding exercises or insights on succeeding in HR interviews. This paper puts forward a smart career preparation platform that integrates skill-based job suggestions, real-time progress monitoring, human resource interview coaching, and coding practice into a unified system. The method utilizes content-based filtering of user skills to match relevant job openings, rule-based adaptive recommendations to customize learning trajectories, and lightweight natural language processing to assess HR interview responses.The functional prototype made with React and Spring Boot was experimented with by student users who claimed their confidence in interview readiness, their preparation being more organized, and their engagement being more pronounced. The recommended procedure demonstrates that the students of the future are already learning how to engage with AI tools and that the teachers of the present are already assimilating technologies and, thus, the communication lines are open between the two worlds.

title: A Comparative Study Between Fractional Order PI, and Classic PI Control for Power Quality Improvement in Microgrid Connected System
authors: Khaoula Nermine Khallouf, Laid Zarour, Samah Bouaroudj
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This work addresses the enhancement of power quality within a hybrid renewable energy system (HRES) that integrates wind energy, photovoltaic panels, and a battery storage unit. These sources are interfaced through a bidirectional DC/DC converter and a multifunctional voltage source inverter (MFVSI). The proposed configuration not only supports the stability and operation of the electrical grid but also ensures proper supply to nonlinear loads. The MFVSI plays a crucial role in mitigating harmonic distortion and regulating reactive power. To maximize the energy extracted from both the solar and wind subsystems, Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithms are employed. The battery storage system is supervised via a bidirectional DC/DC converter enabling controlled charging and discharging. On the grid side, an advanced Direct Power Control (DPC) strategy is implemented to ensure fast and accurate regulation of active and reactive power under varying operating conditions. The entire system is modeled and simulated in MATLAB/Simulink to validate and assess its performance.

title: Privacy-Preserving and Efficient Aggregation for Federated Large Language Models
authors: Jing-Jing Li, Mingyang Fu, Xuanxuan Zhang, Jing Li, Jiliang Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of large language models (LLMs) in intelligent dialogue, code generation, and knowledge reasoning tasks, their training and fine-tuning have become increasingly dependent on massive data volumes and high-quality datasets. Federated Learning (FL) presents a promising alternative to the high overhead and privacy risks of centralized LLM training by keeping data local and utilizing secure aggregation. However, it remains vulnerable to critical challenges such as model inference attacks and dynamic client dropout. To address these issues, this paper introduces an efficient privacy-preserving aggregation scheme by integrating a randomness-augmented Paillier homomorphic encryption method and threshold secret sharing. The scheme supports secure parameter updates and dynamic fault tolerance in LLM finetuning scenarios while maintaining resistance to collusion attacks involving up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$t$</tex> clients. Experimental results on GPT-style finetuning tasks indicate that our approach preserves data privacy with less than 5% degradation in final perplexity, improves encryption and decryption efficiency by approximately 32.8% at a scale of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 k$</tex> dimensions compared with baseline methods, and maintains a communication cost of only about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.005 \times$</tex> that of the traditional Paillier scheme.

title: Design of a Hybrid Hydrogen Storage Microgrid via a New Weighted Mean of Vectors and Beluga Whale Optimization Technique
authors: Alahari Hanumat Prasad, Md Ankushavali, Anandakumar Haldorai, Pradeep Kumar Chilakala Sri Krishna Balaji, Mukesh Kumar, Deekshant Varshney
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: This research paper presents a new hybrid metaheuristic technique combining the use of Weighted Mean of Vectors (WMV) and Beluga Whale Optimization (BWO) to optimally design a hydrogen storage multi-user hybrid microgrid (HMG). The hybrid microgrid is based on the use of photovoltaic (PV) panels, wind turbines (WT), fuel cell (FC) system, electrolyze, hydrogen tank, and battery bank. The aim is to improve the system to cut total net present cost (NPC) down to the least possible amount and at the same time to increase reliability and renewables penetration under different operation constraints. The simulation results proved that the hybrid WMV-BWO algorithm was better than each single WMV, BWO, and other state-of-the-art algorithms. The upper limits and performance of the hybrid WMV-BWO algorithm over a wide range of loads and weather conditions were confirmed by conducting a sensitivity and robustness analysis.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Double-Loop ADRC Scheme for DC–DC Push–Pull Converters
authors: Mohammed Benmiloud, Mohammed Belkhiri, Atallah Benalia
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This paper investigates a double-loop active disturbance rejection control (ADRC) strategy for an isolated DC-DC push-pull converter. The proposed approach employs an inner current loop and an outer voltage loop, each integrating an extended state observer (ESO) to estimate and reject disturbances in real time. The proposed controller achieves precise voltage regulation under reference and load variations without relying on accurate knowledge of the converter parameters. Simulation results demonstrate fast dynamic response, no overshoot, and strong robustness against changes in operating condition, which includes variations in load and input voltage.

title: AI Models for MRI Denoising: Unraveling the Effectiveness of Autoencoders, U-Net, and Noise2Noise
authors: Rohini Mahajan, Manpreet Kaur, Amit Jain, Mukesh Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is an integral part of medical diagnostics. Noise during MRI data acquisition processes creates significant deterioration of image quality causing problems in accurate clinical interpretation. Different types of noise enter MRI scans because of electronic component heat distortion and system technical problems, and the natural bodily activities of patients. Denoising techniques play an essential role in improving diagnostic value in MRI imaging. Traditional methods are useful but they often fail in balancing structure preservation and noise reduction, especially in low signal-to-noise ratio cases. The application of spatial filtering along with wavelet thresholding techniques in MRI denoising produces ineffective restoration of important image characteristics during denoising. The use of deep learning models accomplishes excellent results for identifying subtle details within noisy images to improve the quality of image structures during noise reduction. The MRI denoising has benefited from three deep learning frameworks: autoencoders, U-Net, and Noise2Noise because of their different strengths and favorable experimental results. This paper examines three current deep learning models comprising Autoencoders, U-Net, and Noise2Noise for performing MRI denoising tasks. In image processing, autoencoders are used for effective noise reduction because of their ease of use and ability to maintain feature prominence. To facilitate better recovery of spatial detail and finer features during the denoising phase, the U-Net model is used because of its unique encoder-decoder structure. To emphasize demonstrating noise removal without any clean reference images Noise2Noise, a new method that develops models using only noisy data, is investigated. These models are tested using different MRI datasets, and quantitative evaluation through peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR), structural similarity index (SSIM) and root mean square error (RMSE) is applied.

title: Implementation and Performance Vertical and Crosswise Vedic Fourier Transform Algorithm for Bit-Width Operations in Modern Digital Processing
authors: Mahesh Kumar S V, Ganesh E, Karthik B N, Vijayaraj N, Prabu M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) is vital in signal analysis due to the reason that it converts signals from the time into the frequency domain, for deep analysis and manipulation of the frequency components in the raw signals. FFT forms the backbone of modern digital signal processing, image analysis, and audio engineering. This paper presents a study of FFT algorithms implemented using Vedic mathematics principles, contrasting them with conventional FFT designs in terms of computational speed, efficiency, and resource utilisation. By integrating the Vedic arithmetic sutra Urdhva Tiryagbhyam, the FFT operations can achieve substantially reduced delay and lower power consumption. In experiments, the MATLAB simulations confirm the advantages of Vedic FFT in signal processing and hardware implementation contexts. The computational examples demonstrate up to 40% improvement in multiplication speed, validating the potential of Vedic Multiplier (VM) for modern digital signal processing architectures. Hence, Vedic FFT can be more appropriate for modern digital signal processing architectures.

title: Adaptive Lightweight Elliptic Curve Cryptography Framework for Power-Constrained Iot Devices
authors: Neha Purohit, Shubhalaxmi Joshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystem demands security solutions that combine strong cryptography with energy efficiency for resource-limited devices. Conventional algorithms such as RSA and DSA impose high computational and memory overheads, rendering them unsuitable for power-constrained nodes. This paper proposes an Adaptive Lightweight Elliptic Curve Cryptography (AL-ECC) framework designed for IoT environments. The suggested design use to select which curves to use, create keys on the fly, and change the level of security based on entropy and the power mode of the device. To protect against side-channel threats, the system uses blindfolded hashing, capability tokens, and entropy-driven ey generation. Using a real- world dataset of forest fires and experiments on Raspberry Pi Zero W, Model A+, and Model B shows that the system uses very little power (0.002 J per operation), keeps memory usage fixed (0.4 MB), and quickly reaches low-latency cryptography performance. comparative testing shows that AL-ECC offers a flexible balance between safety and energy economy for a wide range of IoT settings.

title: Efficient ANPR-Based Parking Management Using YOLOv8
authors: Anirudh Kumar, Raghav Garg, Kanderp Narayan Mishra, Amit Bhola, Anubhava Srivastava, Himanshu Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The sudden surge in automobile ownership has resulted in parking issues, such as space management inefficiencies, excessive waiting time, and safety issues. This article introduces an Automated Parking System (APS) incorporating YOLOv8 for real-time detection of vehicles and EasyOCR for recognition of license plates. The system detects and extracts vehicle license plate information automatically, allocates parking spaces, and allows entry and exit tracking. A web-based system facilitates pre-booking, automatic space availability updates in real-time, and electronic payments, which increases user convenience. Experimental outcomes indicate enhanced efficiency, accuracy, and lower processing time than in conventional systems. The system helps achieve smart urban parking solutions by maximizing space utilization and security.

title: Malware Detection in Intrusion Detection Systems Using Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Varun Madhwani, Akshat Agrawal, Arun Prakash Agrawal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Due to the increasing evolution of cybersecurity threats, traditional virus detection systems are becoming less effective in countering them. To improve the accuracy of threat detection, machine learning techniques have become essential. This paper addresses the shortcomings of conventional rule-based and signature-based detection methods, which fail to detect complex malware attacks. The aim of this study is to develop a malware detection system that outperforms traditional detection systems by leveraging Generative Adversarial Networks ( GANs) for threat detection.In this paper, a GAN-based adversarial training technique is employed to synthetically generate malware feature vectors and train a discriminator to distinguish them from legitimate data. This technique stands out by avoiding traditional approaches that rely on static signature databases such as VirusShare, MalwareBazaar, or AVClass, which are often limited by their dependence on previously known patterns, thus offering superior adaptability to unknown threats. Instead of relying on external datasets, the model generates its own synthetic data, replicating malware behavior patterns. Experimental findings demonstrate that the model achieves high accuracy (up to 97.35%), with precision, recall, and F1-score values consistently reaching high levels, validating the effectiveness of adversarial learning for cybersecurity applications.

title: Addressing Temporal Drift in Indian Music Recommendation Systems Using Hybrid Collaborative and Content-Based Filtering
authors: R V Kirrthanaa, Ruchika Todi, Sara Khurshida, Sanjana Prasad, Rashmi K B, Shobhana T S
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Music preferences in the Indian streaming ecosystem changes rapidly over time with cultural trends and changes in user behavior. Traditional recommender systems degrade over time as they rely heavily on outdated data. This study analyzes temporal drift in Indian Spotify data and proposes a hybrid recommendation model. A large-scale global Spotify charts dataset has been filtered, for the Indian region. Collaborative features are extracted using ALS (Alternating Least Squares) for each of the songs in the dataset along with the extraction of content-based features using Spotify API. This data is then combined together to create a hybrid dataset, which will be used for the Temporal-Drift analysis. The hybrid dataset is chronologically split into stale (old), fresh (recent), and future (test) windows, which are trained using LightGBM. The models trained on stale and fresh windows show a significant drift effect, with RMSE increasing from 60,049 (fresh) to 96,516 (stale). Beyond this analysis, a live API-powered hybrid recommender was created combining ALS embeddings with real-time Spotify content features and popularity metrics. (Abstract)

title: Integrating Generative AI with Deep Learning for Enhanced EEG Signal Analysis in Brain–Computer Interfaces
authors: S. Ramya, D. Shalini, M. Revathi, A. Jenifer
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Recent advancement in BCI technologies has en- abled the utilization of systems that can understand human brain signals to execute various cognitive and motor activities. However, the inherent noise and subject specificity of EEG signals pose significant challenges in developing robust deep learning models. This work proposes a Generative Artificial Intelligence-based deep learning framework that includes GANs for generating synthetic EEG data, removing noise, and augmenting it. The model improves EEG classification, cross-subject generalization, and reduces model overfitting on small-sized datasets. In comparison to typical deep neural architectures and state-of-the-art GAN-based EEG models, the proposed system demonstrates improved accuracy with superior signal reconstruction quality on multiple benchmark datasets. The proposed framework can be used as a basis for adaptive BCI systems and medical diagnostic tools pertaining to neuro degenerative disorders.

title: Functional Size Measurement of Hybrid Classical Quantum Software Systems
authors: Hassan Soubra, Tuna Hacaloglu, Alain Abran, Pierre Bourque, Sylvain Béland
abstract: Accurate sizing of software systems is essential for effective project management and resource allocation. Hybrid classical-quantum software systems consist of a quantum component, which executes quantum algorithms and incorporates error correction mechanisms (overhead)-a critical aspect in the Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) era-and a classical component, which controls quantum operations and manages error propagation. This paper explores functional sizing for hybrid classical-quantum software. It highlights the challenges arising from the interaction between the classical and quantum components, particularly with respect to the abstraction layers and granularity levels. A key principle emphasized is that functional sizing should not extend across multiple abstraction layers within a single measurement scope. While quantum software typically operates at a low-level implementation-specific layer, classical software generally functions at a higher level of abstraction. This divergence emphasizes the need to clarify how functional size measurement should be applied using set theory to account for requirements specified at different abstraction layers in hybrid computing environments.

title: Robust Tunnel Point Cloud Matching: A Coarse-to-Fine Framework Integrating PCA and GICP with DBSCAN Noise Reduction
authors: Jianxin Wu, Ruiyang Zhang, Haoliang Yang, Lin Yang, Junwei Nie, Diyang Chen, Qiang Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: This paper proposes a coarse-to-fine matching framework integrating physical priors with probabilistic optimization: First, a physically parameter-driven DBSCAN clustering denoising algorithm is constructed. Based on clearance requirements in tunnel electromechanical installation specifications, the clustering radius is adaptively determined to effectively strip structured noise closely adhering to the wall surface. Second, Principal Component Analysis (PCA) combined with gravity vector constraints and density skewness detection resolves the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8 0}$</tex>-degree ambiguity in the principal axis direction, enabling fully automated initial pose correction. Finally, the Generalized Iterative Nearest Point (GICP) algorithm is introduced. By constructing a probabilistic model of the local covariance matrix, it transforms the matching problem into a plane-to-plane maximum likelihood estimation problem. This model effectively suppresses axial slippage through a mathematical mechanism utilizing high-weight constraints on normals and low-weight tolerances for tangent planes. Field validation in a large-diameter submarine shield tunnel in Shandong demonstrated that this method significantly reduced the matching root mean square error (RMSE) from the standard ICP of 0.0185 meters to 0.0109 m, while increasing the overlap rate to 84.2%. Comparisons with the Normal Distribution Transformation (NDT) algorithm demonstrate that the proposed method maintains sub-centimeter accuracy while exhibiting superior environmental adaptability, providing robust technical support for the digital operation and maintenance of tunnels throughout their entire lifecycle.

title: Adaptive Interval Type-2 Fuzzy-PI Control for Improved Dynamic Power Regulation in DFIG-Based Wind Energy Systems
authors: Rafik Dembri, Mourad Kezai, Lazhar Rahmani
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This work presents an advanced control approach in which interval type-2 fuzzy logic is employed to continually adjust the gains of a PI regulator. By doing so, the rotor-side converter (RSC) of a doubly fed induction generator can manage active and reactive power independently. The entire control scheme was developed in MATLAB/Simulink, where the fuzzy system automatically adapts the PI parameters according to real-time operating conditions. This fusion of classical PI control with type- 2 fuzzy adaptation provides strong robustness and maintains stable dynamic behavior even when the system is exposed to various disturbances. The controller was evaluated through extensive simulation scenarios, including sudden changes in wind speed, random fluctuations, and parameter uncertainties. Results confirm a clear superiority over the traditional PI controller, with notable improvements in transient performance and better conformity to modern grid code standards. Overall, the proposed strategy offers a more dependable and efficient solution for DFIG-based wind energy systems, enhancing both power quality and grid integration.

title: Investigating Violations of Coding Standards in Large Language Models
authors: YongBeom Park, HoYong Lim, Jian Kim, Ki-Il Kim
abstract: Automated code review using Large Language Models (LLM) has attracted many researchers’ interests by detecting suspicious codes. However, its another application for ensuring compliance with specific coding standard such as MISRA-C remains unexplored yet. Even though static code analyzers usually are employed for this purpose, they are often too slow to adapt standard revisions and struggle with company-specific rules. To address these limitations, we propose a new LLM framework to investigate violation of coding standard, called LLM-CS to leverage LLMs as a more flexible alternative. The proposed framework consists of two modules, that is, Identifier and Specification module. The former adapts fine-tuned Positive and Unlabeled (PU) learning to filter suspicious methods from source code while the latter is to specify the exact coding rules violated in the suspicious methods identified by the Identifier. Our study uses the PEP8 standard to validate the framework by considering extension to other coding standards as well. By experimental results, LLM-CS demonstrated valid detection and specification of violation for low external dependency.

title: A Multi-Modal Fusion Approach for Cybersecurity Threat Detection Using the FIT-BERT Model
authors: Sravya Erukulla, Sai Suhas Kandukoori, Pothana Dheeraj Roy Dwivedhi, Basetty Mallikarjuna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Traditional machine learning-based models for detecting cybersecurity threats cannot derive the semantic relations among network traffic and, therefore, have limited precision in sophisticated attacks. This paper introduces the FIT-BERT model. It is a dual-stream fusion architecture for high-precision network intrusion detection. The statistical approach creates a Feature-Interaction Transformer which analyzes numerical flow features in order to identify complex interdependencies in the traffic data. In parallel to that, the semantic flow depends on a pre trained language model DistilBERT for processing autogenerated textual descriptions of the network flow, capturing important contextual insights from the traffic. These numerical and semantic insights are then combined into a single feature vector for final classification. This multimodal method results in significant improvements in the identification of specific, critical threats and attacks. Trained using a complicated Focal Loss function for addressing severe class imbalance, the FIT-BERT model shows a specialized capability for high precision threat detection and has significant capability to help with alert fatigue in highly advanced Security Operations Centers.

title: Design and Implementation of $1 \times 3$ Router Input-Output Protocol for Network on Chip (NoC)
authors: Bhumika Y, M S Varshaa, Remya Jayachandran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This paper describes the architectural design and FPGA implementation of a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times 3$</tex> router protocol optimized for Network-on-Chip (NoC) configurations. With the growing complexity of System-on-Chip (SoC) designs, efficient and scalable on-chip communication has become increasingly important. The router presented here supports one input and three output ports and uses a packet-based protocol with header, payload, and parity segments to provide structured data transmission and data integrity. The architecture comprises required modules, including Finite State Machines (FSM), FIFO buffers, Register, and synchronizer, to facilitate flow control and data routing. To further enhance power efficiency, clock gating is implemented within the FSM module, which results in power reduction compared to traditional designs. After synthesis and testing on the Xilinx Vivado and Nexys Artix-7 FPGA, the router runs at a target frequency of 100 MHz. Simulation results validate the functional correctness of the design and illustrate its applicability to high-speed, low-power NoC implementations, with nearly 50% Power reduction from the Initial Design to the Design with clock-gated FSM.

title: Experimental Comparison of Sunflower Oil and Brick Pieces as Heat Storage Materials for Solar Cooking Applications
authors: Assam Benbaha, Fatiha Yettou, Amor Gama, Azoui Boubekeur
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Solar energy is an abundant and environmentally sustainable resource, and it finds frequent use in thermal applications, including solar cooking devices. The efficiency of such systems can be further improved by introducing appropriate materials capable of storing heat. This investigation examines the thermal storage behavior of two sensible heat storage media—sunflower oil and fragmented masonry bricks—employed within a box-style solar cooker. Two experimental designs were prepared: in the first configuration, sunflower oil was filled into copper piping installed beneath the absorber plate; in the second configuration, pieces of brick coated in black were arranged on top of the cooking surface. Performance evaluation included stagnation testing and heating tests with a cooking load, both carried out under the real outdoor climate of Ghardaïa, Algeria. For the sunflower oil setup, the absorber plate achieved a peak temperature of 121.8° C, the water reached 86 °C, and stayed above <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> for around 420 minutes. Conversely, the brick-based model produced a higher maximum absorber temperature of 143.11° C and maintained water above 37.24° C for close to 540 minutes, reflecting enhanced heat-holding ability. This comparison shows that sunflower oil is advantageous for rapid heat uptake and efficient energy transfer to the cooking pot, whereas masonry brick pieces deliver longer-duration thermal retention at a lower cost. These results demonstrate that both media represent practical and economical options for improving the late-hour cooking potential of box-type solar cookers, especially in low-income or off-grid communities.

title: Entropy-Controlled Ant Systems with Strong Priors and Confidence Alignment
authors: Renu Jangra, Bilal Ahmed, Sahil Rampal, Yogesh Kumar Jakhar
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Based on our earlier published paper of MAS, the present paper introduces NG-MAS, an extended modified ant system that maintains the same simplicity of MAS but it is less sensitive to initialisation and noisy updates. We combine a Prim minimum-spanning tree and a per-node k-nearest neighbours into a LESN prior which warm-starts pheromones and provides edge-wise confidence. Tour construction is a combination of pheromone, inverse-distance heuristic, prior scores, and a rough segment memory that is acquired with the help of k-means. More stability in learning is provided by a confidence-penalised 2-opt local search, soft prior-pheromone alignment, exponent adaptation guided by entropy and numerically safe updates. We compare on synthetic Euclidean TSP problems (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{n} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{5 2} \boldsymbol{,} \mathbf{7 0}$</tex>, 150) under equal compute budgets to MAS (iterations, ants, candidate size, and local-search quota) with five seeds. Our report includes a wall-clock time and fixed percentage gap, anytime curves, and dynamic re-optimization test. NG-MAS is faster than similar runtime and recovers quicker than changes with reproducible CSV logs and figures.

title: Smarter Silicon: AI-Driven Power, Clock, and Floor planning for Sub-5nm and 3D SoCs
authors: Phaneendra Chainulu Sri Adibhatla, Srikanth Aitha, Uday Ashokbhai Korat
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The inevitable reduction to semiconductor technology to less than 5nm nodes presents extremely challenging issues to power management, clock distribution and floor planning of 3D System-on-Chip (SOC) architectures. The traditional Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools do not perform very well in the optimization of the multi-dimensional design space which is enormous. In this empirical study, an optimization system has been proposed using DL wherein convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and reinforcement learning (RL) are applied to automatism and optimize power, clock, and floor planning plans. Thermal hotspots, clock tree synthesis optimization, and interconnect delays minimization is predicted by the proposed model using datasets obtained according to the industry-level design benchmarks. The experimental results indicate up to 27 percent power savings, 19 percent clock skew or 22 percent increase in area usage, compared to first-generation heuristic-based EDA tools. The outcomes generate a novel paradigm based on which the employment of AI-boosted design intelligence will reduce physical design closure dramatically and give it reliability in the context of ultra-scaled semiconductor technologies.

title: X-WDS: Explainable AI Framework for Intrusion Detection in Smart Water Distribution Systems
authors: Pradeep Chandran, K.S. Sunil, Anand V, Ananjana K, Binil Bijoy, Diya Ramesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Smart Water Distribution Systems increasingly rely on digital monitoring and automated control, which also exposes them to cyber–physical security threats. Intrusion Detection Systems deployed in such environments must therefore be accurate, interpretable, and adaptable to changing conditions. This paper presents X-WDS, an explainable intrusion detection framework for smart water distribution systems that integrates an XGBoost-based detection model with adaptive retraining and explainability mechanisms. Rather than employing full policy-based reinforcement learning, X-WDS adopts reinforcement-inspired feedback to guide adaptive retraining of the detection model based on observed misclassification patterns. Explainable AI techniques are incorporated to provide human-readable explanations that support operator understanding and trust. The BATADAL benchmark dataset is used to test the framework. Adaptive retraining makes detection better and fewer false alarms. The observed improvements indicate that X-WDS can respond effectively to concept drift while maintaining stable and interpretable detection behavior, making it suitable for safety-critical smart water infrastructure.

title: Simulation and Analysis Framework for STT-MRAM: Exploring Switching Dynamics, Energy Metrics, and System-Level Reliability
authors: Shivanandham R S, Shanmugasundaram N, Mohan Kumar M, Sharmila Parveen M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This paper examines the behaviour of Spin-Transfer Torque Magnetic Random Access Memory (STT-MRAM) using a model that is designed in MATLAB with a base paper reference from TSMC. The framework describes magnetization dynamics on the basis of the Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert (LLG) equation and controls the switching properties through Monte Carlo simulations, including thermal noise. The findings reveal consistent coherent precessional switching whereby the value of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m_{z}$</tex> is changed between -1 to +1 in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 - 5 0}$</tex> ns, which is just consistent with the experiments. Switching probability also changes abruptly between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-100$</tex> percent in the current density of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times 10^{6}-5 \times 10^{6} \mathbf{A} / \mathbf{c m}^{2}$</tex>, which confirms practical device operation. Energy-delay analysis shows switching energy between 100500 fJ and delay between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20-40 ~\text{ns}$</tex> which similar results are reported in the literature. The equipment is stable to thermal <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(250-400 ~\mathrm{K})$</tex> and damping (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.005-0.03$</tex>) changes and performance measures have a variation less than 10 percent. Endurance Endurance is more than 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup> write cycles, and bit-error rates less than 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-9</sup>, and magnetic immunity is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 0 0 O e}$</tex>. System- level testing ensures stable operation and zero failures during Monte Carlo testing as well as write operations were more powered and took longer than read. Altogether, this framework offers an effective and tested study instrument to analyze and optimize and comprehend the performance features of STT-MRAM.

title: Safegp: Seamless Ai-Driven Framework for Enhanced User-Centric Security Google Pay Transactions
authors: Shristi Verma, Pranshi Goyal, Ravikant Kumar Nirala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The rapid growth of contactless payments has demonstrated the importance of the availability of safe, fast, and trusted digital payment systems. The paper presents a system named SAFEGP, which is designed to enhance Google Pay transactions in a manner that they are safer to the end user. SAFEGP is an amalgamation of intelligent detection of fraud, tokenization which is applicable with every session and how to make the payments smoother and quicker. It also has a personalization option that creates trust depending on the preferences of the user. SAFEGP enhances fraud detection through the correlation of security provisions and user behaviour and payment context. It also enhances the security of payments as well as speed when the payment occurs in real time. Simulation and small-scale real-world tests indicate that SAFEGP performs well in avoiding risk of loss of payment, large number of transactions and giving a convenient experience. These Results demonstrate the potential of SAFEGP to create a new benchmark of secure, smart, and user-friendly, contactless payments.

title: Forensic D-Carver: (A Python-Based High-Performance File Carver)
authors: Alojyoti Jashu, Vikrant Singh Thakur
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Digital investigators have learned the hardships of operating without an operating file system when they are called upon to retrieve files in hard drives or storage devices that were damaged and/or corrupted. The conventional routes and folder layouts in such cases are usually not available or damaged, and conventional recovery tools prove to be of no use. Although it has commercial tools that are known to handle this, including EnCase and FTK, there are issues with these programs, too. They are costly such that most labs and agencies cannot afford them, and they are also closed-source, so nobody is quite sure what is going on under the hood. This is a significant weakness of the forensic work, where certainty and reproducibility are of paramount importance. In order to address this issue, we embarked on the construction of Forensic D-carver. The philosophy behind this project is straightforward: everything to do with digital evidence recovery should be transparent, affordable, and open to investigation. Forensic D-carver is a Python program that is meant to unify trusted open-source tools such as Scalpel and PhotoRec into a single workflow, which is easy to use and automated. Every step is documented - starting with the first scan up to the file carving and the results analysis. Each time a file is recovered, the tool computes a hash of SHA-256, which acts as a fingerprint in such a way that the integrity of the data can be determined at any point. To determine its performance, we ran Forensic D-carver over actual pictures found in forensic cases, and not dummy test pictures. The tool could recover thousands of lost files in other formats, and its performance was similar to the commercially available ones in terms of accuracy and speed. The advantage is not merely cost, although of great importance, but also the flexibility and openness of the system. Due to its modular design, it can be used in the classroom by forensic educators, researchers can construct new behavior, and police agencies with tight budgets can have full-fledged forensic recovery without headaches. The digital forensics process with Forensic D-carver is not just something anyone can do; is recorded, verifiable and reliable from beginning to end.

title: Integration of Aged Electric Vehicle Batteries and Supercapacitors for Enhanced Energy Storage in Solar PV Systems
authors: Aaj Gunasekara, PM Yakupitiyage, JR Lucas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The transition from gasoline-powered to electric vehicles (EVs) have raised multiple concerns regarding their environmental impact of retired EV batteries, which still retain significant capacity for secondary use. This paper investigates the repurposing of aged <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E V$</tex> batteries in conjunction with supercapacitors to develop a cost-effective hybrid energy storage system (HESS) for residential and small-scale off-grid solar photovoltaic (PV) applications. The proposed system integrates a battery management system (BMS) with active cell balancing to optimize energy distribution, reduce stress on aged cells during charge-discharge cycles, and extend their operational lifespan. Supercapacitors are employed to mitigate sudden voltage fluctuations, thereby enhancing system stability and reliability. Experimental results demonstrate that incorporating supercapacitors reduced voltage drops from approximately 2.5 V to 1.0 V, significantly improving output stability under dynamic load conditions. A financial feasibility analysis further confirms that the reuse of aged EV batteries with supercapacitors provides a practical, environmentally sustainable, and economically viable solution for renewable energy storage, addressing both ecological challenges and the high costs associated with conventional battery banks.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning and Language Model Framework for Brain Tumor Detection and Personalized Diagnostic Assistance via Medical Chatbots
authors: Pooja Panapana, Balajee Maram, C Kishor Kumar Reddy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Brain tumors are a major challenge in neuronalology, among which early and accurate recognition for effective treatment and improved patient outcomes is extremely important. This study suggests a hybrid diagnostic framework that integrates deep learning (DL) techniques with large scale speech models (LLM) to improve image analysis and patient commitment. Folding networks (CNNs) are used for accurate characteristic extraction and classification of brain tumors from MRI scans. To complement this, personalized chatbots for medical support were developed using trans-based LLMS and advanced natural language processing technology (NLP). Chatbots provide patient specific knowledge, interpret clinical queries, and recommend specialized healthcare service providers nearby. By fusing LLM-controlled language understanding with visual DL based visual analyses, the system supports accurate tumor localization, intelligent patient interaction, and efficient clinical decision making. This approach highlights the potential for transformation combining visual and verbal models to improve diagnostic accuracy, streamline workflows, and provide accessible and personalized medical support in brain tumor diagnosis.

title: From Vision to Reality Decoding Indias Semiconductor Growth Factors
authors: Hanish Kumar Panavalappil Kumarankutty, Shishir Kumar, Ashwini K Aggarwal
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: While India has notable strengths in designing chips, it does not manufacture semiconductors domestically and is largely import dependent. This became a problem during the pandemic where shortages in the supply of chips slowed down manufacturing for key industries like automotives. Presently India lacks the high-grade production facilities required for large scale chip manufacturing. India's push to become a hub for semiconductor manufacturing is under way, but there is still a long way to go. This research is to identify factors influencing development of semiconductor chips manufacturing industry in India Design/ Methodology/Approach, Grounded Theory & Analytical

title: The Automatic Mark and Grading Handwritten Answer Sheet System
authors: Khantharat Anekboon, Vachiraya Panna, Chanapa Pupiam
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: Exam grading from artificial intelligence remains challenging in education assessment. The traditional exam grading method is made by human efforts. It makes inconvenient for grading many students; it takes time, risk of human error, and health effects. In recent years, artificial intelligence has become widely used in many domain problems. To solve those inconvenient, artificial intelligence is applied. The proposed work is a system contains completely processes not only for exam grading but also include creating answer sheets process and a dashboard that summarize the overall exam's assessment. The proposed system applied the transformer-based optical character recognition technique and document understanding transformer technique for handwritten answers. The proposed system was evaluated by 23 students with 14 multiple-choice questions, 10 true/false questions, and 28 questions from digit handwriting answers. The proposed system gives high F1-scores for all types of answer: multiple choice, true/false handwriting answers, and digit handwriting answers, which are 99.42%, 97.27%, and 93.26%, respectively. The proposed system is able to read the answer while a student uses pale pencil, able to read an answer sheet from a student who changes his/her mind for the answer without erasing the previous answer, able to read an answer sheet from a student who writes in a dirty area, and able to read an answer sheet from a student who write over than a box.

title: Integration of a 60-GHz-Band Wide-Coverage Beamforming Array Antenna by Subarray Switching for Three Orthogonal Beams
authors: Satoshi Yoshida, Suguru Kameda, Noriharu Suematsu
abstract: In this study, a 60-GHz-band beamforming array antenna structure with a wide coverage beamscanning capability is proposed. Conventional 60-GHz-band beamforming array antennas have a narrow beam-scanning capability (narrow coverage) because they consist of only one or two array antennas. Another paper proposed four orthogonal beam switching; however, there was no beamforming capability, resulting in limited coverage. The challenge of the 60-GHz-band beamforming antenna is to expand the coverage as large as possible. Based on this background, we propose and design a structure of a wide-coverage 60-GHz-band beamforming array antenna with the assistance of subarray switching using three orthogonal subarrays. Two 2×4 dipole subarrays and one 2×4 orthogonal-fed patch subarray were packaged into a one-packaged structure to realize orthogonal three-direction beams. A 2-D beamforming is enabled within each subarray. In addition, seamless wide coverage is achieved by switching the three subarrays. The objective of this paper is to realize a wide-coverage 60-GHz-band beamforming array antenna using the designed antenna structure. The integration and manufacturing of the designed antenna structure are significant issues. It is difficult to manufacture a 60-GHz-band large-scale array antenna using conventional packaging techniques. An improvement in the manufacturing yield rate is required. To overcome this issue, a copper ball integration technique with excavation was utilized, and the antenna was successfully realized. This technique is essential for packaging a total array antenna in one package. The measurement result of the total coverage area with over 10 dBi gain at 60 GHz was 7.64 sr, while the ideal one was 6.28 sr. This measurement result has the widest coverage of all previous papers. We successfully confirmed and validated the expansion of the coverage using the proposed antenna structure.

title: Shadowless Projection Mapping for Tabletop Workspaces with Synthetic Aperture Projector
authors: Takahiro Okamoto, Masaki Takeuchi, Masataka Sawayama, Daisuke Iwai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Projection mapping (PM) enables augmented reality (AR) experiences without requiring users to wear head-mounted displays and supports multi-user interaction. It is regarded as a promising technology for a variety of applications in which users interact with content superimposed onto augmented objects in tabletop workspaces, including remote collaboration, healthcare, industrial design, urban planning, artwork creation, and office work. However, conventional PM systems often suffer from projection shadows when users occlude the light path. Prior approaches employing multiple distributed projectors can compensate for occlusion, but suffer from latency due to computational processing, degrading the user experience. In this research, we introduce a synthetic-aperture PM system that uses a significantly larger number of projectors, arranged densely in the environment, to achieve delay-free, shadowless projection for tabletop workspaces without requiring computational compensation. To address spatial resolution degradation caused by subpixel misalignment among overlaid projections, we develop and validate an offline blur compensation method whose computation time remains independent of the number of projectors. Furthermore, we demonstrate that our shadowless PM plays a critical role in achieving a fundamental goal of PM: altering material properties without evoking projection-like impression. Specifically, we define this perceptual impression as “sense of projection (SoP)” and establish a PM design framework to minimize the SoP based on user studies.

title: Ψ-xLSTM: Automated Behavioral Verilog-A Generation from Distilled Physics-Informed xLSTM Networks for High-Frequency Device Modeling
authors: Sorin Liviu Jurj
abstract: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) have emerged as a powerful tool for device modeling, yet they suffer from a fundamental spectral bias, often failing to capture high-frequency transient dynamics essential for modern circuit simulation. While recent implementations of Extended Long Short-Term Memory (xLSTM) networks have successfully mitigated this bias by leveraging exponential gating and matrix memories, they introduce prohibitive computational complexity O(N2) that renders them unsuitable for standard compact modeling and hardware deployment. In this work, we introduce Ψ-xLSTM, a framework that extends the Ψ-HDL methodology to recurrent architectures, enabling the automatic generation of efficient behavioral Verilog-A models that retain the high-frequency fidelity of xLSTMs. We propose a novel Recurrent Relation-Aware Distillation (RRAD) strategy that preserves temporal dynamics and targets the specific architectural innovations of xLSTM. First, we demonstrate that the xLSTM exponential gating mechanisms can be clustered to reveal discrete, physically interpretable time constants (relaxation times), replacing continuous gate values with sparse, physics-based parameters. Second, we apply structural compression to the xLSTM matrix memory (mLSTM), proving that the governing physics of device dynamics resides in a low-rank subspace, allowing the dense matrix to be collapsed into a few dominant eigen-modes. We validate Ψ-xLSTM on high-frequency transient memristor dynamics, where the framework achieves the spectral accuracy of full xLSTM-PINNs while reducing parameter count by up to 84%. Extended evaluation across additional spectral-bias baselines, multiple device classes, small-signal and large-signal active-device benchmarks, and publicly available experimental datasets further demonstrates the robustness and broader applicability of the approach. SPICE circuit simulation validates the complete model generation pipeline with a mean absolute error of 0.40 mA, confirming quantitative accuracy. The resulting models are automatically exported as compact behavioral Verilog-A code that captures high-frequency harmonics missed by standard PINNs, while simulating 7.6× faster in Python, with substantial additional gains expected in compiled SPICE due to elimination of matrix-memory overhead, supported by complexity analysis and behavioral simulation validation.

title: Switching-Cost-Aware Deep Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Port Selection in Fluid Antenna Systems
authors: Jiaxin Liu, Kai Liang, Liqiang Zhao, Gan Zheng, Huixian Gu, Kai-Kit Wong, Chan-Byoung Chae
abstract: Fluid antenna systems (FAS) have emerged as a promising enabling technology for sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks. By dynamically switching ports according to channel characteristics, FAS can exploit spatial diversity and significantly enhance wireless performance. However, most existing studies focus on maximizing instantaneous throughput while neglecting the long-term cost associated with frequent port switching. This work aims to jointly optimize long-term throughput and port-switching cost in FAS, considering spatio-temporally correlated channels and minimum-rate requirements for each user. To address this challenge, we formulate the dynamic port selection problem as a Markov decision process (MDP) and employ a dueling DQN framework to obtain a near-optimal policy. A Transformer encoder is incorporated to capture spatial-temporal dependencies among ports, and a candidate pre-selection mechanism is introduced to reduce training complexity. Simulation results show that the proposed method achieves up to 96% of the per-slot exhaustive-search optimum while significantly reducing decision latency, demonstrating high efficiency and scalability for large-scale FAS deployments.

title: Evaluating Digital Twin Visualizations for Safety and Trust in Robot-Assisted Dressing
authors: Yunus Emre Cogurcu, Mirco Bartolomei, Baslin James, James Law, James A Douthwaite, Yasmin Rafiq, Lyudmila Mihaylova, Sanja Dogramadzi
abstract: None

title: XRFlow: Visualizing Metrics and Insights for Extended Reality
authors: Johannes Günter Herforth, Fiammetta Zanetti, Karsten Schönbein, Annika P.C. Lutz, Jean Botev
publish: Proceedings of the 18th International Workshop on Immersive Mixed and Virtual Environments Systems
abstract: None

title: Cyberattack Detection and Classification using New Custom Filter Design and Explainable-Deep Learning in Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Matthew Oinonen, Walid G. Morsi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: This paper presents a novel explainable Intrusion Detection System (IDS), for the detection and classification of attacks in cyber-physical systems (CPS). The presented approach uses only cyber data to detect attacks at the cyber layer before they reach the physical layer, thus preventing physical damage to the grid's assets. To enhance the attack's feature representation in both time and frequency simultaneously, a set of new wavelet filters are designed and integrated with the dual-tree complex wavelet transform (DT-CWT). This enables the vectorized representation of attack features instead of only the scalar representation as in the case of real transforms, which results in information loss. Furthermore, to address the lack of interpretability of existing IDS methods, this work integrates eXplainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to highlight the important regions in the time-frequency spectrograms that influence the attack classifications' decisions. This makes the model used transparent and more trustworthy. Using the XAI explanations, a new feature analysis technique, namely the XAI-Multiple Attribute Analysis Technique (XAI-MAAT), is proposed to numerically quantify the important features used for classification. The proposed approach has been tested in real-time using OPAL-RT on two IEEE test systems and the results have shown that it can achieve a detection accuracy of up to 99.54%, an increase of up to 60.93% over existing methods. The novel XAI-MAAT technique reveals that the time between sequential messages and the message status numbers are the most important features to classify the attacks at the cyber layer.

title: CCTformer: Calibrated Context-aware Transformer for Correspondence Pruning
authors: Weiwei Zhou, Shengjie Zhao, Yizhang Liu, Yanping Li
abstract: Correspondence pruning is a core problem in computer vision, aiming to distinguish correct matches from a large set of initial correspondences. Recent Transformer-based methods, such as VSFormer, have shown remarkable performance by jointly modeling visual appearance and spatial relationships. Nevertheless, these methods often depend on abstract, scene-level visual representations, which are insufficiently precise for validating individual correspondences. In addition, their context aggregation modules often struggle to capture multi-granularity corresponding features, particularly under complex geometric variations. To overcome these limitations, we propose the Calibrated Context-aware Transformer with two key components. The Calibrated Visual Cues Extractor samples features directly at putative match locations to provide well-aligned, correspondence-specific visual cues. The Dual-Perspective Graph Transformer replaces conventional graph attention to better model both node-level and edge-level interactions. Together, these modules enable more robust local-global context fusion. Extensive experiments on YFCC100 M and SUN3D show that CCTformer consistently outperforms the baseline and other state-of-the-art methods.

title: Approximate Optimal Enclosing Control for UAVs with Performance Guarantees: A Prescribed Time Learning Solution
authors: Wanning Wang, Xingling Shao, Jun Liu, Zhengrong Xiang, Junzhi Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents a prescribed time learning-based near optimal enclosing controller to ensure that unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) encircle around the specified target with prescribed performance constraints and minimum cost efforts. First, a basic enclosing controller is established to achieve the enclosing error stabilization and stable circumnavigation around a given target. Second, a new prescribed time behavior envelope that eliminates the availability on initial error is proposed. To render the satisfaction of performance constraints and optimal enclosing actions, a transformed enclosing error is obtained by enforcing state conversion on original error. Then, aiming at stabilizing the enclosing error to a prescribed accuracy within a prescribed time, a prescribed time learning-based near optimal enclosing controller under a critic-only adaptive dynamic programming (ADP) is explored, approximating the solution of a novel Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman (HJB) equation via pursuing the minimum cost associated with transformed errors. Especially, a novel prescribed time learning rule driven by weight errors is elaborated by revisiting real-time and historical information, such that the convergence of weights is only determined by a user-defined time constant. The prominent merit is that the optimal enclosing with performance guarantees can be achieved by a prescribed time ADP. Lyapunov stability demonstrates that involved error variables are ultimately limited and resultant controller satisfies optimality. Finally, simulations verify the values and superiority of the proposed methodology.

title: Intrinsic Reward-Driven SAC-IRCNet: A Novel Energy-Saving Control Method for HVAC Systems
authors: Jian Cen, Linzhe Zeng, Xi Liu, Xinyao Li, Yongkang Li, Jianming Yang, Chengming Huang, Feiqi Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: With the rising global energy consumption, the energy use of heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems has become a critical concern. Existing deep reinforcement learning control methods for HVAC systems often exhibit slow convergence and poor adaptability to dynamic environments, resulting in significant indoor temperature fluctuations, inefficient temperature control strategies, and consequently, energy waste and failure to meet thermal comfort requirements. To address these challenges, this study proposes a novel HVAC control method based on the Soft Actor-Critic with Intrinsic Reward and Correlation-Aware CNN (SAC-IRCNet) model. The model incorporates cooling load prediction as a constraint to enable on-demand cooling supply. It integrates an Elliptical Dynamics Exploration intrinsic reward mechanism, which accelerates SAC convergence through elliptical exploration rewards and inverse dynamics models, thereby reducing energy waste. Additionally, the Correlation-Aware CNN enhances SAC’s feature extraction capability by leveraging state correlations to better understand contextual information, enabling more accurate responses to environmental changes and improved thermal comfort. Experimental results show that SAC-IRCNet achieves a 3.94% faster reward convergence and a 5.78% higher maximum cumulative reward within five episodes compared to SAC. It reduces energy consumption by up to 17.25% (21,837.58 kW) and lowers thermal discomfort violations by up to 6.44%, demonstrating excellent generalization ability across two datasets.

title: Anomaly Detection Algorithm for Hyperspectral Images Based on Gabor Constraint Collaborative Representation
authors: Zhiyuan Xu, Xiaofei Wang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: To address issues such as the insufficient utilization of spatial information in hyperspectral imagery (HSI), this paper proposes a novel hyperspectral anomaly detection algorithm based on Gabor constraint collaborative representation (GCCRD), building on the traditional collaborative representation detector (CRD) algorithm. First, spatial texture cues and spectral discrepancies between the test pixel and its surrounding circular window are jointly incorporated into a weighting model to enhance background representation. Second, a local consistency constraint is introduced to identify reliable background samples and suppress isolated noise by examining the spatial–spectral consistency among neighboring background pixels. Third, to avoid excessive suppression of anomalous targets, a saliency refinement strategy is adopted by combining the original saliency map with its Gaussian-smoothed counterpart. Experimental results on five real datasets demonstrate that the proposed GCCRD algorithm outperforms six state-of-the-art methods.

title: Deep-Q-Learning-Based Proportional-Integral Event-Triggered Fuzzy Output Control of Variable Speed AGVs Via Proportional-Integral Observer
authors: Shen Yan, Xinyu Hu, Ju H. Park
abstract: This paper focuses on the T-S fuzzy output path tracking control issue for variable speed autonomous ground vehicles (VSAGVs) by using state observer and event-triggered mechanism. The model of VSAGVs with nonlinear dynamics and parameter uncertainty is represented by the uncertain T-S fuzzy system. To enhance the estimation performance of the unknown system state, a fuzzy proportional- integral observer containing an extra integral term of estimation error is designed. To reduce the network communication burden, a novel deep-Q-learning-based proportional- integral event-triggered mechanism by adding the integration of the normal triggering rule and introducing an optimization strategy of the triggering threshold. Compared with the classical normal and dynamic event-triggered mechanisms, a larger inter-event time can be generated by the proposed proportional- integral event-triggered mechanism. In addition, the deep-Q-learning-based optimization strategy is able to dynamically regulate the triggering rate according to the historical triggering performance and control performance. Some sufficient conditions are derived to solve the fuzzy observer and controller gains and the triggering matrix such that the VSAGV is asymptotically stable with given <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> level. Finally, the superiorities of the addressed approach are confirmed through some simulation results.

title: Energy-Conserving Event-Triggered Intermittent Control for Multi-Spacecraft Attitude Coordination
authors: Wenyu Qin, Pengyu Wang, Yueyong Lyu, Bohao Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The reliance on continuous communication and uninterrupted control actions in conventional spacecraft formation control imposes a heavy burden on communication bandwidth and energy resources. To address these challenges, an energy-conserving event-triggered intermittent control strategy is proposed for multi-spacecraft attitude coordination. First, a cooperative observation problem is formulated. Second, a distributed dual-loop neural network event-triggered observer is developed. The observer employs a neural network to approximate the unknown nonlinear dynamics of the leader spacecraft, incorporates a dual-loop observer and a Zeno-free event-triggered mechanism to eliminate the need for continuous communication, and achieves accurate state estimation using only discrete-time information. These features enable short-term state prediction by the observer even when inter-spacecraft communication is unavailable. Third, a prescribed performance intermittent controller is designed based on the estimated states. The controller deactivates the actuators when the estimation becomes unreliable and reactivates them when the tracking error exceeds the prescribed performance bounds, thus reducing energy consumption without compromising tracking accuracy. Finally, numerical simulations are conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy and its advantages in energy efficiency, attitude coordination accuracy, and short-term prediction.

title: Research on Excitation Waveform Classification and Core Loss Prediction Based on Random Forest Model
authors: Yaoteng Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: To address the issues of excitation waveform classification and core loss prediction in electronic devices, this paper first extracts image features from magnetic flux density data, including mean value, standard deviation, zero-crossing rate, and peak count, and employs a random forest model for waveform classification training. Subsequently, by comparing Gaussian process regression, convolutional neural networks, BP neural networks, and random forest models, the random forest model is selected for data feature analysis and model construction. The model's performance is further enhanced through parameter optimization and out-of-bag (OOB) error validation. The results show that the random forest-based waveform classification model achieves a prediction accuracy of 100 %. In core loss prediction, the coefficients of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{R}^{2}\right)$</tex> for the training and testing sets are 0.9858 and 0.9557, respectively, demonstrating excellent performance. The study indicates that the random forest model excels in excitation waveform classification and core loss prediction, offering high reliability and generalization capabilities, thereby providing strong support for the optimized design of electronic devices.

title: A Purechain-Protected Ai-Based Misbehaviour Detection System for Iov
authors: Md Mehedi Hasan Somrat, Esmot Ara Tuli, Mahbuba Iasmin Sumona, Jae-Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) introduces significant security challenges, especially in detecting and mitigating misbehaviour by malicious or compromised vehicles in real-time. This paper presents a robust misbehaviour detection system safeguarded by PureChain blockchain technology. Leveraging PureChain's novel Proof-of-Authority and Association <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{PoA}^{2}$</tex>) consensus mechanism, the system achieves lowlatency, high-throughput, and tamper-proof logging of detected events. At its core, an LSTM-based deep learning model dynamically learns temporal patterns from vehicular data streams, enabling accurate identification of various malicious activities such as spoofing, message tampering, and unauthorized transmissions. The seamless integration of the blockchain layer ensures the immutable and trustworthy recording of all detection alerts, enhancing data integrity and accountability across the IoV network. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate the system's high performance, with detection accuracy exceeding 96%, a perfect logging success rate, and an average blockchain transaction latency of just 1.26 seconds at a throughput of 14.56 transactions per second. This combined approach pushes the boundaries of secure and scalable IoV security, providing a timely, efficient, and reliable solution adaptable to evolving vehicular environments.

title: Dynamic Portfolio Allocation in Algorithmic Trading Using Sentiment Analysis
authors: R.D.M. Perera, U. A. Piumi Ishanka
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Market sentiment, as driven by news and online discussion sites is increasingly impacting algorithmic trading systems. In this work, we integrate financial news, social media sentiment gathered from Yahoo news and reddit platform to identify the sentiment related to the market of Bitcoin cryptocurrency. We trained various machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) sentiment classifiers with a dataset of 11,293 Bitcoin-related news headlines extracted from Yahoo Finance and Reddit. In baseline models, Logistic Regression achieved the best performance (accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$71.7 \%, F 1=0.704$</tex>), and among neural network-based ones, BiLSTM (Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory) does so with accuracy of 70.5%. The model FinBERT also achieved improved classification performance (accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$83.7 \%, F 1=0.835$</tex>), which was further confirmed statically by McNemar's statistical test as compared with the other models. In order to increase the interpretability, Shapley Additive explanations (SHAP) and Local Interpretable Model Agnostic Explanations (LIME) were used to examine the token level contributions to the model decisions, which revealed consistency among the patterns in which the positive and negative terms tend to influence sentiment predictions across the model architecture. This study highlights the importance of incorporating explainability and the sentiment aware modeling into the financial prediction workflows and also provide insights that would be needed for future development of transparent and reliable trading strategies.

title: Performance Analysis of Biosensor of Inverted T Stack Gate Dielectric GaAs Based FinFET
authors: Aprajita Kumari, Rajashree Das
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: This paper proposes a new FinFET based biosensor device that has an inverted t -stack based FinFET with GaAs as a fin material. Biomolecules are injected into cavities and nanocavities for sensing purposes.
