title: Research on Wireless Communication Based on Quantum Cryptography Technology
authors: Yuehua Xing, Hui Li, Jiaying Zhao, Yiming Lu, Miaomiao Du, Hua Shi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the upgrading of rail transit towards high speed, intelligence and networking, the demand for quantum attack resistance of train-ground communication systems has become increasingly urgent. To address the problems of traditional optical Quantum Key Distribution (QKD), such as high deployment costs, insufficient security strength of lightweight encryption algorithms, and poor scalability of hardware trusted encryption chips, this paper constructs a quantum-encrypted wireless communication technology architecture centered on the closed-loop management and control of the full life cycle of keys. It designs an on-board quantum security board integrating components including a quantum random number generator and an algorithm module, and proposes a key derivation and replacement algorithm based on the PBKDF2 function as well as a random salt transmission mechanism relying on Shannon's Perfect Secrecy Theorem. The results of the on-board test show that this scheme can realize the secure encrypted transmission of train-ground service data, with the average handshake delay being less than or equal to 300 ms and the average decryption and forwarding delay being less than or equal to 0.5 ms. It does not interfere with the operation of the original communication link and achieves both security and real-time performance. This research provides a feasible technical path for resisting quantum computing attacks and building a solid security defense line for smart rail transit, which has important theoretical significance and engineering application value.

title: SRE-Med: A Saliency-Aware Illumination Preprocessing Pipeline for Medical Imaging AI Models
authors: Sasi Vadan Muppalla, Lokesh Deekonda, Sriram Deveragatla, V. Harini
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Medical image acquisition in modalities such as chest radiography, retinal fundus imaging, and microscopy is often affected by poor or uneven illumination, which can obscure clinically significant features and reduce the reliability of automated diagnostic systems. Conventional preprocessing methods, including histogram equalization and Retinex-based models, perform global adjustments that may introduce artifacts, amplify noise, or distort color balance. Deep learning approaches have improved overall brightness but frequently fail to preserve localized diagnostic details that are essential for medical interpretation.To address these challenges, this work presents SRE-Med, a saliency-aware illumination preprocessing pipeline designed for medical imaging applications. The method integrates U<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>-Net and FastSAM to extract diagnostically relevant regions, which are processed separately from background areas using a dual-branch encoder–decoder architecture. A U-Net-based fusion stage then restores global consistency while maintaining smooth boundaries and color fidelity. The pipeline was implemented in Python with PyTorch and evaluated on chest X-ray and retinal fundus datasets. Quantitative results, measured by PSNR, SSIM, and F1 score, demonstrate consistent improvements over baseline enhancement techniques, while perceptual metrics such as BRISQUE and MUSIQ confirm the naturalness of the restored images.By preserving fine diagnostic structures under challenging lighting conditions, SRE-Med provides a reliable preprocessing stage that enhances the performance of AI-based medical imaging models. The proposed approach is adaptable across imaging modalities and has the potential to be integrated into both clinical decision support systems and real-time embedded diagnostic devices.

title: Explainable AI-Driven Smart Home Surveillance for Real-Time Anomaly Detection
authors: Ms.T. Saraswathi, Lakshayprethish M S, Pradeep P
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The primary research objective of this study is to develop an automated, explainable smart surveillance system that reduces human error and enhances the reliability of identifying high-risk activities in real-time. While traditional CCTV systems depend on tedious manual monitoring, this work proposes a multi-modal pipeline that integrates YOLOv8 for detecting high-risk objects like weapons and MediaPipe for human pose estimation to recognize suspicious behaviors such as climbing. A key objective is addressing the “black box” nature of AI by incorporating EigenCAM to provide visual heatmaps and the Gemini API to generate human-readable summaries, ensuring transparency for security operators. Key findings indicate that the proposed system achieves high performance, with the object detection module reaching a mean average precision of 92 % and the behavior recognition module achieving 89 % accuracy for climbing detection. Results from the confusion matrix further validate the model's stability, showing a 0.98 accuracy for identifying balaclavas and 0.93 for knives. This framework demonstrates a marked decrease in response times during simulated intrusions, providing a robust solution for home and facility security.

title: A Lightweight Vehicle Detection Model for Complex Traffic Scenarios
authors: Feifan Liu, Yuhu Shi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the development of autonomous driving, the detection of pedestrians and vehicles in complex scenes has been challenged by the problems of misdetection, omission and high model complexity. In order to overcome these problems, a novel lightweight target detection algorithm PCA-YOLO is proposed based on YOLO11 in this study. Firstly, partial convolution is integrated into the backbone network, and the C3k2 module is improved into the P_C3k2 structure, which can efficiently extract spatial features while reducing the number of model parameters and computational overhead. Secondly, a lightweight upsampling operator are added to the neck structure to better capture target information; meanwhile, the adaptive spatial feature fusion module is embedded into the object detector head, which effectively filters out conflicting information. Finally, experimental outcomes on the KITTI dataset show that the model PCA-YOLO can attain higher detection accuracy when compared against YOLO11n and other baseline algorithms, with varying degrees of model size, parameter count, and complexity reduction.

title: Multi Class ECG Arrhythmia Detection Using Scalogram and Deep Learning
authors: Chaitali Asole, Samiksha Gade, Ishwari Belorkar, Aditya Turankar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Arrhythmia or heart rhythm abnormalities are terms used to define a normal heartbeat. Most of the focus of Cardiovascular health today often deals with arrhythmia [15]. The irregularities we will be checking for in the ECG are ventricular tachy-cardiac and atrial fibrillation. Other terms for normal sinus rhythm irregularities, early atrial contraction, and early ventricular contraction are Vtach, Afib, PAC, PVC and NSA, respectively [20]. In this work, we present a multi-class arrhythmia detection method using CWT, which transforms 1-D ECG visualizations into 2-D time-frequency scalograms [5] [7]. We utilize ResNet-50 deep learning models for image classification with the aim of determining the types of arrhythmias [20]. Labeled recordings from the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database were utilized for training and testing. Gradient-weighted Class activation mapping (Grad-CAM) was integrated into our system to provide visualizations of model decisions that are most salient, hence improving interpretability and making the system clinically reliable. Since the tool operates offline, it can be reliably employed in constrained resource settings or remote areas [6]. This is a reliable, sustainable, and accessible means of arrhythmia detection that enhances patient outcomes and healthcare efficiency. The proposed ResNet-50 model achieved an overall accuracy of 83%, with an average precision of 0.82, recall of 0.81, and F1-score.

title: A Multi-modal Fusion Approach for Drug Discovery and Efficacy Prediction
authors: Agnes Chella Wanika R, A.M. Anusha Bamini, R Venkatesan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Discovery of drug behavior performed in clinical laboratories are time consuming and costly, with large percentage of drug samples are failing due to safety or efficacy issues. This work aims to propose a Machine Learning-based framework to predict whether a drug sample is likely to succeed or not using preclinical molecular and trail-related parameters. A dataset of 200 biologically safe-range samples are generated following the Lipinski’s rule of five, including the features such as molecular weight, LogP, hydrogen bond donors and acceptors, toxicity score, and trail phase. Three ML algorithms Linear Regression, Random Forest and XGBoost - were implemented and compared using performance evaluation standards such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC. The Random Forest pattern achieved the best overall performance with an accuracy of 87.5%, demonstrating strong predictive capability and interpretability. Feature importance analysis showed that toxicity score and trail phase were the most influential factors in determining trail success. The work bring forward, transparent and cost-effective approach for early prediction of drug viability, potentially reducing research costs and accelerating the drug discovery development pipeline unlike complex system

title: TSAFN: Temporal Spatial Attention Fusion Network of Alzheimer's Disease Classification in MRI
authors: Janarthan Vijayakumar, Vasavan Mahendrarajah, Luxshi Karunakaran
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: The objective of the proposed study is early and accurate classification of Alzheimer's, as well as Temporal-Spatial Attention Fusion Network (TSAFN) operating on magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) images. Using the publicly available Kaggle Alzheimer's MRI dataset of pictures with four levels of cognitive impairment, such as no impairment, very mild impairment, mild impairment, and moderate impairment, the work applies a group of preprocessing and enhancement strategies to improve the quality of the pictures and diversify the data. The data is divided into training (70), validation (12.5), and testing (17.5) sets. TSAFN is configured to simultaneously segregate both time and space attention features, producing interpretable attention maps that describe AD-influenced brain regions. Training was designed based on the PyTorch template, and the AdamW optimizer, mixed precision computation, and early stopping criteria were used to prevent overfitting. Measurement of performance is done through accuracy, precision, recall, macro-average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> score, confusion matrices, and receiver operating characteristic area under the curve (ROC-AUC). TSAFN produces a test accuracy of 0.9633 on average, a macro F 1 score of 0.9696, and high ROC-AUC values among the diagnostic classes. The interpretability of the attention maps generates clinically relevant data on AD related neuroanatomical alterations, and therefore, the application of the presented system in research and subsequent clinical applications.

title: Spiking neural P system with voltage-gated ion channels
authors: Jiaqi Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: The spiking neural P (SN P ) system is a third-generation spiking neural network that enables distributed and parallel computing and possesses strong biological interpretability. Although various SN P variants have been developed and applied in different fields, relying solely on synaptic connections to transmit spikes remains biologically inadequate. Therefore, drawing inspiration from the mechanism by which voltagegated ion channels in neurons can switch between different channels based on the positive or negative charges received, this study proposes an SN P system equipped with voltage-gated ion channels, referred to as the SNVI P system.

title: Multi Variant Deep Transfer Learning Analytics Model for Improved Health Care Solution Using Big Data
authors: P. Malathi, R. Parameswari
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: The recent surge in healthcare big data has presented new possibilities of intelligent disease prediction, but currently used analytics models fail to handle heterogeneous data, feature imbalance, and are not generalized across clinical domains. This paper tries to resolve these issues with a new Multi Variant Deep Transfer Analytics Model (DVDTAM) of better healthcare decision support. The aim is to improve the accuracy of disease prediction and reduce the rates of false classification. DVDTAM has a Deep Variant Feature Normalizer that uses the Feature Covariance Value to stabilize heterogeneous distributions of features after which a Multi Variant Deep Transformer with transfer learning is used to model complex clinical dependencies. Final disease classification is done using a Multi Factor Disease Absorption Weight mechanism. As it is experimentally assessed on the MIMIC-III dataset, DVDTAM reaches a high accuracy of 96.8 % and is better than state-of-the-art models by up to 2.5 percent, and the false rate is decreased to 0.05. These findings support the usefulness of the suggested structure of scalable and dependable healthcare analytics.

title: CNN-Based Automated Diagnosis and Grading of Diabetic Retinopathy from Fundus Images
authors: Sunitha Medichalam, Anurodh Kumar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: One of the general reasons for blindness in people with diabetes is diabetic retinopathy (DR), which can only be effectively treated if caught early. However, in places where medical treatment is scarce, early detection of DR is a major challenge. The aim of this work is to train a neural network to do three things: (1) identify DR, (2) identify it early, and (3) classify its severity. We looked at 3681 fundus photos from the APTOS DR dataset, which ranges from 0 to 4 (No DR to severe DR). A three-layer convolutional neural network used to train three separate models: a binary classifier, an early detector, and a severity classifier. To keep tabs on the models' training and validation accuracy, a 5-fold cross-validation procedure was utilized. Compared to other models in the literature, the binary classifier achieved a validation accuracy of 99.99%. Although it had trouble differentiating between early and severe DR, early detection nevertheless managed a 99.41% accuracy rate. Although the severity classifier was 99.61% accurate in identifying healthy people, it failed to do so when faced with more severe cases, perhaps due to the model's incapacity to distinguish between subtle changes in later DR degrees. The suggested DR detection method outperforms multiple state-of-the-art approaches, according to extensive experimental data measuring in terms of accuracy.

title: Mitigating Bias and Hallucinations in LLMs Through Prompt Engineering and Knowledge-Grounded Approaches in Healthcare Domain
authors: A.P.D. Ishadya, A. Withanaarachchi, S. Jayalal
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied in healthcare for clinical question answering and information support, yet their deployment is constrained by risks of hallucinated content and embedded bias that can compromise patient safety. This study proposes an integrated mitigation framework that combines advanced prompt engineering with knowledge-grounded approaches, specifically Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and the Model Context Protocol (MCP), without modifying underlying model parameters. Using a mixed-methods design, parallel RAG and MCP pipelines were implemented with shared retrieval and prompting logic and evaluated on NIH-derived MedQuAD subsets (CancerQA and NIDDKQA). Structured prompting strategies including role-based prompts, chain-of-thought, and chain-of-verification were applied consistently across both pipelines. Quantitative evaluation using accuracy, semantic similarity, evidence-backed rate, abstention, recall, and demographic drift metrics shows that MCP consistently outperforms or matches conventional RAG, achieving higher F1 and ROUGE-L scores, stronger evidence grounding, and lower abstention while maintaining minimal demographic drift. Expert review further confirmed improved clinical relevance and reduced unsupported claims. These findings demonstrate that coupling structured prompting with knowledge grounding produces more reliable, safer, and fairer healthcare LLM outputs, offering a practical and scalable pathway for trustworthy AI deployment in high-stakes clinical settings.

title: Analyzing the Impact of Time-Marked Twitter Sentiment on Intraday Stock Prices Using ARIMA Models: A Case Study of Nvidia and Microsoft
authors: Anil Chandrasekaran, Abdulla Atayer, Ahmed AlMarri, Abdulla AlDoobi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: This study examines the influence of real-time Twitter sentiment on intraday stock movements of Nvidia (NVDA) and Microsoft (MSFT). Tweets containing company-related keywords were collected using Python's snscrape module and analyzed with the VADER sentiment model. Aggregated sentiment scores were time-aligned with minute-level stock data from Yahoo Finance. Two econometric models—ARIMA and sentiment-augmented ARIMAX—were compared using RMSE, MAE, and directional accuracy. Results show that integrating Twitter sentiment improves short-term forecasting performance, highlighting social media's measurable impact on market behavior and the value of sentiment-based econometric modeling.

title: Leveraging Synthetic Multimodal Health Data and Federated Learning to Develop Explainable ML Models for Population-Level Precision Medicine
authors: Gokul Narain Natarajan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: The increasing availability of heterogeneous health data from wearables, electronic health records, imaging, genomics, and patient-reported outcomes presents a unique opportunity to build predictive models capable of supporting population-level precision medicine. However, privacy constraints, institutional silos, and imbalance across demographic subgroups limit the creation of robust and generalizable models. This paper introduces a privacy-first framework that integrates synthetic multimodal health data generation with federated learning to enable collaborative model development without exposing real patient information. The approach uses high-fidelity synthetic data to augment underrepresented populations, harmonize modality distributions, and stabilize cross-site learning. Federated optimization enables multiple institutions to jointly train models while maintaining strict data locality. An explainability layer— combining temporal attention, modality-specific attributions, and global feature ranking—supports interpretable insights for clinicians and public health stakeholders. The proposed framework demonstrates a scalable pathway toward equitable, trustworthy, and reproducible precision-medicine analytics for large heterogeneous populations.

title: Enhancing Occupational Safety Through Yolov12-Based Ppe and Hazard Detection
authors: Y. Ganesh, Krishnaraj Natarajan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Worker safety in construction and industrial sectors faces significant challenges due to head injuries from lack of safety helmets or poor usage. PPE compliance supervision remains time-consuming, irregular, and error-prone in dynamic environments with varying illumination and crowd density. Traditional monitoring fails to deliver real-time, scalable detection in harsh site conditions. This research develops a YOLOv12-based computer vision system for accurate helmet and head detection using the SHEL5K dataset. Enhanced augmentations including HSV adjustment, mosaic transformation, and random erasing improve feature diversity and model stability. The YOLOv12-Large architecture incorporates transformer-based neck layers and multi-scale feature fusion, enabling detection of small, occluded, and multi-oriented objects with high accuracy and real-time inference speed. Experimental results show 98.1% Precision, 97.6 % Recall, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.3 \% \text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex>, and 165 FPS, outperforming recent YOLO-based and CNN detectors. This scalable solution provides deployable real-time PPE compliance monitoring for manufacturing facilities, significantly enhancing workplace safety through automated detection and alerting.

title: A Dual-Branch Network for Classifying Pulmonary Disease Using Flow-Volume Loops
authors: Jaeun Kim, Hongjun Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Although flow-volume loops from spirometry are fundamental for diagnosing pulmonary diseases, distinguishing subtle morphological patterns remains challenging—even for experienced clinicians, especially when it comes to differentiating between obstructive and restrictive types. In this paper, a deep learning framework is proposed to automatically classify lung diseases based on these loops. As its core methodology, this work presents a dual-branch architecture that decomposes the complex four-class classification task—comprising normal, obstructive, restrictive, and mixed types—into two independent binary classifiers: one for detecting obstructive types and the other for restrictive types. Additionally, to ensure robust performance despite severe class imbalance in real-world medical data, a generative data augmentation strategy operating within the learned latent space of minority classes is employed. By modeling the underlying data manifold with a Convolutional Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and synthesizing high-fidelity samples, the training distribution is effectively rebalanced. This synergistic combination of a dual-branch structure and generative data augmentation significantly enhances the classifier's generalization performance, demonstrated by an increase of 0.026 in accuracy and 0.126 in F1-score over baseline models, marking a substantial advancement towards robust and scalable automated screening for pulmonary diseases.

title: Study and Implementation of a Partial Discharge Detection System: An Optimized Front-End Circuit for Continuous Monitoring
authors: Rafael Gomes, Mateus Giesbrecht, Fabiano Fruett
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: The reliability of rotating electric machines is critical for power generation, emphasizing the need for continuous monitoring to prevent unexpected failures. Insulation degradation, responsible for approximately 56% of failures in large generators, often results from partial discharges (PDs), which require early detection to mitigate long-term damage. This paper presents the design and implementation of an optimized front-end circuit for PD detection, tailored for continuous monitoring of electrical insulation degradation. The system employs a high-frequency transformer and coupling capacitors as PD sensors, with a signal conditioning circuit optimized for noise rejection and high-sensitivity detection. Additionally, the detection threshold can be adjusted, allowing for refined PD signal analysis and improved adaptability to different insulation conditions. The current implementation enables real-time observation of PD occurrences via an oscilloscope, while future developments will integrate a microcontroller for phase-resolved partial discharge (PRPD) analysis and remote alarm transmission. Operating within a frequency range of up to 1 GHz and powered by two 12V batteries, the system has been tested using a simulated PD circuit inside a Faraday cage. The experimental results confirm the system's ability to detect PD signals effectively. This work provides a cost-effective and optimized solution for continuous PD monitoring, contributing to improved condition-based maintenance strategies and reducing the dependency on costly, operator-dependent equipment.

title: A Predictive Framework for Fault Vulnerabilities in Edge Hardware Accelerators
authors: Ernesto Cristopher Villegas Castillo, Josie E. Rodriguez Condia, Juan-David Guerrero-Balaguera, Felipe Augusto da Silva, Michael Glaß
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Modern Hardware accelerators, such as Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), are increasingly being deployed in edge computing safety-critical applications, including autonomous and semi-autonomous automotive systems. However, evaluating fault vulnerabilities and reliability remains highly resource-intensive and time-consuming when using simulation-based fault injection (FI) campaigns. This challenge is amplified by the architectural complexity of modern devices and the huge number of workload scenarios, making traditional FI approaches impractical. This work introduces and evaluates a high-level modeling framework for the early fault propagation analysis and reliability estimation of hardware accelerators for edge computing. Our framework uses Colored Generalized Stochastic Petri Nets (CGSPNs) to map the most crucial features of a design into a model that allows the prediction of fault vulnerabilities in a design. The framework aims to support designers in estimating key reliability metrics, such as fault propagation rates, before low-level implementation (e.g., gate-level), thereby facilitating informed architectural decisions and the development of more robust systems.

title: Evolutionary Optimization for Low-Voltage, Wide-Temperature Range SRAM Cell Designs
authors: Rafael Knust, Thiago Brito, Fernanda D.V.R. Oliveira, Fabián Olivera
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Variability in nanometer CMOS manufacturing processes, wide operating temperature ranges, and continuous supply voltage scaling in low-power applications present significant challenges for static random-access memory (SRAM) designs, as they substantially degrade the stability of read and write operations. In this paper, we present a variability-aware evolutionary algorithm (EA)-based approach for low-voltage, widetemperature range SRAM cell designs. The proposed framework integrates Monte Carlo simulations into the EA fitness evaluation to enhance robustness against manufacturing process variations. To validate the effectiveness of the methodology, design examples are implemented in IHP 130 nm and TSMC 65 nm CMOS technologies. Extensive Monte Carlo simulations of the resulting SRAM cells are performed, covering both traditional performance margins and the cell operation in a 8 kb SRAM macro, using supply voltage of 0.6 V, temperature range from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> and operation frequency of 40 MHz.

title: Edge-Accelerated Monocular Depth Estimation: A Quantized U-Net on Kria KV260 for Real-Time Interior Scenarios
authors: Nicolás Urbano Pintos, Emanuel Trabes, Carlos Valderrama, Alaeddine Aajouj, Héctor Lacomi
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Real-time monocular depth estimation (MDE) is pivotal for edge applications like autonomous indoor navigation and AR/VR, yet achieving it on power-constrained devices remains a significant challenge. This work presents an optimized, INT8-quantized U-Net model specifically designed for the Xilinx Kria KV260 FPGA. Trained on the NYUDepthV2 dataset and compiled using the Vitis AI toolchain, our implementation achieves a throughput of 41.03 FPS with a latency of 24.3 ms and an RMSE of 0.9553 m. This represents a 2.27× speedup over a desktop-grade Intel i7-11700K CPU while maintaining comparable precision. Our contributions include: (i) a FPGAtailored U-Net (ii) an efficient quantization-to-DPU workflow, and (iii) a demonstration of the real-time MDE indoors on the KV260.

title: MT-MO: Efficient and Robust Non-Profiled Side-Channel Analysis Using Multitask Learning
authors: Huy-Thanh Le, Van-Phuc Hoang, Ngoc-Tuan Do, Xuan-Nam Tran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Multi-Output Deep Learning based Side Channel Analysis (MO-DLSCA) with its variants have demonstrated their efficacy in non-profiled scenarios. However, current MO approaches remain limited to sequential single-byte recovery, failing to exploit shared leakage characteristics across the cryptographic key and resulting in redundant training overhead. This letter introduces a Multitask Multi-Output (MT-MO) deep learning architecture to mitigate this issue. By leveraging a shared feature extraction backbone with vectorized output heads, our model simultaneously recovers multiple key bytes in a single training session. Both Multitask Classification (MT-MOC) and Regression (MT-MOR) variants are introduced to target masking, noise injection, and desynchronization countermeasures. Experimental results obtained from three randomly selected target bytes indicate that the proposed models reduce the execution time by approximately 11 times compared to the sequential single-task model, while maintaining success rates of at least 73.33 %.

title: CS-UNet++: A Cell Segmentation Network Based on Attention Mechanism
authors: Changsheng Zheng, Jiani Sun, Chuanwen Ren, Lihan Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of medical images is a fundamental component in clinical diagnosis, therapeutic planning, and prognosis evaluation. To overcome the shortcomings of conventional approaches in accurately segmenting small targets and handling complex backgrounds, a novel deep learning framework named CSUNet++ is introduced in this study. Built upon the UNet++ architecture, the model introduces a Channel and Spatial Attention Module (CS Module), which dynamically adjusts the channel and spatial feature weights to enhance the model's ability to perceive key features and capture global information. The CS Module is positioned between the encoding layers of UNet++, comprising four modules that effectively improve the expression capability of features at different levels. Experimental results in three publicly available datasets show that CSUNet++ outperforms in metrics like Mean IoU(MIoU) and Validation MIoU(Val_MIoU). In particular, on the TNBC dataset, CS-UNet++ achieved an increase of 8.860 % in MIoU and 4.085 % in Val_MIoU compared to UNet.

title: Cooperative Multi-LiDAR Deployment Using Decentralized Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hyeong Jun Park, Chang Mook Kang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Optimal placement of multiple LiDAR sensors is critical for autonomous driving, robotics, and smart city applications. This study compares three multi-agent coordination strategies—a local-search Greedy baseline, Independent Q-Learning (IQL), and Deep Q-Network (DQN)—for maximizing coverage in discrete, obstacle-rich environments. Experiments with 1-5 LiDAR sensors across varying obstacle densities show that IQL achieves the highest average coverage (89.5%) with low variance, while DQN exhibits irregular scaling and high sensitivity to environment changes. Our reward shaping strategy targets 30–40% sensor overlap to support inter-sensor coordination requirements. We additionally include a zero-shot evaluation for DQN as a transfer probe; the results suggest that generalization remains challenging in this discrete coordination setting. Results indicate that computationally efficient algorithms such as IQL can outperform complex deep learning approaches in robustness, scalability, and computational efficiency. These findings provide practical guidelines for designing multi-sensor systems in dynamic environments such as autonomous vehicles and urban infrastructure.

title: Complexity vs. Prevalence Assessment for Prediction Steps for VVenC Encoding Profiles
authors: Rodrigo Feldens, Sergio Bampi, Felipe Sampaio
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of the prediction tools implemented in the open-source VVenC encoder, focusing on their computational cost, prevalence, and overall efficiency. The experiments target both intra- and inter-frame prediction modules across the five VVenC coding profiles (faster to slower), aiming to capture the encoder's behavior across different scenarios. An efficiency metric was defined relating the prevalence to the measured computational complexity of each prediction tool within the video encoding process. Our results show that inter-frame prediction tools achieve significantly higher efficiency (up to 4.47) compared to intra-frame tools (up to 0.92), primarily due to their higher prevalence and similar computational costs. Thus, VVC novel intra-frame prediction tools present promising opportunities for optimization schemes. From another perspective, the inter-frame prediction's efficiency drops in more complex profiles and at higher resolutions, as heavier implementations of internal prediction tools are enabled, indicating a trade-off between complexity and performance. Looking at inter-frame prediction steps, Integer Motion Estimation (ME) plays an important role and benefits from hardware acceleration, while Fractional ME presents opportunities for adaptive optimizations. Affine ME, despite its computational cost, shows limited prevalence and emerges as the most promising candidate for complexity reduction and hardware optimizations.

title: AI-Powered Community Elderly Care: An Intelligent Companion System Integrating Emotional-Computing and Health Monitoring
authors: Juan Yang, Mei Li, Haoxue Zhou
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: Addressing the dual challenges of accelerated aging and shrinking family structures, this paper proposes an intelligent companion system for community-based elderly care in Chongqing. Integrating artificial intelligence with applied psychology, the system establishes a tripartite framework of “emotional computing-health perception-service adaptation“ to tackle practical difficulties including insufficient service resources, heightened loneliness among the elderly, and lagging chronic disease management. The system constructs a dynamic health profile that synthesizes physiological indicators (e.g., heart rate via PPG) and psychological data (e.g., depression risk via voice analysis and PHQ-9) to enable synergistic intervention. A six-month empirical study conducted in typical Chongqing communities demonstrated the system's significant effectiveness in alleviating loneliness (p < 0.001), reducing depressive symptoms (p < 0.001), and improving chronic disease management adherence (from 65% to 88<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">%</sup>).

title: Agentic AI in Network Slicing
authors: Anshul Anilkumar Mundakatil, Anjan Phani Raj Narla, Akshil Anilkumar Mundakatil, Sai Srinivas Muriki, Ahmed Hambaba
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Computer, Software and Modeling (ICCSM)
abstract: Network slicing has emerged as a pivotal innovation in modern telecommunications, enabling the creation of tailored virtual networks within shared physical infrastructures. By dynamically allocating resources to meet the unique demands of various applications, network slicing fosters both flexibility and efficiency. However, traditional methods for slice orchestration and resource allocation struggle with the complexity of multi-domain environments and rapidly evolving service requirements. To address these challenges, we propose a novel framework leveraging Large Language Models and multi-agent systems for automated slice management within Open Radio Access Network architectures. The system translates user intent into precise technical requirements through LLMs, ensuring seamless alignment between application needs and O-RAN configurations. These requirements are deployed in an Open RAN environment, where real-time performance monitoring and adaptive resource management occur through collaborative learning, enabling continuous system improvement by sharing insights between different components. Our approach incorporates Graph RAG for slice prediction, enhancing the system's ability to model complex relationships and dependencies within the network. We also present a standardscompliant O-RAN testing environment to validate the system's effectiveness in addressing the complexities of modern network slicing. This work underscores the transformative potential of combining LLMs with multi-agent systems to redefine resource allocation and orchestration in next-generation networks.

title: A Comprehensive Authenticated Dataset of Brahmi Script Characters for Recognition
authors: Tushar B. Kute, Premanand P. Ghadekar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: The Brahmi script is among the most ancient and historically prominent systems of writing in South Asia, with applications that warrant corresponding modern Indic scripts. Digitally recognizing Brahmi inscriptions holds the key to epigraphical studies, historical research, and cultural preservation. The efforts remained always undermined for the absence of a greater and authentic dataset. To meet this requirement, a new dataset is introduced, comprising 348 different classes of characters from the Brahmi script. The dataset contains a total of 7880 character images, each such image extracted from letter carvings over stone at seven salient archaeological sites in India: Naneghat Caves, Junnar Caves, Bhaje Caves, Karle Caves, Bedse Caves, Nashik Caves, and Pitalkhora Caves. The dataset design was an iterative and exhaustive process of image acquisition, segmentation of characters, and applications of preprocessing to increase visibility and recognizability of characters therein. The usability and robustness of the dataset were tested using four different deep learning models: a custom Deep Convolutional Neural Network (Deep CNN), InceptionNet, ResNet50, and XceptionNet. The best recognition accuracy of 98.07% was attained with a loss of 0.0958 using ResNet50, which shows the adequacy and quality of the dataset for the training of advanced machine learning models. This will help researchers by providing a valuable resource and accelerating the development of Brahmi script character recognition.

title: SocraTeach: AI Powered Personalized Mentor for Socratic Learnings
authors: Junaidali Shaikh, Varad Vaidya, Ayush Munot, Isha Ghorpade, Anuradha Yenkikar, Nilesh Sable
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: SocraTeach is a novel AI-driven educational platform aimed at altering user approaches towards learning through Socratic pedagogy. SocraTeach guides learners through interaction rather than simply providing information or knowledge. The learner progresses through a uniquely crafted puzzling sequence of questions. A custom cyclone of user answer evaluation, utilizing NLP and Machine Learning, sits at the center. Advanced Answer Checking Algorithms (ACA) measure the thoughtfulness, scope, and originality of the provided answers. Each user assessment analysis is optimized in such a way that further prompts and clarifications encourage deeper thinking and stretching recursive elaboration to refine previous answers. What marks SocraTeach as different is its approach to education, focused on dialogue, simulating a student-educator interaction where the learner takes center stage as an active participant in the process. SocraTeach allows learners from schools and self-learners at any formal education stage to foster curiosity, intellect, and engagement. The use of dynamic knowledge graphs to provide real-time feedback holds tremendous potential to revolutionize SocraTeach.

title: Temporal-Aware Crowd Anomaly Detection Framework for Violence Recognition Under Video Frame Discontinuities
authors: K Karthicka, M Saranya Meenatchi, G Varshini Ramithaa, S Santhi, S Kalaiselvi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Computing and Applications (AICAPS)
abstract: Real world video surveillance systems frequently encounter frame discontinuities due to network failures, occlusions and data corruption which usually degrade the performance of crowd anomaly detection algorithms. Traditional approaches tend to lose temporal consistency that results in lower accuracy for real-time applications. To overcome this limitation, this paper introduces a Temporal Aware Crowd Anomaly Detection Framework (TA-CADF) based on YOLOv8 based spatial feature extraction augmented with a K-Fold Temporal Consistency Module (KTCM) for video frame discontinuities. The proposed model is targeting the extraction of spatiotemporal edge features in order to bridge discontinuities between frames and provide smooth interpretation of scenes. The K-Fold Temporal Consistency Module improves the learning stability and generalization by rigorously testing performance on multiple folds. The experimental results indicate that the proposed approach achieves 97.34% accuracy while effectively reducing the impact of frame discontinuity. These results show the potential of the framework in live surveillance applications with an effective solution for edge-preservation video processing and public surveillance safety.

title: Review of AI-Based Lane and Obstacle Detection Techniques for Embedded Automotive Systems
authors: Aarti Pawar, Rutuja Patil, Sarvesh Patil, Pradumna Bedagkar, Hrishikesh Mehta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Autonomous driving and Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) rely heavily on accurate lane detection, vehicle recognition, and obstacle avoidance to ensure safety and reliability. Traditional computer vision techniques, though widely used, often fail under poor illumination, occlusion, and degraded markings. Deep learning models, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs), attention-based architectures, and YOLO-based frameworks, have advanced detection accuracy and adaptability, while GANs improve robustness under challenging conditions. Hybrid approaches combining lightweight CNNs with classical vision methods demonstrate practical trade-offs between efficiency and robustness for embedded deployment. However, deploying computationally intensive models on automotive-grade processors remains a key challenge due to constraints on latency, power, and functional safety. This paper reviews recent advances AI-driven lane and obstacle detection methods, assesses their suitability for real-time embedded systems, and highlights emerging hardware platforms such as NVIDIA DRIVE Orin, DRIVE Thor, and Qualcomm Snapdragon Ride that aim to bridge the gap between research and real-world deployment.

title: FITS in Electric Bicycle
authors: Vaishali Katkar, Sneha Pokharkar, Shailesh Shinde
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the importance of health monitoring and preventive care. This paper introduces a Fitness Indication and Tracking System (FITS) integrated into an electric bicycle, enabling real-time monitoring of physiological parameters such as heart rate, oxygen saturation, and body temperature. Sensor data is processed through a Wi-Fi-enabled microcontroller and displayed both on the bicycle's handlebar and a web-based application accessible via mobile devices. Experimental validation confirms the feasibility of the proposed system in combining fitness and healthcare monitoring.

title: Anomaly Detection Methods in Social Networks
authors: Rutika Arun Khedkar, Deepti Manoj Singh, Chhaya Gosavi, Aparna Hajare
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)
abstract: Social networks are widely developed and extensively used platforms that facilitate global communication and interaction. Popular sites like Twitter, Facebook, and others have revolutionized the way people connect and share information. However, these networks are often susceptible to misuse by malicious actors who exploit them to breach user privacy, spread spam, or cause harm through various sophisticated techniques. This review examines two advanced approaches to detecting anomalies in social networks: Dual Variational Autoencoder (VAE) with Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) and graphbased anomaly detection using structural graph metrics. The Dual VAE-GAN method combines deep learning techniques to capture nonlinear patterns in network structures and node attributes, ensuring robust anomaly detection with high accuracy on benchmark datasets. In contrast, the graph-based approach uses egonet analysis and metrics like Betweenness Centrality and Brokerage Value to identify nodes deviating from expected structural patterns. Both methods are evaluated for their statistical foundations, detection capabilities, and performance results, showcasing their effectiveness in addressing social network threats while highlighting their respective strengths and limitations.

title: A Short Review of Buffer Overflow Attack and its Prevention
authors: Ahmed Faraz
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: The unallocated random access memory locations provides loop holes to gain the access control of the system to the attacker. This loop hole in the software of computer system provides a big vulnerability to the attacker who gains the access of the system by exploiting Buffer Overflow Vulnerability. The Buffer Overflow attack is hybrid in nature, both the hardware and software are compromised by the attacker by gaining the access control of the system. As Internet of Things technology has been evolved and computer communication networks are more linked with it, the Buffer Overflow attack has become more security concern. In all the types of Buffer Overflow attack launched on the machines, the operating system level control is gained by the attacker which can harm the security of all the machines and devices connected to the compromised machines through Internet of Things. Securing Stack Pointer, Program Counter, Processor Status Word registers in safe locations in memory at the hardware level can prevent Buffer Overflow attack.

title: Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)

title: A Hybrid Spatial-Frequency Approach for Writer-Specific Handwriting Generation
authors: I.P. Anargha, M Sreeram
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: Generating realistic handwriting that mimics a specific writer is a challenging task in document analysis and deep learning. This work proposes a deep learning model that generates personalized handwriting by separating content and style information. A dual-path Style Encoder processes both RGB and Laplacian images to capture a robust style representation for each writer. A Content Encoder extracts character-level features from a standard font representation. These features are fused using Adaptive Instance Normalization (AdaIN) within a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) with a multi-scale discriminator to improve realism. The model is trained and evaluated on the IAM handwriting dataset, producing handwriting images that preserve character content while reflecting individual writer styles. The paper describes the network architecture, loss functions, and training procedure, providing a complete framework for controllable handwriting generation.

title: Comparative Analysis of Silkworm Incubator Based on Arduino UNO and Spartan-3E
authors: R Kamaladevi, A Varshini, Elakkiya, K.R Sathish Kumar, D Senthoor Seruvan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: In sericulture field, farmers are constantly struggling to maintain proper environment condition for silkworms growing to produce efficient quantity of silks. In this research work we propose microcontroller and Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) based approaches to maintain proper environment condition to cultivate healthy silkworm. The ideal temperature of the silkworm incubator is $27^{\circ} \mathrm{c}-30^{\circ} \mathrm{c}$, the light intensity has to be less than 312lux and the humidity has to be between $65 \%$ to $85 \%$. In both the systems, sensors such as temperature, humidity, light and actuators such as cooling fan, Light Emitting Diode (LED) light have been interfaced to maintain the ideal environmental conditions inside the silkworm incubators. A comparative analysis of these two approaches also carried out using Arduino UNO microcontroller and Spartan3E Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) in terms of speed of execution and hardware complexity. Image processing based healthy silkworm classification is also implemented to improve the yield of sericulture field.

title: Prediction Correction Enabled Power-Angle Stability Analysis for Stochastic Excitations-Disturbing OMIB Power System
authors: Limin Ding, Yongdong Chengdu
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: The proliferation of low-carbon energy sources and inverter-interfaced devices is driving the development of power systems has transitioned towards electrification and cleanliness, resulting in larger and more intense external excitations. This paper focuses on establishing effective stochastic dynamic models and numerical algorithms for power systems under Gaussian and Poisson excitations. In this scheme, the stochastic nature of Gaussian and Poisson excitations is defined, followed by the stochastic dynamic model is formulated. The Heun numerical method, based on prediction-correction concept, is employed to iteratively solve the proposed model. The quantitative analysis of power system stability is conducted to assess the impacts stemming from varying excitation intensities. Numerical simulations are undertaken to validate the correctness and rationality of the stochastic dynamic model for power systems under Gaussian-Poisson excitations and the Heun numerical algorithm. This research endeavors to serve as a comprehensive reference for the modeling and simulation of power systems incorporating stochastic elements, thereby facilitating the secure operation and optimal control of such systems.

title: Joint Passive Beamforming and Power Control for Full-Duplex Two-Way Wireless Systems Aided by Intelligent Reflecting Surface
authors: Xiangbai Liao, Jiagang Liu, Zhibin Pan
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Communications and Broadband Networking (ICCBN)
abstract: This paper investigates the adoption of an Intelligent Reflecting Surface (IRS) in a Full-Duplex Two-Way Communication (FD-TWC) system. We aim to minimize the total transmit power of the two FD sources under minimum Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) constraints by jointly optimizing the source power allocation and the IRS reflective beamforming. This joint optimization leads to a complex nonconvex problem. To tackle it, we propose two novel algorithms: a Fractional Approximation-based algorithm and a Maximum Slope Difference algorithm. Specifically, the first algorithm utilizes geometric properties and fundamental inequalities to simplify the constraints, and then employs the Lagrange method and fractional approximation to obtain the suboptimal transmit power allocation and the IRS reflecting beamforming. Alternatively, leveraging the special structure of the formulated problem, the second algorithm employs an iterative method to obtain the transmit power allocation and the IRS reflecting beamforming. In addition, We compare the proposed FD-IRS-TWC system with the traditional MIMO relay, random phase shift IRS and no-IRS schemes. Numerical results show that the proposed FD-IRS-TWC scheme can significantly reduce the power consumption over the conventional scheme.

title: Archives of Low-Illumination Image Enhancement Methods Based on Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Wencheng Wang, Xiaowei Lan, Guodong Zheng, Chenghao Liu, Ruiguo Wang, Weikai He, Dongfeng Li, Muwei Jian
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: Images taken under poor lighting conditions typically exhibit suboptimal quality, characterized by low brightness and diminished contrast. The enhancement of such images remains a pivotal area of investigation within image processing. This paper provides a systematic review of recent progress in deep learning-based methods for low-light image enhancement. Particular emphasis is placed on categorizing and discussing the diverse approaches that utilize Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs). It delves into their core principles and model characteristics, and lists commonly used benchmark datasets in this field. Based on this, the paper summarizes the core challenges currently faced by lowillumination image enhancement technology and provides an outlook on future research trends, with the hope of providing insights for the continuous development of this field.

title: Experimental Investigation to Find the Potential of Regenerative Suspension System Using Piezoelectric Energy Harvester
authors: V L Athul, A P Amal Raveendran, S Sandra, A Akhil, R Sailesh, Subin Michael
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Signal Processing &amp; Computational Intelligence (ICCSPCI)
abstract: The conventional vehicle suspension system dissipates mechanical energy produced due to vibrations caused by road irregularities as heat to its surroundings, leading to significant energy losses. This study focuses on recovering the lost portion of energy by converting it into electrical energy using piezoelectric materials like lead zirconate titanate (PZT4). This recovered electrical energy has been used to power several vehicle sensors. The research involves designing and fabricating an optimized experimental apparatus modeled as a cam irregularity setup, simulating real-world road irregularities. Experimental procedures have been conducted under varying motor speeds using a variable frequency drive (VFD), and energy outputs have been measured. The results demonstrated that a considerable portion of the mechanical energy from the suspension system could be converted into usable electrical energy, validating the feasibility of integrating a piezoelectric energy harvester (PEH) in automotive systems. The findings support the integration of piezoelectric energy harvester (PEH)- based regenerative suspension systems to improve energy efficiency in modern vehicles.

title: Research on Automatic Verification of Safety Risks of Secondary Operation Equipment in Smart Substation
authors: Zhang Li, Wang Dingjun, Wang Lina, Wu Jinyuan, Han Huarong, He Yijie
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of smart substation technology, numerous safety risks have emerged in these systems. This project has systematically collected and organized typical SCD virtual circuit configurations, enabling automated verification of secondary virtual circuit accuracy. By utilizing online monitoring and file download technologies to acquire dynamic equipment models, we achieved consistency checks between static and dynamic models of secondary devices. Additionally, real-time acquisition of device settings and soft-press plates was conducted for crosschecking against preset parameter lists. These comprehensive measures ensure secure and stable operation of smart substations, especially in the smart substation reconstruction and expansion project having a very important application.

title: Bringers of Order: Wearable Technologies and the Manufacturing of Everyday Life: James N. Gilmore: [Book Review]
authors: Maggie Hart
abstract: Presents reviews for the following list of books, Bringers of Order: Wearable Technologies and the Manufacturing of Everyday Life.

title: IGDMRec: Behavior Conditioned Item Graph Diffusion for Multimodal Recommendation
authors: Ziyuan Guo, Jie Guo, Zhenghao Chen, Bin Song, Fei Richard Yu
abstract: Multimodal recommender systems (MRSs) are critical for various online platforms, offering users more accurate personalized recommendations by incorporating multimodal information of items. Structure-based MRSs have achieved state-of-the-art performance by constructing semantic item graphs, which explicitly model relationships between items based on modality feature similarity. However, such semantic item graphs are often noisy due to 1) inherent noise in multimodal information and 2) misalignment between item semantics and user-item co-occurrence relationships, which introduces false links and leads to suboptimal recommendations. To address this challenge, we propose Item Graph Diffusion for Multimodal Recommendation (IGDMRec), a novel method that leverages a diffusion model with classifier-free guidance to denoise the semantic item graph by integrating user behavioral information. Specifically, IGDMRec introduces a Behavior-conditioned Graph Diffusion (BGD) module, incorporating interaction data as conditioning information to guide the denoising of the semantic item graph. Additionally, a Conditional Denoising Network (CD-Net) is designed to implement the denoising process with manageable complexity. Finally, we propose a contrastive representation augmentation scheme that leverages both the denoised item graph and the original item graph to enhance item representations. Extensive experiments on four real-world datasets demonstrate the superiority of IGDMRec over competitive baselines, with robustness analysis validating its denoising capability and ablation studies verifying the effectiveness of its key components.

title: A Novel Integrated Underwater Communication and Positioning Algorithm Based on OFDM-DPPM
authors: Yichen Xue, Wanlong Zhao, Chenguang He, Yufei Cheng
abstract: Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs) are indispensable in ocean exploration. To address their concurrent communication and positioning requirements during underwater operations, Integrated Underwater Communication and Positioning (IUCP) technology has emerged. This letter proposes a novel IUCP algorithm combining Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing and Differential Pulse Position Modulation (OFDM-DPPM), including waveform design and model construction. The OFDM-DPPM scheme demonstrates superior interference resilience and enhanced throughput compared to conventional modulation schemes. Simulations emulating underwater multipath channels validated the advanced performance of OFDM-DPPM in acoustic communication through comparative analysis. Multiple real-test experiments further confirmed its excellent communication performance and high-precision ranging capabilities.

title: Chemical-Disease-Gene Association Prediction based on Pretraining-Prompt-Finetuning Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network for Drug Discovery
authors: Xi Zeng, Jing-Wen Cai, Pei-Yuan Lai, Qing-Yun Dai, Chang-Dong Wang, Min Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Chemical-Disease-Gene (CDG) association prediction-encompassing Chemical-Disease (CD), Disease-Gene (DG), and Chemical-Gene (CG) interactions-is a cornerstone of drug discovery, as it underpins target identification and drug repurposing. While these tasks are inherently synergistic, existing methods often address them in isolation, failing to capture shared heterogeneous semantics and cross-task dependencies. We hypothesize that a unified pretraining framework can learn transferable biomedical semantics across CDG tasks, with task-specific prompt tuning enabling efficient adaptive fine-tuning without full retraining. To test this hypothesis, we propose the Pretraining-Prompt-Finetuning Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network (PPF-HGNN), a two-stage framework built on heterogeneous graph neural networks (GNNs) and prompt learning. Specifically, we construct a CDG heterogeneous graph, employ parameter-free metapath-guided message passing for high-order semantic capture, and optimize generalizable representations via a dual self-supervised objective (association prediction + feature reconstruction) during pretraining. For downstream tasks, task-specific learnable prompt vectors are introduced to adapt frozen pretrained representations to CD, CG and DG association prediction tasks via additive fusion, preserving core semantics while injecting task-specific biases. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate PPF-HGNN's state-of-the-art performance: AUC of 0.9633 (CD), 0.9939 (CG), and 0.9390 (DG), with F1-scores of 0.9157, 0.9668, and 0.8955 respectively-substantially outperforming six existing baselines. This work validates the pretrain-prompt-finetune paradigm for multi-task biomedical association prediction, providing a robust AI-driven tool to accelerate translational research and decipher complex CDG relationships. The source code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/ike-zengxi/PPF-HGNN</uri>.

title: GMPPO: Guided Model Predictive Path Optimization for Mobile Robots in Obstacle-Constrained Environments
authors: Lin Zhang, Wenhao Zhang, Runjiao Bao, Tianwei Niu, Shoukun Wang, Junzheng Wang
abstract: Autonomous navigation of industrial mobile robots in obstacle-constrained environment, such as manufacturing floors, warehouse storage systems, and factory inspection scenarios, poses significant challenges in ensuring path feasibility, safety, and real-time performance. This article proposes a guided model predictive path optimization (GMPPO) framework to address these issues. First, an enhanced convex feasible set algorithm is employed to generate robust initial paths by integrating decoupled dimensionality reduction and a dual-layer fault-tolerant mechanism. Second, based on this reference path, robot geometry, and real-time obstacle information, dynamic safety corridors are constructed to characterize local feasible spaces. Finally, these reference paths and corresponding safety corridors guide a high-fidelity model predictive optimization process, yielding smooth, dynamically feasible, and collision-free trajectories under nonholonomic constraints. Theoretical analysis establishes the resolution completeness and feasibility guarantee of the proposed method. Extensive simulations, real-world robotic experiments, and engineering deployments demonstrate that GMPPO significantly improves planning success rates, trajectory smoothness, and operational safety, while maintaining high computational efficiency and broad applicability.

title: Service Deployment and Task Offloading Algorithm for UAV-Parking-Vehicles Assisted Mobile Edge Computing
authors: Biao Xiao, Zheng Yao, Yan Yan, Baoxian Zhang, Cheng Li
abstract: In this paper, we study a UAV-Parking-Vehicles assisted Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) network for providing enhanced edge computing services, where a UAV serves to relay ground users’ tasks to parking vehicles having idle computing resources in the vicinity for processing.We formulate the problem of average task delay minimization in this case as a long-term discrete mixed integer programming problem. We transform this problem into two subproblems: Service deployment optimization problem at large time scale and task offloading optimization problem at small time scale. For the former, we define a system utility for measuring the effect of a service deployment profile at parking vehicles, formulate the system utility minimization problem for optimizing the service deployment at vehicles, and propose a Computing resource allocation and Genetic Algorithm based Service deployment Algorithm (CGSA) for obtaining optimized service deployment profiles at parking vehicles on a per cycle basis. For the latter, we propose a Bandwidth allocation, Task offloading, and Transmission scheduling Algorithm (BTTA) for determining optimized task offloading profiles for all users on a per time slot basis. Extensive simulation results show the high performance of our proposed algorithms compared with baseline algorithms.

title: Machine Learning Models for Analyzing FBG Pressure Sensor Data in Monitoring Leak in Water Pipeline
authors: Hossam Youssef, Matthias Fabian, Mounib Khanafer, Sumsun Naher, Kenneth TV Grattan, Tong Sun
abstract: Aging water and sewage pipelines require effective monitoring as leakage and burst incidents pose environmental, economic, and public safety risks. Many existing studies rely on laboratory or simulated data from electrical sensors installed in harsh sewer environments, which require regular cleaning and calibration. The novelty of this work lies in presenting a systematic, real-world evaluation of machine learning models for pipeline leak detection using fiber Bragg grating-based pressure sensors deployed in an operational pipeline test facility for the first time. The models evaluated include supervised models, such as Random Forest and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), and unsupervised models, such as Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and LSTM Autoencoder. These models are compared under identical experimental conditions and were evaluated under both serial (single sensor) and parallel (multi-sensor) configurations, using sliding windows of varying lengths. Weighted precision and F1-scores were used to address strong class imbalance across leak sizes. Results show that XGBoost achieved the best overall performance, reaching 77% precision and 52% F1-score at a 45-point window length. The results obtained are better than those reported in previous work, most of which rely on simulations or laboratory datasets and critically do not evaluate models under real operational pipeline conditions, as is done here. The findings demonstrate the robustness of supervised XGBoost when applied to small, noisy real-world datasets and they highlight the feasibility of integrating ML-based FBG sensors into cloud-enabled pipeline monitoring systems.

title: Standalone Augmented Reality Neuronavigation System for Accurate Pulse Delivery in Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation
authors: Wally L. Niu, Christopher C. Cline, Bruce L. Daniel, Christoph Leuze
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) is a noninvasive brain stimulation technique that uses magnetic pulses to safely modulate neural activity in specific brain regions, with particular use in the treatment of major depressive disorder. However, effective treatment protocols require multiple sessions over time and accurate targeting. Current neuronavigation systems can improve pulse delivery compared to standard scalp and measuring tape methods, but are costly and have time-intensive setups for recurring sessions. Wepresent an augmented reality neuronavigation system (AR-NS) that overcomes these limitations. The AR-NS is functionally similar to other neuronavigation systems but can operate entirely within a head-mounted display. Unlike traditional 2D navigation systems that require mentally fusing screen information with real-world actions, the AR interface allows operators to perform the procedure with reduced cognitive and hand-eye coordination demands. We measured the functional targeting accuracy of the AR-NS and the Localite TMSNavigator, a commercial neuronavigation system, using a TMS phantom we developed embedded with Hall effect sensors at four different sites. We determined the coil placement for each site that maximized the sensor response to a magnetic pulse using co-registered X-ray computed tomography and structured light scanner scans. A MagVenture C-B60 coil was placed and fired at each site 30 times in a randomized order according to each neuronavigation system. We measured the resulting magnetic pulse amplitudes. A non-inferiority test with a 5 mT margin and 97.5% confidence intervals indicated that the AR-NS demonstrated similar functional targeting accuracy as the Localite TMS Navigator across all but one stimulation site, indicating that augmented reality neuronavigation systems may offer more accessible delivery of TMS stimulation without sacrificing functional accuracy compared to current systems.

title: Dictionary Multi-Modal Temporal Graph Learning
authors: Meng Liu, Ke Liang, Miaomiao Li, Xueling Zhu, Xinwang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Temporal graph learning focuses on graph deep learning in real-world dynamic scenarios, which uses interaction sequence instead of adjacency matrix to observe the graph dynamic changes more microscopically from the perspective of time evolution. However, current temporal graph methods only focus on extra dynamic information, ignoring the large amount of multi-modal information contained in the real world. These information can reflect the rich changes in the real world from different perspectives. Ignoring them means that temporal graph learning still lacks the ability to restore and mine more complex real-world data. We argue that the main challenges causing the above phenomenon in temporal graph learning are the lack of multi-modal architecture and public multi-modal datasets. To solve the above challenges, we propose ModalTGL, which enhances the computational efficiency of the model in complex dynamic scenarios by introducing the dictionary graph network, and achieves multi-modal fusion by embedding tuning. In addition, we also discuss the effects of different time encoding functions on dynamic information preservation. At the data level, we build several multi-modal temporal graph datasets from different areas, and compare with multiple SOTA methods on these datasets. The experimental results verify the effectiveness of the ModalTGL method, achieving the performance improvement of up to 18.48%. Code and data can be obtained from <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/MGitHubL/ModalTGL</uri>.

title: KICGPTv2: Large Language Model with Knowledge in Context for Knowledge Graph Completion_supp1-3674507.pdf
authors: Yu Zhang
abstract: Knowledge Graph Completion (KGC) is an essential task aimed at mitigating the issue of incompleteness in knowledge graphs, thereby enhancing their utility for various downstream applications. Existing KGC models predominantly fall into two categories: structure-based and semantic-based approaches. Structure-based methods often encounter challenges with long-tail entities due to the scarcity of structural information and imbalanced entity distributions. Conversely, semantic-based methods, while addressing those limitations, necessitate extensive training of language models and specific finetuning for each knowledge graph, thus constraining their practical efficiency. To alleviate those limitations in both approaches, in this paper, we propose KICGPTv2, an innovative framework that synergizes a large language model (LLM) with traditional KGC methods. This integration effectively mitigates the long-tail entity problem without incurring significant additional training overhead. Central to the KICGPTv2 model is a novel in-context learning strategy, termed Knowledge Prompt, which encodes structural knowledge into demonstrations to effectively guide the LLM. Comprehensive evaluations on various KGC tasks, including link prediction, relation prediction, and triple classification, underscore the efficacy of the KICGPTv2 model, highlighting its ability to achieve competitive performance with reduced training demands and without the need for finetuning

title: Enhanced Approaches for Anomaly Detection in Streaming Data: Coupling Gaussian Distributions with Space Trees and Adaptive AutoEncoders
authors: Ercan Gunbilek, Zuleyha Akusta Dagdeviren, Hasan Bulut
abstract: Processing, analyzing and continuously monitoring big data streams in real time is crucial for the security and efficiency of organizations and individuals. While machine learning and deep learning have greatly improved anomaly detection, they also have drawbacks, such as reliance on offline data and poor performance, especially with scarce or fluctuating data. To address this problem, we propose two enhanced methods for anomaly detection in streaming data: Gaussian Space Trees (GSTrees) and the Gaussian Weighted Adwin AutoEncoder (GWAAE). The effectiveness of these methods was evaluated on real datasets (ECG5000, Credit Card Fraud Detection, SMTP) processed as streaming data. For the ECG5000 dataset, GSTrees performed excellently on all metrics, consistently achieving over 94%, while GWAAE excelled with an ROC-AUC of over 89% and over 84% on all other metrics. For the SMTP dataset, the ROC-AUC of both proposed methods is above 80%. GSTrees and GWAAE successfully identified anomalies with a recall of over 82% and a ROC-AUC of over 94% and maintained this success with remarkably low false negative and false positive rates in the Credit Card Fraud Detection dataset. These results emphasize the robustness and applicability of GSTrees and GWAAE for real-time anomaly detection in dynamic environments.

title: All-in-One Initialization of MEMS-INS for Tilt-Compensated GNSS RTK Receivers
authors: Jiahao Ai, Qijin Chen, Xiaoji Niu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: As an enhanced solution of global navigation satellite system (GNSS) real-time kinematics (RTK), RTK with tilt compensation function (tilt RTK) eliminates the need for manual pole leveling during surveying and expands the capability of high-precision RTK to some constrained environment. Through integrating a low-cost microelectromechanical system (MEMS) inertial measurement unit (IMU), tilt RTK derives tilt compensation from inertial navigation system (INS) attitude outputs. However, the navigation states of the INS, including position, velocity, attitude and preferably inertial sensor errors, must be initialized before being used to provide a navigation solution. The existing INS initialization solutions are often implemented step by step, typically requiring both static and dynamic conditions to complete the initialization of all parameters. Due to the lack of specific design for tilt RTK application, these initialization methods prove time-consuming and labor-intensive when applied to tilt RTK scenarios. A rapid and accurate initialization method for estimating all navigation states and inertial sensor errors remains vacancy for low-cost MEMS IMU in tilt RTK equipment. To address this issue, we propose an all-in-one initialization method for low-cost MEMS IMU in tilt RTK receiver, through swinging the surveying pole with its tip contact on the ground at a fixed point. The Corresponding pole motion scheme for such comprehensive initialization is designed. Through simulation and field test, we validate the function, efficiency, and accuracy of the proposed algorithm. The field test demonstrates that an 80-second swinging initialization achieves an attitude alignment accuracy (root mean square error, RMSE) of approximately 0.2 degrees, with velocity initialization accuracy of less than 1 cm/s for horizontal direction and 0.3 cm/s for vertical direction. Furthermore, the calibration precision (standard deviation, STD) is around 25 deg/h for gyroscope bias, and 600 mGal for accelerometer bias.

title: Automated PI Parameter Optimization for LLRF Systems: A Comparative Study of LLM-guided parameter tuning and Bayesian Optimization Approaches
authors: Lijuan Yang, Yilin Miao, Shihui Wei, Zhenglong Zhu, Zhen Gao, Zhaojie Chen, Qizhen Hou, Jiayi Peng, Jinying Ma, Chengye Xu, Xinghao Ding, Guirong Huang, Liepeng Sun, Rihua Zeng, Zhijun Wang, Feng Qiu, Yuan He
abstract: This paper presents a comparative study of LLM-guided parameter tuning and Bayesian Optimization (BO) for automated PI parameter adjustment in Low Level Radio Frequency (LLRF) systems. Both methods demonstrate effective performance across continuous wave and pulsed operation modes, addressing the challenges of traditional manual tuning in superconducting accelerator facilities. Multi-cavity validation experiments were conducted on both operation modes. For continuous wave 2-parameter optimization, both methods converge to optimal regions within 15 iterations. For pulsed 6-parameter optimization involving coupled amplitude-phase control loops, both methods achieve satisfactory results in around 20 iterations, meeting stability requirements for beam operation. Comparative analysis reveals distinct application advantages: BO is better suited for large-scale batch optimization scenarios, particularly excelling in multi-cavity parallel commissioning and high-dimensional parameter space exploration due to its systematic probabilistic framework. The LLM-guided approach is more appropriate for online optimization scenarios with strict operational constraints, where its conservative optimization strategy and traceable reasoning process make it ideal for parameter fine-tuning during operations. The study provides practical method selection guidelines for intelligent operation of large-scale accelerator facilities.

title: Deep-Saliency Foveated Ray Tracing for Real-time VR Rendering
authors: Yang Gao, Wencan Li, Shiyu Liang, Weizichuan Feng, Qing Xia, Shuai Li, Aimin Hao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Immersive VR applications demand high resolutions and refresh rates, posing significant challenges for real-time rendering.
