Foveated rendering mitigates this cost by exploiting properties of the Human Visual System (HVS), but conventional approaches often rely on oversimplified heuristic models that neglect high-level attentional cues, resulting in artifacts in peripheral regions. To this end, we present a neural saliency-driven foveated ray tracing framework that overcomes these limitations. Our method introduces a motion-aware foveation model to capture temporal dynamics and employs a lightweight convolutional neural network to predict saliency maps that reflect complex attentional patterns derived from eye-gaze data. The combination of these guides adaptive path tracing and filtering, enabling perceptually optimized rendering with minimal artifacts. Experimental results show that our approach improves perceptual quality over prior methods while sustaining real-time performance.

title: Dual Body Sensation: Effects of Visio-Tactile Presentation Methods in Parallel Agent Control
authors: Masatoshi Serizawa, Peerawat Pannattee, Yosuke Fukuchi, Vibol Yem, Yasushi Ikei, Nobuyuki Nishiuchi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Controlling multiple agents in parallel is an important challenge in human–robot interaction, with growing applications in remote collaboration, telepresence, and multi-agent coordination. However, empirical work on distributed embodiment remains limited, particularly in scenarios where independently controlled avatars operate in distinct environments. To address this gap, we propose the concept of Dual Body Sensation (DBS): a novel theoretical construct of distributed embodiment, describing a state in which a single operator simultaneously experiences embodiment for two independent robots in distinct environments during their control. We conducted a within-subject experiment to investigate the cognitive feasibility of DBS using a 2×3 factorial design that varied visual presentation (independent view, superimposed view) and tactile presentation (no tactile, bilateral tactile, unified tactile). The results indicated that DBS emerged across all conditions and was significantly enhanced under the unified tactile condition. The combination of the superimposed view and unified tactile conditions also increased the sense of body ownership and the sense of agency, while the superimposed view condition reduced cybersickness and cognitive load. These findings provide empirical support for DBS and inform the design of future multi-agent interaction systems that support flexible distribution of bodily control and awareness.

title: A Novel Indicator for Improving the Fault-Tolerant Bipancyclicity of k -Ary n -Cube Networks with Exponential Faulty Edges
authors: Hongbin Zhuang, Jou-Ming Chang, Xiaohua Jia
abstract: The k-ary n-cube Qkn is a widely employed interconnection network for data center networks (DCNs) due to its favorable properties, including node/edge symmetry, recursive structure, regularity, and ease of deployment. Among the numerous structures studied in Qkn, the cycle is fundamental for addressing various graph-related problems in DCNs. This has motivated extensive research on fault-tolerant cycle embedding within Qkn. However, existing methods for cycle embedding often exhibit limited fault tolerance since they fail to account for the dimension-oriented nature of edge faults in Qkn. In this paper, we overcome this limitation by using the partitioned edge fault (PEF) model, a recently developed model that explicitly considers dimension-specific fault distributions. Under the PEF model, we propose a new indicator called partition-edge fault-tolerant bipancyclicity, which accurately reflects the practical fault characteristics of Qkn. For Qkn with even k ≥ 4, we determine the exact value of this indicator and prove its optimality under the PEF model. Moreover, for Qkn with odd k ≥ 3, we develop three fault-tolerant embedding algorithms that establish a lower bound for this indicator. Furthermore, we conduct numerical evaluations and simulation experiments to analyze the bipancyclicity of Qkn under varying scales of edge faults.

title: From Spectral Detection to Economic Decision: A Hyperspectral AI Framework for Business-Oriented Varroa Mite Control
authors: Jihan Lee, Aiden Lee, Taewoo Park, Sangho Sun, Abhijit Debnath
abstract: The Varroa destructor mite is a leading cause of colony losses, yet most hyperspectral studies end at pixel-level detection and do not indicate when treatment is economically justified. The leakage-controlled testing with spatially blocked and Leave-One-Tile-Out (LOTO) protocols demonstrates strong within-scene robustness under conservative testing, without explicitly stating that cross-scene generalization is the case in view of the current annotation scope. The spectral-to-economic decision framework used here is validated with respect to internal consistency as well as operational feasibility, with a transition to true leave-one-scene-out evaluation postponed for future work upon obtaining additional annotations. Instead of proposing a new spectral backbone, the present work presents a decision-oriented hyperspectral framework based on strong infestation risk estimation while explicitly economically optimizing to ensure interpretability and reliability under limited annotation. Pixel-level probabilities produced by a support vector machine and a random forest are aggregated into a scene-level Hyperspectral Infestation Risk Index, which is then mapped to expected monetary loss. The operating rule is chosen by maximizing net benefit, with a closed-form threshold τ ⋆ ≈ c/(λpy) based on treatment cost c, loss cap λ, honey price p, and expected yield y. Numerical validation shows that, on the BUT-HS1 dataset, the models achieve a macro area under the precision–recall curve of 1.00 and a macro area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 1.00 under standard splits. Under a spatially conservative leave-one-tile-out protocol with buffered regions and hard negative mining, the weighted accuracy is 0.896 with only localized failures at tile boundaries, confirming genuine spectral separability. Decision-curve analysis indicates that, at 2023 United States price and cost levels, τ ⋆ = 0 (treat all) maximizes value; when per-hive treatment cost rises to 8 dollars, τ ⋆ ≈ 0.09 defers about 16–17% of treatments while retaining about 98% of avoidable loss, reduces profit-at-risk by $18–$26 per scene, and saves$2–$8 per colony per application.

title: Virtual Synchronous Compensator with Virtual Braking Resistor for Stable DC-link Voltage Control
authors: A. Camboni, J. Roldán-Pérez, F. Mandrile, M. Prodanovic, R. Bojoi
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The Virtual Synchronous Machine (VSM) algorithm represents a well-established control method for interfacing renewable energy sources with the grid. However, VSM suffers from transient stability issues, particularly when operating as a Virtual Synchronous Generator (VSG). Furthermore, maintaining control stability can be more challenging when the DC-link voltage control is implemented. Indeed, the DC-link voltage is controlled by regulating the power exchanged with the grid. However, the injected power can be limited by the applied current saturation strategy, potentially leading to DC-link overvoltage. Therefore, this article proposes implementing a Virtual Synchronous Compensator (VSC) algorithm to regulate the DC-link voltage. The VSC structure ensures the control remains stable even under current-saturation conditions. Furthermore, the Virtual Braking Resistor (VBR) concept is introduced to keep DC-link operation within the permitted voltage range without requiring communication between the DC/DC and DC/AC converters. The proposed control solution has been experimentally validated using a 30 kVA converter setup and a grid emulator, meeting both AC and DC test requirements.

title: A Two-Stage Modulation Strategy for Active Power Sharing Control of Open-End Winding PMSM With Isolated DC Source
authors: Guozheng Zhang, Yufei Liu, Xin Gu, Tingna Shi, Changliang Xia
abstract: The open-end winding permanent magnet synchronous motor (OW-PMSM) with isolated DC source offers a flexible DC source configuration. Thus, the active control of the charging and discharging of both inverters is required to accommodate the state of charge of different DC sources. A novel two-stage modulation strategy of “power sharing” and “reference voltage synthesis” is proposed in this paper to realize the active power control of the OW-PMSM with isolated DC source. First, two new clamping modulation modes are presented by making full use of the redundant switching states of the dual-inverter. Then three power modulation methods are established by the combination of different clamping modes, which allows the bidirectional energy flow between the two DC sources. Meanwhile, the basic voltage vector sequence to synthesize the reference voltage remains the same under any arbitrary power modulation methods. Consequently, the output current waveform quality of the dual-inverter is not affected. Finally, the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed strategy are validated by the experimental prototype of a dual-inverter fed 4.3 kW OW-PMSM.

title: Separating the Road from the Driver: A Clustering Approach to Unbiased Fuel Efficiency Analysis
authors: João G. S. Botelho, Rodrigo Balani, Janaine D. Rodrigues, Eduardo A. P. Santos, E. P. José
abstract: Fuel efficiency comparisons across different driver types are often misleading because they fail to account for substantial variations in driving context, such as urban versus highway operation. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a context-aware framework that applies clustering techniques to connected vehicle data to group trips into homogeneous driving contexts. Using the k-means algorithm on a large-scale dataset of vehicles operating across Europe, we show that trip-level features such as speed, distance, duration, and the proportion of time spent within specific velocity intervals (e.g., stationary time) are sufficient to reliably identify distinct driving contexts. This work makes three core contributions. First, we introduce a privacy-preserving method for driving context identification that relies exclusively on low-frequency, non-sensitive telematics data, eliminating the need for GPS trajectories. Second, we establish an empirical framework to disentangle and normalize environment-induced biases, enabling fair fuel efficiency comparisons by restricting performance evaluation to drivers operating under comparable conditions. Third, we validate the proposed approach using real-world data, demonstrating that the identified contexts exhibit statistically distinct fuel efficiency distributions, thereby highlighting the necessity of context-based separation for unbiased performance ranking. Beyond supporting fair fuel efficiency assessment and CO2 reduction strategies, the proposed framework is shown to be applicable to fleet behavior analysis, enhanced recommendation systems, and product development tasks, such as electric vehicle battery sizing.

title: Overview of Vehicle Localization: The Shift from Egocentric to Cooperative Positioning
authors: Ivo Silva, Hélder Silva, Fabricio Botelho, Jorge Cabral, Cristiano Pendão
abstract: Accurate and reliable positioning is essential for a wide range of vehicular applications, including navigation, traffic management, Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), and autonomous driving. Traditionally, vehicle localization has relied on egocentric approaches where each vehicle estimates its own position using onboard sensors. Since the introduction of Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS), these methods have greatly advanced with multiple satellite constellations and correction techniques such as Real-Time Kinematic (RTK) and Precise Point Positioning (PPP). Yet, GNSS-based positioning remains challenged by signal obstructions in urban canyons, tunnels, and dense foliage. To overcome these limitations, sensor fusion combines data from inertial sensors, cameras, LiDAR, and radar to enhance accuracy and robustness, especially in GNSS-degraded conditions. Recently, the rise of vehicular communications has enabled a new paradigm: cooperative positioning. By exchanging data among vehicles and infrastructure, this approach mitigates individual sensor errors, improves accuracy, and supports shared situational awareness. This paper presents a comprehensive overview of vehicle localization, covering the transition from egocentric to cooperative positioning. We review key technologies and sensors, analyze cooperative architectures, and classify recent research, providing insights into current trends and future directions in vehicle localization.

title: Prescribed-Time Bipartite Synchronization of Fuzzy Signed Networks via Fuzzy Reinforcement Learning
authors: Tao Dong, Yukang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: This paper investigates the prescribed-time bipartite synchronization (PTBS) problem of fuzzy signed networks (FSNs). To solve this problem, a bipartite synchronization error is constructed and a novel performance value function integrated with the prescribed time and desired accuracy is proposed, which translates the challenge of achieving bipartite synchronization within a prescribed-time into an prescribed-time optimal regulation problem. Subsequently, a fuzzy controller is introduced to address the prescribed-time bipartite synchronization issue. The controller is realized through a fuzzy actor-critic framework, where both actor system and critic system are fuzzy logic system. Additionally, the convergence analysis shows that the bipartite synchronization error can indeed converge to a predefined residual set within the specified time threshold, thereby effectively solving the synchronization problem. Finally, the competitive-cooperative fuzzy recurrent neural network is applied to verify the obtained results. The simulation results show that the proposed algorithm not only enables the network to achieve PTPS, but also exhibits better performance than existing methods.

title: A Reconfigurable Simultaneous Transmitting and Reflecting Array with Independent and Continuous Controls of Phases and Transmission-Reflection Ratio
authors: Kaixu Wang, Zhitao Du, Peifeng Wang, Zeyu Wu, Xue Ren, Qinyu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper proposes a novel simultaneous transmitting and reflecting (STAR) array capable of independently manipulating the transmitted phase, reflected phase, and the transmission-reflection (T-R) power ratio. A reconfigurable metasurface (RMTS) employing a novel controlling scheme is proposed in this paper, which can realize a full range tuning on the transmitted/reflective phases synchronously or a hybrid continuous-discrete control in asynchronous mode. Meanwhile, the T-R ratio of the RMTS can be continuously and arbitrarily tuned from total transmission to total reflection without affecting the phases. The core of the RMTS is a hybrid coupler loaded with two reflective phase shifters with independently controllable phases. Based on this RMTS, a 9×9 STAR array with a center operating frequency of 5.8 GHz is constructed to validate the design. Both simulated and measured results demonstrate that the proposed STAR array achieves ± 45° beam scanning independently on both sides with high aperture efficiency while maintaining arbitrary T-R ratio tuning capability. This design enhances the STAR array’s control capabilities over both phase and power division, making it more favorable in future wireless communication systems.

title: Anomaly Detection in Sensor Data using LSTM Networks for Predictive Maintenance
authors: Atul Srivastava, Geetanjali Singh, Vijay Shankar Sharma, Kamlesh Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Credit card fraud, a pervasive and costly issue, has become increasingly sophisticated as technology advances. Detecting fraudulent transactions promptly is crucial to safeguarding both consumers and financial institutions. This paper explores the challenges and potential solutions associated with credit card fraud detection. The increasing prevalence of fraudulent transactions poses a significant threat to the financial security of individuals and businesses. The increasing prevalence of fraudulent transactions poses a significant threat to the financial security of individuals and businesses. Developing robust and efficient fraud detection systems is essential to mitigate the financial losses associated with fraudulent activity. To detect fraud in real time, this study attempts to create a machine learning model. The detection of fraudulent transactions is made more accurate and efficient with the use of deep learning and anomaly detection. The goal is to provide a valuable tool for financial institutions to protect their customers from financial losses due to fraudulent activity. Our proposed machine learning model achieved a high accuracy rate in distinguishing between legitimate and fraudulent transactions, outperforming traditional rule-based methods. The implementation of real-time fraud detection capabilities significantly reduced the financial losses incurred by the financial institution.

title: Temporal Sentiment Analysis and Explainability in Yelp Reviews: Mitigating Fake Review Impact
authors: Nithish Kumar S, Lalithamani N
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Generally online review platforms such as Yelp, Google, Facebook, Amazon, etc., provide a solid foundation for truly understanding the customer sentiments of any product. But these reviews can be influenced by the presence of fake reviews and the lack of transparent sentiment analysis. In this study, we have used both traditional and modern NLP (Natural Language Processing) techniques such as Logistic Regression, BiLSTM (Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory), and DistilRoBERTa for sentiment prediction using YelpZip and YelpNYC datasets. In addition to that, we have used DistilRoBERTa model for FRD (Fake Review Detection), CPD (Change Point Detection) technique to visualize sudden changes in the temporal trend of the sentiment of a specific restaurant and finally, we have used model explainability techniques such as LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) and SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations). LIME was used for predicted sentiments and for fake review detection tasks, both LIME and SHAP were used. Our analysis revealed that fake reviews in the Yelp datasets have a subtle but noticeable impact on the temporal trends of customer sentiments of a restaurant. For sentiment prediction, Logistic Regression yielded 80.97% accuracy on the YelpZip dataset and 10.34% on YelpNYC, demonstrating its low generalizability. The BiLSTM model performed better, with accuracy of 83.08% on YelpZip and 83.39% on YelpNYC. Notably, the DistilRoBERTa model outperformed both, with accuracy of 86.91% and 89.21% on YelpZip and YelpNYC respectively, signifying their excellent cross-dataset generalization capability. In FRD, DistilRoBERTa also showed strong performance, with an accuracy of 86.79% on YelpZip and 89.84% on YelpNYC.

title: Integrating AI and IoT for Sustainable Operational Performance in the FMCG Industry
authors: A.Jagadhambal, R Priyadharshini, Pavithra R, Nandhini M, Aisurya M M, Dinesh Kumar K
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The manufacturers of fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) have been working under pressure to maintain the high quality of products, lower their prices, and at the same time comply with the world sustainability requirements. Current operational decision-making systems including many rule-based or manually optimized ones, do not deal with the complexity and real-time variability of current production and logistics systems. A new AI-IoT-based Sustainable Operations Optimization Model, AISO-FMCG, that combines resource sensing with the help of IoT, AI-based demand prediction, sustainability-aware optimization of supply chains, and a digital-twin simulation engine. The proposed model has a 57.1% downtime reduction, 91.6% forecast accuracy, 33.3% inventory cost reduction, 23.1% energy savings, 28% carbon reduction, and 35.2% packaging waste reduction, which is a significant improvement over FMCG operations based on multi-modal FMCG data and synthetic operational data. Scalability and robustness of the model are proven by the simulation experiments with MATLAB. The findings indicate that AISO- FMCG is a replicable data-driven framework that can provide sustainable, resilient, and cost-effective FMCG manufacturing.

title: Generative Adversarial Networks for Facies Prediction in Subsurface Modeling
authors: Ajeet Ram Pathak
abstract: Facies classification represents a fundamental component of subsurface modeling, directly influencing reservoir characterization accuracy and seismic interpretation reliability. Traditional seismic inversion techniques frequently encounter challenges related to wave propagation non-linearity and substantial uncertainties from limited subsurface observations. This study presents a data-driven methodology leveraging Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Networks with Gradient Penalty to generate geologically realistic facies distributions from the SEAM Phase I Elastic Earth Model. The workflow encompasses acoustic impedance derivation from velocity and density models, binary lithology proxy generation through percentilebased thresholding, stability-enhanced WGAN-GP architecture design incorporating instance noise and exponential moving average mechanisms, quantitative evaluation using spectral distance metrics, and systematic model selection through convergence diagnostics. The architecture achieves a spectrum distance of 2.61 while maintaining class balance with generated facies proportions of 0.47 matching training distributions. Comparative analysis with conventional geostatistical methods reveals substantial improvements in training stability, sample diversity, and predictive reliability. Visual inspection of generated $1024 \times 1024$ mosaics validates spatial continuity and geological plausibility across multiple scales. These results establish the viability of deep generative models for scalable subsurface facies modeling in high-performance computing environments, with direct applicability to uncertainty quantification workflows requiring multiple equiprobable realizations.

title: Slot-Loaded Multiband Circular Antenna for Satellite and Wireless Applications
authors: Vaishnavi Loganathan, Selvaraj Damodaran, Janani Krishnan Srinivasan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: A compact, high-gain multiband circular slotted patch radiator is proposed for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}, \mathrm{X}$</tex>, and Ku frequency regimes. The design employs a slot-loading progression - circular, hexagonal, and nonagonal - to achieve resonances at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.3,7.2,8.5$</tex>, and 13.8 GHz. Operational metrics are assessed through S11, bandwidth, gain, and VSWR. A detailed parametric analysis is conducted on key structural parameters including patch cut dimensions, substrate thickness, and feed width to optimize performance. The antenna demonstrates return loss below −10 dB and gain magnitudes of 10.3 dBi, confirming its suitability for advanced wireless and satellite communication systems. Future work will explore further optimizations and the potential for integration with advanced communication systems.

title: Comparing the Humanness of Machine-Generated and Human-Authored Text
authors: Autumn Toney, Leticia Bode, Tiago Ventura, Ethan Wilcox, Lisa Singh
abstract: None

title: Transformer with Self Attention Mechanism for Electric Motor Fault Detection
authors: Ashish Kumar Sinha, Prince, Prashant Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Reliable fault detection in electric motors is essential in ensuring operational safety, minimizing downtime, and lowering maintenance costs in modern industrial systems. Long-range temporal relationships and intricate nonlinear patterns found in motor state monitoring data are frequently difficult for conventional signal processing methods and conventional deep learning models to capture. The present manuscript suggests a transformer-based fault detection model with a self-attention mechanism for intelligent electric motor fault diagnosis to address these issues. The proposed methodology enables efficient feature learning without relying on handcrafted features or recurrent neural networks. This is done by directly modeling temporal correlations within multi-sensor motor signals using the Transformer architecture. Under different operating settings, the self-attention mechanism improves sensitivity to subtle fault characteristics by adaptively highlighting signal segments that are relevant to the defect. To learn discriminative fault representations, raw or minimally preprocessed vibration and/or stator current inputs are segmented and embedded before being processed by stacked attention layers. Benchmark motor fault datasets under various operating situations are used to validate the efficacy of the suggested approach. The Transformer-based model consistently outperforms traditional convolutional and recurrent neural network approaches in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, according to experimental results. Furthermore, the model has enhanced generalization performance and great resistance to noise, suggesting its appropriateness for practical industrial applications. These findings demonstrate that attention-based transformer designs provide a potent and scalable solution for sophisticated electric motor status monitoring and problem detection.

title: A BiLSTM-Based Framework for Toxic Comment Classification
authors: Shubhangi Tirpude, Aayush Tirmanwar, Shravani Bhuwan, Ayush Sachan, Pragati Borkar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Toxic language, including hate speech and harassment, is becoming more prevalent as online discussions expand on social media and forums. Intelligent, automated solutions are required because it is impractical to moderate this content by hand. In order to identify harmful comments, this study presents a deep learning method that combines Natural Language Processing(NLP) and a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory Network (BiLSTM) model. In order for the model to learn from all kinds of harmful comments, we first clean and prepare the text data before addressing the problem of imbalanced categories using a number of strategies. Our BiLSTM model classifies comments into six groups, ranging from hate and threats to general toxicity. With a ROC-AUC score of 0.95 and excellent performance, the system is prepared for online real-time use.

title: Efficient Human Facial Emotion Recognition by SNN inspired CNN Features Based model on Video data
authors: Anu Roopa Devi S, Ruban Nersisson
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Human emotion identification from video data is a challenging but crucial endeavour. It can be used in smart systems, mental health monitoring, and human-computer interaction. The proposed methodology examines the performance effects of various feature extraction techniques and classification algorithms. A stand-alone AlexNet model and a hybrid strategy that combines data from InceptionNet and AlexNet are the two feature extraction techniques being contrasted. Through the use of threshold encoding, spikes are produced from these features and fed into Support Vector Machines (SVM) and k-Nearest Neighbours (kNN), two conventional machine learning classifiers. Using the first 10 actors from the RAVDESS dataset, our experiments demonstrate that the hybrid AlexNet + InceptionNet feature extraction outperforms standalone AlexNet, achieving 95% accuracy with SVM and 90.83% with KNN, compared to AlexNet’s 76.66% (SVM) and 64.16% (KNN) accuracy. This study demonstrates the possibility of integrating SNN-inspired spike encoding with potent pre-trained deep learning models for reliable emotion recognition.

title: An application of Neural Network for Human Performance Modelling
authors: Keattikorn Samarnggoon, Mohammad S. Hasan, Hongnian Yu, Russell Campion
abstract: Human Performance Modelling (HPM) is needed in many applications e.g. predicting driver performance for semi-autonomous vehicles, eSports gamers, adaptive machines in manufacturing to improve safety, productivity or a response from a machine. However, it is often challenging to establish a reliable HPM. This paper proposes and validates Neural Network (NN) models for human performance using a simulated Inverted Pendulum Driven Capsule Model (IPDCM) platform as a case study. The joystick data is utilised to build a classification and a regression model to predict the IPDCM’s movement direction and position, respectively. Then the number of neurons in the single hidden layer is optimised for both models using 10-time-10-fold cross-validation. Finally, the optimised classification and regression NN models are applied to a blind test dataset which exhibits accuracies of 92.2% and 77.01%, respectively.

title: Advanced Encryption Algorithm for Medical Image Security Using Chaotic Systems and DNA Computing
authors: Jayanti Rout, Ashutosh Soni, Swatisipra Das, Mrutyunjaya Sathua, Surendra Kumar Nanda
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The medical image is important in digital healthcare systems. They are used to represent different types of scans and diagnostic results. Their illicit modifications can lead to improper medical treatment and harm life. As a result, they require proper security. Various existing encryption algorithms are prone to different cryptanalysis techniques. This necessitates the requirement for an efficient cryptosystem to secure images with minimal compromise in efficiency. This paper presents a novel multi-layer cryptosystem specifically designed for medical imaging applications. It combines chaotic systems, fractal geometry, DNA computing, and reversible matrix operations. This approach was tested on medical CT scans. It achieved an high Number of Pixels Change Rate (NPCR) of 99.605, an entropy of 7.999 bits for the encrypted image, and various other ideal metrics. It also achieves a perfect reconstruction without error. The entire encryption and decryption required 0.8s and 0.7s, respectively. Various other assessments demonstrate the robustness of the approach against various cyberattacks. The domain-agnostic design of the approach makes it flexible to deploy in diverse application scenarios. The perfect reconstruction with robust architectural design makes it ideal for a modern healthcare environment.

title: A Hybrid LSTM Forecasting Models with Dual Signal Decomposition: A Comparative Study for Energy Consumption
authors: Fatima Zohra Oulad Laid, Khaled Ferkous, Charaf Abdelkarim Mosbah, Soundous Sekayar, Abderahmane Bellaour
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Accurate short-term electricity load forecasting is vital for grid reliability and renewable integration. This study evaluates Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) models enhanced with four decomposition methods—WD, WPD, CEEMDAN, and VMD—tested in three settings: raw LSTM, single-stage hybrids, and dualstage hybrids. Using five years of hourly consumption data from a Turkish Mediterranean city, results show that hybrid models significantly outperform the raw LSTM. CEEMDAN-based configurations achieve the highest accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2} > 97\%$</tex>, nRMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 3 \%$</tex>). Overall, dual-decomposition hybrids offer robust, precise forecasts for energy management. While findings are promising, validation on broader datasets is needed, and future work will address scalability and computational efficiency

title: Hybrid Energy Model for Underground Mining: Integrating Diesel Generator, Fuel Cells, and Batteries
authors: Venkatesh G, K.R.M. Vijaya Chandrakala, Sampath Kumar S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This paper operates on the detailing of hybrid isolated power system design, model building, simulative study and performance evaluation of hybrid isolated power system for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50 \text{HP}(37.3 ~\text{kW})$</tex> induction motor (IM). The IM should run continuously with the complete energy requirement of 149.2 kWh for 4 hours utilizing hybrid isolated power system as a backup system. The system consists of a 15 kW proton exchange membrane (PEM) fuel cell, 100 kWh lithium-ion battery, and a diesel generator with four different topologies. The model was simulated and modeled using MATLAB/Simulink ® on editor platform where parameters of every component were tuned to achieve the stable operation, best transient response with the fast dynamic behavior. The PEM fuel cell was fed with DC-DC boost converter and inverter. The lithium-ion battery component relates to the bidirectional DC-DC converter to Battery Management System (BMS), while diesel generator has an automatic voltage regulator (AVR). A control strategy whereby the control unit can provide source transition, load sharing and real-time co-ordination of the elements. The primary performance index of the work is voltage regulation in dynamic situations, Total harmonic distortion < 3% and maintaining the system efficiency above 90 % with green incentive certified system with lower carbon emission footprints

title: From Data to Action: Machine Learning Applications in Predicting and Preventing Waterborne Diseases
authors: Sonam Singh, Saroj Singh, Alok Kumar Gupta, Ayushi Patel, Divya Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Machine learning has grown in significance for data Analysis, categorization, and prediction due to the rapid increase in the large quantity of data on the water environment. Compared to conventional models used for water-related disorders, data-driven models based on machine learning are more effective at solving more complicated nonlinear situations. Within the field of water borne diseases research, numerous systems have been constructed, monitored, simulated, evaluated, and optimized using models and conclusions obtained from machine learning. Waterborne infections remain the leading cause of sickness and death among people worldwide. Waterborne infections are similar to food-borne infections in that they can be caused by Bacteria that are frequently connected to food-borne transmission. Waterborne diseases can occasionally trigger breakouts associated with meals or events where food was first thought to be a component. Although the fundamentals of sanitation and water treatment are widely understood, but many people lack access these because of inadequate funding, lack of inspiring leadership, and lack of good planning. The issues include understanding links to the environment, identifying endemic and pandemic waterborne diseases, chemical contaminants, and emerging pathogens resistant to conventional water filtration methods. New mathematical methods and satellite imagery are providing fresh insights into the study of water diseases. Since diagnosing waterborne diseases is costly, it is easier and more accurate to forecast them with machine learning algorithms. Machine learning may also offer solutions for managing the security of watershed ecosystems, reducing water pollution, and improving water quality. In this research, we investigate the situations in which various water habitats, including drinking water, groundwater, surface water, & seawater, have been subjected to the evaluation of waterborne diseases using machine learning algorithms.

title: Data-Driven Multimodal Fault Diagnosis and Forecasting for Electric Vehicles with Hybrid Temporal Deep Learning Models
authors: Umesh K. Patil, L. S. Patil, Prashant S. Gurupadagol, Swapnil Gadgune
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are the future of vehicle engineering because they produce zero emissions and offer better environmental and green energy benefits compared to traditional fuel-powered vehicles. In previous work, charging/discharging faults were used as a predictive fault identification method to enhance operational reliability, safety, and efficiency. To solve this problem, a novel Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) and Temporal Convolutional Network-Long Short-Term Memory (TCN-LSTM) approach for Battery cell imbalance detection and Motor and inverter faults Input data contain source battery voltage, current, and state of charge (SOC) and motor outcome. Adaptive Noise Cancellation (ANC) preprocessing is applied to reduce noisy multimodal EV data, ensuring reliable distributions for correct fault estimate and prediction. Empirical Wavelet Transform (EWT) is employed for feature extraction, enhancing detection accuracy and computational efficiency. The classification approach should help improve the accuracy of diagnosis and become a controlling implement to promote the effectiveness of conservation and operation of modern EVs. The proposed hybrid classification properly forecasts battery degradation, SOC irregularities, thermal anomalies, motor/inverter faults, and sensor variations under variable driving situations. MATLAB simulations enhance experiment performance, resulting in MAE 0.542, MAPE (%) 54.237, RMSE 0.736 for improving the reliability of EV. This method enables real-time, data-driven decision-making for protective maintenance, enhanced safety, and improved battery lifecycle management

title: A Self-Healing 6G Network Model Using AI-Driven Predictive Analytics
authors: Brijesh Kumar Sharma, Jofia Jose Prakash, Abhishek Dwivedi, Vikas Sharma, Shivanshi Singh, Vineeta Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The 6G era heralds communication networks requiring strongly autonomous, resilient, and self-sustaining architectures capable of providing continuity of service under dynamic and complex operating conditions. This paper presents a Self-Healing 6G Network Model driven by AI-based predictive analytics to anticipate, analyze and self-heal faults in the network before they can impact service quality. The model incorporates deep learning-driven anomaly detection, reinforcement-learning-based fault prediction, and automated reparation policies with the support of Software-defined Networking (SDN) and edge intelligence. By leveraging real-time telemetry, spatio-temporal traffic patterns and multi-domain context-sensitive data, the model achieves early fault prognostication, intelligent root-cause analysis, and autonomous reconfiguration of network resources. Experimental software simulations yield improved accuracy of fault detection, reduced downtime and enhanced Quality of Service (QoS) when compared with traditional reactive self-healing mechanisms using Python MATLAB. The suggestions point towards the potential of AI self-healing as a fundamental foundation block for the ultra-reliable, zero-touch 6G communication networks of the future.

title: An Al-Driven System For DDoS Detection And Forecasting
authors: Raja. J, Anugu Madhav Reddy, Gadhari Sanjay Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks, which can overload servers and networks and prevent authorized users from accessing essential services, are one of the most dangerous threats to modern digital infrastructures. These attacks cost companies money and damage their reputation in addition to causing downtime. To address this growing problem, the proposed study offers an artificial intelligence (AI)-powered framework for detecting and forecasting DDoS attacks using state-of-the-art machine learning techniques. The UNSW-NB15 dataset, which represents contemporary attack behaviors and provides a more accurate benchmark than earlier datasets, is used in the study. Two ensemble-based algorithms, Random Forest and XGBoost, were employed to classify and predict DDoS traffic. Random Forest’s accuracy of 89% was marginally lower than XGBoost’s 90%. The experimental results demonstrate that ensemble learning techniques are effective in distinguishing malicious DDoS traffic from legitimate network activity, achieving high accuracy and robust generalization across attack patterns. Crucially, the framework incorporates predictive analytics to enable the forecasting of attack likelihood, going beyond detection. Organizations are able to foresee threats and fortify defenses prior to disruptions thanks to this proactive approach. The results demonstrate how AI-driven models can improve cybersecurity resilience and provide a promising path for further study in predictive defense tactics.

title: Experimental Energy and Latency Analysis in Multi-Environment MQTT IoT Deployments
authors: Izdihar Wanda Syahputra, Tohari Ahmad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) enables continuous environmental monitoring using low power devices; however, the impact of environmental variability and communication behavior on node level energy consumption is still not well understood in real world deployments. This study evaluates a three node ESP32 based monitoring system operating for 14 days in outdoor, semi-indoor, and indoor environments for mung bean cultivation. Each node measures temperature, soil moisture, light intensity, voltage, current, and latency, and transmits data to both local and cloud MQTT brokers. The results reveal clear differences across environments, reflected in sensing frequency, current usage, and total energy consumption, while correlation analysis shows that individual environmental parameters exhibit weak relationships with overall energy use. Unlike most existing MQTT energy studies that rely on simulations or single environment indoor experiments, this work presents a measurement driven analysis based on real voltage, current, and reconstructed active time data collected from a multi environment agricultural deployment. Furthermore, the study evaluates application level MQTT delivery latency using a dual broker architecture, which differs from pure network round trip time (RTT) analysis.

title: An Overview of Artificial Intelligence in Neuro-Oncology: Early Tumor Detection
authors: Anupam Gupta, Preety Swami
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The incorporation of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) methodologies has several novel opportunities such as earlier tumor detection, better surgical planning, and personalized care. The combination of new methods in the advanced imaging pipeline (pre-processing, segmentation, classification, and decision support) shows that convolutional, hybrid, and ensemble models have the ability to achieve more than 90 % accuracy on public MRI datasets. In some cases, AI models achieve close to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 0 \%}$</tex> accuracy on small datasets through transfer learning and image fusion-based features. According to the review, AI application in neuro-oncological imaging delays diagnosis and thereby increases specialist availability and enables cost-effective triage, especially in underdeveloped areas. For effective AI benefits safe swallow, we emphasize certain prerequisites for clinical implementation, which include: privacy-preserving multi-center data sharing, an explainable AI to reveal solutions for the black-box opacity, strict external validation on heterogeneous populations and different scanners, and workforce/infrastructure preparedness (e.g., federated deployment, optimized protocol, and user training). The review finishes with recommendations and research gaps designed to address the challenges experienced with the implementation studies to provide fair, dependable, and wide-reaching care in neuro-oncology.

title: Existing statistical measures and techniques using Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in diagnosis and prognosis of lung cancer using multi-modal medical images: A systematic review
authors: Sivabarathi M, Kathirvelan J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Lung cancer creates a tremendous source of cancer deaths around the world, calling attention to the requirements of accurate, automated interpretation of lung tumours and predictive approaches to enable effective treatment. "Artificial intelligence and machine learning" were playing a crucial role in localising and classifying lung cancer using "multi-modal medical imaging modalities, including X-ray, Computed Tomography (CT), Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and Positron Emission Tomography (PET)." This systematic review synthesises current research on statistical and computational methods for examining lung cancer, using deep learning models with fusion strategies. This analysis highlights the strengths, limitations, and research gaps in the literature, indicating that hybrid attention-based approaches may surpass traditional techniques. The findings of multi-modal image combinations such as "x-rays and CT scans by modified AlexNet (MAN) with support vector machine (SVM) classifier" achieved accuracy of 97.27%, "CT and PET scans by MAN-SVM classifier" attained accuracy of 98.10%, "X-rays and CT scans with gold standard data (Histopathological data) by Inception-ResNet" has 95.35% of accuracy, also "CT and PET scans with clinical and genomic data by bi-directional encoder representation from image transformer (BEiT) based vision transformer model" has 94.0±0.7% of accuracy, which suggest that while AI-driven models hold significant promise for lung cancer diagnosis, further progress in dataset standardisation and cross-model fusion is essential for successful professional execution.

title: Emotion-Aware Learning: A Real-Time Facial Expression Approach
authors: Suraj Singh, Shailendra K. Mishra, Sneha Soni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The subject of detecting emotions is expanding quickly and is crucial to enhancing human-computer interaction. Recent studie demonstrate significant advancements in facial emotion recognition using computer vision as well as deep learning, particularly with datasets such a FER-2013. Studies do, but also highlight the difficulties that existing approaches still have when integrating various data kinds and preserving accuracy under practical circumstances. Researcher are currently concentrating on multimodal systems that employ text, audio, even brain signals like: EEG in addition to facial expressions to better comprehend emotions in a more comprehensive and context-aware manner in order to get around all these limitations. Based on these findings, a hybrid deep learning system designed specifically for an online learning platforms is presented in this work. The architecture employs Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCNs) to describe temporal emotional dynamics, a Convolutional Block focus Module (CBAM) for adaptive focus on prominent face regions, and ResNet50 for spatial feature extraction. In certain scenarios, efficient preprocessing methods enhance system generalization. With accuracies of 91.86% on RAF-DB, 91.71% on FER2013, 95.85% on CK+, and 97.08% on KDEF, experimental evaluations show cutting-edge performance. Additionally, the system runs in real time, with a GPU speed of 28 frames per second (fps) and a Jetson Nano speed of 15 fps.The review and proposed framework together show that while computer vision and deep learning are useful techniques for identifying emotions, future developments will be made in multimodal, context-aware, and application-specific systems that go beyond controlled environments and achieve dependable real- world deployment.

title: SPR-PCF Sensor with Gold-Coated Flat Surfaces for Tunable RI Detection
authors: Srishti Singh, Dharmendra Kumar, Vijay Shanker Chaudhary, Santosh Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This study details the design and numerical analysis of a highly sensitive photonic crystal fiber (PCF) sensor. This sensor utilizes surface plasmon resonance (SPR) to detect refractive indices between 1.34 and 1.39. The sensor structure consists of a solid-core PCF featuring a hexagonal arrangement of air holes with varying geometries (circular and elliptical), along with a flat gold-coated surface to improve the interaction between guided light and surface plasmons. Finite element method (FEM) simulations are employed to evaluate the sensor’s performance. The sensor demonstrates strong confinement loss peaks that shift with changes in analyte RI, enabling precise detection. With a peak wavelength sensitivity of $6000 \mathrm{~nm} / \mathrm{RIU}$ and an average of $2400 \mathrm{~nm} / \mathrm{RIU}$, the proposed sensor is capable of detecting refractive index changes as small as $1.66 \times 10^{-5}$.

title: Generative AI and Its Implications for the Authenticity and Legal Use of Video Evidence
authors: RWMR Dharani Rajasinghe
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The advancement of generative artificial intelligence via deepfake technology presents significant challenges for the trustworthiness of video evidence and the requirements for legal acceptance in investigative and judicial processes. This document examines the legal implications of AI video alteration techniques by investigating how AI undermines confidence in evidence and assessing the inadequate capabilities of current detection methods. The research concludes, based on thorough technological and legal analysis, that AI-modified media necessitates urgent technological development, enhancements in legal frameworks, and ethical progress to mitigate potential risks. Studies indicate that current forensic tools are ineffective in identifying sophisticated deepfake material, necessitating the creation of more robust verification techniques, as well as a reevaluation of standard evidence assessments. The paper concludes with a recommendation for the incorporation of interdisciplinary approaches to ensure the integrity of video evidence through technological advancements aligned with legal reforms and global regulatory practices in the age of AI.

title: A Hybrid AI Approach for Precision Agriculture: Combining Time-Series Analysis with Large Language Models
authors: Kiran Patil, Harish Vijay V, Manasha Arunachalam, Ippatapu Venkata Srichandra, Keerthika T
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Selection of crop is important decision in agriculture which impacts profitability and sustainability. Farmers and land owners often face this challenges in choosing best crops in variable factors like climate conditions, fluctuating market prices, and other financial constraints. This project presents the design of hybrid approach(LLM and ML) which helps in Multi-Criteria Decision Support System (MCDSS) in crop selection by integrating multiple data sources based on trends. Traditional decision making approaches are experience-based, which makes them inefficient in handling complex, dynamic data patterns. To address these limitations and constraints, this system uses predictive models to estimate profit projections for viable crops under given conditions and also making use of Large Language Model (LLM) to synthesize complex outputs into natural language recommendations and supporting realtime, context-aware query resolution through a chatbot. This application aims to help in decision accuracy, improve the return on investment (ROI) in agriculture sector.

title: Optimized V2G Strategies for Enhancing Grid Stability in Renewable Integrated Energy Systems
authors: Purnima Sharma, Rishabh Dev Shukla, Sunny Vig
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This paper presents a simulation-based study on the integration of electric vehicles (EVs) into a renewable energy grid using Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology. The study is aimed at enhancing grid stability and reducing the necessity for costly energy storage facilities based on stored energy in EV batteries. A 24-hour energy model is created using MATLAB with the incorporation of solar and wind power generation, dynamic load demand, and bidirectional EV energy interaction. ML(ML) approaches are also investigated to optimize V2G operations through data-driven models trained on historic and real-time data. The models are used to assist charge/discharge decisions according to dynamic grid conditions. The research illustrates how ML-capable V2G scheduling has the potential to minimize peak load, reduce operating expenditures, and pave the way for a cleaner and more resilient power system. The findings illustrate the capability of smart V2G control systems to pave the way for the installation of intelligent grid infrastructures and cleaner energy use practices.

title: Deepshield34: Frame Level Deepfake Detection Using CNN with ResNet34 Backbone
authors: Tejas V. Kalal, Kamini A. Shirsath, Shital S. Bhandare, Nikhil Singh Jodha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: As deepfakes become more common on digital platforms, reliable detection is vital. For this study, we propose a hybrid ResNet-34-based deepfake detection model with a preprocessing pipeline that involves frame extraction, face detection, and resizing followed by frame-level classification. The model was tested on FaceForensics++ and Celeb-DF datasets and achieved the highest accuracy of 87.5%, demonstrating solid performance between real and fake frame discrimination. Frame-level predictions were also aggregated for the final video-level classification to further strengthen reliability. The entire system was also deployed as a web application, where the users could upload video files and obtain interpretations from clear visual output

title: Rural Spatial Evolution Characteristics in the Suburbs of Guangzhou Based on ArcGIS and Dual Quantitative Models
authors: Hua Huang, Chen Zhang, Xiaolan Zhuo
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: To reveal the dynamic evolution law of spatial resources in rural areas of urban outer suburbs, this study takes 20 towns and streets in the outer suburbs of Guangzhou as the research object, selects four time sections from 2005 to 2020, and based on the 30m-resolution China Land Use/Cover Dataset (CNLUCC). It integrates ArcGIS spatial analysis and Python automated processing technology to construct a system of “remote sensing interpretation - technical modeling - spatial visualization”, and collaboratively applies the land use dynamic degree and transfer matrix models to analyze the evolution characteristics of primary and secondary spatial types and the law of land use type transfer. The results show that from 2005 to 2020, the regional space presented three-stage characteristics: “urban space expanded rapidly first and then stabilized, agricultural space continued to shrink but at a slowing rate, and ecological space remained overall stable but sensitive areas decreased”. During the core urbanization expansion period from 2005 to 2010, the dynamic degree of urban space reached 13.99 %, and after 2010, it shifted to the coordinated development of “development protection - sustainability”. The standardized technical framework constructed in this study provides scientific support for the optimization of territorial space planning and urban-rural integrated development in rural areas of outer suburbs.

title: Deep Audio–Visual Feature Extraction for Multimodal Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Mamta Goyal, Vivek Srivastava
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: This work shows a deep audio-visual feature extraction framework for multimodal speech emotion recognition. Such an approach aims for robust emotion classification by jointly employing complementary information from speech and facial expression variables. On the audio side, handcrafted descriptors such as MFCC-based prosodic features are combined with CNN–BiLSTM embeddings obtained from log-Mel spectrograms in order to capture both low-level and high-level temporal patterns. The visual branch extracts facecentered frames, applies a fine-tuned residual network to obtain deep spatial features, and models expression dynamics with recurrent or transformer-based encoders and self-attention. Individual modality embeddings are projected into a shared latent space and jointly fused by a gated cross-modal attention mechanism that identifies the more reliable modality for each utterance dynamically. Experiments on benchmark multimodal emotion corpora have revealed that the proposed hybrid audio representation and gated fusion considerably outperform unimodal baselines and very simple concatenation with considerable improvement in accuracy, UAR, and macro-F1 score. This shows that a principled design of features with adaptive multimodal fusion plays a key role for the accurate and robust speech emotion recognition system.

title: Examination of Power System Stability in High-Penetration Renewable Scenarios
authors: Bishro.H. Rasool, Asra. H. Al-Denenawe, Nikolay V. Korovkin
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: The proliferation of inverter-based resources, such as wind and solar photovoltaics, is driving a fundamental transformation of the global energy landscape. This is a necessary step toward decarbonization, but it also presents significant challenges to power system stability. This paper discusses some of the stability-related problems that are being experienced in systems with high renewable penetration. We review the decline in system inertia and its direct implications for frequency stability, the impact on voltage regulation, and the emergence of sub-synchronous oscillations. Time-domain simulations on a modified IEEE 9-bus test system are conducted to compare the dynamic performance under a conventional and a high-renewable penetration scenario. Results quantitatively show a 65% increase in Rate-of-Change-of-Frequency (RoCoF) and a 0.28 pu deeper frequency nadir following a generation loss contingency. The paper concludes by discussing mitigations, including grid-forming inverters, synthetic inertia, and advanced energy storage systems, that are integral to ensuring the secure and reliable operation of future power systems.

title: An Innovative Energy Management Techniques for Hybrid EVs
authors: Gundi Ice, Sundeep Siddula
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Innovative energy management systems in hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs) have advanced as a result of the move towards sustainable transportation options. This abstract examines the effects of combining ultracapacitors, batteries, and fuel cells in these systems, emphasizing how they may improve vehicle performance and have positive environmental effects. Fuel cells offer high energy efficiency and extended range by converting hydrogen into electricity, while batteries provide substantial energy storage and power for continuous driving and regenerative braking. Ultracapacitors, with their rapid charge and discharge capabilities, handle peak power demands and boost vehicle responsiveness. The synergy of these technologies within a unified energy management system optimizes power distribution, extends vehicle range, reduces emissions, this strategy not only enhances fuel efficiency but also helps cut down on greenhouse gas emissions and reduces reliance on fossil fuels. but also fosters the development of more adaptable and resilient transportation systems. The research highlights the many benefits of these hybrid setups, such as improved performance, more energy efficiency, and more operational flexibility, and it represents a major advancement in environmentally friendly automobile technology.

title: An Efficient Anaphora Resolution Framework for Text Summarization
authors: Farjana Farvin Sahapudeen, D. Vaishnavi, N. Moratanch, M. Anuradha, R. Ramya
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Anaphora resolution is a challenging discourse-level task that involves identifying the correct antecedents of referential expressions in text. This work presents a novel rule-based approach for resolving pronominal and nominal anaphora, including references to verb phrase antecedents. Unlike conventional methods that rely mainly on gender and number agreement, the proposed system incorporates high-level syntactic and semantic rules to improve resolution accuracy. These rules are integrated with fine-grained linguistic constraints reported in earlier studies, enhancing overall efficiency and robustness. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms standard anaphora resolution systems by effectively utilizing rich linguistic information for antecedent identification. Anaphora resolution plays a vital role in natural language processing tasks such as term extraction, question answering, and text summarization. In this study, the proposed pronominal anaphora resolution method is applied within a cluster-based summarization framework to improve summary coherence and reduce ambiguity caused by unresolved references and achieved 4.7 ROUGE Score.

title: Optimizing the Accuracy of Spam Filtration Techniques Through Q-Learning
authors: Mrunali Bharadwaj D S, Ruhika Sai S, T. V. Smitha
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: This paper puts forward an adaptive reinforcement learning-based approach for spam detection in Online Social Networks. The framework of this approach allows it to effectively handle the rampant problem of concept drift by classifying the spam detection problem as a sequential decision-making problem using the Markov Decision Process. This paper proposes the usage of a Q-learning-based agent to dynamically learn the most optimal policy through continuous interactions between the agent and the environment. The proposed model penalizes false positives and false negatives while rewarding correct classifications, thereby refining the policy over time. The environmental feedback allows the agent to learn continuously, ensuring sustained performance even with evolving spam patterns. This work highlights the promising framework of reinforcement learning models in spam detection platforms while also enabling the development of a long-term spam detection model facilitating improved spam detection in Online Social Networks.

title: Developer Sentiment in Open Source: Effects of Commit Frequency, Time, and Issue Characteristics
authors: Mahmudur Rahman, Mohammad Kawsar Ahamed Siam, Moudud Hassan, Shohel Ahmed
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Developer emotions in open-source projects strongly influence productivity, code quality, creativity, and team dynamics. Sentiment analysis of commit messages provides insights into these emotional states and their impact on project outcomes. In this study, we analyzed 630,164 commit messages from the 20 MAD dataset using a fine-tuned seBERT model. Contributors were grouped by commit frequency to examine how sentiment varies with activity levels. We also studied correlations with commit time, issue type (bug vs. non-bug), and priority. Our findings show that low and medium-frequency contributors express more negative sentiment. Commits made in the afternoon are generally more negative than morning or late-night commits. High-priority bug-related tasks trigger stronger negative emotions. We validated our findings using multiple parametric tests for statistical significance. Our findings can help project managers intervene early with support, adjust schedules, manage workloads, and provide emotional guidance during stressful periods.

title: A Hybrid LSTM-VGG16 and Fuzzy Logic Framework for Illegal Land Occupation and Flood Damage Assessment from Satellite Imagery
authors: S. Madhavan, V. Logeshwar, G. Sivagurunathan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Under this work, a combined scheme of tracking down cases of illegal land occupation and measuring flood damages through satellite images are also presented. The method of analyzing the pattern of space and time land use uses a deep learning and fuzzy logic method. High-level spatial features are extracted in images using a pre-trained VGG16 convolutional neural network. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network is used to sequence and analyze these features to display dynamic variations and discover illegal land occupation. In flood damage assessment, fuzzy logic system is applied to data uncertainties occurring with pre and post event imagery to facilitate subtle damage assessment. The framework creates real time notifications to the authorities automatically to aid quick response to disasters and sound decision making in land management. The system, written in Python and powered by geospatial libraries, shows potential to improve the urban planning and environmental agency in terms of providing accurate and timely understanding of land use dynamics and the effects of disasters.

title: A Spatial-Temporal Learning Framework for Robust Deepfake Video Detection
authors: G. Shanmukhi Rama, Swathi Edem, Isha Padhy
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Deepfakes have become increasingly popular due to the rapid growth of generative artificial intelligence (AI) technology. These convincingly modified media typically contain invented material that is difficult to differentiate from reality. In the digital age, identifying these deepfakes is a crucial task for guaranteeing the authenticity and originality of information. Existing solutions primarily focus on spatial consistency, often neglecting temporal inconsistencies that occur between frames in deepfake videos. To address this, we present a deepfake detection model that leverages the Swin Transformer for spatial feature extraction and 3D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for temporal consistency learning. The extracted feature representations are fused using a feature concatenation strategy to enhance classification performance. The model was trained on the FaceForensics++ dataset and evaluated for generalization on Celeb-DF(v2), achieving an accuracy of 89%. These results demonstrate its robustness in detecting deepfakes across different datasets, highlighting its effectiveness in diverse and challenging evaluation settings.

title: Parameter-Efficient Federated Multimodal Alignment Tuning for Heterogeneous Clients
authors: Xu Tan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Large-scale vision-language models like CLIP achieve breakthrough performance in zero-shot classification and cross-modal retrieval by learning aligned representations from massive image-text pairs. However, their centralized training paradigm faces data sharing challenges in privacy-sensitive domains. Although federated learning enables collaborative training without sharing raw data, it encounters the cross-modal alignment challenge with heterogeneous client modalities. To address this challenge, a parameter-efficient federated multimodal alignment tuning framework Fed-MoAlign is proposed in this paper. In Fed-MoAlign, the model is decomposed into vision encoder, text encoder, and alignment layer. The backbone is frozen and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) layers are inserted into encoders based on pretrained CLIP. Unimodal clients train corresponding encoder LoRA parameters independently, while multimodal clients additionally update the alignment layer. To enable unimodal clients alignment training, a dynamic prototype-based pseudo-modality generation algorithm is proposed where prototypes are maintained at the server from multimodal clients, and pseudo features are generated by unimodal clients via similarity-weighted prototype retrieval. Furthermore, a hierarchical selective aggregation mechanism based on cross-modal mutual information is introduced, designing differentiated strategies for different network layers according to their roles in multimodal alignment. Experiments on MS-COCO and Flickr30K demonstrate that Fed-MoAlign achieves 55.1% and 35.6% I2T R@1 respectively, outperforming state-of-the-art federated multimodal methods CreamFL by 3.0% and 3.3%. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of our proposed components.

title: Bias-Compensated Maximum Quaternion Correntropy for Adaptive Filtering
authors: Minyong Wei, Qiangqiang Zhang, Zhengwen Tu, Xueqin Yang, Jianfeng Liu, Dongyuan Lin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Quaternion adaptive filters have been widely used in processing three-dimensional (3-D) and 4-D signals. To improve the filtering performance and estimation accuracy in the quaternion errors-in-variables (QEIV) model where the output signal is disturbed by non-Gaussian impulsive noises, this paper addresses the issue of quaternion parameter estimation. First, a novel bias-compensated maximum quaternion correntropy (BCMQC) criterion is proposed to resist impulsive outliers and mitigate the bias induced by noisy inputs, simultaneously. Then, based on an alternating optimization strategy involving explicit input compensation, the BCMQC algorithm is derived using the quaternion generalized Hamilton-real (GHR) calculus. Moreover, the Sherman-Morrison formula is employed to derive a computationally efficient update rule. Finally, simulations demonstrate the improved filtering performance and robustness of the proposed algorithm in the QEIV model.

title: Transformer-Based NLP Models for Predictive Consumer Behavior Analysis
authors: Venkatesh Ankarla Sri Ramuloo, Lokesh Vegi, Sumanth Banakar, Awanit Kumar, Rohit Maheshwari, Ravinder
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: The social media has transformed to be one of the top drivers of consumer perception and purchase intention especially in new luxury markets like India. In this paper, transformer-based Natural Language Processing techniques (NLP) will be suggested as predictive, AI-based methodology to model consumer behavior. Unlike the other past descriptive studies, we use a BERT based classifier of sentiment and intent to predict purchase forecast in social media content. It was further extended to 5,100 Instagram captions and 1,200 online product reviews each having the positive, neutral or negative sentiment labels and the binary purchase-intent indicators annotated on them individually. The models that were analyzed include Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and BERT. According to the experimental outcomes, it has been established that BERT is always superior to the traditional machine learning and deep learning methods with an accuracy of 91.6, F1-score of 0.92 and an area under the ROC curve (AUC) of 0.94 in the prediction of purchase intent. Such results demonstrate that contextual language representations provide a better ability to capture the subtle emotional and aspiration signals in the luxury consumption environment compared to rule-based or association-mining systems. The study provides a predictive low scale alternative to Apriori based market basket analysis and has explicit applications to personalized marketing campaigns, recommendation systems and real-time social listening.

title: Design and Implementation of a Bifacial Solar-Powered Water Pumping System Using Boost Converter for Off-Grid Homes
authors: Kiruthiga Devi V, Veena P, Jeyabharath R, Prabhakaran K, Loganath D, Pradeep M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The aim of this research project is to design and implement a Gallium Nitride FET (GaN-FET) boost converter in conjunction with a bi-facial photovoltaic panel-based water pump to enhance the reliability of energy usage in off-grid homes and to improve the overall efficiency of the energy usage. In this research, there are two groups. Group 1 refers to the conventional monofacial solar panel + silicon-MOSFET converter configuration, and Group 2 refers to the proposed bifacial solar panel + GaN-FET converter configuration. The system performance was evaluated under 6 different irradiance levels using MATLAB/Simulink R2025a simulation and hardware prototype testing. Using the GaN-FET based converter, the system consistently generated a stable <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$48-\mathrm{V}$</tex> output with less than 0.5 V peak-to-peak ripple across all irradiances from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 ~\text{kW} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 ~\mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>. With respect to the irradiance level, the bi-facial configuration produced between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}-\mathbf{2 5} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> more power than the monofacial module. The average efficiency of the complete system was approximately 96.5 %. With regard to the battery backup, the transition from bright light conditions to dark light conditions resulted in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 8 - 3 2 \%}$</tex> more average power delivery and approximately 30 % more total daily volume of water production compared to the monofacial + Si-MOSFET system. Bifacial Solar-Powered Water Pump with a GaN-FET Converter Offers Improved Reliability and Efficiency of Energy Usage in Comparison to Monofacial Systems and is therefore suitable for residential off-grid water supplies.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Secure Data Sharing in Cyber-Physical Systems: A Comparative Study
authors: P. Arthi, Swapna S, Sangeetha K, Chandrika G, Moorthy Agoramoorthy, K. Malathy
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) are computer-based systems that combine computer-related intelligence and realworld phenomena and are actively used in such spheres as intelligent transportation, smart healthcare, industrial control, energy systems, and others. Reliable and safe information exchange between heterogeneous CPS components has been a significant obstacle because of the problem of data integrity, access control, scalability, and management of trust. The promising element to mitigate these challenges has been the blockchain technology that offers decentralized, immutable and auditable data exchange mechanisms. The paper provides a comparative analysis of data sharing solutions in CPS environments, based on blockchain-enabled secure data sharing. The paper compares various blockchain designs, consensus algorithms and data management plans with regard to security, latency, scalability and system reliability. By making comparative assessment, the paper shows the advantages and shortcomings of the currently available blockchain-based CPS solutions and makes significant design trade-offs to deploy CPS in the real world. The results have proved that blockchain can greatly contribute to transparency and trust in sharing CPS data, but lightweight and hybrid designs are needed to address the real-time and resources requirements of cyber-physical settings. The research will inform the researchers and practitioners to develop sustainable, secure, and resilient CPS data-sharing models.

title: Strengthening Password Security in Virtual Learning Environments: An Analysis of Protocols, Compliance, and User Behavior
authors: Bin Hu, Ifrah Malik, Fanyu Zeng, Noman Sohail
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: This study evaluates password security protocols in virtual learning environments (VLEs), addressing critical gaps in current practices and proposing enhancements to improve security and user satisfaction. Using hypothetical four platforms (A, B, C, and D), the study examines the effectiveness of various security measures, including minimum password length, special character requirements, multi-factor authentication (MFA), and password expiry periods. The findings reveal significant disparities in security practices across platforms, with Platform D, which implements stringent password policies and MFA, demonstrating the highest user compliance, fewest security incidents, and greatest user satisfaction. Conversely, Platform B, with the least stringent measures and no MFA, shows the highest incidence of security breaches. The study highlights the crucial role of MFA in enhancing security, as platforms with MFA report significantly fewer incidents. A strong inverse correlation between password strength and security incidents underscores the need for robust password policies. User behavior, such as password reuse and poor password hygiene, remains a challenge, emphasizing the importance of user education programs. Additionally, the study advocates for standardized security practices across VLEs to ensure consistent protection.
