Implications of this research suggest that educational institutions must adopt comprehensive security measures, including stringent password policies, MFA, and user education, to safeguard sensitive information and improve user satisfaction. The findings provide a framework for developing more secure VLEs and highlight the critical role of cybersecurity in supporting the future of digital education. This study contributes to the broader effort of enhancing VLE security and fostering a safer online learning environment.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning-Based Remaining Useful Life Prediction for Industrial Motors
authors: Debasis Jana, Suprakash Gupta, Sukomal Pal, Sandip Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Precise Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction of industrial machinery and its components is important for ensuring high operational availability, reducing unplanned downtime, and optimizing maintenance costs. Industrial machinery are operates in dynamic and harsh environmental condition leading to a complex, non-linear and non-stationary degradation. It is challenging to model such degradation pattern using the traditional statistical models and classical machine learning techniques. To overcome this issue, a deep learning based hybrid model comprises of a Bi-directional Long Short Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) network and a Transformer architecture, has been developed for predicting the RUL of industrial machinery in this study. The developed model is trained and tested using the multivariate time-series sensor data collected from a heavy duty industrial DC motor operated in a mining machinery. The developed model exhibits superior performance over the standalone model of Bi-LSTM and Transformer, achieving a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 231.77 and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.94. the results demonstrate strong predictive capability of the hybrid model. The study highlights the interpretability through attention mechanisms, providing insights into critical degradation patterns of industrial motor for inform maintenance decisions.

title: Asymmetric Impact of Oil Price Shock on Main Islamic Stock Indices: An MTNARDL Analysis
authors: Somar Al-Mohamad, Amamr Jreisat, Mohamed Sraieb, Audil Rashid Khaki, Nasser El-Kanj, Imad Chehade
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: The aim of this paper is to measure the financial connectedness and extreme tail effect among the oil, bitcoin and gold prices, and returns on the major global Islamic Stock Indices between 2020 and 2025. We employ the Nonlinear Autoregressive Distributed Lag (NARDL) and Multiple Threshold NARDL (MTNARDL) models. The study focuses on capturing the effects of extreme shocks in global oil market along on the main Islamic stock indices by introducing MTNARDL adjusted to reflect extreme shock effects. The outcomes reveal that Islamic indices are generally resilient to extreme shocks in global oil demand and fluctuations in the bitcoin market. However, oil supply shocks demonstrate significant long-run effects on the Dow Jones Islamic World Emerging Index, suggesting that emerging Islamic markets are more vulnerable to supply-side disturbances. Moreover, gold price shocks exhibit strong short- and long-run connectedness with all Islamic indices, indicating possible contagion effects and increased portfolio risk when combined with Islamic stocks. This study contributes to the existing literature on the dynamic interactions and connectedness among cryptocurrency and precious metal prices and Islamic financial instruments and offers noticeable insights into their hedging potential and risk exposures, particularly under extreme market conditions.

title: Human-Centric Digital Leadership and e-HRM Effectiveness in Emerging Economies: A Post-Digital Transformation Perspective
authors: Ansa Savad Salim, Karim Ben Yahia
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: The post-digital transformation era necessitates a reevaluation of leadership paradigms, shifting focus from technological adoption toward human-technology synergy. This study introduces and empirically validates the construct of Human-Centric Digital Leadership (HCDL), integrating ethical awareness, digital empathy, employee vitality, and community orientation within digitalized work environments. Using a quantitative, cross-sectional survey design, the research draws on the Digital Humanism framework and the Information Systems Success Model to examine how HCDL influences Digital Human Resource Management Effectiveness (DHRE). A total of 410 employees from 85 SMEs in Tunisia, Bahrain, and India participated in the study, selected because these firms had undergone significant digital transition and had used a formal e-HRM system for at least two years-ensuring respondents had relevant experience with digital HR practices. Data were analyzed using correlation and regression techniques. Findings show that HCDL significantly predicts DHRE, with ethical awareness and digital empathy emerging as the strongest drivers of system quality, user satisfaction, and perceived net benefits. This study advances humancentric digital leadership research in emerging economy contexts and offers a validated framework for fostering sustainable, human-led digital transformation.

title: Explainable Heart-Disease Risk Prediction Through Boosting-Based Classification and Shap-Guided Clinical Feature Interpretation
authors: S. Vairachilai, Markala Karthik, Sandeep Madarapu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Heart disease is one of the major causes of death in the world and it is important that it is predicted at an early stage to enable medical attention. The proposed study suggests an idealized clinical data-informed model, which is comprised of the preprocessing, SMOTE-assisted class balancing, features characteristics selection, and comparative modelling in improving prediction accuracy. Among the entire range of clinical variables, six strongly discriminating features were identified and employed to train Machine Learning and Ensemble Learning models. The findings indicate that the Ensemble Learning performed better than the other methods as AdaBoost had the greatest overall performance of 0.8833 with precision of 1.0000 and ROC-AUC of 0.9621. To guarantee interpretability, SHAP was integrated to make visual the contribution of each feature on the AdaBoost prediction at the individual and global level that would give clinical transparency as opposed to a black-box result. The results prove that the combination of optimized feature subsets, boosting-based learning and SHAP-enabled explainability can provide high predictive power and medical interpretability. The given framework has a high potential of becoming a valid and reliable decision-support tool in terms of early heart-disease risk assessment.

title: Electricity Price Forecasting Using BiLSTM Model with Carbon Market Correlation
authors: Kheleshwar Sahu, Ashish Prajesh, Prerna Jain
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Carbon emissions are mostly caused by the energy industry, particularly by the production of power. Carbon price has become a key tool to deter excessive emissions among the numerous mitigation strategies. With an emphasis on hourly energy price forecasting and carbon cost pass-through, this work examines the connection between electricity market prices and carbon pricing in the Spanish power system. A deep learning framework built on Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks is employed to capture temporal dependencies in both directions and model long-range relationships in electricity price formation. Correlation research confirms the effectiveness of the model by revealing strong correlations between carbon pricing and actual and predicted electricity costs across Pearson, Spearman, and Kendall Tau metrics. Ordinary Least Squares regression also suggests a carbon cost pass-through of about 2.86 for expected electricity prices and about 2.04 for actual market prices. These findings show how the BiLSTM framework can identify and amplify carbon market signals, providing regulators, policymakers, and market participants with useful information in the context of the ongoing decarbonisation of the power sector.

title: Phishdet: an Online Phishing Detection System Based on Cnn Model
authors: Minxing Kuang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: This work firstly presents a functional, integrated prototype system that bridges the gap between offline deep learning models and online network security applications. Secondly, through a comprehensive empirical study, it validates the practical suitability of a character-level CNN (PhishDet) as a stable and accurate core for online inference, despite known computational constraints. Our system demonstrates a viable pathway towards shifting the protection paradigm from passive analysis to active warning, providing a foundational framework for future low-latency phishing defense systems.

title: Redefining Machine Learning Models Performance: What matters?
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)

title: Research on Interactive Optimization Model of Educational Robot Based on Speech Recognition in Oral English Teaching
authors: Zhuoying Zhou, Junlin Zhai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computing, Robotics and System Sciences (ICRSS)
abstract: This study improves the teaching effect of educational robots by optimizing speech recognition technology, interactive process and providing personalized learning support. In the optimization of speech recognition technology, advanced noise filtering algorithm and highsensitivity microphone array design are studied to reduce the influence of background noise and improve the quality of speech signal acquisition. Aiming at the problem of accent recognition, a multi-accent speech recognition model is constructed, and the user adaptive mechanism is introduced to dynamically adjust the model parameters according to the pronunciation characteristics of students, which significantly improves the recognition accuracy. In the optimization of interaction process, the wake-up mechanism and semantic understanding module of voice interaction are improved, and the natural language generation technology is adopted to make the robot reply more natural and smooth. At the same time, dialogue management module and parallel processing technology are introduced to shorten the response time and improve the naturalness of interaction. In terms of personalized learning support, by analyzing students' pronunciation characteristics, speech speed, intonation and other information, we can understand their oral English level and learning style, offering customized learning materials and feedback accordingly. Teaching content and degree of hardship are dynamically adapted with respect to the learning process and achievements of students, so as to inspire their interest and motivation for study. 100 student in the second grade of a middle school were chosen as experimental and controlled group respectively. Results show the experimental group has a significantly higher score than control group in speech recognition accuracy, interactive response time and overall improvement of oral expression ability. It shows that the optimized educational robot can recognize students' speech input more accurately, respond to instructions faster, and effectively improve students' oral expression ability, which provides an intelligent, efficient and personalized solution for oral English teaching.

title: Optimization Model of AIGC Mainstream Ideology Communication Strategy Based on Intent Deconstruction
authors: Na Zhao, Mutong Sun, Hongfang Xu, Xuanjin Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: In the context of the current Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC) technology reshaping the ideological communication pattern, this research aims to construct a mainstream ideological communication strategy optimization model based on intention deconstruction to cope with the opportunities and risks brought by technology. The research adopts a multi-disciplinary approach, combined with deep learning, natural language processing and technology, and designs an algorithm framework including multi-level intention analysis algorithms and geo-cultural adaptation factors. The experimental results show that the model can increase the response time of the system by 33.15 %, the processing throughput by 44.68 %, and the cultural adaptation accuracy by 21.90 %. Moreover, the model's audience ideological recognition increased by 16.89%-19.64%, and the model maintained 76.5 % performance under heavy data poisoning attacks (18.2% better than traditional methods). The research conclusion confirms that the model effectively solves the adaptability problem of cross-cultural communication. It performs well in the ideological management scenario of universities and can help educational institutions achieve precise and intelligent ideological education and communication, but it has some shortcomings such as limited processing ability of non-text content. Therefore, follow-up research will expand multi-modal fusion and extreme scenario adaptability.

title: Dispatch Algorithm for Power Service Repair Resources Based on Multi-Source Data Fusion
authors: Jianguang Yao, Yanfang Mao, Zhonghao Qian, Xiaoyi Xu, Xiaoxiang Lv
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Technology, Communication Ecosystem and Management (ITCEM)
abstract: This paper proposes a multi-time-interval fault repair strategy considering coordinated restoration between transportation network operation and regional distribution networks. A system implementation recovery index is introduced to quantify the current restoration level of the distribution network, with corresponding adjustments made to the transportation network operation based on this indicator. The transportation network transmits the solved minimum movement time matrix of repair crews to the distribution network for fault recovery operations, scheduling both the repair plan for maintenance teams and the access plan for mobile energy storage devices. Leveraging feeder telemetry data from the distribution network automation system, a multiple regression analysis algorithm is employed for efficient identification of potential fault locations and types. An intelligent analysis system performs comparative assessment of distribution transformer health conditions and historical operational data to precisely locate fault sources. Case studies ultimately validate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: HHM-UNet: Hierarchical Heterogeneous State Space Modeling for Residential Floor Plan Semantic Segmentation
authors: Liangliang Su, Haoran Jiang, Jiamu Sheng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Semantic segmentation of residential floor plans is a fundamental task for building information modeling and indoor space parsing, yet it remains challenging due to complex spatial structures, multi-scale components, and long-range semantic dependencies. To address these issues, we propose Hierarchical Heterogeneous Mamba-UNet (HHM-UNet), a novel semantic segmentation framework based on hierarchical state space modeling. Built upon the U-Net architecture, HHM-UNet employs a dual-branch convolutional encoder and selectively integrates state space modules across different semantic levels. Specifically, boundary-aware state space modeling enhances local structural details at shallow layers, directional scanning modules capture oriented architectural patterns at intermediate layers, and cross-layer state space modeling encodes global semantic relationships at deeper layers. FloorStruct1025 dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves substantial performance improvements, outperforming existing state-of-the-art approaches by 7.6% in mean Intersection over Union (mIoU). In addition, HHM-UNet exhibits strong robustness and high accuracy in segmenting room regions, structural components, and fine-grained objects.

title: Dynamic Spectrum Allocation via Quantum-Driven Learning for Cognitive Radio Systems
authors: G. Amjad Khan, Rajganesh A, P. Thenmozhi, V. Anitha Lakshmi, R. Krishnamoorthy, R. Thiagarajan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: The sharp increase in the number of wireless devices has increased the need of dynamic and smart spectrum management within Cognitive Radio Networks (CRNs). The traditional methods of static allocation are not efficient towards responding to real-time spectrum scarcity and dynamic channel environment. In this paper, a new Quantum-Driven Reinforcement Learning (QRL) paradigm is proposed as an efficient and safe method of allocating dynamically the spectrum in CRNs. The suggested model uses the principles of quantum amplitude encoding and Grover-inspired quantum search to allow the Secondary Users (SUs) to acquire the knowledge of spectrum availability at a higher rate of convergence and reduced collision risk with the Primary Users (PUs). The CRN simulation environment has real world limitations, that are multipath fading, user mobility and adversarial interference. The suggested QRL model was tested in comparison with the classical Q-learning, Deep Q-Networks (DQN), and Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifiers. Findings indicate that the QRL model attains 95.0 % efficiency in spectrum utilization, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 7 \%} \text{PU}$</tex> interference and a faster convergence rate (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 \%}$</tex>) than DQN. In addition, the QRL architecture has less inference latency and scales to multiple SU agents, and it is also computationally efficient. These advancements confirm the possibilities of quantum-enhanced learning to real-time spectrum decision-making of next-generation 6 G and IoTdriven wireless networks. The paper draws a conclusion that quantum-based decision agents will be capable of offering adaptive, energy efficient and interference aware spectrum allocation even in highly dynamic settings. Further development of this project will be to execute quantum circuit optimization and to test the model on quantum processors hardware to determine the viability of real-world implementation in distributed spectrum systems.

title: Real-Time Multilingual Chat Translator
authors: Goutham R, Hamjath S, Sabeer Ahamed N, B. Mathiarasi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Rapidly growing global digital communication increases the demand for real-time language translation systems that are secure and privacy-preserving. Most currently available multilingual chat applications utilize centralized servers to route messages and translate their contents, hence exposing sensitive user data and metadata to security risks and unauthorized access. To meet these needs and challenges, this paper describes a Real-Time Multilingual Chat Translator, which is a decentralized peer-to-peer communication platform that integrates end-to-end encryption with artificial intelligence-based language translation. The model proposes WebRTC for establishing direct device-to-device connections, thereby eliminating the use of centralized messaging servers. To provide confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity of user communications, a hybrid cryptographic framework using RSA-OAEP for secure key exchange and AES-GCM for message encryption has been implemented. Google Gemini AI has been integrated for offering accurate, context-aware, real-time translation support in multiple languages. Regarding privacy, real-time translation is performed only on demand, initiated solely by the user. The application has been implemented using React and TypeScript, allowing for a responsive and intuitive user interface while enabling users to work comfortably with desktop and mobile platforms. Experimental evaluation proves that the proposed system enables low-latency communication while ensuring robust security and reliable accuracy of translation. Thereafter, the Real-Time Multilingual Chat Translator offers effective crossing of language barriers without violating user privacy, thereby making the scalable solution viable for global real-time communication.

title: Neural Machine Translation in English to Tamil Using Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning
authors: Arsath S.M, Tharukshan P., J. Jananie
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Neural Machine Translation (NMT) faces challenges with low-resource language pairs like English-Tamil due to data scarcity and linguistic complexity. This paper investigates enhancing English-to-Tamil translation by efficiently fine-tuning Google's Gemma 3 4B instruction-tuned model. We employed Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), optimized via the Unsloth library, on a 4-bit quantized model. Training was conducted for two epochs on a synthetic English-Tamil dataset (1836 pairs) using the SFTTrainer. Evaluating on a 204-pair test set, our fine-tuned model achieved a BLEU score of 36.12 and a chrF++ score of 65.25. This significantly surpasses the base Gemma 3 4B (BLEU 28.84, chrF++ 59.95) and Llama 3 8B Instruct (BLEU 2.94, chrF++ 21.35) baselines. The results confirm that LoRA-based PEFT, even with synthetic data, provides a resource-efficient and effective method for adapting Large Language Models (LLMs) to improve NMT quality for low-resource languages like Tamil. The fine-tuned model is publicly available.

title: A Multi-Modal AI Framework for Exotic Fruit Classification and Soil Suitability Mapping Using Deep Learning and Agro-Environmental Data
authors: Rini PR, Kavitha R, Dalvin Vinoth Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Rising demand for exotic fruit is challenging accurate identification of crops and evaluation of soil suitability as there are currently no integrated Decision Support Systems (DSS) that incorporate visual and agronomic-environmental information. Methods currently being used for assessing plant potential & determining soil suitability typically address these concerns separately, thereby limiting decision-making accuracy in constructing detailed cultivation plans. This research provides a multi-modal Artificial Intelligence (AI) platform that merges Deep Learning for Images with Machine Learning for soils for the production of exotic fruits. In this framework, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) are employed to classify images representing both fruit and soil. The Random Forest (RF) algorithm evaluates the potential of a site by utilizing several agronomic and chemical properties of the soil such as pH, Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium (N-P-K) values. This multi-pathway design allows for fruit recommendations or soil recommendations based on either method. With respect to Experimental validation using a self-developed dataset, EfficientNetB0 achieved an accuracy of 93% while MobileNetV2 demonstrated comparable performance (91 %) with the added benefit of having less CPU utilization. The proposed system is a practical, easy-to-use solution for providing guidance to an individual with respect to environmentally friendly exotic fruit farming practices.

title: Intelligent Resume Analysis and Skill Matching Using NLP
authors: Dontamsetti Nageswarrao, M Sai Kiran, V Sai Krishna, V Sree Hansitha, Bunga Sekhar, Sultana Saba
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Resume screening that is performed manually is also tedious, expensive, and can be biased and does not usually result in the rightful matching of job descriptions with the candidate. The proposed solution to these dilemmas is described in the paper that offers an intelligent resume analysis and skill-matching system based on the application of Natural Language Processing (NLP) and machine learning methods. The system uses SpaCy and PyResparser to get structured information like skills, education, and experience using unstructured resumes. The features extracted are then converted into a form of vectors and the cosine similarity is used to generate the relevance scores between resumes and job descriptions. On these scores, the candidates are ranked so as to help the recruiters in short listing the most appropriate candidates. They are experimentally tested and prove that the offered system is characterized by the high accuracy of resume parsing, generating rankings which are similar to the expectations of recruiters, causing a significant shortening of the screening process, as opposed to the traditional ones. Moreover, real-time rejection and selection messages are used to increase the transparency of the recruitment process. The suggested solution is a fair, efficient, and scalable method of automated hiring and enhances the efficiency of the contemporary recruitment system appropriately.

title: Automated Brain Tumor Detection and Segmentation using Rob flow annotation & YOLOv11 Model
authors: Nivedita Gudigenavar, Manjunath Managuli, Nikhil Inamdar, Ramesh Koti, Jagadish S Jakati, Sharanappa P H
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: A brain tumor, which can be either benign or malignant, is an abnormal development of brain cells. For better treatment outcomes and increased survival rates, early identification is essential. Traditional analytical techniques such as CT and MRI provide valuable structural and locational information but rely heavily on manual interpretation. Biopsy remains a gold standard but is invasive and time-consuming. In this work, we employ Artificial Intelligence (AI) to enhance brain tumor detection using MRI scans. The dataset was annotated and preprocessed using Rob flow, incorporating data augmentation to improve model generalization. A YOLO-based deep learning framework was trained in Google Colab with GPU support, enabling efficient and instantaneous discovery of tumor regions. The proposed structure integrates Rob flow’s dataset management with YOLO’s high-speed object detection, adapted for tumor segmentation. Performance evaluation was carried out expending metrics such as mean Average Precision (mAP), Correctness, F1-score, Recall, and confusion matrix. Investigational results demonstrate the capability of the approach to support radiologists in early diagnosis, offering a reliable, automated, and non-invasive solution for brain tumor detection.

title: Perceptual Quality Assessment of Compressed Vibrotactile Signals Based on Weber–Fechner Threshold Adjustment
authors: Yixuan Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Vibrotactile signal compression is essential for efficient transmission in the Tactile Internet. However, distortions introduced during compression often degrade perceptual quality, while conventional objective metrics based on signal-domain errors fail to accurately reflect human tactile perception. To address this issue, this paper proposes a biomimetic multi-channel vibrotactile perceptual quality assessment framework based on Weber–Fechner threshold adjustment. Inspired by the frequency selectivity of Fast-Adapting Type I (FA-I) and Fast-Adapting Type II (FA-II) mechanoreceptors, the proposed method decomposes vibrotactile signals into multiple perceptual channels and incorporates Just Noticeable Difference (JND) calibration within each physiological channel. Grounded in standardized psychophysical thresholds, the proposed model establishes a perception-consistent evaluation baseline in the signal domain. Experiments conducted on diverse subsets of the HapticGen dataset demonstrate that the proposed method maintains stable accuracy under low compression ratios, while achieving an average improvement of approximately 0.2–0.3 in Pearson Linear Correlation Coefficient (PLCC) compared to existing mainstream metrics in high-compression scenarios.

title: An Adaptive Genetic Algorithm-Based Energy Storage Optimization Method for Microgrids with Dynamic Electricity Pricing and Reliability Awareness
authors: Ye Xiaohui, Li Ziang, Tan Fucheng
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: Existing microgrid energy storage scheduling strategies often neglect the two-way interaction between storage operation and locational marginal prices, while reliability evaluations relying on static scenarios may underestimate realtime system risks. This paper proposes a game-theoretic model that integrates dynamic LMP feedback and reliability assessment based on Monte Carlo Markov Chain. A novel dynamic state-ofcharge penalty coefficient is introduced to adjust multi-objective weights in the genetic algorithm. Experimental results from a 5node microgrid system demonstrate that, compared to static models, the proposed method achieves a 10.5 % improvement in comprehensive reliability with an income loss of only 6.51 %, thereby realizing better risk-reward balance.

title: Study of Fault Location in Distribution Network with Inverter-Based Distributed Power Supply
authors: Xia Wang, Qingxue Liu, Zhe Zhang, Xinzhi Li, Yun Su, Jianghong Wang
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: The Distribution network is the most frequent fault link in the power system, accurate and fast fault location algorithm plays a crucial role in the safe operation of the distribution network. A large number of IIDG (Inverter Interfaced Distributed Generation) connections not only change the power flow distribution of the distribution network under the normal state, but also change the current characteristics under the fault state the traditional fault location methods cannot be directly applied to the distribution network with IIDG. Therefore, according to the national requirements of Low Voltage Ride Through (LVRT) control strategy for IIDG, this paper proposes a method of rapid fault identification using the difference of the feeder terminal unit (Feeder Terminal Unit, FTU); finally, the method is verified using MATLAB / Simulink software.

title: Optimized Arithmetic Architecture in Cryptographic Processor for IoT Embedded System
authors: Sivanandam K, Sugan N, Vijay P, Yasvonth V L
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The arithmetic architecture of a cryptographic processor with a low-power and high-throughput model with Internet-of-things (IoT) nodes using minimal resources. Resourceconstrained Internet of Things (IoT) devices demand cryptographic solutions that offer strong security with minimal power and hardware overhead. In this work, an optimized arithmetic architecture for a cryptographic processor is presented, targeting low-power IoT embedded systems. The proposed design focuses on improving modular arithmetic operations by replacing conventional adder–multiplexer structures with a LUT-based arithmetic unit optimized for FPGA implementation. This architecture reduces critical path delay, power consumption, and logic utilization while supporting essential cryptographic operations such as modular addition and subtraction. The design is implemented and verified on a Xilinx FPGA using Vivado, where performance is evaluated in terms of power, delay, and power–delay product (PDP). Experimental results demonstrate a significant reduction in power consumption (up to 50%) and improved PDP compared to the existing architecture, validating the suitability of the proposed design for energy-efficient and secure IoT applications.

title: Study on the Armour Loss of Three-Core Submarine Cables
authors: Shihu Yu, Yongbin Liu, Ji Wu, Nan Cao, Xin Yu
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: In recent years, the installed capacity of offshore wind power has increased significantly, leading to a substantial rise in the number of submarine cables used for power transmission. A thorough understanding of the armor losses in submarine cables is a prerequisite for the economical design of power cables. This paper uses the COMSOL simulation platform to build a multi-physical field coupling model of electromagnetic current for three-core submarine cables, calculates the armor losses and current-carrying capacity of submarine cables using the finite element method, and corrects the IEC calculation results based on the simulation results. The results show that for the three-core submarine cables studied in this paper, the IEC standard overestimates the cable losses and underestimates the cable's current-carrying capacity. The corrected current-carrying capacity is about 7% higher than the value calculated by the IEC formula. This study can provide ideas for increasing the currentcarrying capacity of submarine cables and reducing economic costs.

title: MultiMotion: A Scalable Multi-Person Interactive Motion Generation Framework
authors: Shuaibo Li, Ke Lv, Kun Dong, Jian Xue
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Multi-person motion generation is a fundamental problem in computer vision, with broad applications in gaming, animation, and virtual environments. This task entails the joint modeling of multi-person motion synthesis and interactive dynamics—an inherently challenging problem due to the complex spatiotemporal dependencies among participants. Existing approaches are largely confined to single-person or dyadic (two-person) settings and struggle to generate coherent, scalable multi-person motion sequences. In this paper, we rethinks the formulation of multi-person motion generation and proposes MultiMotion, a conditional collaborative motion generation framework. MultiMotion comprises two key components: a generation module and an extension module. The generation module synthesizes realistic and diverse single-person motion sequences. The extension module coordinates and guides the motion generation process during diffusion by conditioning on the motion sequences of other agents, thereby enabling seamless scalability from individual motions to multi-person collaborative and interactive behaviors. furthermore, our framework utilizes a collaborative diffusion mechanism that exchanges noisy motion representations among participants at every diffusion step. By leveraging these shared intermediate signals, the extension module dynamically extends single-person actions into multi-person collaborative interactions throughout the denoising process. Comprehensive evaluations confirm that our approach is effective and capable of simultaneously inferring motions involving either individual or multiple persons within a unified generative framework.

title: Comparative Analysis of Classical and Genetic Algorithm Based Portfolio Optimization Using Historical Stock Data
authors: Vinod Jain
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: A key issue in financial analytics is portfolio optimization, which aims to optimize asset allocation to maximize returns while minimizing risk. Due to their simplicity and theoretical rigor, traditional methods like Markowitz meanvariance optimization have been used extensively. However, nonlinearity, dynamic asset behavior, numerous objectives, and market limits present difficulties for these traditional approaches in real-world financial markets. By imitating natural selection and evolution, evolutionary algorithms, like as Genetic Algorithms (GA), offer a viable substitute for portfolio optimization in complicated situations. Using historical stock price data from Microsoft (MSFT), Apple (AAPL), Amazon (AMZN), and Google (GOOG) from February 2022 to February 2023, this article gives a thorough comparison between GA-based optimization and classical minimum-risk portfolio optimization. The paper describes the dataset's structure, portfolio generation methods, assessment measures, and visualization strategies. The results show that the GA strategy delivers a more diversified allocation (maximum weight of 0.3030) with somewhat higher volatility <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\sigma p=0.3571)$</tex>, whereas the conventional approach produces a lower-risk, highly concentrated portfolio (minimum variance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sigma p=0.3372$</tex> and a Sharpe ratio of - 0.4146). This comparative analysis shows that the GA's flexibility and search capabilities produce a robustly diversified strategy, a crucial benefit in complex and uncertain financial markets, whereas traditional optimization excels at minimum-variance allocation.

title: CSDSM: A Multi-Layered Cybersecurity Framework for Threat Detection and Mitigation in Social Media
authors: Ajit Baral, Bijay Kumar Paikaray, Sumati Baral
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Social media platforms have revolutionized human communication and information sharing, and simultaneously created unprecedented cybersecurity challenges. This focuses on the significant role of cybersecurity in solving the dark side of social media, including malicious activities such as cyberbullying, misinformation campaigns, identity theft, privacy violations and coordinated attacks. Through comprehensive analysis of current threats, existing security frameworks and emerging protective technologies, this study highlights the multifaceted nature of social media security challenges and proposes integrated cybersecurity strategies. The research emphasizes the effective cybersecurity measures are important not only for protecting individual users but also for maintaining societal trust in digital communication platforms and upholding democratic discourse in the digital era.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Antenna Metamaterial Structures for Wireless IoT Applications
authors: Sakthevel. T. C, Sugumar. D, Sherlin Leela Princy. S, Allwin Devaraj. S
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Artificially created structures known as metamaterials have distinct electromagnetic properties not present in natural materials. Split Ring Resonators (SRR), Complementary SRR (CSRR), Omega structures, Mushroom structures, Frequency Selective Surfaces (FSS), High-Impedance Surfaces (HIS), Chiral metamaterials (CMM), and Fractal geometries (FG) are just a few of the metamaterial structures that are thoroughly examined in this paper. Each structure is examined according to its operating principle, equivalent LC circuit, resonance mechanism, and possible uses in electromagnetic manipulation, filtering, and antenna miniaturization. Researchers working on metamaterial-based antennas and RF components can refer to this review.

title: E-GPT: A Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Intelligent Educational Assistance
authors: Tabassum Ara, Shreyansu Panda, Jason Samuel Das, Amit Ranjan Das, Sidharth Vivek Prabhugoankar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Recent developments in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) have significantly influenced the education sector, mainly through Large Language Models (LLMs) and Multi-Agent Systems (MAS). Educational chatbots such as GPTutor use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and transformer-based models to offer more intelligent tutoring and automated assessment support. Still, most existing systems depend on centralized APIs and monolithic designs, which restrict scalability, adaptability to different contexts, and data privacy.In this paper, we introduce E-GPT, a locally deployable multi-agent educational chatbot framework designed to divide learning tasks among independent, specialized agents. These include a PDF-to-Quiz Generator, PDF-to-Technical Article Writer, RAG-based Chat Assistant, Question Paper Generator, and an OCR-enabled PDF Analyzer. The system integrates fine-tuned versions of LLaMA3 and Mistral models with a MongoDB-FAISS vector database, allowing efficient context retrieval and smooth collaboration between agents.Experimental results show that E-GPT improves contextual accuracy, modular scalability, and response time while maintaining user data privacy. By distributing different cognitive functions across coordinated agents, the system takes a step toward building a more adaptive, transparent, and scalable AI-driven learning environment.

title: An ML Model to detect Cyber-attacks for Wireless Sensor Networks in Microgrids
authors: Harshitha H B, Pavithra A C, Manjunath K, Juslin F
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: Microgrids' operating efficiency has been completely transformed by the development of smart energy systems and the incorporation of Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs). The stability, security, and dependability of microgrid infrastructures are seriously threatened by these technical advancements, which also increase susceptibility to cyberattacks. WSNs are especially vulnerable to a range of cyberattacks, such as Denial of Service (DoS), data integrity breaches, reconnaissance assaults, and more sophisticated persistent threats, because of their resource-constrained design, wireless communication protocols, and widespread deployment A thorough machine learning-based approach for identifying cyberattacks in WSNs installed in microgrid systems is presented in this study. The suggested system is intended to use intelligent classification algorithms to effectively categorize hostile actions, learn from past patterns, and proactively monitor network traffic and sensor data. To find the best method for real-time intrusion detection under microgrid-specific limitations, a number of machine learning approaches were assessed, including decision trees, random forests, support vector machines, and ensemble learning. Data collection and preprocessing were the first steps of a multi-phase methodology that included feature selection, model training, and a thorough performance evaluation. Both publicly accessible benchmark datasets designed for network intrusion detection and simulated cyberattack scenarios were used to train and evaluate the model. To guarantee a fair trade- off between detection efficacy and computing efficiency, key performance parameters including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and detection latency were examined The study's findings show that the suggested model may effectively generalize to novel and changing assault patterns while achieving high detection accuracy and a low false positive rate. Additionally, the system is flexible and scalable, which allows it to be integrated into actual microgrid designs without sacrificing their functionality. To sum up, this work offers a fresh and useful approach to the increasing cybersecurity issues that contemporary energy systems face. The framework improves microgrid resilience and lays the groundwork for further study of safe, sustainable, and self-sufficient energy systems by utilizing machine learning for intelligent threat identification.

title: A Multidimensional Data Warehouse Approach to Exploring the Poverty's Role in Child Abuse and Teenage Pregnancy in Sri Lanka
authors: Nishadi Jayasooriya, Kavinda Gihan Bandaranayake, Diyorina Fernando, Tharushika Silva, Dinujaya Liyanaarachchi, PPG Dinesh Asanka
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
abstract: Child abuse and teenage pregnancy remain a critical social challenge in developing countries like Sri Lanka where poverty constitutes a main social determinant influencing child welfare. Most of such vulnerabilities continue to be influenced by complex interactions between economic, social, and demographic factors. As a major limitation in existing research, absence of an integrated analytical framework to study the interactions between such factors across populations could be identified. This study addresses that gap by applying a multidimensional data warehouse and OLAP-based analytical methodology to explore the relationship of poverty with child abuse and teenage pregnancy in the country. A heterogeneous dataset covering 31000 records from 16 districts of the year 2024-2025 was integrated through a structured ETL process and modeled using a galaxy schema data warehouse. Results indicate only a weak positive correlation between poverty and child abuse and poverty and teenage pregnancy challenging the poverty centric interventions on such problems in the country. The findings suggest that economic deprivation alone does not sufficiently explain these outcomes and demonstrate the need for further studying into other determinants on such social issues. The significance of this study lies in both its empirical and methodological contributions. Empirically it challenges the emphasis on poverty as a singular determinant of child and teenage vulnerability and suggests the need for holistic interventions. Methodologically it emphasizes the application of multi- dimensional data warehousing approach as an evidence based decision support tool for social surveys.

title: Research on Decision-Level Fusion Algorithm for Unmanned Surface Vehicle Object Detection
authors: Jianjun Guo, Jinchao Xiao, Zhijia Zhang, Jihai Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the increasing use of unmanned surface vehicles (USVs) in waterborne inspection, channel monitoring, and port security, higher demands are placed on the accuracy and robustness of environmental perception. However, in complex water surface environments, single sensors suffer from inherent limitations and cannot provide reliable perception. In this work, YOLOv5s is adopted as the image-based baseline detector, and four lightweight backbone networks are evaluated. The results show that EfficientViT with a 20% channel reduction achieves a good balance between inference speed and model size. For point cloud object detection, SECOND, PointPillars, and CenterPoint are trained and compared. CenterPoint achieves the best performance, reaching a BEV AP of 87.7% and a 3D AP of 78.6% at an IoU threshold of 0.5, and a lightweight version of CenterPoint is further designed to reduce computational cost. Finally, a decision-level fusion algorithm is proposed to integrate image and point cloud detections. For each 3D detection, the point cloud inside the bounding box is projected onto the image plane, and a 2D bounding box is generated from the projected points. The IoU between these boxes and image detections is used to construct a cost matrix together with confidence scores, which is solved by the Hungarian algorithm. The final fused results are obtained through confidence-weighted fusion.

title: Semantic fusion of dual perspectives on genomic sequences and quorum sensing for bacteriophage lifestyle prediction
authors: Zhen Xiao, Ankang Wei, Yi Jia, Weizhong Zhao, Xingpeng Jiang
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: As the most ubiquitous and abundant viral community, bacteriophages (phages for short) play a vital role in regulating the ecological balance by infecting bacteria and archaea. Phages can be classified into two types based on their lifestyles: virulent phages and temperate phages, which are closely related to their functional characteristics and influence their interaction patterns with hosts. Therefore, identifying phage lifestyle is critical for understanding the mechanisms by which phages infect bacteria and represents a key step in mastering their functions and potential applications. In this paper, we propose a novel method for phage lifestyle identification by considering two perspectives. One perspective is based on the genomic sequences of phages, in which both local and global semantic features are integrated. The other perspective focuses on the host quorum sensing phenomena that influence phage lysogen-lysis decisions. Specifically, we first capture local sequence variation patterns by extracting the relative positional information of nucleotide fragments at different intervals, which enables robust representation of local genomic semantics. Secondly, the pretrained nucleotide language model DNABERT is applied to capture the semantics of genome sequences by considering the global contextual information. Finally, combined with quorum sensing signals from the bacterial host, a final fusion representation is obtained, which is fed into a predictive model to identify the phage's lifestyle. Experimental results show that our method has excellent and stable performance in both phage complete genome and short contigs from metagenomic data. We also investigate early-life viral colonization in the human gut metagenome, further validating the model's generalizability and real-world applicability.

title: Federated Learning Framework for Privacy-Preserving Explainable AI-Driven Clinical Decision-Making
authors: Emad-ul-Haq Qazi, Waleed Khalid AL-Ghanem, Muhammad Hamza Faheem, Hanif Ullah
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: The application of artificial intelligence (AI) in clinical diagnostics has shown substantial potential; however, conventional centralized learning frameworks often encounter critical limitations related to patient data privacy, data heterogeneity, and limited generalizability. To address these challenges, we propose a novel Federated Deep Learning (FDL) framework tailored for privacy-preserving, AI-driven clinical decision support. The proposed architecture integrates Vision Transformers (ViT) with DINOv2-based self-supervised learning to enable effective representation learning in the absence of extensive labeled datasets. Furthermore, personalized model updates are facilitated using Federated Self-Supervised Learning (FedSSL) in conjunction with FedProx, ensuring client-specific adaptation in non-identically distributed (non-IID) data environments. Privacy preservation is ensured through the application of differential privacy mechanisms at the model update level, coupled with Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) for secure communication. To enhance clinical transparency and interpretability, the framework incorporates Grad-CAM and LIME for sample-level explainability. The proposed system is evaluated on three publicly available medical imaging datasets encompassing Tuberculosis (TB) detection from chest X-rays, Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) from fundus images, and Brain Tumor (BT) classification from MRI scans. The federated model achieved an accuracy and F1-score of 99.80% for Tuberculosis, 89.0% for Diabetic Retinopathy (DR), and 97.1% for Brain Tumors (BTs), reflecting high overall diagnostic performance across all tasks. These findings validate the efficacy, scalability, and privacy-resilience of the proposed method, positioning it as a robust candidate for real-world clinical deployment in distributed healthcare environments.

title: Characterization of FDSOI Technology for Energy-Efficient Cryo-CMOS Circuits
authors: Arpit Rao, David R. Daughton, Benjamin Chacko, Jason Chonko, Yukang Kuang, Sarah Guenther, Subhanshu Gupta
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: High-performance cryo-CMOS circuits are the foundation for quantum computing hardware. The energy efficiency of cryo-CMOS circuits is limited by the changing operating point of the device as the temperature drops. Operating point calibration becomes imperative to achieve energy-efficient designs, yet accurate threshold-voltage calibration at deep-cryogenic temperatures proves challenging. At cryogenic temperatures, conventional threshold-voltage extraction methods fail as the device operating point exhibits a nonlinear shift with temperature, rendering standard threshold voltage extraction methods unreliable, particularly in the low-VGS regime where cryo-CMOS circuits are typically biased for energy efficiency. This work characterizes flip-well 22 nm FDSOI technology and demonstrates a robust methodology for operating point calibration using back-gate control across a variety of VDS. Established extraction methods, including constant current, maximum transconductance, second-derivative, third-derivative, linear extraction, and transconductance-to-current ratio, are performed to assess their applicability under cryogenic conditions. In doing so, we demonstrate that back-gate tuning provides an effective and scalable mechanism for compensating operating-point shifts without emphasis on the absolute value of the threshold voltage. Calibrated threshold voltage results across temperature are presented for a 22 nm FDSOI super-low-VT (SLVT) transistor using body bias sweeps from 0 to +2 V. To enable circuit-level calibration in the low-VGS regime, this work identifies the body-coefficient η as a relevant metric for biasing, capturing the effective sensitivity of gate threshold voltage to back-gate bias. The extracted η exhibits nonlinearity with respect to both temperature and bias conditions, providing a physically meaningful and design-relevant parameter for energy-efficient cryo-CMOS calibration.

title: Novel Switching Frequency Injection Sensorless Control for SPMSM in Finite-Control-Set Deadbeat Predictive Current Control
authors: Yonglin Cui, Siqi Wang, Shanmei Cheng
abstract: When switching frequency injection (SFI) is implemented in finite-control-set (FCS) model predictive control (MPC) frame, position error signal will deteriorate due to inaccurate voltage injection. This paper establishes novel switching frequency injection sensorless control with proposed FCS deadbeat predictive current control (DPCC) based on novel angular domain iterative optimization method with extended control set (ECS). The inherent limitations of the FCS method inevitably introduce injection errors, which degrade current control performance. Compensating for these errors requires significantly extended execution time to achieve acceptable results. To address these issues, this paper further proposes a novel injection-time based SFI method, realizing precise injection with greatly reduced execution time. With these contributions, the proposed SFI sensorless control strategy based on d-axis current offset is successfully implemented on a target surface-mounted PMSM (SPMSM). Moreover, a simple but effective initial position detection method is proposed. And speed oscillation induced by current offset is studied. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed methods and validate the theoretical analysis.

title: A Three-layer AI-driven Image Filtering for Efficient LEO Satellite Remote Sensing
authors: Nourhen Sboui, Hakim Ghazzai, Sameh Najeh, Lokman Sboui
abstract: Low-earth orbit (LEO) satellites are considered essential tools for remote sensing (RS) and Earth observation (EO) applications thanks to their high spatial resolution images.However, limited onboard resources (storage, downlink throughput, and bandwidth) make it challenging to manage the large volume of data collected. Real-time data management is needed to avoid wasting on-board compute and downlink budget, especially when dealing with a significant influx of anomalous data images resulting from factors such as redundancy, seasonal changes, sensor properties, and satellite orbit dynamics. This paper introduces a novel three-layer filtering solution designed to optimize satellite image transmission for EO. Incorporating a fusion of machine learning and deep learning methodologies, our data management solution mitigates critical challenges associated with LEO satellite resource constraints. The proposed solution includes a no-reference deep image quality assessment (NoR-DIQA) model using a convolutional neural network architecture to identify and filter out distorted images. Then, it employs a perceptual hashing redundancy detection approach to eliminate duplicated images, and finally, it employs a classification model that categorizes suitable RS and EO applications for each image set before transmission. This framework effectively maximizes data utility and optimizes resource allocation for RS and EO satellites. The proposed framework was tested on different RS and EO datasets, where each layer was benchmarked against established models and consistently achieved superior results (e.g., higher correlation and lower error in image quality assessment, improved redundancy detection, and classification accuracy up to 99.4%), demonstrating the robustness and reliability of the three-layer filtering solution.

title: Purpose-Driven Data Sanitization for Secure AI/ML Applications in Next Generation Mobile Networks
authors: Chaitanya Aggarwal, Divya G. Nair, Huanzhuo Wu, Christian Mannweiler, Emrah Tomur, Jörg Ott
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AI/ML) into 5G and forthcoming 6G networks critically relies upon User Equipment (UE) collected data. However, this dependency creates a vulnerability to data poisoning, as current defenses on UEs lack contextual understanding of the collected data’s intended use by data consumers. This paper proposes SANITGUARD-UE, a novel data consumer purpose-driven solution designed to prevent the ingress of poisoned data. It seamlessly integrates with the 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) architecture by enabling UE sanitization capability signaling, Data Collection Configuration Function (DCCF)-configured purpose-driven data sanitization and an enhanced L2 header. Leveraging the use case of cell load estimation, empirical experiments demonstrated that SANITGUARD-UE significantly improves AI/ML model accuracy, outperforming purpose-agnostic sanitization by a factor of 3.73 to 7.66. This research establishes the essential role for purpose-driven data sanitization to build the trustworthiness and to optimize the performance of AI/ML services in next-generation mobile networks.

title: Data-Driven Motion Planning for Uncertain Nonlinear Systems
authors: Babak Esmaeili, Hamidreza Modares, Stefano Di Cairano
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: This article presents a data-driven motion-planning framework for nonlinear systems based on constructing a sequence of overlapping invariant polytopes. The planner randomly generates waypoints, and around each sampled waypoint, it learns a locally invariant polytopic safe set together with a corresponding local state-feedback gain. Each invariant polytope is obtained as the convex hull of a collection of invariant ellipsoids induced by a piecewise-affine controller. Safe and smooth transitions between waypoints are ensured by introducing an intermediate waypoint. Control gains are interpolated in real time via simplex-based interpolation, keeping the state inside the invariant polytopes throughout the motion. Unlike traditional approaches that rely on system dynamics models, our method requires only data to compute safe regions and design state-feedback controllers. The approach is validated through simulations by comparing against a containment-based invariant-ellipsoid baseline and reporting planning time, sampling effort, and trajectory quality, demonstrating safe and dynamically feasible navigation in complex environments.

title: AI-Driven Feature-Enhanced Stacking Ensemble with Global-Context Vision Transformers for Breast Cancer Classification in Ultrasound Images
authors: Nghia Trong Vo, Hoang Phi Yen Duong, Tuan Thanh Nguyen, Nhan Duc Le, Trung Q. Duong
abstract: Breast cancer remains a leading cause of death among women worldwide. Early detection of breast cancer is a crucial step towards improving survival rates for patients affected by the disease and is typically performed with the help of ultrasound imaging. Current rapid advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) research have produced a plethora of machine learning methods that aid in building automated diagnostic assistance systems for early cancer detection, including breast cancer detection. While deep learning has shown promise in medical image analysis, most existing approaches rely on single models or simple ensemble methods that fail to fully exploit complementary feature representations across architectures. This paper introduces a novel feature-enhanced stacking ensemble framework that combines state-of-the-art global context vision transformer (GCViT) with well-established convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures (ResNet-50V2, ConvNeXt-Tiny, and EfficientNetV2-B3) for automated breast cancer classification from ultrasound images. Unlike conventional ensembles that aggregate only prediction probabilities, our approach extracts deep feature embeddings from a dedicated CNN branch and concatenates them with base model predictions as input to a meta-learner, a multi-layer perceptron (MLP), enabling the ensemble to leverage both decision-level and feature-level information. When incorporating a meta model with feature representations from a CNN-based feature extractor, we are able to produce superior performance across multiple metrics compared to prior works. We accomplish top performance of 94.23% accuracy, 95.47% AUC-ROC. To further evaluate the robustness and generalizability of our approach, we conduct additional experiments on the melanoma cancer image dataset and achieve 95.4% accuracy. We provide comprehensive explainability analysis through shapley additive explanations (SHAP) values for feature attribution, permutation importance for model contribution quantification, and saliency maps for visual interpretation from base models and the end-to-end ensemble model to explain their contributions to final predictions.

title: Multifocal metalens for photodetector arrays based on material library fitting
authors: Junjing Huang, Kai Wang
abstract: In this paper we proposed a metalens design scheme based on material library fitting, which utilizes mathematical models, functional relationships, and a small amount of database resources to quickly predict the properties of the materials without the need to scan each sample one by one for simulation. The calculation time is about 3/13 of the traditional method. This greatly reduces the simulation time and improves the efficiency. In addition, we used this method to design a high performance quadfocal metalens with a focusing efficiency of 64% at 1310nm, an average transmittance of 70% and a maximum transmittance of 82% at 1000nm-2000nm. This is a significant advantage over the work of other researchers. The proposed work will significantly improve the design efficiency of metalens, thereby facilitating the advancement of the technology.

title: Deploy-Safe Day-Ahead Multi-Step PV Power Forecasting Using Meteo-Only Inputs
authors: G. Tulum, V. Esen, A.S. Sarkın, T. Dindar, E. Alp
abstract: This study investigates day-ahead multi-step photovoltaic (PV) power forecasting with 15-minute resolution in a realistic deployment-safe framework based solely on meteorological inputs. Using real-world field data from December 2020–January 2025 for a PV plant with a capacity of approximately 110 kWe–129 kWp located in Konya, Turkey, a 96-step generation profile is predicted sequentially for each day. Unlike previous studies, delayed plant measurements are completely excluded, and only meteorological variables and deterministic time features are used. The proposed end-to-end workflow includes day-level quality control, input-output alignment analysis, deploy-safe feature engineering, and two-stage feature selection. Deep learning (BiLSTM, LSTM, Transformer), classical machine learning (Boosting, ElasticNet, ExtraTrees, MLP), and NBEATSx-based models are compared under a common evaluation protocol. Experimental results show that the many-to-many BiLSTM architecture provides the best performance (RMSE = 9.487 kW, R² = 0.92) and that boosting-based classical methods offer a robust alternative with similar accuracy. The residual correction module added to NBEATSx improved performance by up to 4%, particularly in absolute error metrics. Log-error analyses reveal heavy-tailed behavior driven by operational noise across all models. The findings confirm the superiority of deep trailing models in safe deployment scenarios operating with only meteorological inputs, while also highlighting the practical applicability of classical methods.

title: Hyperloop Communications: Intra-tube Channel Capacity Enhancement via Multi-layered Pod Structures
authors: J. Kim, K. J. Han, H. Kim
abstract: Hyperloop, a futuristic transportation system, requires robust intra-tube wireless communications for safety and passenger connectivity. Specifically, wireless communications enable seamless real-time monitoring and control of pods, while also aiming at providing passengers with high-capacity and reliable Internet access. This paper evaluates per-pod downlink channel capacity using an electromagnetic (EM) propagation analysis methodology. Conventional metallic pods, widely proposed in Hyperloop designs, fail to meet onboard Internet service demands due to intra-tube interference. Therefore, we introduce two novel pod structures: a double-layered design with an EM absorbing outer layer covering a metallic inner shell, and a triple-layered design with dual EM absorbing outer layers. Evaluations demonstrated these configurations successfully suppress interference, achieving sufficient capacity to support 4K ultra high definition (UHD) video streaming for every in-cabin passenger. Furthermore, we optimized channel capacity by adjusting EM absorbing layer thickness and selecting the optimal EM propagation mode. These findings highlight the importance of the pod structural design in meeting the high-capacity requirements for Hyperloop wireless communications.

title: Quantifying Computational Reliability of Task Assignment in Extreme Edge Computing
authors: MHD Saria Allahham, Hossam S. Hassanein
abstract: This paper presents a reliability analysis framework for distributed computing in extreme edge computing (XEC) with limited information availability. XEC pushes computation to the outermost boundaries of networks by leveraging consumer-owned devices, known as Extreme Edge Devices (XEDs). Unlike traditional distributed systems with defined computational resource states, XEC operates under uncertainty due to consumer device usage patterns, varying computational capacities, and local scheduling algorithms. In this work, we address discrete task assignment particularly. The framework analyzes scenarios for computational reliability assessments with minimal knowledge of XED capabilities and service requirements. The framework adapts to different levels of available information, from operational limits to historical performance data, providing refined reliability estimates. The aim of this work is to provide generalized reliability models for distributed computing in XEC that allow decision-makers (e.g. service orchestrators) to make informed decisions about task allocation, service placement, and resource allocation under uncertainty in XEC. Simulations and experimental analysis demonstrate the framework's effectiveness in estimating reliability under various system conditions.

title: PointRL: Reinforcement Learning-Based Approach for Air-Ground Communications Using Multi-Dimensional Target Sensing Point Cloud
authors: Leyan Chen, Kai Liu, Peng Yang, Zehui Xiong, Tony Q. S. Quek, Jisi Fang, Zhibo Zhang
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly used in smart city communications for air-ground communications due to their flexibility, low cost, and independence from ground conditions, enabling high data rates for future networks. This paper explores UAV-to-vehicle (U2V) mmWave integrated sensing and communication (ISAC), where vehicles are represented as rigid shapes in a 3D radar point cloud. Considering maximizing channel capacity with multi-user interference and radar performance, two adaptive optimization problems are proposed, incorporating vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) communication for interference mitigation. The radar point cloud-driven reinforcement learning (PointRL) algorithm is designed to solve these problems. It includes a point cloud-based deep neural network (PDNN) for extracting action spaces from 3D radar data and a decision network that reduces network complexity through segmentation and connection. A linear weighted sliding window reward mechanism is also designed to enhance decision-making in dynamic environments. Simulation results show that the proposed PointRL outperforms benchmark methods.

title: High-Resolution Spatial Downscaling of Multi-Frequency Brightness Temperature in Global Mare Basalts on the Moon
authors: Shuangshuang Wu, Jianping Chen
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Research on lunar maria offers key constraints on the Moon’s endogenic evolution, yet thermal characterization of mare basalts remains limited by the low spatial resolution of Chang’e-2 microwave radiometer (MRM) data. To address this issue, we develop a SHAP-assisted downscaling framework for lunar difference brightness temperature (dTB). Using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), we quantify the contributions of candidate kernel-driven factors and identify FeO, TiO₂, and the digital elevation model (DEM) as the most influential kernels. Two regression models (Ordinary Least Squares, OLS; Geographically Weighted Regression, GWR) combined with two kernel sets (full and SHAP-selected) are compared to evaluate the effects of regression strategy and feature configuration. The SHAP-screened GWR model (GWR-3) delivers the best performance, attaining R² values of 0.954, 0.956, 0.951, and 0.950 at 3.0, 7.8, 19.35, and 37.0 GHz, respectively. The resulting ~2 km dTB products represent the highest-resolution multi-frequency dTB datasets to date. This SHAP-assisted framework offers an effective tool for enhancing lunar thermal analyses and supports refined investigations into the thermal heterogeneity and geological evolution of mare basalts.

title: Period-Two Dynamics of an Optically Injected Semiconductor Laser for Range-Sensitive Resonance Detection
authors: Wen-Hao Jiang, Sze-Chun Chan
abstract: Period-doubling nonlinear dynamics of a laser is experimentally found to be highly sensitive for ranging the distance of an optoelectronic feedback loop. The semiconductor laser is first optically injected for period-one (P1) dynamics at a fundamental frequency <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The optoelectronic feedback then induces period-doubling bifurcation in yielding period-two (P2) dynamics at the subharmonic frequency <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0}/2$</tex-math></inline-formula>. By adopting current modulation, the periodic dynamics adiabatically exhibits frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) behavior in general, but a resonance is unveiled when the modulation frequency <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{\mathrm{{m}}}$</tex-math></inline-formula> matches the reciprocal of the feedback delay <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Such a resonance is manifested through broadening the FMCW comb around the fundamental and concurrently suppressing the P2 dynamics at the subharmonic. It can be detected by simply monitoring the subharmonic power without frequency mixing. It allows for measuring <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau$</tex-math></inline-formula> to determine the effective absolute distance of the feedback loop with a microwave path included. It is also highly sensitive to fine changes in distance without being limited by the FMCW microwave coherence length. For the laser operating at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0} = 15$</tex-math></inline-formula> GHz with feedback of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1/\tau = 17$</tex-math></inline-formula> MHz, the resonance is sensitive to fractional changes of the order of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-4}$</tex-math></inline-formula> in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Ranging at 8.8 m is demonstrated with the capability to resolve down to 1 mm, where the required bandwidth is no more than 3 GHz. The novel form of resonance as detected through the P2 suppression enables the applications of range-sensitive nonlinear dynamics.

title: Closing the UV-Induced Photodegradation Gap Through Global Scale Modeling of Fixed Tilt and Tracking Photovoltaic Systems
authors: S. Poddar, S. Liu, P. Hamer, M. Kay, B. Hoex
abstract: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation accelerates the degradation of photovoltaic (PV) modules, both at the cell and module levels, leading to reduced efficiency and a shorter lifespan. While reliability testing has advanced, current standards often fail to reflect real-world UV exposure, especially across diverse climates. Global UV irradiance varies widely, from <30 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in high-latitude regions to >80 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in arid zones, while standard tests such as IEC-61215 only test up to a dose of 15 kWh /m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> that can be reached in less than 50 days in some locations. This study develops a high-precision model to estimate UV radiation on tilted surfaces and assesses the influence on UV photodegradation. The model shows minimum bias (<±3.87 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, i.e., <±4.28%) when compared with observations. Results show notable global variation in UV irradiance on tilted surfaces between single-axis tracking (SAT) and fixed-tilt systems, hence affecting degradation rates. SAT systems experience approximately twice the degradation in arid and semi-arid climates compared to fixed-tilt systems, where UV exposure is most intense. These findings highlight the critical need for climate-specific degradation assessments even for identical module technologies. To ensure outdoor durability and long-term performance, advanced regionally adaptive reliability testing thresholds should be adapted for better-informed material selection and manufacturing.

title: A 71–86-GHz 1024-QAM Direct-Carrier Phase-Modulating Transmitter With Digital-to-Phase Converter and Constant-Envelope Phasor Combiner
authors: Jia Zhou, Jieqiong Du, Chao-Jen Tien, Christopher Chen, Jhih-Wei Chen, Lachlan Cuskelly, Francisco Cardenas Beltran, Adrian Tang, Sai-Wang Tam, Mau-Chung Frank Chang
abstract: This article presents a 71–86-GHz 1024-QAM direct-carrier phase-modulating transmitter (DCPM-TX) that synthesizes high-order QAM symbols using phase modulation only. The proposed approach employs dual 9-bit digital-to-phase converters (DTPCs) and a constant-envelope phasor combiner. Each DTPC uses coarse/fine-segmented digitally controlled switched-capacitor transmission lines (SCTLs) (true-time-delay operation) plus a sign mixer, providing a 360° phase range and sub-1° rms phase error while preserving wide carrier and modulation bandwidth. To avoid linear power amplifier (PA) back-off, the modulated phasors are driven into saturation and combined to realize high output power over 71–86 GHz. Using direct-digital-synthesis control with on-chip SRAM, the transmitter also generates a 77–81-GHz FMCW chirp in 5 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s with 84 kHz (0.0021%) rms frequency modulation (FM) error. The prototype DCPM-TX achieves 32 and 25.6 Gbps using 1024-QAM at 73 and 83 GHz, respectively, with peak P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">out</sub> over 16 dBm while consuming 340-mW DC power and 0.675-mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> core silicon area on 28-nm bulk CMOS.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A

title: Enhanced Dynamic Representation via Gradient-Motion Modeling for Micro-Expression Recognition
authors: Tong Zhang, Shiting Xu, C. L. Philip Chen
abstract: Micro-expressions (MEs) are brief, involuntary facial expressions revealing inner emotions. The unique occurrence mechanism of ME brings challenges for micro-expression recognition (MER). However, existing methods often suffer from task-irrelevant and overly localized representations, which lead to the loss of crucial discriminative information and thus limit recognition performance. In this paper, an enhanced dynamic representation via gradient-motion modeling (EDR) is proposed to obtain a complete and discriminative representation of the dynamic variations in ME. We provide theoretical support for EDR based on the complementarity of gradient and motion information in the computational rules of traditional optical flow. Specifically, a self-attentive gradient module (SAGM) is designed to enhance the sensitivity and localization of subtle motions. Inspired by the analytical solution of optical flow, we design a cross-attentive motion module (CAMM). Besides, dual-stage training is proposed to enhance fine-grained perception and generalization by first learning local-global correlations through a mask modeling strategy, followed by optimization under a standard recognition objective.
