Extensive experiments show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods. Ablation studies also demonstrate the effectiveness of each component.

title: Follow the Signs or the Crowd? Effects of Environmental Load and Crowd Dynamics in VR Evacuation
authors: Zheng Wei, Jingchen Gao, Zhe Yan, Yihan Liu, Linjie Qiu, Xian Xu, Yun Huang, Huamin Qu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Emergency evacuation in VR must balance realism with clear guidance. However, most prior studies strengthen either sensory or social factors in isolation, leaving equal-geometry causal estimates of load versus crowd still lacking. We present SAFE-VR, a controlled testbed that orthogonally varies Environmental Load (low vs. high) and Crowd Dynamics (orderly vs. chaotic) while keeping layout, signage, and spawn constant. In a preregistered 2×2 between-subjects experiment (N = 80), we analyzed time-to-exit, frame-coded behavior, presence, and workload to distangle sensory from social effects. Both factors impaired egress, with the High×Chaotic condition performing worst overall. For time-to-exit, effects were additive (no reliable Load×Crowd interaction); in contrast, Temporal demand showed a crossed interaction. High load increased effort, frustration, and object contacts; while chaotic f low increased route deviations, human contacts, and slowed exits. These patterns align with reliability-weighted cueing: as guidance becomes harder to perceive, participants may shift toward crowd-following, especially when flow is unstable. SAFE-VR thus delineates how load and crowd structure jointly shape route fidelity, collisions, and evacuation time, and highlights conditions where subjective time pressure diverges from objective delay.

title: A Vibration Suppression Method Based on Anisotropic Wedge for FSCW-PMSMs
authors: Siyao Huang, Wei Wang, Zheng Xu, Wenxiang Zhao, Ming Cheng
abstract: Fractional-slot concentrated-winding (FSCW) permanent magnet (PM) synchronous machines (PMSMs) exhibit numerous low-spatial-order radial force waves (RFWs), which are a dominant source of vibration and noise. This article investigates the influence of slotting structures on flux density harmonics, RFWs, and vibration and proposes a new vibration suppression method tailored for FSCW-PMSMs. First, analytical and numerical studies reveal that the closed-slot structure can effectively mitigate the non-integer-pole-pair vibrations induced by slotting effects, but it increases flux leakage, leading to torque loss. To address the torque deficiency of the closed-slot structure design, an anisotropic wedge-based vibration suppression method was proposed to achieve simultaneous torque retention and vibration attenuation. Next, the effectiveness of the proposed method for the FSCW-PMSMs with different pole–slot combinations is validated via finite element analysis (FEA) on two groups of FSCW-PMSMs, where each group comprises three machines—employing open slot, anisotropic wedge, and closed slot, respectively—while maintaining identical geometries. Finally, experimental verification using three prototyped machines confirms that the proposed method can significantly reduce the vibration acceleration without compromising electromagnetic torque for FSCW-PMSMs.

title: TriCloud: Drug-Target-Disease Ternary Network for Drug Repositioning Research Based on Point Cloud Modeling
authors: Xiwei Tang, Wanjun Ma, Anzheng Gao, Mengyun Yang, Weijun Liang, Wenjun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: In recent years, the “drug-target-disease” association has become increasingly complex and data-scarce. Existing methods are limited by information loss caused by explicit graph construction when modeling triplet relationships, making it difficult to effectively capture geometric structures and long-range dependencies, thereby affecting prediction performance. To address this, this paper proposes a new method based on point cloud modeling—TriCloud. This method represents each triad as a spatial point cloud, encoding semantic and topological relationships through geometric coordinates, and performs feature learning directly on an unordered point set, avoiding reliance on predefined graph structures. Based on the PointNet architecture, it introduces a multi-view feature extraction and fusion mechanism to enhance the modeling capability of global structures and complex spatial patterns. Experimental results show that TriCloud significantly outperforms existing methods on multiple benchmark datasets, achieving an AUC of 0.9995 and an AUPR of 0.9996, with all metrics ranking first. External validation demonstrates its excellent generalization ability. Feature analysis reveals that the geometric-semantic joint features of positive samples play a dominant role in classification. This study provides an efficient and reliable computational framework for drug repurposing, contributing to the development of precision medicine.

title: IEEE Trial-Use Guide for Testing Methods for Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell Systems in Automotive Applications Using Compressed Hydrogen as Fuel
authors: N/A
abstract: The methodology for the test of proton exchange membrane fuel cell systems for automotive applications using compressed hydrogen as fuel is provided as a guidance in this document, including the recommendations on the accuracy of the test instruments, preparation before the test, test conditions, and calculation processes.

title: The Standards Landscape in the IEEE Computer Society
authors: Darren Galpin
abstract: Here, we highlight the work done by IEEE Computer Society’s various standards committees by showcasing the standards and project authorization requests approved in the December 2025 New Standards Committee (NesCom), Standards Review Committee (RevCom), and IEEE Standards Association Standards Board (SASB) meeting series.

title: Voltage-dependent Capacitance as a Measure of Pore Accessibility of Supercapacitor Electrodes_supp1-3677937.pdf
authors: Nandini Gupta
abstract: Despite extensive research over the past two decades, achieving simultaneous high energy and power density in supercapacitors (SCs) remains a challenge. The key lies in designing nanoporous networks that offer a very high surface area while also maximizing electrolyte-ion accessibility of the pores. However, experimentally measuring pore accessibility in supercapacitor electrodes is challenging. This study employs a Particle-In-Cell-based computational approach to simulate charge transport, governed by the Poisson-Nernst-Planck equation, from the bulk electrolyte into porous electrodes. The influence of applied potential and pore morphology on charge dynamics of the SC is investigated. Results reveal that highly tortuous pores, along with low porosity, significantly reduce the charging rate. However, this adverse effect is mitigated by applying higher voltages, making initially inaccessible pores accessible, and thereby enhancing capacitance. Hence, the extent of voltage-dependence of capacitance is introduced as a measurable index for quantifying the pore accessibility in SC electrodes. Experimental validation confirms that this metric effectively distinguishes pore networks of various SC electrodes in terms of pore accessibility, using simple capacitance measurements. The proposed metric thus serves as a practical tool for designing high-performance SC electrodes for energy storage in electric vehicles and renewable energy systems.

title: Digital Twin-Based Motion Planning and Virtual-Physical Mapping Control for an OMR Through Narrow Corners
authors: Hongjiu Yang, Guowei Zhao, Yuanqing Xia, Peng Li
abstract: In this paper, a digital twin system is presented to enable static motion planning, reliable digital simulation and dynamic state monitoring for an omnidirectional mobile robot (OMR) through narrow corners. A virtual robot is constructed to realize precise collision detection and real-time motion visualization for the OMR which is taken for a physical robot. A precise configuration space is obtained based on the virtual robot in a virtual environment which is used to map the real environment. A six-neighborhood three-dimensional <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$A^{*}$</tex-math></inline-formula> algorithm is designed to find the optimal path from an initial configuration to a target configuration. A virtual-physical mapping control strategy is designed to realize trajectory tracking and dynamic monitoring of the physical robot and the virtual robot. Experimental results show effectiveness of the proposed motion planning and virtual-physical mapping control for the OMR through narrow corners.

title: Benchmarking CNN Components in EZKL: A Layer-Level Analysis for EVM-Compatible Deployment
authors: George Chidera Akor, Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Zero-knowledge machine learning (ZKML) enables verifiable inference on private data, but deploying convolutional neural networks (CNNs) in production remains constrained by a multi-dimensional tradeoff between proof-generation latency, bandwidth consumption, and computational complexity. Existing ZKML frameworks and engineering blogs provide qualitative heuristics, yet practitioners lack systematic, layer-level measurements to guide architecture design under these constraints. This work presents the first systematic, layer-level characterization of CNN component costs in EZKL, a Halo2-based ZKML framework targeting EVM-compatible blockchains. We profile 8 feasible layer types (activations, pooling, normalization, and linear) across two EZKL precision settings (scale 7 and 10), measuring proof-generation time, proof size, circuit complexity, and peak memory in 26 experiments. We reveal critical infrastructure requirements by documenting 10 additional experiments that exceeded hardware limits (Conv2d operations, LayerNorm, and ReLU-based composite CNNs requiring <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>125\ \text{GB}$</tex> RAM). Contrary to conventional wisdom, we find that precision configuration has a negligible performance impact (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.002 \times$</tex> ratio), and that system RAM, not GPU VRAM, is the primary bottleneck. We release an open-source profiling toolkit and a public dataset that enable practitioners to query expected costs for their architectures and constraints.

title: Motion Trajectory Prediction and Multi-View Synthesis Based on CycleGAN
authors: Jun Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Aiming at the shortcomings of traditional models in dynamic scene adaptability and multi-view consistency, this paper proposes a synthesis method based on CycleGAN. By improving the generator structure of CycleGAN and introducing a temporal attention mechanism to capture the temporal dependencies of trajectories, a dual-discriminator system is designed to constrain trajectory realism and view consistency respectively. A composite loss function integrating adversarial loss, cycle consistency loss, and temporal smoothness loss is constructed to ensure the rationality of predicted trajectories and the accuracy of multi-view synthesis. Experiments on the ETH, UCY datasets, and a custom multiview trajectory dataset show that the proposed method reduces the ADE and FDE metrics by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 \% - 2 2 \%}$</tex> compared to traditional methods. The PSNR and SSIM of the multi-view synthesis results reach 28.6 dB and 0.89, respectively, demonstrating the method's effectiveness and superiority in complex dynamic scenes.

title: Comparison of YOLOv8 and RT-DETR for Road Damage Detection
authors: Chenghuan Jia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: To solve the road damage detection problem, various object detection models have been applied to this task due to the rapid development of deep learning, yet their relative strengths under consistent settings remain underexplored. This study presents a comparative analysis of YOLOv8-m and RT-DETR-I on the subset of the Road Damage Detection (RDD) 2022 dataset using identical training configurations. Both models achieved similar detection accuracy, with minor differences across standard metrics. Whereas, there were also differences in the results. First, Mosaic augmentation yielded model-dependent effects, slightly improving YOLOv8-m while reducing RT-DETR-1 performance. Furthermore, differences in category sensitivity and detection behavior were revealed by per-class heatmaps. RT-DETR performed better on particular categories according to the heatmaps. The findings explain the differences in the aspect of working principles of convolutional and transformer-based detectors.

title: Message from the General and Program Chairs: CW 2025
authors: N/A
abstract: Welcome to the 24th International Conference on Cyberworlds (CW 2025), hosted by Taiyuan University of Technology (TYUT) in Jinzhong, Shanxi Province, China, from October 14 to 16, 2025. As one of China's century-old and prestigious universities, TYUT takes great pride in organizing this international event. Shanxi, the venue of CW 2025, is renowned as a “treasure house of cultural relics,” offering a unique blend of rich historical heritage and vibrant modern development, which provides an inspiring backdrop for academic exchange and innovation.

title: Dual-Lane Voice-Preserving Real-Time Speech Translation: A System Architecture for Cross-Lingual Speaker Identity Retention
authors: Aryuemaan Chowdhury, Marpini Himabindu, Madhumithaa S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: In real-time multilingual systems, maintaining speaker identity is still a major technical difficulty. The majority of pipelines now in use handle voice synthesis and translation as separate operations, producing output that eliminates the vocal qualities of the original speaker. In order to preserve vocal identity during cross-language transmission, this research suggests a parallelized architecture. Through the use of a dual-lane framework with synchronized speaker embedding, we are able to maintain a voice similarity score greater than 0.85 in English, Spanish, and French while achieving end-to-end latency below 3.2 seconds. The suggested system incorporates ECAPA-TDNN embeddings feeding into NLLB translation, a hybrid ASR technique with fallback logic, and adaptive speech activity detection. YourTTS, which has been adjusted for cross-lingual adaptability, is used for synthesis. The architecture effectively maintains speaker identification without sacrificing the real-time limitations necessary for conversational usability, according to the results.

title: A Hybrid BiLSTM–Transformer–Attention Model for Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: S. M. Ahanaf Tahmid, Ling Lu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) remains challenging under speaker variability, recording conditions, and multi-dataset evaluation. To capture both local and global dependencies in emotional speech, this paper proposes a hybrid BiLSTM–Transformer–Attention model that integrates bidirectional sequence modeling with multi-head self-attention. Robustness is enhanced through 40-dimensional MFCC features and waveform-level data augmentation (noise injection, time shifting, and time stretching) applied exclusively during training. Experiments on the TESS and RAVDESS datasets use a fixed 80/10/10 train-validation-test split with a random seed of 42. The proposed model achieves 99.91% accuracy on TESS and 92.88% accuracy on RAVDESS, demonstrating consistent performance across datasets with differing levels of variability.

title: Tutorials
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 Open Source Modelling and Simulation of Energy Systems (OSMSES)

title: Research on Vehicle Multi-Working-Condition Braking Force Detection Platform Based on Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Ziyi Sun, Jiapeng Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of accurately evaluating vehicle braking performance under complex road and thermal environments by proposing a multi-workingcondition braking force detection platform integrated with the Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithm. The platform simulates road conditions with varying friction coefficients <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.15 \sim 0.85$</tex>, a span of 467 %) through replaceable modular friction plates, including dry asphalt, wet slippery surfaces, and snow/ice-covered roads. A built-in air-guided thermal control system in the support rollers enables active regulation of friction surface temperature. The PSO algorithm is introduced to identify key parameters of the LuGre dynamic friction model, while a one-dimensional transient heat conduction-PID feedback control model based on Fourier's law is established to achieve dynamic collaborative regulation of friction-thermal coupling factors. Simulation results show that the braking distance error under typical working conditions is less than 5%, and the temperature field uniformity is less than 1.5° C, significantly improving the efficiency and accuracy of braking performance testing in complex scenarios. The platform can be applied to braking system development, friction material screening, and vehicle multi-condition verification. Future research may integrate intelligent condition recognition and CAN bus communication to expand testing capabilities for advanced braking strategies such as ABS/ESC.

title: Design of a Rehabilitation Device for Hand Grip and Wrist Mobility with an Interactive Interface and IoT Capabilities
authors: Castalia Leiva-Cordero, Arys Carrasquilla-Batista, Gabriela Ortiz-León
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: This article presents a prototype of a rehabilitation system for grip strength and wrist mobility. It is part of the REHAB-GYM project in the Mechatronics and Automation Research Laboratory, whose main objective is to address the challenges related to the accessibility of rehabilitation devices in Costa Rica and to improve user progress tracking through technology. The outcomes allow for the construction of a rehabilitation device that measures the grip strength applied by the user and the angles of wrist movements using potentiometers as sensors and a Teensy microcontroller. The system includes a playful and interactive graphical interface, which is an Android application that allows the user to set goals and transmit information via IoT.

title: Research on Multi-Dimensional Prediction Methods Based on Random Forest and Xgboost Algorithms
authors: Huiwan Wang, Bingyan Li, Keyi Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: This study established a multi-dimensional prediction framework centred on random forest and XGBoost. Firstly, baseline prediction values are generated using a theoretical model, combined with time series decomposition and K-means clustering to extract multi-scale features, thereby completing system characteristic diagnosis. Secondly, three core models are constructed for different scenarios: when relying solely on historical data, the ‘Fourier basis function’ baseline is combined with an LSTM/TCN recurrent collaborative framework, integrating lagged time series features and sine/cosine periodic signals, and achieving multi-step prediction through the collaborative use of SAR linear and non-linear models. In multi-source data scenarios, an XGBoost residual correction framework is constructed to learn the mapping relationship between external parameters and prediction residuals, combined with quantile mapping algorithms to suppress systematic biases. For spatial scale adaptation, a dualpath downscaling framework combining ‘random forest + convolutional super-resolution network’ is proposed, using static factors to downscale coarse-grid fields, followed by Kriging interpolation and linear response coefficients to correct the results. This framework achieves high accuracy and strong robustness in complex scenario predictions through the deep integration of mechanistic models and data-driven algorithms, validated by significance tests, providing a transferable technical paradigm for related fields.

title: Stock Trading Prediction Based on the DQN Series Algorithm
authors: Yichuan Lu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Stock trading decisions hold significant importance in financial trading. Traditional stock trading decisions rely on fundamental analysis, technical analysis, or expert experience, which have shortcomings such as strong subjectivity. As technology advances, reinforcement learning techniques are also being applied to stock trading decisions. Based on the reinforcement learning theory, agents are allowed to autonomously learn stock trading strategies in the stock market environment to achieve maximum trading returns. To overcome the limitations of traditional methods, this paper uses three reinforcement learning algorithms: DQN, Double DQN (DDQN), and Dueling Double DQN (DDDQN) for stock trading decisions, and carry out tests and comparative analysis. The experimental results show that all three methods can achieve certain returns in the training set and the test set. Among them, the Dueling Double DQN algorithm performs the best overall. The Double DQN algorithm performs well, and the stability of the DQN algorithm needs to be improved. The effectiveness of the algorithm is verified, and the reproducible solution of stock trading decision is provided in this study.

title: Data-Driven Classification and Recognition Algorithm for Traditional Chinese Architectural Styles
authors: Sun Luyao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Aiming at the needs of automatic classification and recognition of traditional Chinese architectural styles, this paper proposes an original algorithm based on multi-scale attention fusion. The algorithm is data-driven. First, a dataset of 10,000 images covering 10 styles such as official buildings and Jiangnan folk houses is constructed. The data quality is improved through preprocessing and enhancement operations such as rotation, scaling, and brightness adjustment. Then, ResNet50 is used as the basic network to extract multi-scale features from conv2_x to conv5_x layers, which include both architectural detail features and global features. Then, channel and spatial attention mechanisms are designed to weight feature channels and spatial regions respectively, enhance the expression of key style features, and then fuse the processed multi-scale features into a comprehensive feature vector. Finally, the classification is completed through the fully connected layer combined with the softmax function. Experimental simulation shows that the algorithm has an accuracy of 92.5 %, a precision of 91.8 %, a recall of 92.1 %, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> value of 91.9 % on the test set. Compared with the traditional SIFT+SVM method (accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 2. 3 \%}$</tex>), it is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0. 2 \%}$</tex> higher, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6. 9 \%}$</tex> higher than ResNet50 (85.6 %), and 10.4 % higher than VGG16 (82.1 %). The ablation experiment verifies the effectiveness of multi-scale features and attention mechanisms. At the same time, the algorithm takes 8.5 hours to train 100 rounds, and the reasoning of a single image is only 0.025 seconds. It takes both accuracy and efficiency to provide an efficient and feasible technical solution for the classification of traditional Chinese architectural styles.

title: Technology Trend Forecasting and Idea Support Incorporating Generative AI Using Patent Information
authors: Yurie Okuhara, António Oliveira Nzinga René
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The rapid accumulation of patent documents presents challenges for traditional analysis methods due to the high dimensionality and volume of data. This study proposes an integrated framework that combines Transformer-based Sentence-BERT embeddings, UMAP dimensionality reduction, and k-medoids clustering to extract and visualize meaningful patent clusters. Selected clusters are analyzed through 2D and 3D visualizations to reveal core and peripheral technological concepts, semantic densities, and inter-cluster relationships. Furthermore, generative AI is employed to provide descriptive summaries and predictive insights for each cluster, enabling the identification of emerging technological trends and potential applications. The proposed approach enhances interpretability, supports strategic decision-making in research and development, and demonstrates a scalable method for AI-assisted patent landscape analysis.

title: PC-CDS: Real-Time Secure Authentication and Intelligent Steering for Hybrid TN-NTN Networks
authors: Abdul Samim, Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Jae Min Lee, Dong-Seong Kim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: The integration of Terrestrial Networks (TN) and Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) represents a cornerstone for 6G wireless systems, promising ubiquitous connectivity and enhanced quality of service. However, hybrid TN-NTN architectures introduce critical security vulnerabilities and complex network steering challenges. This paper proposes PureChainsecured Cognitive Dual-Steering (PC-CDS), integrating a custom blockchain authentication with Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)-based intelligent network steering. PureChain achieves a mean authentication delay of 15 ms with a 99.8% success rate, compared to 100 ms and 98.5% for traditional blockchain. The PPO-based cognitive agent dynamically orchestrates user connections between terrestrial gNBs and Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) satellites. Extensive simulations with 80 users, 25 terrestrial gNBs, and 3 VLEO satellites over 200 episodes demonstrate superior performance: 87.5% VLEO connectivity, network switching latency under 20 ms versus 100 ms for traditional blockchain, stable throughput at 90 Mbps versus 85 Mbps degradation, and controlled packet loss at 11% versus 17%. PPO outperforms Soft Actor-Critic, achieving a median reward of 160 compared to 140. PC-CDS lays the foundation for secure, intelligent 6G hybrid networks.

title: ATPG-Compliant Digital Modeling of MRAM for Cell-Aware Testing
authors: Arshid Nisar, Lorena Anghel, Gregory di Pendina
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: As memory complexity increases, traditional fault models are falling short in ensuring test quality. Cell-Aware (CA) testing, using post-layout transistor-level analysis, has proven effective for static random access memory (SRAM). The methodology uses detailed CA models which are further processed by Automatic Test Pattern Generators (ATPG) to create targeted test patterns that ensure high fault coverage. Despite its success in digital circuits, the application of ATPG-based CA testing to emerging non-volatile memory technologies such as Magnetic Random Access Memory (MRAM) remains largely unexplored. In this work, we propose a methodology dedicated to MRAM testing by developing a Verilog model derived from an analog SPICE netlist. This digital model accurately captures the architectural and behavioral traits of the underlying MRAM cell, ensuring compatibility with standard digital test tools such as Automatic Test Pattern Generators (ATPG). The proposed framework enables the generation of ATPG-based test patterns and supports fault simulation, paving the way for realistic, robust and accurate test strategies for MRAM devices.

title: Exploring Spatial Redundancy for Boosting the Performance of Stochastic Adcs in Deep Nanometer Cmos
authors: Rodrigo N. Wuerdig, Georges Gielen, Sergio Bampi
publish: 2026 IEEE 17th Latin America Symposium on Circuits and System (LASCAS)
abstract: Employing spatial redundancy allows stochastic ADCs to statistically mitigate non-idealities such as mismatch and noise without relying on precise analog components or calibration. This paper introduces the key principles of stochastic averaging, exploring the boundaries in scaled CMOS processes. We demonstrate that the commonly assumed SNDR improvement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \cdot \log_{10}(N) dB$</tex> reaches its limits when the quantization noise overwhelms the thermal noise. We highlight that stochastic yields meaningful improvements in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F o M_{S}$</tex> only when the power increases sub-linearly with the number of sampling channels. We identify architectural strategies that mitigate the power overhead. Finally, this work outlines key design trade-offs and guides when and how to deploy stochastic principles effectively in deepnanometer ADC architectures.

title: Research on Chinese News Text Classification Based on Improved TF-IDF and MNB
authors: Ting Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: With the rapid development of Internet technology, news data has experienced explosive growth. How to classify massive news quickly and accurately has become an important research topic in the field of information processing. As Chinese news texts are diverse and complex in content, the traditional TF-IDF algorithm merely weights word frequency by inverse document frequency when extracting thematic feature words, ignoring the influence of factors such as part of speech, word length, word position, and word span on word information content, as well as the impact of word distribution in different documents on the importance of keywords. This paper proposes a Chinese text classification model based on improved TF-IDF and MNB. On the basis of the traditional TF-IDF algorithm, the model integrates combined feature factors and dispersion to weight feature words, and applies the MNB classification algorithm to Chinese news text data classification. Experimental results show that the model can effectively improve the automatic classification performance of Chinese news texts, providing strong support for practical news classification scenarios.

title: Hybrid Blockchain-Assisted Federated Learning Intrusion Detection System for DoS Attacks in UAV Sensor Network
authors: Odinachi U. Nwankwo, Simeon Okechukwu Ajakwe, Dong-Seong Kim, Jae Min Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly deployed in mission-critical domains, raising significant security concerns. This paper presents a Federated Learning-based Intrusion Detection System (PureChain-FL) for UAV clients, focusing on Denial-of-Service (DoS) attack detection. To enhance trust and auditability, the proposed architecture integrates PureChain, a lightweight blockchain framework based on the Proof of Authority Association (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PoA}^{2}$</tex>) consensus algorithm for secure logging of detected threats. The system features client-based localized model training using the WSN-DS dataset and a global FL server for model aggregation. A smart contract governs audit logging, enabling verifiable and tamper-resistant record-keeping. Evaluation of multilayer perceptron (MLP), one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D CNN), and CNN combined with long-short-term memory (CNN-LSTM) models over 20 FL rounds with five clients using the Flower framework and TensorFlow in a Google collaborative environment revealed CNN-LSTM as the best-performing model with 99.3 % accuracy. Figures and diagrams illustrating system architecture, communication flow, and model training are integrated into the discussion for clarity. The results show promise in the system's effectiveness in privacypreserving and trustworthy intrusion detection for unmanned aerial wireless sensor networks.

title: Blue Finance Policy for Sustainable Development Goals: An Evidence-Based Approach
authors: Naglaa Fathy El Dessouky
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance (SIBF)
abstract: Blue finance has emerged as an instrumental policy tool for directing funds to associated ocean activities. It supports the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), specifically SDG 14. Maritime ecologies face increasing challenges, ranging from climate change to risky pollution and unsustainable resource utilization, which underscores the need for rational financial mechanisms to support sustainable ocean policies. Given the scarcity of research on this topic and its novelty, the paper aimed to deepen understanding and address that gap. The research methodology is framed in a qualitative, evidence-based approach that utilizes critical case study analysis to investigate marine sustainability and blue finance initiatives across selected MENA and GCC countries. The paper examined the conceptual foundations of the blue finance notion, its policy instruments and tools, its governance and networking framework, and its policy elaboration within international contexts. Through the analysis of these leading initiatives, the study examined the intervention approach, policy instruments, stakeholder engagement, and governance modes of the blue finance policy design. The study emphasized that, despite contextual differences, countries consistently aligned their initiatives with international standards for the Sustainable Development Goals. The paper concluded with practical recommendations to strengthen public policy design, enhance policymakers' interventions in policy formulation and implementation, improve stakeholders' governance, and inform impact strategies.

title: Research on Ceramic Product Classification and Localization Algorithm Based on Deep Learning
authors: Yihong Yuan, Yanke Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Due to uncertainties in the manufacturing process and interference from human factors, various anomalies can occur in ceramic products during production. These anomalies not only affect the quality of the products but may also pose a threat to user safety. Therefore, using anomaly detection methods is crucial for improving the quality and safety of ceramic products. In light of this, this paper proposes a target detection algorithm based on YOLOv8 for the classification and localization of ceramic products, leveraging computer vision detection technology. The algorithm employs a lightweight model and incorporates an attention mechanism, ensuring high accuracy while simultaneously enhancing the inference speed. This makes the algorithm more accurate and efficient in collecting object category information and key point location information. The improved algorithm is then tested, and experimental results demonstrate a significant improvement in both accuracy and speed, achieving an average precision of 97.8% on the test set. Compared to manual inspection, this method improves efficiency and accuracy and addresses the long-tailed distribution problem of positive and negative samples encountered in actual industrial production.

title: Field Oriented Control of PMSM Drive: A Comparative Study of Sinusoidal and Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation Techniques
authors: Manish Kumar, Vinod Kumar Bussa, Tarakanath Kobaku, R Jeyasenthil
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSMs) are multipurpose motors as they can be applied in electric vehicles, robotics, and factory automation due to a broad range of operations as highly efficient and compact inside and a high extent of torque feature. The control of PMSMs implies a high level of precision as well to allow the maximum level of performance to be preferred under various operational circumstances. This paper will place a comparison of two popular modulation techniques: Sinusoidal Pulse Width Modulation (SPWM) and Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation (SVPWM) in a Field-Oriented Control (FOC) model. The test is applied on dynamic response, torque ripple, harmonic distortion and overall efficiency. The simplest of these SPWM generates the comparison between sinusoidal references and a high frequency (HF) triangular carrier to generate switching pulses, but has the poorest overall harmonic distortion (THD) and poorest DC bus usage. On the other hand, SVPWM calculates the optimum voltage vectors, and this is used to offer a superior voltage utilization and low THD. Simulation proves that the SVPWM can be better in the case of a lower torque ripple as well as the quality of harmonics hence can be utilized in high-performance applications. However, in less complex systems where the performance requirements are moderate, it is possible to employ SPWM. The current research can be useful in selecting the modulation techniques that are expected to be employed in the PMSM-based controllers.

title: Numerical Modeling of Radiosonde-Assisted Compact Ranges for Electromagnetic Propagation under Atmospheric Variability
authors: Sujit Kumar Chakravarty, Amul Batra, Mohd Imran Ansari, Vijay Kumar Soni
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th Uttar Pradesh Section International Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPCON)
abstract: Conventional compact-range electromagnetic (EM) characterization assumes homogeneous atmospheric conditions and ignores thermodynamic gradients that distort propagation. In this paper, we propose a RadiosondeAssisted Compact Range (RACR) framework to integrate real-time temperature, pressure, and humidity profiles into Maxwell-based full-wave simulations using continuous altitude-dependent refractivity mapping. RACR preserves vertical dielectric gradients while enabling closed-loop validation through S-parameter analysis, which is not possible with discrete-layer or ITU-based models. The proposed method reduces transmission prediction RMSE by 61 % across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-5 \text{GHz}$</tex>, enhances correlation with measurements to 0.97, and limits phase-delay deviation to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 1.2 \%$</tex>. The results demonstrate enhanced beam coherence and transmission fidelity in stratified and humid atmospheres, paving the way for weather-adaptive electromagnetic calibration.

title: Saliency-Guided YOLO11 for Robust Player Detection in Counterstrike 2 Game Scenes
authors: Chenxi Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Control (PEEEC)
abstract: Detecting players in first-person shooting (FPS) games such as Counterstrike 2 (CS2) is a challenge mission due to rapid movements, complex lighting, and varied character poses. To solve these difficulties, this paper introduces a Saliency-Guided YOLO11 framework that augments standard detection by integrating a visual saliency attention branch. This extended module enables the network to learn both object localization and saliency prediction simultaneously, guiding the detector to emphasize crucial visual cues like player contours and weapon edges. The model is trained on a custom CS2 dataset with diverse data augmentation strategies-such as Mosaic, Mix-up, and random perspective-to better mimic dynamic in-game environments. Experimental evaluations shows that our method achieves an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{mAP} @ 0.5$</tex> of 0.784, outperforming the baseline YOLO11 by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+2.6 \%$</tex>, and showing improved resilience against occlusion, low contrast, and fastmotion conditions. Further analysis through Precision-Recall and F1-Confidence curves indicates steadier detection confidence and fewer false positives. The results suggest that giving detection models a sense of visual saliency helps them see and recognize game scenes more accurately in real time.

title: Energy Communities as the Next Step in Smart Grids: A Decision-Making Approach for Decentralized Energy Exchange
authors: David González-Asenjo, Aarón Rodríguez-Marrero, Álvaro Galerón-Saiz, Javier Sedano
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Power Engineering (AIPE)
abstract: Digitalization of electricity infrastructures has accelerated the evolution of Smart Grids, with Energy Communities representing a pivotal advancement in decentralized energy management. This study introduces a decision-making framework that optimizes energy exchanges through a dynamic evaluation process, adapting in real time to participants' preferences on energy usage, considered as User Policies (UPs). To achieve optimal energy coordination, the proposed methodology incorporates demand response strategies, enabling participants to regulate consumption through controllable loads and storage assets. A structured multi-objective optimization approach formalizes decision-making, embedding the five key User Policies (i.e. economic efficiency, peak power mitigation, sustainability, comfort, and energy efficiency) into an evaluation function that quantifies prosumer preferences. Fairness and arbitrage mechanisms are employed to maintain system equilibrium and prevent inefficiencies in energy trading to achieve the goal of long-term sustainability. The framework dynamically reacts to fluctuations, optimizing energy distribution while maintaining system stability and long-term adaptability. Computationally, the framework leverages genetic optimization techniques to enhance prediction accuracy and decision-making efficiency. The results demonstrate significant improvements in energy allocation, peak demand reduction, and financial optimization, validating the framework's effectiveness. By systematically aligning individual and collective energy objectives, this methodology provides a scalable and adaptive approach to decentralized energy management, reinforcing the resilience and intelligence of next-generation Smart Grids.

title: EfficientNetB0-based Feature Extraction for Single and Double-Hand Mudra Recognition
authors: Prathviraj Chavan, Pooja Choudhary, Shubhi Upadhyay, Saniya Devarshi, Sumitra Sureliya, Awanit Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Bharatanatyam is an ancient classical dance form of India that depicts mudras as a systemic language representing emotions, storytelling, and spiritual expression. Modern learners find it challenging to interpret these correctly due to their visual complexity and minute variations of the hands forming different mudras. We propose a deep-learning-based single-hand and double-hand Bharatanatyam mudra recognition framework using a dataset collected for the purpose. We adopt EfficientNetB0 for feature extraction and classification purposes and implement the deep network using PyTorch. Further, the system is deployed with a modern FastAPI backend and React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS frontend. Our dataset consists of 51 mudras (28 single-hand, 23 double-hand) with 300 images in each class, which our team has fully collected and annotated. The model obtains an accuracy of 99.71 % and 99.89 % for single- and double-hand mudra recognitions, respectively, which is exceptionally good. This work represents one of the pioneering attempts to develop an automated recognition framework of Bharatanatyam mudras using deep learning, hence contributing to the preservation of culture, digital learning, and performing-arts education.

title: AI-based Emergency Help Service
authors: Afraim Uzzaman Romeo, Maisha Rahman Diya, Ataur Rahman, Mahady Hasan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: In today's world, timely response during emergencies is a matter of life and death. This paper highlights the critical need for a fast and reliable emergency help system that is capable of alerting emergency contacts, authorities, and first aid services immediately. The goal of this system is to build a safe and efficient platform capable of sending emergency notifications, providing real-time location, establishing secure communication channels, offline communication, and assisting users with AIdriven first aid assistance. In this paper, we conducted a survey and an interview. In our survey, we wanted to find out if people actually needed the mobile app in emergency situations. After developing a user interface design, we conducted an interview for user satisfaction to understand how well it worked and what could be improved. Based on their feedback, we made some changes. Their insights helped us refine the design further, were reliable, and easy to use. The system was created following Agile methodology, with iterative design and regular user feedback. In short, the Emergency Help Service system adequately improves emergency response. Future improvements will aim to integrate IoT devices, predictive AI, and multiple communication channels to make the platform stronger and smarter.

title: Bengali Speech Based Medical Assistant Using Retrieval Augmented Generation for Symptom-Based Diagnosis and Treatment
authors: Dhruba Chowdhury, Saklain Abdullah, Riad Hossain, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin, M. M. Musharaf Hussain
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Thousands of rural and marginalized people in Bangladesh still face serious barriers to accessing basic healthcare due to language limitations, low literacy, and social prejudice. Women and adolescent girls are especially affected, as they often lack a safe and private way to ask health-related questions in their native language. Since most digital health systems are designed primarily in English, a large number of Bengali speakers remain excluded from these services. To address this challenge, this paper presents a Bengali voice-based medical assistant developed using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The system understands spoken Bengali, retrieves trusted medical information from a verified knowledge base, and generates clear and reliable healthcare advice. The proposed framework integrates Speech-to-Text, hybrid information retrieval (fuzzy matching for direct disease queries and semantic search using FAISS for symptombased queries), and a large language model to provide an end-to-end speech-to-speech healthcare assistant. The system was evaluated on a doctor-verified dataset of 100 diseases and symptom profiles, achieving a macro F1-score of 0.93. This performance is significantly higher than approaches relying only on keyword search, semantic retrieval, or a language model without retrieval support. The results demonstrate that combining knowledge-based retrieval with generative AI can deliver safe, accurate, and understandable medical guidance in Bengali.

title: A Hyperparameter Exploration of LSTM Models for U.S. Airline Tweet Sentiment Analysis
authors: Krish Pillai, Atharva Kawtikwar, Masooda Modak
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Advancement in Futuristic Technologies (ICAFT)
abstract: This paper examines how deep learning can be used to analyze the reviews of United States airlines on Twitter in terms of sentiment analysis. Although Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks are highly applicable in sequence-based tasks such as text classification, their execution highly relies on the choice of hyperparameters. The paper itself is a systematic experimental research, which aims to measure the influence of major hyperparameters on the accuracy of the model. We search a grid of 18 different model configurations, including an experimentation of three optimizers (Adam, RMSProp, SGD), three dropout rates (0.2, 0.4, 0.6), and two LSTM activation functions (tanh, relu). The models are trained and tested on a dataset consisting of more than 14000 labelled tweets. We find that the choice of optimizer and activation function have a considerable effect on test accuracy, with the Adam and RMSProp optimization functions always better than the SGD and the tanh activation function more effective. The current study offers empirical results that can be used to select the best hyperparameters to use in sentiment analysis of the airline industry.

title: Explainable Hybrid PSO-SVM Framework for EEG-Based Cognitive Confusion Detection
authors: Ravichander Janapati, Vasupalli Manoj, Usha Desai, Viswaprakash Babu, Ghousia Sanober Sabreen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Cognitive confusion is an urgent affective condition that affects learning effectiveness and decision-making performance. Identification of such states with the help of electroencephalography (EEG) helps in human cognitive processes and allow the adaptation of educational or neurofeedback systems. This study proposes an explainable hybrid approach to Particle Swarm Optimization-Support Vector Machine (PSO-SVM) to detect the presence of confusion states in a dataset of publicly available Wanghaohan Confused Student EEG Brainwave Dataset with sustainable accuracy and interpretability. In this study, EEG data recorded through a single-channel NeuroSky MindSet device, which has power spectral features, such as delta, theta, alpha, beta, and gamma, as well as the indices of attention and meditation during the e-learning activities. PSO is used in the proposed framework to optimize the SVM hyperparameters and subsets of features, which guarantees better generalization and less overfitting. The model attained high classification rates in identifying the confounded and non-confounded cognitive states compared to baseline machine learning classifiers. Moreover, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) models, such as SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and feature importance analysis, were combined to understand the value of each EEG feature in the model decision. The findings prove that high-frequency gamma and attention scores are the prevailing signs of confusion. The proposed PSO-SVM-XAI system is accurate as well as transparent, providing scalability of intelligent human-computer interaction and adaptive learning systems based on monitoring the cognitive state.

title: Organizing Committee
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Trends and Innovations in ICT (ICEI)

title: Quantum Computing Approaches for Option Pricing Models
authors: Chandrashekhar Ramtirthkar, Sharyu Ikhar, Ananta Narayana, Jaganathan D, Sodiq Ziyodullayev, Adilbaev Ismayıl Bekbaulievich
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: The article provides a literature review and analysis of quantum computing methods in the context of option pricing, comparing them against a strict classical benchmark and recommending a viable way forward to quantum computing implementation. We assess the capabilities and the weaknesses of Windows-choice models closed-form legacy formulae of vanillas, lattice/PDE true hockeygoal models of early exercise, and Monte Carlo models of flexibleness and point out where classical run attracted and precision restraints are shown to apply in the new generation(s): complexity of variables, in this path dependence, in a portfolio composing. We proceed to describe quantum algorithms that are based on quantum Monte Carlo which is centered on amplitude-estimation-based algorithms to cut down on contact with sampling; avoiding the scale of classical Monte Carlo and are particularly appealing to complex derivatives; we describe circuit components to prepare states, encode payoffs, and approximate expectations. The study establishes research priorities that achieve scale implies: depth frequently used to design loaders in, and variants of payoff circuits, workflow standardizations that exchange the exchange of error-resilient quantum modules and technological risk engines in the NISQ age. In general, the paper offers a single source of information on classical-quantum option pricing, clean insertion architecture to deploy hybrids and a roadmap between demonstrations and reliable production.

title: Day-ahead Scheduling and Reserve Optimization Strategy for Renewable Energy
authors: Peng Yuan, Junjie Sun, Xinwei Li, Dai Cui, Fei Xu, Ling Hao
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: The escalating penetration of renewable energy (RE) sources, notably photovoltaic (PV) and wind power, introduces substantial operational complexities for day-ahead scheduling and reserve management in modern power systems, owing to the inherent unpredictability of $\mathbf{R E}$ generation. To address the above challenges, this study proposes a collaborative optimization strategy for day-ahead scheduling and reserve configuration that explicitly accounts for RE uncertainty. The methodology is structured into three sequential stages: first, the probability distribution of RE forecast errors is derived through statistical analysis of historical data, providing a quantitative basis for characterizing uncertainty; second, reserve requirements are systematically determined based on the obtained error distribution to mitigate potential imbalances between supply and demand; and finally, a comprehensive cost-minimization model is developed, incorporating risk costs to hedge against uncertainties. The proposed model rigorously enforces constraints related to power balance, operational limits of thermal units, energy storage systems (ESSs), and grid interactions, while integrating reserve constraints to ensure system stability. Results of the simulations indicate that the proposed day-ahead scheduling framework enables an optimal allocation of reserve capacity, effectively balancing operational security and economic efficiency under uncertain RE conditions.

title: Visualization and Search Method for Secondary Full Circuit of Substation Based on SDD and Forward-Probing and Backward-Tracking Search Algorithm
authors: Jin Li, Weiguo Yu, Yuansheng Chen, Hongjun Liu
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problem that existing SCD analysis software and methods cannot achieve complete visualization of the secondary circuit of substations, a visualization and search method for the secondary full circuit of substations based on SDD and forward-probing and backward-tracking search algorithm is proposed. Firstly, a secondary circuit digital description file (SDD) for the secondary circuit is defined, which includes the secondary system grid identification system code and secondary circuit cable information based on the overall fiber optic physical circuit configuration file. Then, combining the SSD model and the grid information model (GIM), a visualization system for the secondary full circuit of the substation is constructed, which includes geometric element intersection calculation, model cutting, model fitting, and other steps. Finally, the forward-probing and backward-tracking search algorithm is used to search for the secondary circuit of the substation, which involves searching for the target point from the source point through forward search and obtaining the connected path between the two points through backtracking, ensuring fast and accurate search for the secondary circuit. Experimental tests are conducted on the proposed method based on the secondary circuit of a 220 kV substation. The results shows that when the number of circuits is 120, the visualization and search accuracies are about 88% and 93%, respectively. The search time does not exceed 10s, which can meet the needs of intelligent development of substations.

title: Improved Droop Control Strategy for Islanded Operation of Energy Storage Converter for Underground Local Ventilation Fan
authors: Fangjun Han
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: To make sure the continuous and reliable power supply of local ventilation fan in underground coal mine, for the problem of traditional inductive sag control failure due to the high resistance-inductance ratio of underground low-voltage distribution network, this paper puts forward a three-loop P-U sag power control strategy based on the calibration of the low-voltage distribution network, and builds a two-stage storage converter emergency power supply model in Matlab/Simulink for simulation and validation. A two-stage energy storage converter emergency power supply model is constructed in Matlab/Simulink for simulation verification. The results reveal that the proposed control strategy can effectively maintain the voltage and frequency stability of the islanded system, realize the precise distribution and fast regulation of power, with a low total harmonic distortion rate of the output current and a good dynamic response, which ensures the stable operation of the energy storage converter in the emergency power supply mode, so as to provide uninterrupted electric power support for the local ventilator, and can effectively improve the level of safe production in coal mines.

title: Novel and Efficient Plant Disease Prediction Using ViT and YOLOv8 Deep Learning Models
authors: S. Vetriselvi, A. Balamurali, Shakhboz Meylikulov, Yadgarova Nazokat Saparbayevna, Anorgul Ashirova, B. Venkataramanaiah
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Plant health is affected by several abnormal conditions which indicate virus, bacteria and fungus caused by pathogens. Environment conditions cause plant disease such as water stress, pollution and nutrient lack. These diseases cause several symptoms on plants such as spots, stunted growth and wilting. Sanitation is used for curing plants from plant diseases. Plant diseases need to be monitored frequently and early disease prediction can save plant health. Image processing techniques used earlier for plant disease prediction and machine learning techniques replace image processing techniques in disease prediction. Deep learning models are introduced and show accurate results over machine learning. Proposed research uses YOLOv8, Convolution Neural Network(CNN) and Vision Transformer(ViT) deep learning models to predict plant diseases. YOLOv8 deep learning model predicts plant diseases with accuracy 98% over lower accuracy results from CNN and ViT. Proposed research is useful for agriculture field where plant diseases can be predicted more accurate than other existing works.

title: High-Accuracy Facial Emotion Recognition for Visually Impaired Using Multi-Stage Transfer Learning and Attention-Based CNN
authors: P. Kalyanasundaram, P. Meenakshi Devi, N Saravanan, R. Bharanitharan, B. Bharath, Hariprasath M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: Aim: To design an accurate facial emotion recognition system that assists visually impaired users by identifying emotions during conversation using an attentionbased CNN and multi-stage transfer learning approach. Materials and Methods: Group 1 is a lightweight CNN model based on partial transfer learning. Group 2 is a multi-stage transfer learning model with an attention-based CNN. A sample size of 2000 images from the FER+ dataset is used for each group, and the models are evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, class-wise recognition rate, and confusionmatrix performance. Result: The propose–d emotion recognition model identified facial emotions with the overall accuracy of 82.80%. The performance was reliable across different emotion classes. Conclusion: The developed facial emotion recognition model has achieved multiple evaluation matrix helps assist visually impaired users to understand people emotions during social interactions.

title: ADODroid: A Static Detection Model for Android Malware Based on GCN and BiLSTM
authors: Shuoyuan Zhang, Xinyou Zhang, Zonghai Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: This article proposes a new Android malware detection model ADODroid. The model extracts three types of software features through static analysis: API call graph, data flow, and opcode sequence. The API call graph provides spatial semantic information of the software, the opcode sequence provides contextual semantic information of the software, and the data flow can enhance the integrity of spatial semantic features. Abstracting the API call graph to enhance the model's anti-obfuscation ability and reduce computational complexity, then embedding the data flow into the graph to enrich the spatial semantics of features, while converting the opcode sequence into embedding vectors. Finally, inject the graph data and embedding vectors into a fusion neural network composed of GCN and BiLSTM, and output the classification results. The experimental results on the AndroZoo and VirusShare datasets show that the detection precision of the trained ADODroid can reach 96%, while having stronger robustness than other similar methods.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Method for Separating Overlapping ADS-B Signals in Continuous Single-Antenna Reception
authors: Chenchen Wang, Wenyi Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast (ADS-B) signals are prone to in-air overlapping, which leads to decoding failures. Most existing deep learning-based methods are trained and evaluated on pre-segmented overlapping signals, which differs from real reception scenarios. In practice, continuous signals received by a single antenna are dominated by noise and interference, with only a few randomly occurring ADS-B signal segments. To address this issue, an end-to-end time-domain ADS-B signal separation method is proposed to process continuous observation signals directly. This work is the first to introduce the overlap-add (OLA) strategy into this task, enabling direct recovery of overlapping signals. Considering the low proportion of effective signals in continuous data and the insufficient constraints of conventional scale-invariant source-to-noise ratio (SI-SNR) loss, a log-envelope region-weighted SI-SNR (LERW-SNR) loss is designed. The proposed loss enhances the emphasis on effective signal regions during training while suppressing the influence of background noise. Experimental results on real-world continuous observation signals show that, compared with the standard SI-SNR loss, the proposed method improves cyclic redundancy check (CRC) pass rates by 3.27% and 11.24% on Dual-Path Recurrent Neural Network (DPRNN) and Separation Transformer (Sepformer) networks, respectively. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method for continuous signals received by a single antenna.

title: Motion-Robust Vital Signs Detection Using Millimeter-Wave FMCW Radar Based on DCT-Wavelet Compressed Sensing*
authors: Xin Xiang, Bo Yang, Zehua Nie, Fu Zhang, Kunming Zhou, Ling Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the increasing application of millimeter-wave radar technology in non-contact health monitoring, motion interference has become a core challenge limiting its accuracy. This paper proposes an adaptive motion perception and signal reconstruction method based on Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW) radar, aiming to achieve robust extraction of respiration and heartbeat signals in dynamic scenarios. The method first utilizes range-dimension Fast Fourier Transform (FFT), Moving Target Indication (MTI), and Constant False Alarm Rate (CA-CFAR) detection to precisely locate the target's chest range bin, effectively overcoming range migration caused by motion. Subsequently, the Extended Differentiate and Cross-Multiply (DACM) algorithm is employed for phase demodulation to avoid phase wrapping distortion. The core contribution lies in the design of a motion detection mechanism based on phase variance. For identified motion interference signals, a Compressed Sensing (CS) reconstruction algorithm combining Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) and Wavelet bases is proposed. This algorithm effectively suppresses motion noise while accurately reconstructing vital sign signals. Experiments conducted on a platform equipped with a TI IWR6843 radar show that in various motion scenarios, such as walking, the average detection accuracy of respiratory rate and heart rate reached 95.0% and 95.9%, respectively, with Mean Absolute Errors (MAE) of 2.48 BPM and 2.74 BPM. These results verify the robustness and accuracy of the proposed method in dynamic environments, providing reliable technical support for continuous health monitoring.

title: Deep Learning-Powered Probing Architecture for Intelligent Anomalous Event Tracking
authors: S. Munirullah, Krishnaraj Natarajan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: The expansion of digital ecosystems has intensified the frequency and complexity of anomalous behaviors across networks, applications, and human interactions. Traditional rule-based detection systems struggle to adapt to evolving threats, resulting in delayed incident response and reduced reliability. This study proposes ProbeAIT, an AI-driven framework for real-time anomalous event identification, explanation, and tracking. ProbeAIT integrates multi-stage feature probing for granular behavioral analysis, temporal pattern extraction via dual-stream processing, and adaptive learning modules combining statistical baselines, deep learning classifiers, and contextual correlation engines to distinguish normal from malicious deviations while remaining interpretable. Empirical evaluation across diverse datasets demonstrates ProbeAIT's superior performance: 96.4% detection accuracy, 97.1% recall, 15% reduction in false positives, and 38 FPS real-time processing compared to CNNand transformer-based baselines. Its self-evolving architecture enables dynamic pattern adaptation. These results establish ProbeAIT as a scalable, intelligent solution for modern anomaly monitoring, bridging gaps in adaptability, interpretability, and operational efficiency.

title: A Transient Stability Assessment Model Based on the Broad Learning System
authors: Shenghong Liu, Bo Wei, Jinghao Zhang, Zhengdan Wang, Kuiping Ding, Xiyuan Zhang, Junyi Bian, Jun Liu
publish: 2025 International Power and Electrical Engineering Conference (IPEE)
abstract: Accurate online assessment of transient stability remains a key challenge in power system analysis. To overcome the limitations of time-domain simulation and direct methods in practical use, this paper proposes a data-driven model based on the Broad Learning System (BLS). A data-driven framework for online transient stability assessment is established, and feature augmentation is achieved using the algorithmic principles of BLS. An online incremental learning model is developed for transient stability assessment. Simulation studies on the IEEE 39-bus system demonstrate that the proposed BLS-based model achieves superior accuracy and shorter training time compared to several benchmark models, highlighting its potential for practical engineering applications.

title: Problem Formalization and Benchmarking of Multimodal Learning for Text, Image, Audio, and Video Understanding to Promote Inclusive and Sustainable Knowledge Access
authors: K. Ramu Dev Burman, Moorthy Agoramoorthy
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The Multimodal learning, being a combination of text, image, audio and video information has come up as the essential paradigm of creating intelligent and context sensitive information system. They are also being used more in education, healthcare, governance, and media, where the successful combination of various types of data can be used to improve knowledge, decision-making, and accessibility. Although there has been much progress, there have been a number of challenges such as the absence of standard definitions of the problem, disparities between benchmarking protocols and the problem of equitable and inclusive knowledge access. The current paper will provide a detailed system toward formalizing and benchmarking of multimodal learning models, especially in terms of interpretability, fairness, and resource efficiency. Representative architectures are analyzed in a systematic manner, in a variety of modalities, to measure performance, robustness as well as computation requirements trade-offs. The results offer viable lessons to researchers and practitioners in order to create not only accurate and scalable multimodal systems but also socially accountable and able to promote inclusive knowledge sharing. Besides, the research points out the most influential areas where research should focus in the future to develop effective, accessible, and sustainable multimodal models.

title: Hierarchical Session-Aware Recommendation Model Based on Temporal Reasoning
authors: Li QinGhai, Li Yanhong, Xu Renyi, Li Yan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive and Intelligent Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Session-Based Recommendation Systems (SRS) aim to predict users' next behaviors via anonymous sessions, with the core challenge of simulating the temporal relationships of user behaviors and depicting user interests using limited sessions. Existing methods mostly model behavior patterns based on the temporal relationships of adjacent items in sessions and selectively aggregate item information as user interests. This paper proposes a Temporal Reasoning-based Hierarchical Session-Aware Recommendation Model (TRHSR): on one hand, it breaks the assumption of “adjacent items being relevant”, infers dependencies between items to learn more flexible temporal relationships; on the other hand, it aggregates information at both the item and item feature levels to achieve fine-grained interest inference. Experiments on two public datasets (Diginetica and Retailrocket) show that the model outperforms other benchmark models, verifying its effectiveness.

title: Efficient Spectrum Utilization in Cognitive Radio Networks Using Advanced Cluster-Assisted Spectrum Sensing Techniques
authors: Savita, Aarif Ahamed S, Keerti Rai
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science and Statistics (MACS)
abstract: Cognitive Radio Networks (CRNs) have emerged as a promising solution to address spectrum scarcity and inefficient spectrum utilization in modern wireless communication systems. However, an essential challenge in CRNs is achieving accurate and efficient spectrum sensing, which ensures reliable detection of available spectrum opportunities while minimizing false alarms and missed detections. This research proposes an advanced Cluster-Assisted Spectrum Sensing (CASS) technique that leverages a Machine Learning (ML)-based approach to enhance detection performance while reducing energy consumption. An Adaptive Tunicate Swarm-optimized Scalable Elman neural network (ATS-SENN) is introduced to improve spectrum gap identification, ensuring reliable detection of Primary User (PU) activity. By aggregating data at Cluster Heads (CHs), the suggested clustering mechanism reduces the burden on the fusion center (FC) by grouping Secondary Users (SUs) according to similarity criteria. Adaptive Tunicate Swarm Optimization (ATSO) is used to fine-tune the network's parameters dynamically, improving its ability to detect available spectrum gaps accurately. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the Hybrid Support Vector Machine-Red Deer Algorithm (SVM-RDA) achieves a high probability of finding solutions and reduces the likelihood of error to a low level, outperforming conventional clustering techniques in both accuracy and computational efficiency. These findings highlight the effectiveness of the proposed CASS framework in enhancing spectrum, maximizing Energy Efficiency (EE), and minimizing probability error, combined with a Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR) of 0.9548, and maximizing the throughput and optimizing spectrum sensing in CRNs.

title: Blockchain-Based Auditable Power Data Sharing Scheme
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
abstract: To facilitate the intelligent and digital transformation of the power grid, Grid Corporations have undertaken the construction of a data center-oriented infrastructure. This architecture enables the real-time acquisition, analysis, and sharing of massive volumes of electricity data, thereby significantly enhancing the efficiency of grid dispatching and the utilization of energy resources. However, the existing power data-sharing systems suffer from a lack of comprehensive auditing mechanisms, giving rise to several critical risks—including difficulty in tracking access behaviors, opacity in data transmission processes, and challenges in tracing the source of data leakage. These issues collectively compromise the trustworthiness and operational stability of the overall system. In response to these concerns, this paper proposes a blockchain-based auditable data-sharing scheme, which integrates smart contracts and cryptographic primitives to implement decentralized access control and behavioral auditing. The proposed solution ensures not only secure and traceable data interactions but also maintains favorable performance and scalability, as validated through experimental evaluation.

title: Closed-form High-order Parameter Estimation for Polarimetric Arrays of Arbitrary Configurations
authors: Ruiqian Liao, Fuqian Nie, Hang Zhou, Yaxing Yue
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Vision, Image and Signal Processing (ICVISP)
abstract: High-order parameter estimation using a polarimetric array benefits from enhanced effective aperture thanks to the exploitation of high-order statistics (HOS). However, when dealing with polarimetric arrays of arbitrary configurations, the estimation procedure faces high computational complexity because the involved parameters, namely direction-of-arrival and polarization, are coupled and require multi-dimensional spectral searching. To address this, we study the closed-form high-order parameter estimation problem for polarimetric arrays by leveraging the polarimetric element space technique. In our proposed approach, we present a general framework to derive the polynomial coefficients involved in the estimation procedure. Thanks to the proposed approach that combines closed-form estimation with HOS, our method achieves low computational complexity while maintaining high effective aperture. Simulation results validate the performance advantages of the proposed approach.

title: Standardized Approach for Assessing Wireless IoT Devices in Millimeter-Wave Industrial Work-Cell Channels
authors: Mohamed Kashef, Kate A. Remley, Paritosh Manurkar, Joshua M. Kast, Iyemeh E. Uchendu, Robert D. Horansky, Dylan Williams, Matthew Simons, Michael Frey, Lucas Koepke, Richard Candell
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: We present a standardized, repeatable test approach that replicates key features of measured industrial environments in a fully-characterized hybrid test chamber. The hybrid chamber consists of an anechoic chamber along with movable metallic spheres that provide discrete reconfigurable multipath components. The chamber is characterized by a synthetic-aperture system providing a reference measurement that captures the non-idealities of the chamber along with the intended spatial multipath, completing characterization of the test environment. A key feature of our approach is the propagation of correlated uncertainties through to the final system response – including uncertainties specific to synthetic apertures such as cable bending due to scanning and inexact positioner locations. The uncertainty analysis provides statistically accurate knowledge of the environment, allowing separation of measurement errors from errors related to the device or system under test. We have also extracted three exemplar channels that capture key features of a work-cell-sized industrial environment from measurements made with the same synthetic aperture. Each exemplar channel presents different, realistic angular-diverse multipath challenges to a millimeter-wave internet-of-things device, subsystem, or active antenna under test. This allows engineers to evaluate their hardware designs in a physical, repeatable test environment and also facilitates future standardization of test methods for these and other millimeter-wave wireless devices.

title: Spatio-Temporal Delay Aware Causality: A Self-Interpretable Framework for Soft Sensing
authors: Xueqiong Tian, Han Liu, Runyuan Guo, Lingyun Wei, Ding Liu, Youmin Zhang
abstract: With the increasing complexity of industrial systems and process data, deep learning has achieved superior performance in soft sensing but remains constrained by limited interpretability. Most existing interpretability techniques are correlation based, capturing statistical dependencies but providing little insight into underlying mechanisms. Causal modeling, by contrast, offers stronger interpretability by revealing directional and temporal influences, thereby improving both reliability and understanding. Although some recent methods consider time delays, their treatment of lags remains coarse and limited, and cannot adequately capture heterogeneous cross variable delay patterns in industrial time series.
